### ATL_Paleo — 2013-02-03T20:20:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-73979>

Recently I received my Magnetico 10 gauss sleep pad. After sleeping 9 nights on it, I am very impressed. I have noticed 3 primary benefits:

1\. Sleep quality is improving. In particular, I am experiencing more "deep" sleep.
2\. My appetite has been reduced. I am eating less food, and thoughts of food between my two meals have declined.
3\. Strength at the gym has improved some, and recovery from weight lifting is definitely better.

These benefits are also benefits of doing CT. I am gaining these benefits without doing any CT recently.
(I am not advocating no CT; this is my n=1 experiment .... also I am not trying to lose any body fat or weight.)

As Dr. Kruse says "question everything" ... so I started w/ 10 gauss instead of 20 gauss. Since their system is modular, I can add the additional 10 gauss pad in the future if I need.

### marthabob2u — 2013-02-04T20:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-74110>

Some people may smell snake oil, but we have used a Nikken magnetic mattress at home and a portable Nikken magnetic mattress pad for travelling for years and have always felt it was beneficial. When I was a cleaning and restoration tech and got sore muscles from moving furniture and carrying an 84 pound machine up and down stairs and felt I would not be able to move the next day, I always woke up in fine shape, able to work again.

My scientist sister did an accidental double-blind study when they borrowed the pad. Her husband loved it so she tried it for a couple of nights. The first night she felt good, the second night not. Turns out when her husband changed the bedsheets the second day, he reversed the polarity of the mattress by accident.

Nikken is costly but it works.

### RobH — 2013-03-24T18:47:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-81630>

Quick update.

Bought a 20g magnetic pad and started sleeping on it about a month ago. I have always felt like I sleep well. Now I feel like I wake up a little bit earlier and a little bit more energetic. Don't notice other huge differences but haven't run any labs yet. My wife says she doesn't notice anything, but some nagging chronic issues of hers (constipation) have disappeared in the last month or so -- hard to tell whether it was the Magnetico pad, turning off the wi-fi / wiring the internet in the house, getting rid of dect phones, putting our cell phones on airplane mode more, salt lamps all over the place after sundown, earlier bed times, lights off more at night, seasonal changes, etc. Changes coming thick and fast around here.... and I know all these little directional changes really manifest themselves over months and years (like correcting your compass 1 degree now makes a huge difference on whether you make it to Hawaii or end up somewhere adrift in the ocean... 8^)) -- not good for the ADHD mindset / diagnose it and fix-it-now mindset of my profession.

Bought a 10g model for my youngest son about a week ago. He is the one we worry the most about and hadn't seem to be doing so well in school / sports / etc. as his brothers. I think he didn't quite get the same in utero environment / DHA and my wife was under tremendous stress with FT work, 2 other little kids, etc when she was pregnant with him. He's a wonderful kid but we had allowed him to become addicted to technology stuff because it is such an easy distraction for the youngest when parents are busy / stressed etc. Not any more.

Since turning off the wi-fi the kids play way less with their ipods and do their homework and play more music...

Interestingly in the last week my wife has been commenting on my youngest finally catching on to some things at school that he never could get before, and he scored two goals last Monday night in Hockey -- 2 days after he started sleeping on the pad. He'd never scored them before. Wife thinks coincidence ... and his teacher said a lot of kids start to really catch on at school in the spring... Coincidence? I'm not so sure.

My oldest son ran the fastest mile of anybody in 5th grade last week. He's been fast for a while for several months but seems to be getting faster than his peers are getting faster. He's a pretty good kid who listens well, cares about his health, eats about 80% paleo not much seafood thought, puts on his orange glasses at night... haven't done much to get family actively doing CT (yet...) but we kept the house pretty cold this winter and they have done well with it...

Anyway, I think the magnetico sleep pads for myself and wife are having effect. I'm thinking that is the case for my youngest also. Going to be getting two more one of these days...

Best to all.

RobH

### BJK77 — 2013-03-24T20:13:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-81637>

That's great to hear Rob. We started using a 10 gauss pad about 2 weeks ago and I really can't say my husband or I are noticing any difference at all. I wish I could if not for just the $$ spent! I still have some nights where I seem to sleep ok and others where my sleep is horrendous. We used to sleep on a grounding sheet plugged into the outlet and stopped that right before putting the magnetico pad on.

Just yesterday DH got an 8' copper stake in the ground and hooked it up to the earthing sheet, but again we didn't notice any difference at all in our sleep last night. I'm really not sure what to think about all of this anymore.

I think the one thing that has had the greatest benefit for us (or me anyway; nothing seems to bother my husband) has been turning off wifi at night.

### lioness7 — 2013-03-24T21:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-81643>

I managed to find a 6 month used magnetico (half price minus taxes and shipping)king 20 gauss (4 pads) a few months ago. I put the ten gauss on our king bed and split up the other two ten gauss between two beds as a blind experiment( 4 singles). Well, that experimental idea without anyone knowing theoretically worked until I had to haul 400 lbs worth of stuff upstairs....total fail. I guess I thought I was stronger than I thought - coupled with the fact that I washed the covers and then could not discern which were the bottom/top mattresses, used magnetics to figure it all out. Since there are many wondering about how these work....

My experience so far, once I turned them the right way ( and they are HEAVY, you need two people for sure, at 120 lbs myself, no way....had to enlist the kids to help), once I recovered from moving a king mattress and placing the pads , twice correctly....I have been waking up easier though I do feel like I am hungover. My Dh has been complaining of either sleeping soundly or poorly, though I think it is too soon to tell as his issues are far deeper than mine. We do not have any grounding equipment. I have another emf dude coming in this week, with an audience of relatives, so they too can learn. I have not had a scan (aside from a new wifi hub,  there is nothing different in our home) done in six years, but am concerned about a new tower erected within 2km of us recently. I am a laptop research addict at points during the day when i get bored with housework. I have noticed that if I stop during daylight hours, my sleep is better. (that is actually my only health concern at 50, which I know means something) I prefer to find the cause rather than supplement, if I can help it. So, I wonder if the pad, the blu block glasses and avoiding emfs will all work in conjunction...continuing to play with it all, but noticing a difference, just hate waking up earlier yet like there is a ten pound weight on each eye lid...but I guess/hope this too soon shall pass. Magnetico comes with a six month return policy....I don't think we should be expecting overnight results.

### Martin — 2013-03-28T14:42:46+0200

### nonchalant — 2013-03-28T15:27:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-82048>

I used a compass. The "N" is actually the south end of the compass needle, so it points to the north side of the magnets.
Yes, I've thought about making a pad also. Two layers of thick material, glue magnets in one line, sew, glue on another line, sew, etc.

### curtwht@yahoo.com — 2013-03-28T17:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-82058>

[http://www.magnet4less.com/product\_info.php?cPath=2\_6&products\_id=255](http://www.magnet4less.com/product_info.php?cPath=2_6&products_id=255) these magnets look similar to magnetico's. The magnetico description on ebay describes similar dimension and gauss. The magnetico single classic has 384 magnets, you can buy 500 ceramic from magnet 4 less for around 150$ plus 40$ shipping. Here is another link with interesting info [http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5456673/5395084.htm](http://www.azunimags.com/catalog/item/5456673/5395084.htm) I don't know if these are as good as magnetico.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-08T04:59:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-83326>

I've had my Magnetico pad for a week. Since I live in Las Vegas I was able to pick it up from the 'factory' and save on shipping costs. Nice folks there...family business...I was able to take advantage of the Kruse discount and the extended payment plan, so the sticker shock was survivable.

I've been following the protocol with respect to artificial light... blue blockers after sundown. I also have been sleeping with a grounding strap around my foot, placed over the K1 kidney meridian, for about a month. That has resulted in a significant improvement in sleep, and I found that while I still woke up once a night to go pee, that didn't happen every night and I was sleeping more soundly than I had been in a very long time. With the addition of the magnetic mattress pad, I am now sleeping through the night without waking up...this has happened every night since I've had the pad. I was not skeptical beforehand, but wow! I definitely am a believer now! This certainly works for me and my n=1 experiment.

I have a Schumann generator en route, so we'll see what that adds to the mix once it arrives next week.

All of this is good stuff. If you can find a way to make it happen for you, I would most definitely encourage you to check it out and see if it works for you.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-09T18:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-83499>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=83411)
>
> Wow, the foot - aren't you afraid you might trip if you have to get up in the morning??
>
> Can't wait to hear your results. Love my magnetico sleep pad too!!
>
> Would love to hear about the resonator!!! Did you get rid of wifi? I am so hoping to convince my DH to do it too....

LOL re: tripping... no, since I started grounding myself in this fashion, I seem to be aware that the strap is on my foot when I wake up, so I remove it. As a safety measure, if I were to forget and overextend the wire, it would pull out of a connector attached to a longer wire that runs between my mattress and box spring, with less force than required to trip me up.

Last night's sleep was the best yet...first time with the resonator on. When I first unpacked it I plugged it into a socket in my second floor home office and immediately noticed feeling different... calm yet acutely aware of my surroundings. I turned on my hi-fi rig and the music was much more engaging than I had recalled...I spent more time than I had planned listening to music last night! At sundown I transferred the resonator into my upstairs bedroom and plugged it into third grounding hole on my wall outlet using the supplied cable...the instructions say that doing so sends the Schumann signal throughout your house utilizing the ground wires. As an aside, I paid Lessemf.com an extra ten bucks to mark the 7.83 Hz position on the adjustment dial, and I have not as yet experimented with adjusting the dial away from this mark. The 7.83 Hz signal seems to suit me just fine so far.

Another thing before I forget... I've been turning off the Wi-fi before retiring each night. I have a 'network over power line' kit coming from Amazon tomorrow, and then I can ditch my Airport Extreme unit and go totally wired. The downer is that I will probably be selling my iPad...either that or get into the habit of going to Starbucks for periodic updating, but that would defeat the purpose of this exercise, wouldn't it? Actually, going back to reading email on the computer wouldn't be the end of the world...why is it that we feel we have to be connected to the Matrix 24/7? I'm pondering that one...

So anyway... I slept with 1) grounding strap, 2) 10 Gauss Magnetico pad between box spring and 10" TempurPedic foam mattress, 3) no wi-fi and 4) Schumann resonator at 7.83 Hz. Fell asleep very quickly around 8:30 pm PST. Did not wake up having to pee even once, which before my experiments with grounding and magnetics was very unusual. With the Schumann resonator it was even better. I was remotely aware of my varying stages of sleep, but did not waken, and when my normal rise time of 4:30 am came around, I was wide awake and felt terrific. This has carried on into my morning as I write this now. I am feeling more focused than usual this morning, and my sense of well-being is noticeably improved.

This is only one data point, so time will tell as the days and weeks progress. I have to tell you that, while not a skeptic, I was not prepared for the results I am seeing with my experiment(s) so far. To give you some background, I am a Ph.D. chemist by training and have a little familiarity with 'conventional wisdom' on this subject. CW would say that this is probably 'woo' but I'm not so sure anymore. I tend to trust my experience more and more lately, and less of what the 'experts' say. In that light, my ideas about water are changing as well.

I'll keep you guys posted as this experiment continues. Remember, this is my n=1... YMMV. So far, I'm extremely amazed and impressed.

I wish I could choose for him but you are right. He is an adult and making his choices. If he had started with decent health it would be bad enough but he has a lot of allergies, asthma where's he had ended up on a ventilator for a couple of weeks and some other issues as well. I was committed myself for awhile and drifted away but now am full steam ahead to do what is right for my health. So much damage already done and I can only go from here but I am moving forward with lots of changes. I'll keep you posted. Tonight will be my 1st night sleeping on my magnetico!

Christi.. I feel with you. Hubs used to be like that also.. with the drinking. I decided to not want it in my life anymore and he is not allowed to arrive drunk here. He has respected the rules so far and he has even stopped drinking and takes Antabus to not be tempted (I have not told him to do it it is all he's choice). I just have a hard time to look from the side when someone is destroying themselves, I am just way too sensible and that is why I have promised myself to not have to be close to such stuff anymore. I wonder how your hubby can work, living like that? My hubs always destroyed he's business with he's heavy drinking... so many times. He build them.. and soon destroyed them. All those years.

Take care Christi. Take good care of you. You are so worth it.
I am so glad you got the magnetico!

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-28T15:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-84990>

> [marcoc said:](/goto/post?id=84969)
>
> Magnetico Pad owners that had it for a month or more. Please help me decide to buy or not.
> Since you started using Magnetico, what are negative and positive effects since you started sleeping with it ? After reading most of info on this forum, i'm still not convinced.
>
> Thank you in advance !

I'm at the end of my fourth week of Magnetico ownership and use, and like Shijin13 my sleep has become awesome. You can go back a few pages and read my two introductory posts to see my story. My usual routine is to lie on the bed with my red light and/or blue blockers and read before retiring...I can't make it more than thirty minutes before I get really drowsy. Also, I no longer wake up in the middle of the night to pee. This in itself is terrific, as I remember my past 'sleep' as being very light and I was easily awakened...I have two cats so that is a given. With the magnetic mattress pad, coupled with grounding and the Schumann resonator, I close my eyes and the next thing I know it's morning. Cats or no cats, makes no difference. I have noticed that my dreams are more lucid and quite cinematic in nature...the story lines are fascinating... must have quite the imagination. ;-)

My 83 yo mother, on the other hand, has had a rocky start to her Magnetico experience. She has fibromyalgia, arthritis, and in general is pretty broken and toxic. She noticed a difference in sleep the first night, but the next night started to experience some detox symptoms. She spoke with Dr. Bonlie at Magnetico and he recommended that she start taking chlorella to tie up the toxins that were being released so she could excrete them. That seems to be working as she now reports that her sleep is much improved and she has noticed a reduction in pain.

Would I spend the money all over again? Absolutely!

### bionaut — 2013-04-28T15:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-84992>

I have been working a night shift for about a month. I got a 10 gauss queen magnetico mattress off of ebay (40% off woohoo!). I can say that I felt a huge difference using it. I wake up supercharged, my energy gets down to normal after a few hrs but I feel great when I get up now. When I first hauled the mat up the stairs and had my hands all over it while installing, I FELT WEIRD! Had tingling in the hands sharp pains in little points of my body and felt irritated. There were definite detox symptoms for me. I felt a little ill, had minor pains but that gradually lessened over 2 days while taking chlorella. My wife also noticed a difference, when we hop in bed it is like we have been karate chopped. It is so easy for us to fall asleep on it. However, when my wife wakes up she is still tired but when I wake up I am pretty pumped. We both eat similarly but there are a few key differences between us. She has higher stress then me, is dependent on coffee, hasn't been through a ct protocol and does not drink RO water. I suspect it is the RO water I leave out in the sun getting pulled into the cells cytoskeleton as I sleep... I haven't tested that with her yet.

### Danco3636 — 2013-05-12T07:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-85882>

Interesting, my Personal Radiation Detector came in.... A few things to note:
The microwave sends the readings in to the red and unsafe...
Even when the microwave isn't running it sends it up to the red when up close.
The readings from the microwave being on are still high even when out of the kitchen and in another room.
The wifi when up close goes red but when on the other side of the house doesn't register much.
When I cover the wifi modem with foil the readings go down.
My iPhone, I have a Pong case on it.... surprisingly it works pretty good at directing the radiation or radio waves out the back. The readings on the front where low but on the back it went pretty high.
My iPad not such good luck with my Pong case.... the readings where high on the front as well as the back.
The fridge was low unless up close to the ice-maker then it went red.
The washer and dryer where low and in the yellow on readings.
My Nikkin air purifier was high in one spot on the front of it. Put a think pillow in front of it and readings went down.
The TV was low but the receiver for the cable system was high.... not surprising as that runs of wifi.

Now keep in mind I have two Schumann Generators in the house and a personal one I carry in my pocket. I also run a Earth Pulse and grounding pads in the bed. That and magnet sheets in the fridge and on water. I also wear magnet pads on my feet when I sleep at night.

Now on some of these readings when I put my personal EMF protector (QuWave Harmonizer) that I carry in my pocket up close of in between the EMF objects the readings whet down slightly.... But nothing stopped or slowed down the microwave.... Those readings where through the roof and registered dangerous...

Fun stuff and fun to know.... going to keep testing others things....: Car, outside lines, smart meters etc. Brings light to what needs to be done.

### Nick — 2013-06-23T04:59:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-88710>

Thought it might be helpful for people to hear of our experiences with the Magnetico. We have a 3yo son who has severe allergies and eczema which we consulted with Jack about a couple of months ago. His advice was to get the house checked by an EMF engineer, drink Fluoride free water, keto diet, watch for the light cycles and purchase a Magnetico mattress. No surprises there. Our house was sea of EMF which is now massively reduced, we turn off the lights at 7.30, eat well, drink heaps of fresh spring water though I cant convince my 3yo to jump in the ice bath with me!

The interesting part has been the effect of the 5g mattress, though it is a little hard to say exactly what is effecting what as a lot of variables were changed in rapid succession. His sleeping patterns changed quickly, instead of predictable itching, screaming and blood and baths at 2am we instead had restless nights sleeps. Lots of squirming in his sleep and not a great nights sleep by the standards of this forum but a big change and improvement for us. Since that first week, the sleeps are getting better and better, but there has been some big changes during the day. We are watching his skin clearing up bit by bit, our bad days are not nearly as bad as they used to be and our good days are much much better. We now have big patches of clear skin, not many acute itching episodes and we are able to start reducing the amount of steroid cream being used. I feel hopeful that this trend will continue and we will try and go up to a 10g mattress soon.

Jack gave us a preview of a blog thats coming up in the future about children and Myelin sheathing and we have been throwing everything at this to try and change him while there is a window open to us. We cant thank Jack enough. This is probably not of huge interest to most people here but for anyone that has a child with eczema or allergies I imagine they will look at this experience closely.

In the meantime we have bought a 10g mattress for ourselves. I love it! I was sleeping well before this but this is like being hit with sleeping gas every time I lie down. I cant stay awake for more than 15-30 minutes before my eyes close which means I don't get much reading in. My sleeps are generally an hour longer than before and I have had two 10 hour sleeps. I think I needed it at that time and went with it, I trust my body so much more these days. I dont think I have had much detox going on, I am pretty sure I went through most of that with all of the CT I have been doing but I feel much better for sleeping on it. How and why I cant explain and neither can my wife. We are trusting our instincts and they are telling us it is a fundamentally good thing. I am addicted and would buy them both again in a heartbeat.

Anyway that is our Magnetico story so far, I hope it helps someone see some light at the end of the tunnel if they are stuck in the same place we were.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-19T22:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-90401>

I was very restless when I put on that first layer of the magnetico. Had to force myself to lay in bed. I was awake way more than I was asleep. (Much like when I first started the Leptin Reset and was breaking my habit of waking in the middle of the night to get on the computer.) After my sleep went back to normal, I expected more problems with the second layer, but I didn't have more sleep issues.

I don't always sleep well now, but I can usually figure out what I did to mess up my sleep, like eating too late. My husband, however, falls asleep extremely fast now, in just a couple minutes. He seems to snore much less than he used to. It still takes me 15-30 minutes to fall asleep.

I did notice some energy improvement at first, but it seems that I have gotten used to that, and no longer notice it. I think it helps my back in a minor way, even if I don't have the grounding sheet on. I have noticed a loss of appetite which still continues. I am way less interested in food now. I used to rely on my appetite, but now I have to be more methodical. It might be an effect of summertime, but it started right after I got the mattress pads.

If I work hard and tire my muscles, I can still revive myself by laying on the bed for 15-30 minutes. Works like magic!

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2013-07-23T03:55:20+0300

Hi guys,

Are magnets "Safe?"

I was talking to Mary Cordaro ([http://marycordaro.com/](http://marycordaro.com/)) who is a bau-biologie healthy home consultant based out of LA. I was asking her about her RO water filters for sale, and she said they USED TO clean the water with magnets.

They were actually using the magnets to "erase the memory" of the water, which is part of her water filter strategy/philosophy ([http://marycordaro.com/products/water.html](http://marycordaro.com/products/water.html)).

But she said the magnets not only wiped out the memory of the water very effectively, but then they found they could not imbue the water with "new memories" - and it was like the water had alzheimers, which was very unhealthy.

I asked her if she had heard of the magnetico mattress pads. I had the sense that she had not, but she said that she WOULD NOT recommend sleeping on magnets because they were "very powerful" and we "didn't know enough about them."

I don't think she knew about the uni-directional aspect of the magnetico mattress pads, and maybe she is talking about the problems associated with sustained use of the bi-polar field that Dr. Dean talks about (depleting your chi/vitality).

If anyone knows or has comments about this, can you please elaborate?

I am in the process of negotiating to buy two of these mattress pads for my kids, and now I'm a little nervous. My son has ASD, and I read a ton about health, including the quantum physics stuff. I like the way it sounds, but it's really hard for me to follow all the hard science.

Caroline. Sounds about right to me. It is all about choices we make. I have lived through periods of sleep deprivation and always felt like total s\*\*\*t during those times. Now, I have the luxury of sleeping in until I actually wake up--no alarm--and I have to admit, it is wonderful. Our culture really doesn't value sleep. We make our kids get up early to go to school, then totally engage them with activities and homework until they are exhausted and then do it again. Same with our work schedules when working for the government or big corporations. Totally insane hours. Not to mention the cortisol spike from dealing with rush hour traffic. We as a culture and civilization have chosen to get sick. Cattle to slaughter. I have always loved those one to two hours of sleep after you sort of wake up in the morning. You roll over and sleep some more. I have always called that period of time the "sleep of the dead." How ironic. It is the sleep we need but never get. I am getting it, and I love it.

Q'Rad Randy

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-06T09:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-magnetico-sleep-pad.5697/post-160398>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=96898)
>
> Why put the magnet in the water, Gretchen? Nothing stops the magnetic force, it will go right through the tank.

Metal can distort the flux lines. The flux (magnetic lines of force) will always choose the path of least resistance, which is any ferrous metal (metal that has iron in it). But if the tank is made out of fiberglass or something that is not metal, it will penetrate.
Magnetism has a circuit, just like electricity does. The earth is the battery and the poles are the terminals. The lines of force is like the wire. A magnetic circuit still can use Ohm's law, the values are just labelled different. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Liam, omegajosh, Ben777 and Cpt. Tired:

If you look at:

[http://www.shokos.com/compare-magnetic-beds.htm](http://www.shokos.com/compare-magnetic-beds.htm) (are there any other websites that talk about the Magnetico? That's all I found),

It goes into a little detail about the different magnet bed configurations. It explains the logic of magnetic mattress design.

Several things suggest themselves as important. Observe: Magnetico has a diagram of the lines of force as they come off the different mattresses.

[http://www.shokos.com/compare-magnetic-beds.htm](http://www.shokos.com/compare-magnetic-beds.htm)

The problem with Nikken is that you sleep too close to the magnets, and there is a certain amount of \[undesirable\] South pole energy that wraps around the magnet and will reach you. If you are further from the magnet, it must be stronger (Magnetico), but you are completely away from the the south energy field of the magnet.

As far as the idea of cutting larger magnets goes, it's not practical. Ceramic magnets are super hard and must be cut with diamond tools, and throw off flammable magnetic particles that stick everywhere and can hit you in the eye or cut you elsewhere:

[http://www.first4magnets.com/tech-centre-i61/frequently-asked-questions-i69#anchor10](http://www.first4magnets.com/tech-centre-i61/frequently-asked-questions-i69#anchor10)

so unless you are a skilled machinist with time to kill, a well equipped machine shop, and big magnets made from pure Obtainium, source them premanufactured to the correct size (_or smaller_). This brings us to the next consideration: stacking magnets will add their force, making one bigger magnet, so going in the other direction (adding instead of subtracting), you could get the force you want. You can stack magnets until their diameter equals their length, and then additional magnets add little additional force to that stack.

I am looking at several older Nikken pads on Craigslist. If I can hack them by dismantling them, removing the magnets and then stacking them and/or decreasing the pitch (the center to center distance of each magnet), then I could eventually reach the total field strength (and energy density at any spot) I want. The aim is to equal or surpass the Magnetico. I can place the DIY pad under my 4" thick foam mattress. Because the magnets will be under the mattress and subject to little wear, all I need is a large enough piece or pieces of cardboard (too big = too heavy, so it may be in sections) to hold the magnets (with glue or tape. No need for sewing skillz). The super easy way to do this instead, tho, might be to just get three or four Nikken pads and stack them up. There's your greater Gauss. However, if the individual magnets are not touching, I suspect they may not add their forces together as strongly. I dunno. Takes more research and tinkering.
_
As far as the footprint of the pad is concerned, the field must be larger than anyone or any group of people who sleep on the bed, so that everyone on the surface of the bed is within the North flux lines:
[http://www.shokos.com/fields.gif](http://www.shokos.com/fields.gif)_

I.e., if you have a twin pad for yourself on your side of the bed, and nothing underneath the side of the mattress where your opposite number (_Reason for Living_? Sweet Babboo?) is sleeping, that person/entity/sentient being will be in the path of the flux lines coming around from the South. Stay away from the Dark Side of the Force.
However, if you're strapped for cash, and all you can do is a twin size, and you want to both sleep on it at once, just, um... _stack_ yourselfs on top of the magnets. The Beast with Two Backs, as Shakespeare put it.

The Nikken pads in Denver CL I see today are $35 to $50 for a twin size. I don't know how many I would need to get to 40 Gauss _per magnet_, but there is a Gauss calculator:

[https://www.adamsmagnetic.com/centerline\_gauss\_calculator\_of\_disc\_or\_ring\_magnet.php](https://www.adamsmagnetic.com/centerline_gauss_calculator_of_disc_or_ring_magnet.php)

to put me in the ballpark. Gauss is directly proportional to volume, though, so get your magnet stack the same size/total volume and you should be close. We're not building a bridge here. Next question: if I have a 40 Gauss magnet, at 4" below my body, what is the optimum density of these magnets?

So look at this picture:

[http://www.shokos.com/Magnetico2.jpg](http://www.shokos.com/Magnetico2.jpg)

and if the magnet is 1 7/8 x 7/8 x 3/8", and these look spaced closer to each other than one magnet width, eyeball TLAR (That Looks About Right) engineering should suffice. Here's the thing: look at the diagram of the force field around the Nikken magnets :

[http://www.shokos.com/nikken.jpg](http://www.shokos.com/nikken.jpg)

see how far apart they are? I wonder if the Magnetico magnets being spaced closer than the width and length of the magnets pushes the flux lines of the South pole away more forcefully? In other words, if the magnets are touching at their edges, then would that push all the flux lines together and make them go further out to the side? Look at this diagram:

[http://www.shokos.com/fields.gif](http://www.shokos.com/fields.gif)

if it is accurate, the individual fields of the magnets coalesce into one large field that fluxes out away from the bed two or three feet horizontally. I suspect that those huge flux lines are what may keep certain animals from _approaching_ the bed, even if it feels amazing once you get through the 'shield'. I also wonder if it feels crummy to stand next to the bed? "_Sorry, honey, can't make the bed, the magnetic flux hits me right in the balls."_ Research shows that there is an inverse relationship between how much housework a man does and how often he gets laid, so Don't go there.

They also tell us how many magnets per mattress at:

[http://www.shokos.com/product.htm](http://www.shokos.com/product.htm)

so it looks like the DIY reverse engineering is straightforward. Did Jack ever spill any details/pix/stories about his DIY magnet bed effort beyond ''it came out in favor of the Magnetico''?

We'll be talking the price down with Craigslist people by showing them the state of the art and telling them that their mattress, while it was nice in it's time, is really only suited for use as raw materials today. It only makes sense to use their Nikken to make something that, practically speaking, works much better. Because of the smaller and fewer magnets of the Nikken product, to make this DIY work economically, you can't pay more than maybe US$25 for a twin Nikken, if that. I see a Nikken King for $200 in Denver today and it's already had the fabric stripped off it, and I can see the magnets are _much_ smaller than Magnetico's, and many fewer of them. Bargaining skillz required! Don't pay more for the Force than you would pay off eBay; you should pay less, because DIY, and your time is worth something to keep their Nikken out of the landfill.

Here's how I talk people down on CL:
do it in person, people hate being talked down over the phone
praise what they have and show emotional appreciation, let them talk about how much they like it
conspiratorially tell them about the market realities
"I think the market is pretty hard on these, even tho it's a nice \[whatever\]"
"Have you turned down any offers on it?"
"How long has it been on the market"
"If you've thought about it, what do you feel you really need to get for it?"
"_Because_ \[magic word\] of what people are doing these days, I'd have to offer you \[_much less than the price you had it listed_\]

Any other tricks anyone has for plundering CL ? I'm all ears.

Good luck

Harv

I have a 20 gauss Magnetico for the last 8 years......... I have often felt something was draining my energy............ so I would play around with it and take one or two pads off and on " and those of you who have the 100lb pads know it is not easy. .. suspecting something was wrong as I would feel like I was vibrating... ..........
Well I was just visiting my Mother out of town and did not sleep on the magnetic/pad for 2 weeks and was supprised how I was not getting headaches daily.. " the #1 issue mentioned by Jacobson" and when I returned home to the Magnetico pad the headaches returned and so did the body aches and fatigue. It has killed me to want to admit the pad may be the cause of my adrenal fatigue, headaches and overall fatigue after spending $3000 plus for the King.......... but it is, what it is......... the pads were off " 10 gauss off" again last night , I slept well with no morning headache........... and more time will tell... I may take the other 10gauss off and see if I felt as well as when I was on vacation and felt much better............ I am just looking for the truth "for me" and to feel better........ without the ego getting involved with ........products, principles, and personalities........ RJ

# Thread: Should I do a leptin reset?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janelle22 — 2013-02-02T17:22:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73790>

Hello everyone,
I'm sorry if I posted this in the wrong area but I was wondering if I should partake in a leptin reset. I'm currently 23 years old, 5'4", 134 pounds, and 22% BF. I've maintained this 130-134 weight range for about two years now. I have no idea what my rT3 is because I'm in medical school and ironically do not have a PCP where I am currently located. I am interested in doing a leptin reset because I have dealt with weight issues all my life and went about it all wrong for the first 5 years, with 1,000 calorie days, Sacred Heart Diets, Master Cleanses that I am sure have messed up metabolism. I am also fairly sure I have messed up my hormonal balance and gut flora with recurrent courses of antibiotics for a bunch of chronic respiratory issues, infections, and acne. I've been low carb for the past two years but strict Paleo since August, eating low carb except for one high carb day where I eat sweet potato or plantain an hour post workout, tons of fat from coconut oil, avocado, coconut butter, and ghee, and pastured eggs, grassfed beef, poultry, and occasionally salmon. I also started lifting heavy and sprinting in November. Since going Paleo and lifting/sprinting, I have noticed no migraines, clear skin with no breakouts whatsoever, better ability to fall asleep, and more energy. However, I still have issues with PCOS symptoms and trouble staying asleep (I toss and turn and wake up in my sleep countless times). I also have 10 pounds I'd like to lose that are clinging on for dear life. Would a leptin reset be beneficial in this case?

### RobH — 2013-02-02T17:56:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73798>

Janelle,

Welcome aboard from a practicing physician. Sure why not? Trying to reset your circadian cycles and food timing can only be beneficial to you, and I am so happy to see a budding physician here. Wish I would have known what I know now in Med School... Don't believe everything you learn there, or better yet, understand the limitations of the paradigm that is modern medicine.

RobH

### Janelle22 — 2013-02-02T18:15:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73801>

Thank you so much! Another reason I want to do this n=1 experiment is to see how it personally affects me and be able to pass this personal info along to my future patients for encouragement. I am 90% sure I want to go into Family Medicine with a concentration in Prevention and Nutrition so this is right up my alley. Fortunately, the medical school curriculum seems to be way better now, nutrition-education-wise, in that we are learning concepts that are in line with the Paleo way of thinking. I remember explaining my diet to my new friends during orientation (I'm just a first-year lol) when they asked why I wouldn't eat the sandwiches and pizza and seeing the skepticism on their faces. And then a few weeks later learning about how a high fat diet primes the body to consistently use fat as fuel, how saturated fat has actually not been linked to heart disease, how grains and legumes contain anti-nutrients, the importance of omega 6:3 balance, and the absolute lack of need for straight up glucose and fructose ...and promptly telling my friends, 'Ha I told you so!'. I'll be sure to post my own progress as time goes on, so leptin reset here I come!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-02T18:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73804>

I would suggest you you get an ASI (adrenal stress index) before you do the reset........if your ASI is low in the AM or high in the PM it is 100% for you. If you have extra cash......add in a salivary melatonin.......if it is off then yes to the Rx again. You would not pass the mirror test at 22% BF but you may benefit if you know what to look for specifically. Talk it over with your doc.

### Janelle22 — 2013-02-02T20:34:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73820>

Thanks for the advice Dr. Kruse!
I can't order these labs to be done without a doctor's prescription in NY state, so I'll have to wait til I see my own PCP over my next break to talk it over with him.
Just so I am prepared should I do a leptin reset, are the workout restrictions only for the overweight? Would I still be to lift heavy and/or sprint after 5 pm during the reset or would I have to do nothing?

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-02T20:47:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73822>

Hey Janelle Good luck for your med school journey!! At least I know ONE extra doctor is out there now who will be understanding of proper health care---yayyy!! I think we are starting slowly to see this trend. So by 2020's it will be even a few more people, then 2030s even more...etc. etc. By the time I'm in my 80s I am optimistic that that generation will be more like the people who have started from now.

Another off topic thought. People are very pessamistic about 'our generation' (under 30) being left with 'nothing' from the baby boomers. I am starting to think that yes financially we might have 'nothing' but in terms of resources and services and facilities/space we will actually have MORE since their generation is a larger population than ours. So we will find a way to make those other resources fit our needs etc. It's not so bad (I think?). I'm not trying to be delusional either...I just think that is what will happen...hmmm....

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-02T20:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73824>

Also I feel like it's almost dichotomy that things are simultaneously getting WORSE yet BETTER at the same time whereas before it was either only a 'trend of improvement' or a 'trend of decline'. Correct me if I'm wrong.

I just downed a huge heaping of magnesium chloride...lol.

### Janelle22 — 2013-02-02T21:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73829>

Hi Zorica! Thanks for the well wishes!
I completely agree; there is a slow but steady increase in the number of doctors who are abandoning the traditional ideas of health in favor of these proven, effective Paleo-based tips. I still deal with tons of doctors who ask why I don't eat grains, etc. but for every doctor like that, there is another that is understanding and inquisitive about this lifestyle and that is one small victory. I also agree that we will have tons of resources from the baby boomers. This site is proof of this. Information is more easily disseminated now in this age of technology and armed with that knowledge, our generation has the ultimate power in shaping those resources for our own good. And I think that this knowledge is imperative in fixing that dichotomy, at least in the health sphere. For instance, we Paleo-ists seem to be getting better health outcomes while people who don't follow this lifestyle seem to be deteriorating. However, bridging the gap of knowledge to create understanding should fix this so that everyone is improving. I'm particularly interested in public/community health (I'm planning on getting a master's in public health in two years in addition to my medical degree) in order to study how these gaps and lacks of access to knowledge occur and how to fix them.

### caroline — 2013-02-02T23:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-do-a-leptin-reset.5710/post-73846>

Welcome Janelle22 .... you sound like a very on the ball young woman. As Dr.Kruse said - you wouldn't pass the mirror test. Personally - I don't think you should be doing any workouts until you are reset - you must be so busy as it is. Do you live near the ocean? can you walk in the surf? Don't forget to hug a tree or two!!

# Thread: Puberty / Developmental Growth

### BJK77 — 2013-02-02T21:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73832>

Ideally, when should teens/pre-teens go through puberty and begin to develop? I look at my tiny almost 13-year old daughter and then I look at all of her friends who are much, much more developed than she is and wonder . . .

Thankfully she hasn't had crap dairy or meat for years now, so she's not getting all of the extra hormones that most of these other poor children are and I'm sure that has something to do with it. I'm not concerned that she isn't developing or hasn't started TOTM, just wondering what the age should be since we've obviously gotten so far away from what IS normal for our bodies.

### kathylu — 2013-02-02T23:34:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73851>

Just from what I've read, menstruation used to begin at around age 14 to 17. That is before 1900 or so.
Good for you getting the crap foods out of your daughter's diet!

### diane — 2013-02-03T00:10:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73858>

I have no idea what normal is, but my niece started at 10. And she had underarm and pubic hair at 8. When I saw it, I knew that there was something wrong, and really started worrying for the kids.

### Thor — 2013-02-03T02:06:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73877>

.
The key to puberty Development is Methylation. In boys it's very important - improper methylation leads to very small penis and testicular shrinkage into adulthood. Since 60% of the population has some defect it's safe to say it should be checked prior to puberty and treated by a doctor. I only trust Dr Kendal Stewart, MD with methyl issues. He's out of Texas.

Signs to look out for if your baby/child have methylation issues:

Neonatal and infantile central hypotonia, improving with age

Feeding problems and poor weight gain in infancy

Excessive or rapid weight gain between 1 and 6 years of age; central obesity in the absence of intervention

**Hypogonadism**—genital hypoplasia, including undescended testes
and small penis in males; delayed or incomplete gonadal maturation
and delayed pubertal signs after age 16, including scant or no
menses in women

Global developmental delay before age 6; mild to moderate mental
retardation or learning problems in older children

Hyperphagia/food foraging/obsession with food

Characteristic behavior problems—temper tantrums, violent outbursts, obsessive/compulsive behavior; tendency to be argumentative, oppositional, rigid, manipulative, possessive, and stubborn; perseverating, stealing, lying

Sleep disturbance or sleep apnea

Short stature for genetic background by age 15

Scoliosis

Nighttime enuresis

Dental problems

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-03T05:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73893>

Sounds like your daughter has a good health head start! Ya, puberty really shouldn't start until closer to 15. I swear my boobs are smaller now then when I was about 16...EW! (The EW is referring to when I was 16 and not now.  ) It's also really weird to see in a lot of teens where they are actually very skinny but HUGE boobs (not implants either). It never made sense to me...but I guess it's possible. I was never 'fat' just bad composition instead. (Which of course I hated).

So glad to hear that your daughter looks like a 13 yr old like how a 13 yr old should look like.  She's probably a happier pre-teen too. Hopefully she still goes outside and is active etc. Unlike her friends....although sad if they are active but have all those hormonal issues. Ew.

I once asked Dr Kruse this question in an Ask Jack (my daughter is a similar age). He said 12-13 is optimal.

### Destiny — 2013-02-03T16:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-73939>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=73832)
>
> Ideally, when should teens/pre-teens go through puberty and begin to develop? I look at my tiny almost 13-year old daughter and then I look at all of her friends who are much, much more developed than she is and wonder . . .
>
> Thankfully she hasn't had crap dairy or meat for years now, so she's not getting all of the extra hormones that most of these other poor children are and I'm sure that has something to do with it. I'm not concerned that she isn't developing or hasn't started TOTM, just wondering what the age should be since we've obviously gotten so far away from what IS normal for our bodies.

My older daughter was over 12 yrs old and younger one was almost 14 yrs old when they began to develop. Now it makes more sense to me - the younger one always ate better than the older one.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-07T03:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74536>

I'm glad Dr. Kruse just cleared this up in the Ask Jack, b/c 12-13 is NOT optimal. 14-16 was normal throughout the world before the industrial age. In the US, the age started going up around 100 years ago.

I've heard lots of theories (hormones in beef, hormones in milk, unrelated adult men living in the house, many more...), but I still don't know what it is. Here's what I know -

\- The #1 factor in guessing when a girl will start is when her mother started.
\- I started at 11.
\- My daughter is 14, and still hasn't started.

I cannot be more grateful about this. She is finally showing some visible (to random people) signs of puberty, finally hit 5'0", and it's wonderful she's developing so slowly. Her emotional maturity is actually well ahead of her physical maturity, and I have to think this is how it should be. Biologically, it makes NO SENSE for children to look like adults for 10 years before marriage is reasonable (b/c of emotional maturity). And that's just what happens. I was fully grown - FULLY GROWN - and menstruating at age 11. I married unusually young for these days at 21.

I can honestly say that by the time my daughter is 16, she will be emotionally mature enough to marry and start a family and run a household. I don't want her to, lol, but she could if she still lived in an era that did that. I know that is a combination of parenting (for us, privileges are ALWAYS a result of fulfilling responsibilities) and health.

I just have no idea what the health component was. I always thought it would take years to change someone from developing over-fast to developing appropriately slowly. But while I fed her CW healthy (very little sugar, lots of "healthy whole grains" and vegetables) for her first 9 years, she was in fact 9 when we switched to WAP (whole, unprocessed foods, "properly prepared" grains, lots more fat and protein, no more hormone-tainted beef or milk), which is an age where a lot of girls are already developing prematurely. She was 12 when we removed grains & sugar for everything but special occasions.

I don't know what it is. Exposure to other prematurely developing girls could play a role (think of how it's said that girls in a dorm get on the same menstruation cycle), and she doesn't have that (homeschooled), but other hs'd friends also developed well before she started.

BTW, it's also abnormal to menstruate till 51. We've added a full decade to our fertile years, strangely, and that's not even taking into account the fact that biologically, our bodies expect to menstruate very little even between 14 and 44, because of pregnancy and extended ecological breastfeeding.

### kathylu — 2013-02-07T03:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74542>

Just an anecdote, but my mother-in-law had 7 healthy, wonderful kids starting at 21 and ending at 38. She menstruated regularly until she was 65. She is 83 now and in very good health. She looks much younger than her age.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-07T03:51:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74544>

kathylu You're mother is very lucky, indeed. (Well at least heath wise I don't know her life at all before I make too sweeping of a statement/assuming).

MamaG: Cool info and story. Well by today's society I suppose I was 'normal' for puberty ie. 13 (and the young side of 13 at that). Maybe I was also lucky b/c my cycles were pretty long for the first year or so like every other month. Well ....;-) They always tend to be 'long' even now a days hehehe. But I had more regularity (still a bit long) from about 14/15 to about 26. Since 26 they get long and/or unpredictable again. For the last year they get really long like either 3 months or just recently it was 6 months. LOL Maybe my body wants/wanted to 'pretend' it was pregnant? Since evolutionary there would have been more pregnancies? lol I'm pulling straws here and justifying long cycles. lol

Hmmm shouldn't be menstrating after 51. hmmm. And you say that traditionally it was even 10 years before that to stop menstrating. So this leads to an interesting question regarding the decision to use BHRT when one reaches menopause. I still have many years until then (27 yrs old here. lol) but I have until then to figure out if a. I would benefit from BHRT b. If we REALLY need them if we can some how reach health goals as naturally as possible and c. is it only b/c of modern circumstances which call for the need for BHRT whereas in the past if a woman reached menopause at 40 it did not simultaneously mean all failure and crashing of bodily systems.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-07T03:53:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74546>

Cool! I've been looking up anthropological works & scholarly journal articles for years, and they seem to agree that 44 is pretty typical for menopause for women both pre-development and in developing nations (where there is often a case where they went to the industrial norm of 51, but now the avg is regressing b/c of nutritient deprivation). No decade of peri-menopause, either.

Makes a lot of sense.

We can't find out what it used to be for Western folk b/c our menarche & menopause numbers have been shifting for 100-150 years now.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-07T03:54:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74547>

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-07T15:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74621>

Here's some things that get my attention and that I really appreciate (not able to practise all just yet, but in due time, so I start with the free ones lol)

\-grounding is ACTUALLY a miracle
\-I would certainly try putting a magnet under my bed (it seems better than a grounding wire or fiddling with wires and electricity...not for me).
\-Neuro-feedback which many others such as Nora Gedgaudas and even Dave Asprey are fans of (and probably Terry Wahls too)
\-I would also like to try the heartmath HRV device that you can use to help control good heart rate etc.

For now I go out in bare feet even if it's snow (so that gets in CT lol), hug trees and look at the sun (if it is out, if not then just at the sky).

I still wear blue blockers at night b/c I feel like it helps me to stay relaxed and ready for bed. Even if I am not able to go to bed until 11pm now a days (part of my new 'weird' experiment). \*I can't complain about this new bed time b/c I am still getting up around the same time as when I was going to bed at about 10pm. Thankfully. So it's about 8-8.5hrs before it was 9 or 9.5 (maybe that is too long?)

I will still contemplate the use of a light box in the AM and not at weird hours or night (obviously) for this Fall, but I MAY not need it if other things go well and fall in line leading up to then.

I still would like to invest in a humidifier and get extra plants for my room and/or living space.

Right...menopause. I REFUSE TO HAVE TO HAVE A DECADE OF PERI-MENOPAUSE with pain and crap health. I just one day to have my cycle 'stop' and 'that's it'. Just easy peasy. lol (Although haha my body is not very excited to have cycles even now...lol Maybe I'll reach menopause in my 40s!  )

### BJK77 — 2013-02-07T15:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74625>

> [MamaGrok said:](/goto/post?id=74536)
>

Biologically, it makes NO SENSE for children to look like adults for 10 years before marriage is reasonable (b/c of emotional maturity). And that's just what happens. I was fully grown - FULLY GROWN - and menstruating at age 11. I married unusually young for these days at 21.

I don't want her to, lol, but she could if she still lived in an era that did that. I know that is a combination of parenting (for us, privileges are ALWAYS a result of fulfilling responsibilities) and health.
>
> I just have no idea what the health component was. I always thought it would take years to change someone from developing over-fast to developing appropriately slowly. But while I fed her CW healthy (very little sugar, lots of "healthy whole grains" and vegetables) for her first 9 years, she was in fact 9 when we switched to WAP (whole, unprocessed foods, "properly prepared" grains, lots more fat and protein, no more hormone-tainted beef or milk), which is an age where a lot of girls are already developing prematurely. She was 12 when we removed grains & sugar for everything but special occasions.
>
> I don't know what it is. Exposure to other prematurely developing girls could play a role (think of how it's said that girls in a dorm get on the same menstruation cycle), and she doesn't have that (homeschooled), but other hs'd friends also developed well before she started.
>

> BTW, it's also abnormal to menstruate till 51. We've added a full decade to our fertile years, strangely, and that's not even taking into account the fact that biologically, our bodies expect to menstruate very little even between 14 and 44, because of pregnancy and extended ecological breastfeeding.

I'm glad you shared about your daughter MG. She sounds very much like mine and I'm so glad I asked this question now. It's sad that our idea of "normal" is so skewed by our current, unhealthy society. While my daughter is in middle school around many other girls who have definitely gone through puberty, she is not. Many of her best friends who practically live at our house on the weekends have all matured physically, and thankfully this seems to have no effect on her.

Oddly enough, as you said, my daughter is much more emotionally mature than most of the girls I see. I've often wondered whether this had more to do with her diet or possibly the fact that we have always tried to treat her based on her age, if that makes any sense. As far as diet, I started out great with her as a baby and made all homemade, organic baby foods until I got so much pressure from family/friends for being odd that I caved and started buying the processed junk - so many regrets now. So, it really wasn't until she was 9 as well that we started to go back to organic and raw dairy and eliminated all processed foods and then around age 10 we cut gluten and went to all pastured, organic meats. What I mean when I say we treat her according to her age is that I feel like so many parents encourage their children to grow up too quickly. My daughter is 12 and still not allowed to watch PG-13 movies. 12 is still a child in my eyes and she doesn't need to be exposed to sexual encounters, drugs, etc, etc. We've talked about these things and if she hears anything in school that she wants to talk about, we have a very open relationship and I'm more than willing to answer her questions. I'd rather her hear it from me anyway, but I don't believe it's necessary for her to watch such things as a child.

All of her friends, on the other hand, are permitted to watch rated R movies and have seen things that I wouldn't be comfortable watching as an adult . . . and I'm far from a prude

So many of her friends and other girls her age are unbelievably boy crazy. I don't remember being that way at 12 years old, but then again I wasn't watching movies with naked men/boys in them either. My daughter really has no interest in boys right now, other than as friends. I suppose that will come with puberty, but again it seems so rushed for CHILDREN at 12 years old to be going through all of this.

As you said with your daughter, I truly believe as well that my daughter would be emotionally mature enough to marry at 16 years old. Most of my family has said since she was about 8 years old that she is much more mature than many adults!

One last note (I could really go off on some tangents about all of this!) - I couldn't agree more with you about children looking like children. Females go through puberty to make them attractive to males, yes? And to be able to carry babies, yes? So why on earth should this be happening at age 11 and sometimes even younger? I want my daughter to enjoy being a child and not having to worry about bras and periods and everything else that comes along with puberty. Ok, off my soapbox . . .

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-07T15:56:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74631>

Yep, I feel strongly about this, and only wish I could know exactly what affects the health component so I could pass it on to other mothers who have been asking what's our secret. I just don't know!

I do feel diet must play a huge role. Could it be processed oils? (We never had many of those, never ate a lot of fast food, never ate a lot of processed boxed junk other than cereal.)

Being physically mature for more than a decade before you can expect to be emotionally mature makes no biological sense.

I was boy crazy starting around 11, IIRC. When I was 12, I was "going with" a 15 year old! INSANE! My daughter at 14 is simply not interested in boys. She's interested in writing, reading great works, being with her friends, developing her faith, and loving her family. I know the boy part will come, but in due time, not too early. I wasted all my years from 8th-12th grade spending 80% of every day obsessing over boys. I'm so glad she's not caught up in this. (I think this, too, is a combination of whatever's going on hormonally and also upbringing. She's not around boy-crazy girls at all, and I'm sure that helps some.)

She still looks like a big little girl at 14. I can't imagine any full-grown boy (like 15) being attracted to her yet, even though she's the same height as my mother & grandmother & great-grandmother. She looks like a girl. Not a woman. I really think that's "normal" for 14, not at all "common" anymore, though.

I'm blessed. She's a wonderful teenager, not at all like the stereotypes, not at all like I was. I'm truly blessed.

(FWIW, she's not any less exposed to EMF than I was. Given the modern wifi world, she's more exposed. She's barefoot more, but not barefoot & grounded.)

Sometimes it's so easy to get distressed watching what's happening to our children. It's great to know there are others out there who are unaffected. I'm so thankful that she really does have some great friends who accept her for who she is and don't force the boy issue on her or tease her about her lack of development, but at other times I can't help but worry this will at some point cause social problems for her. Being so much more emotionally mature than your peers can be a difficult position to be in. It's a huge burden to always be the responsible one . . .

I may have my daughter read your posts later just so she can see she's not the only one!

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-07T21:14:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puberty-developmental-growth.5711/post-74672>

I love their strong sense of self-worth, not attached to what pubescent boys think of them. My daughter's friends certainly haven't teased her, but she's not in middle or high school, where I know teasing is a way of life. It's so good to hear that your daughter is having similar experiences even in school. Very heartening!

I'm glad they're able to work out who they are outside of what someone else's opinion of them is, in these years where that is such a major part of what's going on.

Sub optimal environments place is in perpetual summer. Longevity is more associated with autophagy induced by cold, fasting, etc.

# Thread: Floatation Tank
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-02-02T22:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73837>

I'm sure I already started a thread about this, but a search and check of all my recent posts is not showing it?? Weird . . . maybe it has something to do with switching the forum back to vBulletin??

Anyway, I finally had my first experience in a floatation tank and it was amazing! Just wanted to share in case anyone is considering it since Jack mentioned it a few times in the past couple of webinars. I wasn't sure what to expect and had to drive over an hour to get to the closest one I could find, but it was sooo worth it. Instead of an enclosed tank, this place actually has a small room built with a shower that leads directly into the floatation unit, which is basically just an oversized bathtub that has a wall separating it from the shower that rises to the ceiling. Hard to explain, but it totally eliminated any claustrophobic fears I had going into it.

I really wasn't sure what I would get out of floating. On the drive there I kept thinking there was no way I'd be able to lay in it for an hour. I'm "antsy" and don't do well just sitting around. I can't nap during the day - never have been able to no matter how dark a room is or how tired I am.

I became so relaxed floating that I didn't ever want to get out! It was unbelievable. They let me stay in for an hour and a half and it felt much too short! I'm sure I dozed off for a bit. What surprised me the most was that even though my mind was actively thinking about anything and everything, it was in such a calm way that I almost began to feel like I was dreaming things. I felt so positive and peaceful. I had strange thoughts almost like premonitions. I could "see" DH being given a job offer . . . he has been looking for awhile, but nothing has panned out yet. I saw it happen though . . . complete with details of the offer. I "saw" us at the beach with the kids playing and laughing in the ocean. It was so bizarre, yet wonderful all at the same time. I've never experienced like it and I can't wait to go back!

I'm really considering installing something like this in my house. I'm guessing if I can't move to the ocean, a grounded floatation tank would be the next best thing, right??

So if any of you are considering it or even if you haven't thought about it, DO IT NOW!!

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-02T23:00:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73841>

Thanks for the glowing report...I'm waiting to try the same. There is a guy starting a business about an hour away from us, so in a few months I'll be one of his first customers...sounds divine!!!

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T00:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73861>

Float tanks are awesome! Glad you had a go in one

### vkiernan — 2013-02-03T02:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73888>

That is on my list along with the HBOT. All good things for our mitochondria. Thanks for sharing. I know there is one 40 minutes from me so it is very doable.

### freesia — 2013-02-03T09:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73916>

I've done courses of HBOT (loved it) and I've been curious about flotation tanks -- so thank you for sharing your experience BJK77.
I don't know if there is one near me or not, but it sounds like a nice project to try and build at home anyway.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-03T14:44:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73921>

Thanks for reminding me about HBOT. I've been wanting to try that too. There's actually one very close to me at an Amish home of all places! An Amish family purchased it to help heal their son's brain injuries from a fall and apparently they had great success with it.

Hmmm . . . wondering if one of these therapies should be prioritized over another or if it's great to do them in conjunction with each other . . .

### Lyndra — 2013-02-08T18:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-74876>

One thought about floatation tanks - if they use magnesium sulfate and you have methylation problems relating to sulfate, it might not be a good idea. I've been advised to avoid epsom salt baths along with supps like MSM and DMSO. I checked out a couple of local float places and they both use mag sulfate.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-08T20:22:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-74903>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=74876)
>
> One thought about floatation tanks - if they use magnesium sulfate and you have methylation problems relating to sulfate, it might not be a good idea. I've been advised to avoid epsom salt baths along with supps like MSM and DMSO. I checked out a couple of local float places and they both use mag sulfate.

I was thinking about this too as I was reading about my CBS defect yesterday . . . good thing I didn't immediately jump in and buy a tank like I wanted to. The odd thing is I felt fantastic after floating and actually slept that night. Several nights before that and several nights after (still going on) I've had so much trouble waking in the middle of the night and being unable to go back to sleep for HOURS, but that night I slept so well. I just felt so light and carefree too. Although the week right after I was a complete emotional wreck - I would imagine that probably had more to do with TOTM though than the sulfate.

I'm not sure though. I guess if I can't figure out how to deal with this on my own I'll have to get a consultation with Dr. Tim or another methylation expert. I'm still hoping after I read all of Jack's EMF blogs I'll be able to make better sense of this and take more control.

# Thread: TOTM Issues . . . where my girls at??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/totm-issues-where-my-girls-at.5713/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-02-03T01:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/totm-issues-where-my-girls-at.5713/post-73863>

Ok ladies, I need some help here! Now that I'm finally cycling regularly again (after 2 years of having 1-2 cycles a year), I'm dealing with some persistent problems I never had before. I suffer with major insomnia starting about a week before TOTM starts. It gets a bit better the closer I get to my new cycle starting, but I still wake up in the middle of the night burning up. The closer I get to my cycle though, the easier it is at least for me to fall back to sleep.

What's the deal with the extreme hotness? These aren't night flashes, which I've had before, it's more like I'm just super, super warm. I need to figure out what's causing the insomnia too as it makes me quite miserable :/

The other problem is that I find myself getting constipated every time I start a new cycle. In my younger years I had the exact opposite happen and I always had loose stools.

Someone please explain the hormones that are behind all of this . . . I'm currently taking 100 mg Prometrium + 100 mg Progesterone orally (my levels are still low), but I stop both of them when my cycle starts. I also take 50 mg DHEA and 25 mg Pregnenolone daily. Anything I can try adding to help with this mess??

bad clock is the real reason......and poor ATP re cycling.

# Thread: Leptin/Epilepsy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### danrosz — 2013-02-03T07:24:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73896>

Nice forum here.... Going to try
and keep this as brief as possible... I'm 28 years and had my first
seizure when I was 18 years old. No focuses... Nothing irregular.
Played drug roulette last ten years ago. Did have seizure while under
24 hour EEG in hospital two years ago... Was told that seizure
appeared to originate in parietal lobe.

Anyway, I've tried exploring everything the last ten years to get a
hold of this. Felbatol and lorazepam when aura arises has actually
been pretty effective the last 20 months. With that being said, I
still have too many auras for my liking. The leptin connection is
this... Very thin as a teenager growing up. Started lifting weights
when I was 17. Typical stuff... Bench, Squats, etc... Put on a good
amount of good weight... Unfortunately, it was almost like I broke my
appetite... As the years went on, it would seem like the the 72 hours
after lifting something was wrong. It was this hunger that I couldn't
really satisfy. It wasn't your typical hunger... To this day, I don't
reallly exercise like I used to, but when I do, there is something
broken. These weird hunger feelings will turn into auras and then
sometimes seizures. These last ten years, I knew there was something
wrong with my body besides just my brain. This wasn't normal. The
best way I can describe the hunger is that it isn't a hunger in your
stomach... It's a hunger in your arms and legs and there is tingling.
Very ood thing.... I saw an endo a few years ago and he said my tests
were fine....

The reason leptin came up was because I was reading a published study
about how zinc supplementation seems to have a positive impact on
treatment of epilepsy. Was curious as to why and it's there that I
read about how some people think zinc helps control leptin levels. A
quick Google search lists numerous articles linking leptin and
epilepsy. I absolutely think there is a link in my case.

Out of curiosity, do my symptoms sound like my leptin are drop too low
or are they rising too high? Is there a way to correct this in a way
that I could fix this via diet, supplementation, etc.? Is there
anybody you would recommend in the Philadelphia area to see about
this? It would be nice to be able to exercise, live, and have a
healthy brain and body.... I honestly believe this may be the key.
Thanks for taking the time to listen.

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T08:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73903>

This sounds like it will be an interesting n=1. I'm betting on the after exercise hunger thing being your body stealing ingredients to make ATP.. What's your exposure to emf like? How about sleep?

### danrosz — 2013-02-03T16:58:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73947>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=73903)
>
> This sounds like it will be an interesting n=1. I'm betting on the after exercise hunger thing being your body stealing ingredients to make ATP.. What's your exposure to emf like? How about sleep?

Not well-versed in emf's but this hunger can get worse when reading off of cell phone computer screens. Just figured that was unrelated to leptin and was part of parietal love issue. You google leptin and parietal lobe and there are some links. Have no idea how much of a role all this plays but I know it plays a big one.

Sleep is not great. Mostly due to felbatol.

### danrosz — 2013-02-03T17:02:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73948>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=73903)
>
> This sounds like it will be an interesting n=1. I'm betting on the after exercise hunger thing being your body stealing ingredients to make ATP.. What's your exposure to emf like? How about sleep?

Hunger isn't right after lifting weights. No issues directly after. Days after weight training.
