### BJK77 — 2013-02-10T23:09:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-75281>

Following this thread with interest since I'm finally getting my drive back . . . took long enough and I've got a LOT of time to make up for

I haven't had a drive for years, probably 15+ now and was terrified I'd never get it back. Since everyone is "going there," I'll share my story. I finally realized my drive was coming back this past November when I had to go to FL for work and DH ended up coming down to meet me for a few extra days. We made a nice mini-vacation out of it and I had just started pregnenolone ( in addition to the DHEA and Progesterone I had been taking). My drive kicked in MASSIVELY that week like I have never before experienced EVER.

I've always loved my DH and have truly been attracted to him and felt he was super sexy and good looking, but it's always been on more of a spiritual and emotional level. That week, there was so much physical attraction I couldn't make it a few hours without showing him  After putting up with my lack of libido for years, the poor guy really deserved it anyway!

What I find odd is that we were in a condo in downtown Orlando and obviously surrounded by excess wifi and EMF. We did swim in the pool, but other than that no CT except for our last day in the ocean. Temps were also quite warm. I was eating a lot of fresh, wild seafood, but I try to do that at home as well. I know I have less EMF exposure and do more CT here, but I haven't gotten that drive back just yet. I still have it, thankfully, but not to that extreme!! It really was the craziest week of my life.

Problem is I still have trouble with orgasms, but I don't mind focusing on him and making sure he gets plenty of oxytocin  Plus, I figure I'm getting some just from the closeness and sensuality of our togetherness.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-11T01:43:40+0200

You know, I used to think that since I'm always a bit more amorous when away from home, but nothing like this! DH and I often take trips just the two of us and we've had fantastic times, but it usually involved alcohol as much as I hate to admit it. This was so natural and I just felt so alive. I also was actually more stressed because of what I was doing there for work and I also happened to have a nasty chest cold at the time, so my body was quite stressed in multiple ways.

There have also been vacations where I have felt poorly and definitely not in the mood at all - even though we were away from home and should have been more relaxed, so I'm doubtful that the holiday was the reason behind this.

I'm also quite happy that this is the first time I can say the improvements in my libido seem to be sticking. Yes, it has definitely dialed down a LOT since that trip, but even at home I'm still feeling things I haven't felt in years.

I do love the idea of camping though. I've been talking about going camping lately, but hadn't really thought about it the way you put it - the idea of combining all these healing elements with my love sounds so wonderful. I need to make that happen

### fitness@home — 2013-02-18T02:08:33+0200

Caroline, my kids remember some of our silliest adventures with great glee.

Like one time when they were little (between 5-10 years old) and we were in my small car stopped at a traffic light. The radio was on and turned up loud. Can't recall the song but there they were, lined up in my backseat bopping their heads back and forth in unison and really getting into it.

My little car was rocking and the other people at that intersection were all smiling. This didn't cost us a penny and all three carry this memory with them today

I sometimes miss those days. It would be fun to go back for one day and spend it with them at that age...

It isn't too late for us oldies either - not by a long shot!

### Hope — 2013-03-14T02:19:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-80448>

Hey thanks Caroline, I just now finished to the end of the thread. Great stuff and I definitely need it!! I am behind on this for sure. I definitely have to add SS' books to my reading list, after I finish the EMF blog series (still working through it!). I just want to know more about hormones, period....ultimately, to see if I can help myself get pregnant, despite my low libido (ugh) and advanced medical age of almost 40 and messed up hormones (but super low HS CRP, go figure).

I definitely LOVE this thread.....I need the encouragement to remember to do it no matter what...or try to, anyway. We have a set of friends that make a lot of O together and they got pregnant twice super easy, on the first and second try despite her AMA of 38 and 40. Whew. We do not. Sometimes it's me (tired, stress, whatever), sometimes DH...he is really struggling and it came to me recently that I need to work harder on getting him more optimal...even if it's just with eating a LOT more seafood which he loves (but he's not into supplements, CT, thinks my passion against EMF is bull etc)....he has issues at times and it makes it difficult...sorta like a vicious cycle in his brain as he is anxious by nature anyway. But it also gives me time to work on my own getting optimal and working on the SO too LOL. My own MD actually gave me a special oxytocin (not nasal, but apparently better absorption than the regular pill....[http://www.belmarpharmacy.com/oxytocin.htm](http://www.belmarpharmacy.com/oxytocin.htm) but so far I see no difference...will try nasal next

I definitely hope Dr K will do a Sex webinar tying it all together for THAT...I think that could get DH to listen...as he didn't used to have issues when younger and his MD starts talking drugs and age when he just doesn't get the whole hormone thing. So we'll see.

Anyway thanks for starting this thread and keeping it going. I am taking coriander as my goal  and hopefully when I am 50 I will feel better than I do now at almost 40, LOL.

### caroline — 2013-03-14T02:38:25+0200

BUT I realize I wanna help DH too.....while working on me too. I saw some improvement for a while but then lately have tanked again, in energy level so am now pushing more CT, grounding, being outside.....a lot more. Can't wait for spring (on its way).

Camping would be great if I can figure out a way to use my CPAP there LOL (zombie without). DH has been told by MD's 'it's your age' (mid forties)....a great excuse to not push for optimal. He is in a stressful job, in EMF bathtub....for years.....none of it helps.

It caused additional stress for him worrying about it and there were times I thought "what's wrong with me".

His T levels were at the lowest end of the scale and the doctor gave him the T creme. Not much of a help so he found a longevity clinic, had more labs, and started on their program of T shots. He's had a slightly enlarged prostate for a few years. At his physical last week, doctor prescribed low dose daily Cialis...supposed to help this and also lower BP? (Should put question in Ask Jack about that). Anyway, he is loving it! Makes him feel young to have morning wood again

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-02T22:07:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-85339>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=85318)
>
> Perhaps CT is such a blast of goodness everything gets a bump?

The cold (or CT) may favor a decrease in plasma T4 binding, an elevation of the pituitary T4 "set point," a preferential shift in deiodinase activity from reverse T3 to T3 production, and an increase in the thyroidal secretion of T3.

At least that is what is observed in hamsters exposed to cold.
Isn't cold a signal for procreation to take place?

### cantweight — 2013-05-03T01:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-85347>

Hi my name is Christi and I am and oxytocin addict. lol

Always a fan of oxytocin, something I discovered when nursing my babies, I loved being cuddled up in our oxy coma after a good nursing. Then I let my kids have pacifiers so they could go there without me and I longed for the blank stare I saw wash over their little faces as I sat there fearing I was screwing up their teeth with that paci.

Always a sexual/sensual being...I was quite a looker back in my day  However after my babies were born illness took hold of my body. DH and I still had sex a good bit because I was drinker. It helped with pain and was the only way I could fall asleep...my cortisol levels were bonkers at that time. Being drunk there were no orgasms and the sex was for him not me. Fast forward a few years and I was not drinking like that at all and had regained much of my health. I began to S/O everynight as it was the only way I could sleep....I didnt realize it was oxy I was after...I told my girlfriend it was masturbation meditation. I got so good at it! Can pop one off in 30-60 seconds...every time!!

DH was in his video game phase so I had a lot of time to myself. Even mastered the art of female ejaculation (ok I just totally blushed and considered deleting that!). I remember thinking...jeez if only I had done this 15 yrs ago I could have made big money..lol!!

I have become insatiable, my T was high a few years back but has been stable for some time now. I really can not get enough. Twice a day is more of a necessity than a goal. I feel like a weirdo! I am so glad I am not a man!! Other wise people would be able to see how I am feeling!!! lol

My factory does shut down for about 10 days mid cycle. Ovulation brings an ugly cloud every time and I don't feel sexy. But a few days before period and I am on fire. DH and I have so much fun. He's hot and fit, I'm fat and he adores me anyway. About to start my 30 day salivary hormone panel and my biggest fear is that thy will fix my hormones and I will lose my spark. Honestly I think I'd rather stay fat and horny than be thin with no sex drive....and I really really want to be thin, but not if that is the cost....

CT definitely gets me going...very little doesn't at this point..lol HCG made me go through the roof...was too intense of a drive...the hcg forum had some crazy sex threads!! lol

Ok its out there going to click submit....deep breath....

### caroline — 2013-05-03T01:55:06+0300

I always felt so fat and ugly and unattractive and wanted to hide ..... you rock girl!

Your DH is a very special man ... and by the sounds of things - a very happy man!

### cantweight — 2013-05-21T06:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-86587>

I'm curious about the de-masculinization of men. That is probably not even a a word. Men a whole seem less masculine to me and now that I have a 10 yr old son with low DHEA it seems it lies in hormone disruption.

I really want to figure out how my body functions so optimally sexually yet fails me on so many other levels. I also meant to ask about too high sex drive. Could it signify a similar mismatch when someone is over stimulated versus those lacking...did the hormones disrupt in a different pattern?

A healthy sex life has seriously changed my life. I am happier, relaxed, my marriage is rock solid, my kids get something great out of seeing an attentive and affectionate healthy relationship. My parents hardly ever even kissed in from of us...sex was a forbidden topic. My kids are still young but 2 of them have had sex ed and we are very matter of fact about it all. No shame, I think that should promote healthier sexual views.

I dont understand the lack of attention to womens sexual health, function, and happiness by doctors. Before handing out the prozac why not suggest 6 months of daily masturbation? With men they have pills to get their penis up so they can use it. Nobody really cares about womens parts working...like we're just a hole you can throw some lube in if they are dry....wake up this is huge for women too! The parts have to have healthy function. If ED means heart issue what does vaginal dysfunction signify besides hormones...thats the easy answer. I honestly think that female ejaculation is the orgasm women are supposed to have. Men can orgasm w/o ejaculation, its how they can have multiples. But the men will say the big release when they do ejaculate is the jackpot. Its 2013 and there are still those in the medical profession that think female ejaculation doesnt happen! This is nuts to me.

Im so sick of women getting the short end of the stick in medicine.

Stepping off soapbox now...lol

### Kiera2 — 2013-05-23T08:00:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-86712>

Oh you make me laugh out loud! Sex alians! hahaha

Loving your partner doesn't mean that you'll have an orgasm when you have penis-in-vagina sex. It just means you trust him enough to _let him do that._
I mean, my grandmother got pregnant at 15 without knowing how babies were made. I don't think anyone was discussing how to enjoy practicing! And I know a lady who was told, the day before her wedding, 'just lift your nighty and look the other way'. That was the only advice she received regarding her 'marital obligation'. I think that going to the dr to receive treatment for 'hysteria' was a result of the incredible repression and subjugation of women at the time. There was no room for 'I don't like this' and 'I like that', when you weren't even allowed to mention particular body parts. It's hard not to generalise, but the Victorian era was such that pleasures of the flesh were just not discussed in polite society. And I'm sure it was only 'polite society' women knocking on the dr's door each week. Anyone else would have been having it off with the butcher, the baker and the stable boy! (perhaps that last says more about me than anyone else!)

I guess my point is that great sex requires more than love. It requires self knowledge, vulnerability, wanting to share, courage to dare, and ability to laugh at yourself. The joy of exploration can only come about when you know that you have freedom to explore in the first place. I have known women who were deeply in love with their partner, but would never dare tell them that they were unhappy in the bedroom. How is a man supposed to know what heights a woman can reach if no-one ever tells him?

I wish we could just meet for coffee and talk for hours! Gotta go.

### cantweight — 2013-05-23T16:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-86727>

Oh my goodness yall are too much! Anosmiacs..lol!!!

I think we all put way too much stock in the vagina. Dr Kruse said it is basically just a tube meant to fit a dumptruck through...lol. I dont think my vagina is that talented but I did have an almost 10lb kid! It seemed he was focusing on the parts that provide much more arousal- clitoris, nipples, skin, brain.

It took me years to get all my parts to do their thing. I'm 38 now and its only been a few years of this being a consistent occurrence in my life.

As i write all this stuff I keep telling myself not to be embarassed...lol. omg.

So the g-spot I believe is where a particular ligament runs from the posterior side of the urethra to the anterior side of the outer vaginal wall. About 1 inch in, on the upperside..toward the bellybutton. The skin feels different...kind of spongy with a wavy texture...maybe an area the size of a nickel. Try to hook your finger and pull down and force the area toward the pubic bone. It will cause swelling similar to that of an erect penis, the vaginal walls will swell a bit too. With the hooked finger and lots of coconut oil allow your palm to cover your labia and clit. your other hand is free for breast stimlation or anything else that floats your boat

I also think that when learning to orgasm you should not put anything in vagina....until that muscle (not even sure it is a muscle...whatever it is that contacts at orgasm) is strong. I cant think of a good analogy....maybe like trying to make a fist with a soup can in your hand. You can only get a weak grip, take the can out and you can really clench your fist. Same with the vaginal contractions that happen at orgasm....if there is nothing in their way they can contract much harder and over time become much stronger and develop muscle memory that make contracting easier and stronger. It took a few years for me to have a good strong contraction with penetration, to this day they are still stronger without.

I think there is so much nonsense in our world that completely ignores the amazing and complex sexual nature of women. We are not men. Its not lick it stick it done for us. Society portrays in movies and tv these 2 minute trysts that culminate in sweating bucking glory....such bullshit. The average woman after a quick one like that feels messy and would have rather been sleeping..lol. We chastise ourselves like there is something wrong with us or just lose interest in sex...who can blame us?

Taking the time to figure this stuff out has done wonders for my health, happiness, relationship and self confidence. I dont think even the best lover can figure this stuff out for us. Its our body we have to figure out what it likes and doesnt so we can tell our lover.

Oh man....I really hope this is not TMI!

### Inger — 2013-05-31T20:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-87208>

Today I had something totally strange happend. It so made me think of Jacks sex rx webinar....

I was allowed to leave from work earlier as it was quite silent today, everyone was outside in the nice and sunny weather.. so I went to the train. Then I realized I had no cash and I had to go and get some to pay for the train ticket, and so I missed the train and had to take the next one.. As I stayed by the cash automat waiting for my turn to lift cash.. I all of a sudden realized a man staying maybe 2 meter away from me. I looked at he's face.. it seemed so familiar? He looked stright into my eyes.. oh wow.. he had such beautiful eyes. He sai, hi! And I said hi too and then told him I thought I knew him but I did not (as an excuse for me staring..lol). He said, you are so beautiful. He looked at me like in awe.. OMG I kind of drowned in he's eyes.. they were so blue.. and he looked so much like George Clooney.. wow. Just WOW. I thought how the heck can this good looking man think I am beautiful? With all my pimples, coming from work sweaty and unfresh... carrying a long wide skirt I got from my mom and a tight top that let my back and shoulders nude (only because it was so hot!) and I always thought I had so big arms way too big and also my white spots on my back and all this stuff but I told myself today morning putting this top on I do not care one bit if it looks ugly, I just so much wanted to feel the wind on mys skin on the way to work. And then... I remembered so many things from the webinar.. I bet he smelled me.. I had also used only lemon juice for deo.. what works perfect but I bet my natural scent also might be more noticable.. and also I use no soap just swim in the river.. and then I am close to ovulation in my period...

He's voice was so deep and beautiful. The whole man was just so very manly.. everything I think (not sure because I could not let go of he's eyes tho..lol)

We chatted about the weather at first.. and then he asked and asked so many questions! Turned out he used to live in Germany too. And then he said really would like to take me out for dinner... would that be ok? maybe he could have my phone number? I gave it. I am maybe crazy but I did, to a complete stranger. I told him to call my phone right there to see if he got it right.. and he did. So now I has he's number too. All way home I thought about this.. and so much wanted to write him a message. But I am not. I will let him do and if he does not it is how it should be.. me thinks. Oh my.. such a lesson about hormones......

Now i am not scared at all anymore i will never meet a man.. look what can happend when you look for it the least! With all this pimples and stuff too! Somehow it was just like magic

### Lahelada — 2013-07-12T17:50:33+0300

I used to attend St JohnÂ´s ambulance first aid courses. We had an instructor in his seventies who was a professional firstaider in an ambulance all his life. He used to say the following: I want you to take this away from here. When you have done all the alleviating/lifesaving measures and steps your job is not done !You have one more job and that is to show tenderness. Just a gentle rub on the shoulder or massaging hands will do , something that does not invade personal space. You have no idea what it means being on that floor feeling at someones mercy. That act only can save a life. " I think that he discovered the power of oxytocin without ever knowing the word.
Sorry to change the direction of the thread slightly.

### beach bunny — 2013-07-14T06:41:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-90093>

I just have to say my heart sings with gratitude to be part of a forum with such incredibly empowered Goddesses! Dr. Betty Dodson would be so proud 
I don’t even know where to begin in terms of oxytocin and orgasm. They continue to shape me…as if I am a living, breathing work of art…constantly evolving, learning and growing.
I really got into SO when travelling away from my lover for an extended period of time. I was shocked at how incredibly natural and erotic my solo sexual repertoires became. Partially they were fueled by a deep longing and missing of the man I love…and partially (as I have now learned) the orgasms helped my brain cope with the infections that were slowly beginning to take over.
Although I have had hormone issues my entire life (“train wreck” is the term Dr. K used I believe) my sex drive has always been through the roof! (except when I was dying from the infections and completely lost my libido) Ironically my outrageous drive has been a source of pain, misery and confusion for a large part of my journey…primarily because my partners could barely keep up with my insatiable appetite…and me blaming myself.
It is such a relief to realize that the more I recover, the more clearly I know that intimacy, sexuality, sensuality are as fundamental to my health as the beautiful sunshine, clean air and water, sleep and nutritious food. I have also been blessed with a Soul Mate who is not only the most loving, gentle and nurturing person I have ever met, but is eager to share in my crazy wild side. There really is no comparison to how much more I enjoy “Oxytocining” with him than solo! (but the SO earlier today was pretty good I have to say hehe!)

Devour life I say! the good, the bad and the ugly.

so - put on a mini skirt girl - and go out and dance the nite away

### caroline — 2014-02-09T11:57:46+0200

I loved my DH dearly - I adored him .... but I couldn't understand his preoccupation with always wanting sex - now I get it ...way, way too late. It was my problem not his ..... I didn't know any better - it was the dark ages I think! Sex was never talked about - I knew virtually nothing. extremely and heart breakingly sad and now it is too late....

I want you guys to all have the wonderful life you deserve ... and much, much more. That doesn't meant there are no sad days or bad days or hard days. ### margor_j — 2014-02-09T20:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-109308>

we get embarrassing bodies on tv in nz to. yeah, they had a breast special on it here, wowsers! i really pity some of those women - to think they go YEARS suffering, and the waiting list for an op is up to two years (public health) - and i was able to get mine within 4 days o/s! the best thing was, my surgeon took my word for it that i was ready, willing and able . no dilly-dallying to make sure im not gonna regret it / cant handle it etc - like theyd faff around doing in nz. i say - in, out, shake em all about!! - but only once the stitches are out!  (over 200)

i would gladly spend time in thailand helping other women who wanted to do it - i still cant believe how freaked out i was! lol. i lay in the OR pre-op freaking, but also thinking - to hell with it, im GOING to do this! thousands of women have gone before me and done the same thing! and when they woke me after, the first thing i did was put a hand up to my chest, to check! haha wahh still seems a bit surreal talking about it!
personally, i think.. we all lose the boobs of youth, they definitely change BUT, if youre dealing with chronic neck / shoulder / back pain (i used to have to do 20 mins of traction on my neck per day, with up to 4kg weight) and youre self conscious then its not worth it. thats life changing - unless youre happy with all that!

### caroline — 2014-05-03T20:47:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-124905>

I want to talk about touch....

I have been extra intrigued ever since Jeremy - he talked about touch a lot ....... Tailor your touch etc.

Dr. K. Said he needed to learn that .. I always assumed that a Doctor would have an extremely sensitive touch.

I realize that it is about the collagen....

I have been hugging and touching a lot of people this last couple of weeks ...how lucky am I ...extremely!

Take the maid for example ... Her hug and touch had so much love and kindness - very palpable.

Josh has an amazing hug - he puts everything into it .. He is sharing himself.... He has great tenderness and kindness in spite of certain things in his life. I sometimes worry that he gives too much of himself away

All the people we shared time with ...these hugs were about ... I am so glad that we had this opportunity to meet and talk and there was so much value and kindness and caring transferring one to the other in a very real and honest way.

The pony tail/ leather jacket Doc ... He had a very tender touch and the most mega watt smile that briefly lit your space.

I shared so many lovely conversations with Jerry - such a caring Doc - he took my breath away. His care and thoughtfullness for his patients is extra ordinary and he was so excited to learn from Jack - he listened intently to all of us. I need to say "Hi" to him - he will be here reading!

Stella and I, simply put, fell completely and utterly in love with each other.... A lifetime connection made on so many levels. Those parting hugs and tears were really hard .... But re inforced by all the things dr. K. Said to me and all the changes I have to make .... And so pretty scarey - but very exciting!

And now .... I have to get to Dr. K. .....I don't know where to go with this quite yet.....

Jack is caring and kind and generous and on the other hand, We all know how direct he is and can generally piss everyone off without even half trying..... He loves controversy and getting in every ones face!

I think I worry that he is giving too much of himself away .... But he does know how to have a good time! So I worry less...

But ...... Jack has a very clinical touch .. I would guess that a Doc has too? He has said that emotion has no place in surgery .... And, of course, I understand.... But does your guard always have to be up? I am just asking because I don't begin to understand the horror and tradegy you see every day on your operating room table and how you move in and out of the world you inhabit.

Jack has, and is, dedicating his life to something so huge .... And monumental and life altering.....

Maybe this is a thank you to Sandy for keeping you Grounded .. . Giving you what you need ....you were pretty descriptive about that ...lol

I think I have to think a lot more about this ..... I don't think I said what I wanted to just yet....

Anyway .... Touch between friends and even strangers ...is so huge ....amazing oxy on so many levels.

Jeremy has much to teach us ..

Maybe this should have been a separate thread ... But I am feeling the love and oxy from a lot of very special people....what could be more important? I feel full to overflowing....

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-06T19:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-125519>

Re: touch, as part of my psychology module we're covering a lot of the classic psychological experiments that have been done over the last 100 years, most of which would now be considered unethical. It used to be thought that when bringing up your children you should keep them at arms' length and if you start hugging and kissing them they'll end up causing you all sorts of problems. They felt that children love their parents, especially their mothers, because of "cupboard love."

So Harry Harlow decided to conduct a variety of experiments with baby monkeys, he started off giving them the choice between a wire mesh surrogate mother with a milk bottle or one covered in soft terry-cloth but no milk bottle. The babies always choose the soft mothers for comfort and security and would only go to the wire monkeys for the milk. Unfortunately his research became more and more sadistic and it's because of this we now have ethical standards when working with animals.

### sooperb — 2014-05-06T20:05:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxytocin.5785/post-125521>

I really dislike animal experimentation, it's wrong on so many levels, I don't care how much it's taught us. Medical science is prolonging the lives of so many beyond what it should have been.

"Presenter: When you worked with Dr Harlow did you see the set up of the wire monkeys and the cloth monkeys?

Dr. Melinda Novak: That had all gone by then. However, I will tell you that in the graduate student office there was a huge, and I mean about five foot high, cloth surrogate so we could all go over and hug the surrogate when we were feeling stressed. Real fun."

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-23T16:29:45+0200

Vibration = oscillation.......you asked about this before.......every electromagnetic field has its own vibrational pattern that dictates fluxes, kinetics, and biochemistry.......once it changes it all changes. This is complex enough and I do not want to move there until we get the foundations of just what an altered electromagnetic field does to all biochemistry. It is quite simple if you use this analogy. We all know how baseball is played at Yankee Stadium in NYC. There are 4 bases and they are at right angles to one another and there are foul lines that run from home plate thru third and first base until they reach the outfield fences. So the limits of the park are well defined. This is precisely what the Schumann resonance is for life and biochemistry. It is the park that biology on life must play. When you alter that park the game changes substantially. So if we were to remove first and second based and extend right field 500 feet and place a huge sinkhole in centerfield would the game of baseball in Yankee stadium be the same as it was before these changes? I think the answer is intuitive. This changes is precisely parallel to what modern pulsed EMF does to biochemistry today. What we used to expect we no longer can expect, and when we treat people in this new realm we need to think about how the game has changed to explain what we are observing. What we are observing is known. Then we can look at the current playing field and make experiments to model what we think has happened. The Russian cosmonaut experience from MIR is such and example. My weight loss and use of CT are other examples of this observation and the fact I have reproduced it in many people shows us we need to question our beliefs about how biochemistry now acts when space time is distorted on our planet. When the field has changes it changes everything.

Dogs sleeping on the bed even further away from the floor (one of my pet peeves LOL). I know that it can impact the behaviour because of boredom and lack of mental stimulation - but now I see there's so much more. I've known so many dogs who are dying from cancer at younger and younger ages. Behavioural issues are increasing, and as you said, people are shoving shit for food in a dog's mouth constantly.

Like you I see so much crap that just makes me cringe. Posts all over Facebook - don't let your dog out in the heat (mine will sit in the sun on those days, then go to the shade to cool off the smart pups), don't let your dog out in the cold. They have no idea what they're doing. My hope is to take this information you're sharing and figure out how to change how people treat their dogs.

### Inger — 2013-02-07T19:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunglasses-and-male-anttention.5789/post-74664>

I wonder if any other experience this weird thing too.
I use my dark sunglasses always after sunset. So I was on my way from the airport with a really heavy bag, walking through the railwaystation with my sunglasses on. A nice looking guy opened the door for me. He followed after me to the train and helped to carry the bag inside. He sat down and started to talk, he asked where I am going. I told him I cannot tell that. He said he was lonely. I told him he should go out in the forest and he will feel no lonely no more. I told him I do that too. We talked a while about these things. He then raised, shaked my hand and said goodbye. He told me I was so beautiful. It was weird but nice. There are often mens turning aroung looking at me, and such stuff, when I have my glasses on. I wonder why?

I actually love the protected feeling they give me, from the busy city life. I am behind my dark lenses and it feels so nice and peaceful...

Lol.. I could do it better! I had HWC in my coffee at work again today.. but maybe all the raw heart and oysters balances it out..lol

I got a kiss from our chef too yesterday (haha.. he loves that I am washing he's dishes all the time..). And today a hug (and no, it was nothing sexual, he has a wife and all but it was still so tender and kind and made me feel happy!). I like this life..!!!! I walked to the railwaystation today as I had to go to work and it had snowed and everything was so extremely soft and white and beautiful. I felt so blessed and happy I worried the happy feeling could not fit in me anymore huh!
I cannot complain.. it is all magic.. The weird thing is, as the train start rolling there is nothing that can stop it anymore it seems..

# Thread: How did Eukaryotes form?  Key point about Schumann must be made in EMF 3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-eukaryotes-form-key-point-about-schumann-must-be-made-in-emf-3.5791/>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-07T21:49:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-eukaryotes-form-key-point-about-schumann-must-be-made-in-emf-3.5791/post-74681>

HOW DID EUKARYOTES FORM?

Biologist look at evolutionary trees to try to make sense of where life came from. They have a term called, LUCA, which stands for Last Universal Common Ancestor of all life. Whatever LUCA did, meant that is how the rest of life had to go. Bill Miller did experiments that showed LUCA could not have fermented in an evolutionary time span. Life has 3 domains. Archaea, Bacteria, and Eukaryotes. Eukaryotes are a combination of Archaea and Bacteria that occurred when a process of endosymbiosis happened. Endosymbiosis is when two prokaryotes fused to become one, where the engulfed bacteria became the forerunner of our modern day mitochondria. It has never happened before or after this one time on our planet. From this joining of two primitive life forms, all complex multicellular life came from it. In fact, we are products of this set of circumstances. This fusion event required lots of energy to happen.

This was a fusion event for life. I like to think about it like a nuclear explosion. Some amazing amount of energy had to occur to these two primitive forms to cause them to fuse. I believe the event was a coronal mass ejection from the Sun occured before our atmosphere was protective as it is today from the sunâ€™s electromagnetic debris. This amount of energy would have been massive and would have dissipated to levels these primitive cells could have tolerated by the oceans depths. The Schumann Resonance actually controls when these coronal mass ejections occur too. We will get to that too, soon enough.

Photosynthesis is found in cyanobacteria, algae, and plants. The last two use chloroplasts to make ATP. Archaea never use photosynthesis. This was a major clue to Bill Miller. If fermentation was the primordial path to ATP it should have been used in both forerunners, to the eukaryotes. Both Bacteria and Archaea ferment, but they use different enzymes to catalyze the steps in the pathway. This meant by definition, LUCA could not ferment. This also meant that Mitchellâ€™s ideas of energy production had to be correct because chemiosmosis is present in both and identical. This implied LUCA also used chemiosmosis.

The first cell of life therefore had to use protons to set up a gradient across a membrane, driven by electron flow. Both Bacteria and Archea do this. Moreover, both share an identical ATPase and respiratory chains too. Respiration is not complex. It was easy to form at the hydrothermal vents because of the amount of electrons, protons and reducing chemicals they constantly supplied.

But there was another part of the equation to think about, the long term coherent Schumann resonance effect. In 1974, Dr. Cole and Dr. Graf of New Orleans completed a theoretical analysis of the Pre Cambrian Earthâ€™s magnetic field that fit the model I am sharing with you perfectly. They reasoned since the atmosphere was much larger back then, it must have pushed the ionosphere much further out into space. Today this would be where we would see the Van Allen Belts. This meant that the Earth would have had an electromagnetic resonator (Larger Schumann) of two concentric spheres consisting of the upper and atmosphere and the surface. This implied that in the Pre Cambrian Era, the fluctuations of current in the Van Allen Belts would have generated huge currents in the ionosphere compared to today. This would have provided a massive energy boost and it would have also would be coupled to large coronal mass ejections. Since the Earthâ€™s core is an excellent conductor, the ionosphere currents would have coupled to this energy producing an enormous and constant electrical discharge through the atmosphere and into the Earth.

Since the distance around the core was equal to 1 wavelength of EMF at 10 cycles per second throughout the resonant cavity, roughly 18,000 miles, the discharge would have pulsed at 10 Hz throughout the atmosphere and the surface of the planet dramatically increasing electron flow. This would have produced massive heat, UV radiation to oxidize the oceans, and created infrasound pressure waves. All these forms of energy would have increase the energy in the Earthâ€™s crust to generate massive chemical activity to foster the origin of life. Moreover, such a supercharged ionosphere would favored spiraling vectors in the electromagnetic field depending upon whether the reactions occurred in the Northern or Southern hemisphere. This difference would have been found in the formation of organic chemicals with an L or D iso-form.

AMAZING TRUTH BOMB: If this sounds crazy to you, consider what is going on right now in the Great Red spot on Jupiter. This is precisely what is happening daily in this great electrical hurricane on Jupiter today. If this was correct, it would predict all life forms on Earth formed within the 10 Hz discharge, and all their descendants would also resonate at the same frequency. Moreover, they would show an extreme sensitivity to that frequency even if the original power source was disconnected from it. This is precisely what life shows today on Earth.

This is why the Schumann resonance is the gate keeper to EEGâ€™s in all animals. It also means it is the cipher to reset circadian cycles in all animals when they are cut off from from the normal magnetic fields on Earth.

As the ionosphere gradually descended and disconnected from the Van Allen Belts, the ionosphere currents would have become too small to couple to the Earthâ€™s core. The atmospheric cavity would have been too small to resonate at the coreâ€™s prescribed frequency. The plug would have been pulled on lifeâ€™s formation but all it needed was its initial jump start. And this is how I believe it happened. Todayâ€™s remnant Schumann resonant frequency is proof of that initial energy burst.

# Thread: Chicken Broth Hazardous to your Health??????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chicken-broth-hazardous-to-your-health.5792/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lioness7 — 2013-02-07T21:53:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chicken-broth-hazardous-to-your-health.5792/post-74682>

Just came across this. Does anyone have access to the actual article so that we can scrutinize this a bit better? I make cauldrons of this stuff every week........

Med Hypotheses. 2013 Jan 30. pii: S0306-9877(13)00013-3. doi: 10.1016/j.mehy.2012.12.026. \[Epub ahead of print\]
The risk of lead contamination in bone broth diets.
Monro JA, Leon R, Puri BK.
Source
Breakspear Medical Group, Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, UK.

Abstract
The preparation and consumption of bone broth is being increasingly recommended to patients, for example as part of the gut and psychology syndrome (GAPS) diet for autism, attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder, dyslexia, dyspraxia, depression and schizophrenia, and as part of the paleolithic diet. However, bones are known to sequester the heavy metal lead, contamination with which is widespread throughout the modern environment. Such sequestered lead can then be mobilised from the bones. We therefore hypothesised that bone broth might carry a risk of being contaminated with lead. A small, blinded, controlled study of lead concentrations in three different types of organic chicken broth showed that such broths do indeed contain several times the lead concentration of the water with which the broth is made. In particular, broth made from skin and cartilage taken off the bone once the chicken had been cooked with the bones in situ, and chicken-bone broth, were both found to have markedly high lead concentrations, of 9.5 and 7.01μgL(-1), respectively (compared with a control value for tap water treated in the same way of 0.89μgL(-1)). In view of the dangers of lead consumption to the human body, we recommend that doctors and nutritionists take the risk of lead contamination into consideration when advising patients about bone broth diets.
Copyright Â© 2013 Elsevier Ltd. All rights reserved.
PMID: 23375414 \[PubMed - as supplied by publisher\]

What kind of chickens did they cook and what were they fed? What was the true source of the lead? When they say they analyzed organic chicken broth, was it the boxed or homemade kind? etc..Funded by Campbell's lol ?

### freesia — 2013-02-08T00:11:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chicken-broth-hazardous-to-your-health.5792/post-74711>

It says in the abstract Anna. Lead is present in the modern environment and it gets concentrated in the bones.

I guess some of us have lead sequestered in our bones too...so perhaps taking on lots of chickens lead isn't a great idea. Modern chickens have problems too! No surprise there then?

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-08T01:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chicken-broth-hazardous-to-your-health.5792/post-74731>

Unless there's something different either with a) our own personal lead levels or b) the chickens' lead levels than in the past, I'd ignore it. My grandmother & great-grandmother made it all the time, no lead poisoning.

Puh-leeze.

Now, somebody got evidence that either a or b is true, I'm all ears.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-08T01:43:22+0200

I still don't buy it.

Perhaps the same environmental factor might even cause the chickens to hold onto lead rather than detox it too?!

Hmm....really just thinking aloud here.
I made chicken broth regularly while we were eating a GAPS diet and I didn't notice any ill effects from the broth...just from the fat. I never made friends with chicken fat, and of course now I know why.

### kathylu — 2013-02-08T03:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/post-74762>

A lot of people like to use the Dzugan recommended levels, which are higher than your lab's values. My last DHEA-S was around 327, which is exactly where Dzugan wants it. You might want to get a copy of his book "Your Blood Doesn't Lie" by Sergey Dzugan, MD. Lots of case studies and he gives you optimal hormone ranges.

Remember that lab values are based on measurements done in a large number of people, many of whom may have been ill or have tanked hormones for various reasons. So, normal does not necessarily equal healthy or optimal.

### CarolMorris — 2013-03-01T20:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/post-78524>

Claudia..I did lots of bone broth and lots of coconut oil...I put it in a shot glass, 5 TBLS at a shot. At first 3x a day, then 2x when i went to 2 meals. It's funny, I work in a medical building and several of the Docs asked how I've lost so much weight, when I tell them I was taking up to 1800 cals a day in coconut oil, not one believed me! I'm trying to remember the supplements, resveratrol, curcumin and a heavy duty probiotic.

Your blood tests give the best clues, I don't have my notes here, but I think it's Hdl, dhea and Vit D-anyone please correct me if that's wrong.I got big jumps in labs with no changes in dosages. I never had any of the classic symptoms my symptoms were and still are food sensitivities, dairy and especially egg. I've gotten over the majority of the other ones. I followed Dr. K's blog on Leaky gut...great info in there.

### shilohman — 2013-03-02T21:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/post-78671>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=78651)
>
> I agree with all your 3 posts, Dr. K. Believe it or not I am making a progress but my inner self is still not very intuitive. Is 7-keto as good as DHEA? Or nothing compares to DHEA?

Destiny, if you don't mind my jumping in here, but by your quote above I sense your frustration, but I think that you are missing what Dr. Kruse is getting at.
I know that you are probably already aware of the following, but for completeness, let me summarize:
DHEA - Dr. K says 275-400 ug/dL of DHEA for women with 7-keto being preferred for
women. Note that the top range is 400 ug/dL. Dr. Kruse says that a high DHEA level
implies that IL-6 is low, which is a good thing for any type of cancer. A high DHEA level
also implies high melatonin, which is the number one antioxidant in the brain and
major protector against all epithelial cancers (i.e. breast cancer).
So I can understand your focus on DHEA. But here is another quote from Dr. Kruse,

> "Pulsed EMF does the same thing thermodynamically as an asteroid does to water (it evaporates water). \* It does something else tooâ€¦â€¦.it blocks the secretion of melatonin from the pineal gland which alters all circadian cycles too."

IMMHO, I think what Dr. Kruse is trying to point to you is that you are focused on the wrong thing, your focus should be on increasing your electrons/ATP which in turn will increase your DHEA/melatonin/etc.

Go re-read Dr. Kruse's long post beginning with

> "In the reality of Optimal..."

and I think you may realize the golden nugget that he is giving you.

I went mostly by labs and changes to how I react to foods and supplements...e.g. when I started taking coconut oil it would make my stomach cramp, I didn't connect that until later. I had bone both twice a day most days, I can eat tomatoes and peppers and a few other things. I have to say the only thing I miss now is my Guinness
Labs, Vit D went from 45 to 95; dhea from 170 to 1100; hdl 47 to 65; b12 278 to 844; pg/e2 ration up to 506 after increasing prometrium from 200 mg to 400 mg(Jack suggested in consult)

also a big factor for me going on t3 only, my ratio of free t3 to reverse t3 went from 7.0 to 16.8(that was Aug to Nov) I'm re-testing in a few weeks. Just seems to me that my body is finally using what I've been giving it.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T00:04:19+0300

### Psyche — 2015-10-21T21:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-s-and-teststerone-results.5794/post-180092>

Thank you for the response, Jack. My particular N=1, I suppose. It makes me wonder about my unusual reaction to Prozac- the only side effect I had when it was once prescribed to me is intense sleepiness, so much so that I could not continue taking it.

**Circadian rhythms in the eye: the physiological significance of melatonin receptors in ocular tissues.**
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18316227](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18316227)

The circadian signaling molecule, melatonin, is secreted into the circulation from the pineal gland, _**and is also produced within specific ocular cells such as retinal photoreceptors, ciliary epithelial cells, and perhaps cells of the lens.**_ Melatonin appears to entrain many aspects of the biological clock via activation of specific G-protein-coupled integral membrane melatonin receptors. Melatonin receptors have been identified in many ocular tissues, including the neural retina, retinal pigment epithelium, ciliary body, cornea, sclera, and lens.

# Thread: Website Changes--Please Note
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-changes-please-note.5798/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2013-02-08T04:27:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-changes-please-note.5798/post-74770>

Hi all,

Just thought I'd pop in to offer a heads-up on changes to the site. Most of you have already received this news via email, but I thought I'd pop in here to post an update.

Two major changes and a couple notes:

1) **No Member Dashboard**. In lieu of the Dashboard where members login and access webinars and sign up for consults, we have transitioned to an email-based system to deliver all your member content and benefits to you via email. Please make sure [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) is on your safe list and save the emails we send you, as they include links to download your content, sign up for consults, etc.

We are adding a "Member Portal" section to the site shortly that will include links for you to be able to sign up for consults and email lab consults if you are a member... That info is in the emails we sent you, however, so refer to them in the meantime for links, etc. to sign up.

Questions or issues? Email [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) or open a ticket: [http://jackkruse.zendesk.com](http://jackkruse.zendesk.com)

2) **Forums are back**! We've brought back the vBulletin forums, as you all requested, so you can access them with tapatalk. Glory be! We are going to add a banner here with links to the menu shortly. If you can't remember your password, go here and follow the prompts: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/login.php?do=lostpw](http://jackkruse.com/forum/login.php?do=lostpw).

If you still can't login, you know the drill: Email [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) or open a ticket: [http://jackkruse.zendesk.com](http://jackkruse.zendesk.com).

**The Optimal Reset!** We are several weeks in to the Optimal Reset now. I hope ya'll are enjoying it! this program isn't perfect. It's brand-spanking new and we are using this run to help iron out all the kinks. So please be patient with us. We are letting everyone in the community participate at no charge, and Dr Kruse is donating his time to do complimentary Q&As to support you guys as you're going through it. I hope ya'll are finding the structured program to be helpful and are enjoying supporting each other! Hop on over to the Optimal Reset forum for more help and encouragement.

We are going to be launching a newsletter soon... You guys have any ideas for a name? We'll use it to recap activity on the site, share upcoming news and events, and to do some special contests. Comment here if you have some ideas for us!

I know the last four months have seen a lot of changes, bumps and bobbles. If there's one thing we've learned, it's that you can't please everyone! I think we've about settled into "this is the way it's going to be," at least for a while. Never-ending change is hard on everyone. We've tried out so many of your suggestions and are grateful for each one of you who took the time to share your thoughts, ideas and experiences. Collectively, we've all tried hard to make the site better and to create more community. Jack has been on a roll with killer content this year, and we are about to start doing a push to spread some of this great information around the interwebs.

If you are experiencing egregious usability issues, I want to hear from you... Please open a ticket. If you wish the dashboard were back, or we would do some things like we used to, all I can say is we need to settle in and focus on some other things that I really feel like you're going to love! I hope you can make the site work for you. The most important thing is content and community, and we have that in spades.

Happy New Year (a little late).

Misty

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-canadians-handle-snow-storms.5808/post-74893>

Ok, not everyone. But love this pic!
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/snow-lunges-jpg.15/)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-08T19:40:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-canadians-handle-snow-storms.5808/post-74899>

On my FB page Mitch Baird from the First nations just posted a pick in his speedo shoveling 8 inches of snow.......I meet Mitch at Paleo Fx last yr and wrote about him here: [http://www.jackkruse.com/my-top-ten-paleo-fx-moments/](http://www.jackkruse.com/my-top-ten-paleo-fx-moments/)

[https://www.facebook.com/notes/mitch-baird/month-3-of-ctketopaleo-whatever/10151168303631856So](https://www.facebook.com/notes/mitch-baird/month-3-of-ctketopaleo-whatever/10151168303631856So) now I’m 3 month in on my winter experiment with fat loss. During the Christmas holidays I stayed really strict to the diet and really tried to focus on the bigger picture of seeing what would happen if I keep going down this path of eating/health/training/cold exposure etc.

1) Cold tub has dropped to 46 degrees as of last night…my days in the tub have been good. I get in 5-6 times a week and when I can’t or I’m away I have turned the shower way down. Temp has dropped from around 50-52 last month to 46 as the temp has followed the weather. At 50-52 I am ok to stay in to 20 minutes without much problem, but now at 46 I am good after about 15 mins. Using Pranayama breathing techniques have been a big help. Ujjayi breath really help to relax in the tub. I have stopped shivering now and seem to be used to it without much in the way of discomfort. Things are progressing well here.

2) Yoga has been going well but I am beginning to teach more which can held back my time for personal practice. My kettle bell work has been increased as well, I am doing 2 sessions a week for 20 mins per session, these have gotten a lot easier and I can tell my strength has increased in my overhead press and squats. I’m using the same 30 lbs bell but I am definitely more controlled. My weight is 181lbs and I’m about 5’6” as of yesterday. No idea what my body fat percentage is but according to the BMI chart I am obese. (ok) here her posts his six pack pics.......LOL
3) Diet has been the same. No change on that front. I do like peanuts and like many in the paleo community I try to avoid them AMAP but similar to wheat they are in so many foods they are tough to avoid. I’ve been a lot more conscious on avoiding these foods. So peanuts are all the way out now as I had some last week and my stomach paid for it. Live and Learn. Loving Liver right now and Beef heart. if you don't eat organ meat start. that is all.4) Body fat seems to be evening out as maybe I have gotten to a place my body is comfortable. But the kettle bells and yoga have helped me with my core strength that now I feel very balanced in the way I feel. Julie’s parents came to visit and her mom was very sick and so was her dad. After going to the doctor her mom had pneumonia and dad the flu and we hung around them for 3 days. I took my vitamins each day ( D3 Magnesium Krill and Salmon oil)and extra vitamin C each day when they visited and I thought for sure I was going to get sick. That plus many sick people at my work you always think something has got to happen. But luckily nothing. (knock on wood)

I attended a few of the Idle No More protests in my area, and not just in the native community but in the overall population there seems to be a dis-satisfaction with government and big corporations. But so many of the people fighting against the government for sovereignty and engaging big corporations for better accountability willingly eat and drink big corporate products which puts them on a beeline course to the biggest government program there is, Health Care. If more people opted out of big corporate food then avoided the big government health care system to me that would be the ultimate form of sovereignty, personal sovereignty that depends only on yourself to make decisions on your health. Not some government bureaucracy. Not sure why we think government and big corporations never have our best interest in mind when it come to the environment, taxes, treaty rights, but are perfectly altruistic when it comes to the food we eat and the health care we receive? If anyone can educate me on why I’d love to know.

You got to see his pics in the link......

# Thread: Tallow for the skin

### Rachelle — 2013-02-08T22:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-74925>

Thought this might be the place to post my favorite skin softener. No other oil makes my skin come alive.

I use grass-fed beef tallow, real olive oil, and essential oils. I noticed a difference in my skin the moment I used it on my rough dry hands. Honestly, if all else fails try it. Oh and it is cheap to make.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-10T05:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75176>

> [Rachelle said:](/goto/post?id=74925)
>
> Thought this might be the place to post my favorite skin softener. No other oil makes my skin come alive.
>
> I use grass-fed beef tallow, real olive oil, and essential oils. I noticed a difference in my skin the moment I used it on my rough dry hands. Honestly, if all else fails try it. Oh and it is cheap to make.

Wonderful idea. Two questions: 1) can you use the fat that emerges on the top of home made bone broth after it's put in the fridge? 2) could you use duck fat? Thanks!

### Kiera2 — 2013-02-10T07:17:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75185>

I would Lauren, but first I'd render it. Once rendered, it will keep for a long time, and you don't need to refridgerate.
Scoop the fat off the broth (easier when it's cooled down), then slowly simmer the fat in a pan over a low flame. Just the same as making ghee; any solids will fall to the bottom, the water evaporates, and you're left with clear fat. Pour it into a jar, and it will become quite solid when cool.

You probably already know that, but I thought it might be useful info for someone else.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-10T07:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75188>

> [Coriander said:](/goto/post?id=75185)
>
> I would Lauren, but first I'd render it. Once rendered, it will keep for a long time, and you don't need to refridgerate.
> Scoop the fat off the broth (easier when it's cooled down), then slowly simmer the fat in a pan over a low flame. Just the same as making ghee; any solids will fall to the bottom, the water evaporates, and you're left with clear fat. Pour it into a jar, and it will become quite solid when cool.
>
> You probably already know that, but I thought it might be useful info for someone else.

Thank you. And, no, I did NOT already know that. I'm going to give it a try!

### Rachelle — 2013-02-10T17:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75222>

@KiwiLauren..Here is a good site I used when I rendered my beef fat. She gives very good advice. [http://www.theprairiehomestead.com/2012/02/how-to-render-beef-tallow.html](http://www.theprairiehomestead.com/2012/02/how-to-render-beef-tallow.html)

I wanted to say also that those who live in dry climates would need to spray water on body first before applying any moisturizers. Nothing happens when there is no humidity to absorb cream. I really dislike dry climates and winters that are dry. Have always felt better in humid climates.

Any other ideas?

I did a batch last summer - but haven't been inspired to go that route again, although for creams etc I might consider it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-02-10T19:01:06+0200

### diane — 2013-02-10T21:13:12+0200

### Lyndra — 2013-02-10T22:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75273>

I've done both the wet and dry methods of rendering. I found the wet method to be more foolproof in terms of avoiding burning. I also found grinding up the fat first to be far more effective than just dicing, as it melts at a more uniform pace when ground. One bit of advice - don't do this if you aren't feeling good as the smell may really get to you. I did it the day after being glutened when I was sick to my stomach and I now have unhappy memories associated with lard.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-02-10T23:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75280>

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-11T04:41:01+0200

I have practically every essential oil in my cupboard. Thanks for the post!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-12T20:18:40+0200

I just made some with a little lavender oil and something called Peace & Calming oil. It smells good. I will try it after it chills down and has that buttery feeling to it.

### nonchalant — 2013-02-12T21:45:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75594>

For 3 days now I have pried off a small chunk of tallow from the jar in the fridge (I rendered it a year ago) and rubbed it on my hand with the dermatitis/eczema/whatever rash. I like the effect. I first dunk my hand in water with sea salt, epsom salts, and seaweed, let the back of my hand air dry, and then rub on the tallow. I used to do this several times a day, using olive oil after the dunk. And if I am diligent, my rash stays away. But the tallow is now allowing me to just treat my hand once/day, with no itching later in the day. I can barely tell the difference now in the skin of both hands!

Thanks so much, Rachelle, for suggesting this!

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-15T00:55:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75908>

I made the tallow and added essential oils. It stunk when I was making it (definitely grass fed bones) and it stunk when it was finished. I wish I could afford to add an entire bottle of lavender oil! In any case, the smell is mostly gone with the addition of lavender and lemon oils. I have been using it on my feet (it has a beautiful velvety consistency that I love - it's like what I imagine super expensive lotions to be like) and my feet have never been so baby soft (and that's with going most of the time barefoot, too!).

### Rachelle — 2013-02-15T19:03:11+0200

# Thread: I'm living right next to ...practically under power lines..am I doomed?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-living-right-next-to-practically-under-power-lines-am-i-doomed.5816/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2

### Raiken3712 — 2013-02-09T07:18:24+0200

# Thread: Kruse is deadly wrong in condemning all high intensity exercise.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx

### LightForTheNight — 2013-02-09T20:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75050>

PS this was supposed to get posted in moster thread, not adrenal RX, appreciate if it could be moved.

Mitochondrial redox state correlates with Ca2+ spark activity. Ca2+ sparks develop fastest in fibers poor in mitochondria and slowest in cells rich in mitochondria. The appearance of ca2+ sparks in permeabilized skeletal muscle cells depends on the fibre's oxidative strength and that misbalance between mitochondial ROS production and the fibre's ability to fight oxidative stress is likely to be responsible for unmasking ca2+ sparks in skinned preperations.

Skeletal muscle depends on ATP supply to meet its energy demands. There are three major sources of ATP in muscle: creatine phosphate, anaerobix glycolysis and oxidative phosphorylation. The relative contribution of each ATP source varies among muscle fibre types. Type 1 (slow-twitch, oxidative) and type IIa (fast-twitch, glycotic-oxidative) fibres are rich in mitochondria. They rely for their ATP production on oxidative phosphorylation. In contrast, type IIb (fast-twitch, glycolytic) fibres, are MITOCHONDRIA POOR and have very effective glycolytic ATP sythesis.

What this means is that **fast twitch musles correlate with Mitochondrial REDOX state**. If you want to build a strong mitochondrial base FOR YOUR BODY, if you want health then working out type IIb fibres is one of the single best things you can do for yourself (period).

During short intense exercise, glycogen in the muscle is used up (by IIb fibres).
Sugar is then shuttled to these cells and all is well.
However if glycogen stores are never depleted sugar gets shunted into fat instead, resulting in obesity.

Depleting glycogen activates hormone sensitive lipase so that fats are mobilised from body stores and made available for energy supply (good for people trying to lose fat).
If sugar in the blood stream following a meal cannot be taken up because glycogen stres are full, then this excess sugar sticks on to other things creating Advanced Glycation End products or AGE's , which accelerate the normal aging process. One such example is glycosylated haemoglobin which is measured to assess blood sugar control in diabetics.

Good fast twitch fibres = good mitochondrial function = good cardiovascular fitness.

Look, Jack has great ideas, and I've learned a ton about the ETC, mitochondria and circadian biology. And I think, no I KNOW he is right that it is at the ROOT of neolithic disease. But this bullshit he preaches about waiting 2 years to become leptin sensitive is crap. He is probably right that we need electron dense foods and most his advice is good-great BUT HIGH intensity exercise, for a VERY short duration is only going to result in MORE fast twitch IIb fibres, which is going to be positively correlated with mitochondrial REDOX state. This means keeping calcium where it needs to be and exceptional CARDIOVASCULAR health.

Do NOT think that I'm advocating endurance training, or any crap like Cross Fit. I'm saying that very short duration HIGH intensity exercises are ESSENTIAL for sublime mitochondrial function and I don't give a sh\*t what Jack says, he is WRONG, he might be right about limiting carbs, but he is DEAD wrong about avoiding all high intensity very short duration exercises. I fully expect this to be deleted as I'm right and it contradicts Jack who seems to have Alec Baldwin's God Complex. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8g2dkDh4ov4](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8g2dkDh4ov4)

### LightForTheNight — 2013-02-09T22:24:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75071>

@caveman, a person's slow twitch:fast-twitch:super fast twitch is definitely changeable over your lifetime. I don't have the studies off hand but if you want them I'll find them.

@tellmisty, vo2 max is fine to measure but it's not necessarily predictive of mitochondrial function. A marathon runner may score very high on vo2 max because slow twitch muscle fibers are so much denser in mitochondria than fast twitch and will TF give you a higher vo2 max score, this is not predictive of health realistically though. What you want is Efficienct mitochondria, which is indicated by things other than vo2 max, maybe cytochrome - c for example.

Jack has said point blank that HIIT is not for people who are leptin resistant and I disagree ENTIRELY with that statement, HIIT increases insulin and Leptin sensitivity and lowers resting glucose.

@**Jack Kruse** I acknowledged in my thread that **I disagree with both cross-fit and endurance exercises**, as I consider them one and the same. I don't need to be shown why, you do a good job at proving it, but I already know they are bad... And you, **JACK KRUSE**, said the words "**Not if your LR...........it means you need more electrons......before you can lose them again**" in response to "**Aren't anaerobic exercises like HIIT good for mitochondrial function?**". TF telling the VAST majority of obese and overweight individuals **NOT to do HIIT**. What you said leaves little room for misinterpretation and I'm upset that you acuse me of responding to what I heard and not what you said, I am most definitely responding to what you said.

@freesia Okay, I've been following this blog since about the same time as you and have read most of Jack's blogs, enough to know what his public pov on the subject is, I'm not sure what you're implying I missed?

Look, Jack, you are going into great detail with a lot of these processes and that's great, but you are seriously touting these semi-bogus claims which include grounding and basing everything on diet. Sure maybe you eventually say that people who have already become leptin sensitive should maybe try HIIT, but that's bogus, this is information people should hear right away. It immediately will help the vast majority of your followers to improve mitochondrial function and it just really pisses me off that you have so much great information and then come to such seemingly inadequate conclusions.

How about if you want to increase mitochondrial function and live in a reduced state you start incorporating 15-25 second all out bursts of energy followed by a 1-2 minute rest repeated 5-6 times a few times a week? Your information is only as good as the results it gets people, body recomposition is what most people need for health body recomposition without sacrificing their overall health. If anyone seriously wants to improve their mitochondrial function by dramatic figures they should be doing activities that increase IIb fibres, such as a program possibly like Sprint 8 or something comparable.

The things you are recommending people do are a 6 on a scale from 1-10. If you start seriously looking into this and suggesting it to people that'll change to a 9/10. If you seriously care about helping people you'll incorporate some serious protocols instead of this hippy grounding crap.

### Lyndra — 2013-02-10T04:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75171>

> [Zorica Vuletic said:](/goto/post?id=75136)
>
> Lyndra, how did your sleep go down hill? Was it merely in length or did you feel unrested? Could it be theorized/tested that if sleep is a bit less but the quality is improved? Example 7 hrs of very high quality sleep vs. 8 hrs of lesser quality...?
>
>
> I am curious and I shall test (to varying degrees) on myself. I will see how my sleep goes tonight.

Sleep has been bad for almost 2 years since I quit synthetic hormone replacement cold turkey. I started BHRT a year ago and am still trying to get everything dialed in (thyroid, adrenals, methylation, etc.). So my n=1 on sleep is not going to be applicable to many out there. I log it based on how many times I wake up during the night and how long it takes me to get back to sleep.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-10T04:47:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75173>

Lyndra, strange how the synthetic hormones did not cause (or seemed to have caused you) any troubles? Unless ...if there was a reason outside of synthetic that you quit cold turkey.

I hope you can figure things out soon. Hormones is a sucky thing to have to figure out. Ugh.

Ok, so maybe for your case it was not the d-ribose 'per se' that affected your sleep....

Now there are lots of different experiments I want to try with this d-ribose. lol

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-02-10T18:57:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75229>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=75171)
>
> Sleep has been bad for almost 2 years since I quit synthetic hormone replacement cold turkey. I started BHRT a year ago and am still trying to get everything dialed in (thyroid, adrenals, methylation, etc.). So my n=1 on sleep is not going to be applicable to many out there. I log it based on how many times I wake up during the night and how long it takes me to get back to sleep.

Lyndra - have you looked into the work done on relevance of amino acids to things like sleep? My husband is back on Thursday - so if not perhaps we can get our oysters in on Friday and discuss!!!

### Lyndra — 2013-02-10T19:13:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75233>

No, I haven't looked at that. Anytime I've tried taking aminos, I've gotten a migraine. Jack says that indicates poor mitochondrial function.

Now I am wondering whether my hot flash episodes are related to iodine (I'm convinced they are due to estrogen deficiency; I'm on plenty and the hot flashes don't feel the same). I was going hyperthyroid and stopped Armour several weeks ago but still get mild hot flashes every couple of hours, day and night. I just read of a woman whose insomnia and hot flashes went away after stopping supplemental iodine so I tapered and am now off that.

Oh, and I'm up for oysters any time!

### Lyndra — 2013-02-10T19:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75235>

> [Zorica Vuletic said:](/goto/post?id=75173)
>
> Lyndra, strange how the synthetic hormones did not cause (or seemed to have caused you) any troubles? Unless ...if there was a reason outside of synthetic that you quit cold turkey.
>
> I hope you can figure things out soon. Hormones is a sucky thing to have to figure out. Ugh.
>
> Ok, so maybe for your case it was not the d-ribose 'per se' that affected your sleep....
>
> Now there are lots of different experiments I want to try with this d-ribose. lol

Maybe not so strange. Because of a hysterectomy at 35, I had very low hormone levels so the synthetics were filling in for what I didn't have. I used to sleep like a rock - my husband used to tease me about how easy it was for me to fall and stay asleep. I quit because I was afraid of cancer from the synthetic estrogen.

I think things would have gone downhill eventually but I accelerated the process by depriving my body of hormones which caused my sleep to fall apart. I kept up exercising and tanked my adrenals and hit bottom. I think we're doing well at getting the female hormones dialed in but something is still not quite right.

I have been changing a lot in the last month (stopped Armour; stopped iodine; added d-ribose, Q10 and lipoic acid; added lactoferrin, Vit E, C, and zinc; decreased EMF exposure) so who know effect any one thing is having. Nothing like having a multitude of variables to contend with

### freesia — 2013-02-10T21:33:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75264>

I took 10g d-Ribose again yesterday and woke again at 4am this morning! I did go back to sleep...probably within half an hour, but I am sticking with just 5g d-Ribose today (I take it in the am) to see if it makes any difference.

I know we've taken the original topic off course so I'll post and further updates in the d-Ribose thread where it might be more useful for anyone researching or whatever later on?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-11T06:03:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-is-deadly-wrong-in-condemning-all-high-intensity-exercise.5820/post-75364>

I think Stephen has clarified his issues on the blog comments of EMF 4 quite well recently........I think this thread has run its course.

# Thread: CT and Raise Money Too!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-raise-money-too.5822/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2013-02-09T22:49:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-raise-money-too.5822/post-75076>

I did the Vermont Special Olympics Penguin Plunge today. Outside temps were 14degrees and the wind was howling from the remains of east coast storm.

I thought I would post a few pictures:

The group I went in with disappeared pretty quick...so I took a few laps around the water to the amusement of the fireman who were in survival suits. One of my sandals never made it to the dressing room so I had a sock on one foot which promptly floated off so that was another source of amusement for everyone! All in all a fine day and I raised almost 300. for the athletes. I was trying to go for a beach look in my sun hat and glasses, but the wind kind of ruined the effect!

I spoke to as many people as I could about CT and my pond dips. I wouldn't be surprised if the Newport local paper doesn't call. The Firefighters were aware of the practice too...so I did my bit to spread the word...and it was gratifying to show-off just a little. I'm not somebody who likes crowds or big groups, so this was a stretch for me, but well worth it.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-09T22:55:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-raise-money-too.5822/post-75078>

Awesome !!! SEahorse.. LOLing at the firefighters in the cold water survival suits... Yeah for stepping outside your comfort zone, rocking the CT And being a Starfish... you are a rockstar!
