### MamaGrok — 2013-02-23T02:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77182>

I am very excited to find this product. Certainly its time has come. I really would love to hear your story. Was grounding the only change you made at the time, and what were the health changes you experienced, and how dramatic?

If you have already posted this story elsewhere, such as on your website, feel free to point me there.

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-02-23T08:54:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77230>

> [MamaGrok said:](/goto/post?id=77182)
>
> I am very excited to find this product. Certainly its time has come. I really would love to hear your story. Was grounding the only change you made at the time, and what were the health changes you experienced, and how dramatic?
>
> If you have already posted this story elsewhere, such as on your website, feel free to point me there.

Since childhood, I have endured a persistent neuralgia issue and one of the most effective means of managing this condition has been through earthing. After reading the book on earthing, it explained the relief I found going barefoot outdoors and inspired me to help share this experience with others.

You can check out our bio's and how it all started here on the website [http://www.earthrunners.com/about](http://www.earthrunners.com/about)

We also made a video telling our story for our kickstarter campaign.\[video=youtube;GEDXR-kWtHo\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GEDXR-kWtHo\[/video\]

### Rachelle — 2013-03-06T13:57:50+0200

When a person is born with flat feet its forever. Not sure if a rolfer could help a baby with this or not, but they cannot undo this as an adult.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-06T14:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79290>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=79203)
>
> Would love to get a pair, but my feet are completely flat i.e. no arch whatsoever, so I couldn't walk far in these things until pain sets in.

I am completely flat footed and experience no pain when I walk barefoot. It's a pain in the ass to not have ankle support along with no arch. Thinking I am probably better off allowing my feet to just do what is natural to them and not force them to hold my ankle up. That for me hurt me more than anything and it was only after reading and having rolfing done that I understood the ramifications of forcing my feet to hold my ankle up that hurt me the most. I never could wear the expensive orthotics that were made for me, especially after rolfing.

I was also born with my feet turned in and according to Ida Rolf the doctors did more damage in the way they forced my feet to turn straight. If only my mother would have known better and taken me to a rolfer maybe things would have been different.

Sure glad I am over 50 and not lquite as vain as I use to be. I can now allow myself to live with having my feet look a bit deformed. Well hell...cause they are.

### JoeBranca — 2013-03-06T22:12:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79342>

Good stuff, Mike. I've had my Ultralight (no bedding) with nylon laces for a couple of weeks now. They were actually on order when I was out "earthing" with Jack and Erwan in Santa Fe in January ; )

The shape and minimalist feel are great. I am still futzing with the nylon straps most days, I am not sure if this is a break in period still or I'm just doing it wrong (I did watch the videos). Seems the tightness I need to make on the straps to keep the heel from sliding back and off the edge, for example, ends up being so tight that some blood circulation to the front of the foot is partially impaired. So it's like I'm trying to find that "loose but not too loose" strapping a lot, at least if I want to do running or more off road stuff with them and not worry about the foot getting out of position.

I went for the model with the least support, but now I suppose the Quantums are the choice for better off road tread. Most of the time the Ultralight is fine, but when I'm going up natural terrain on an uphill, the grip is not as much as I need at times (for example climbing up on a layer of damp wood chips on a steep incline). Also I'm doing lots more MovNat this year... which I'm still thinking barefoot is best anyways, but at least this is good to get me to MovNat destinations, except for the occasional slippage.

Unless I successfully jerry rig a copper wiring in the soles of my KSO Treks or something like, maybe getting a pair of Quantums for more rugged terrains and activities is the ticket?

thanks

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-06T23:11:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79350>

Joe, are these the same sandals Erwan wore? I'm considering buying some, as well, but not sure if I'd better off trying to run in my moccasins.

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-03-06T23:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79356>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=79342)
>
> Good stuff, Mike. I've had my Ultralight (no bedding) with nylon laces for a couple of weeks now. They were actually on order when I was out "earthing" with Jack and Erwan in Santa Fe in January ; )
>
> The shape and minimalist feel are great. I am still futzing with the nylon straps most days, I am not sure if this is a break in period still or I'm just doing it wrong (I did watch the videos). Seems the tightness I need to make on the straps to keep the heel from sliding back and off the edge, for example, ends up being so tight that some blood circulation to the front of the foot is partially impaired. So it's like I'm trying to find that "loose but not too loose" strapping a lot, at least if I want to do running or more off road stuff with them and not worry about the foot getting out of position.
>
> I went for the model with the least support, but now I suppose the Quantums are the choice for better off road tread. Most of the time the Ultralight is fine, but when I'm going up natural terrain on an uphill, the grip is not as much as I need at times (for example climbing up on a layer of damp wood chips on a steep incline). Also I'm doing lots more MovNat this year... which I'm still thinking barefoot is best anyways, but at least this is good to get me to MovNat destinations, except for the occasional slippage.
>
> Unless I successfully jerry rig a copper wiring in the soles of my KSO Treks or something like, maybe getting a pair of Quantums for more rugged terrains and activities is the ticket?
>
> thanks

Hey Joe, thanks for the feedback! Glad to here from a fellow movnatter.

Our [Ultralight model](http://www.earthrunners.com/products-page/product-category/ultralight-earth-runners) is definitely our most barefoot sandal and I mean this in terms of ground feel and traction. As the sandals continue to self mold it should help hold your foot in place for more demanding applications. The laces also need to be re-tightened regularly until the sandal has completely formed, due to the fact that the loops will come closer and closer to the foot. Over time the laces will settle in help making putting them on easier. The one thing you want to be sure of to ensure the security of the laces is that they criss-cross as they go through the loops on both sides of the ankle. This prevents the heel strap from loosening when going up hill.

Our [Quantum model](http://www.earthrunners.com/products-page/product-category/quantum-earth-runners) is great for movnat due to the traction is provides. You can keep the models rather minimal and lightweight with 2mm Vibram tread (better than Ultralight tr) or go for ultimate traction with the 4mm mm heavy tread. The one thing that also helps is the heel cup. When moving and climbing through nature the foot experiences a lot of shear force and the heel cup helps to hold the sandal in place for these applications.

Stay tuned as we plan to offer a flat sole sandal with heavy tread soon!

### Rachelle — 2013-03-07T16:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79466>

Well that's it, next month I want to buy a pair of Ultralight and see how they do. I am pretty sure they will work out, especially if they mold to your feet to hold them in place. Thanks much, Earthrunner.

### shilohman — 2013-03-07T19:49:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79494>

Earthrunner, have you ever thought about producing a sandal that can attach to the bottom of walking shoes? I know there are some problems to iron out, like getting conduction to the skin and securely attaching the sandal to the bottom of the shoe. # Thread: HELP! I'm a mess!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HeidiHen — 2013-02-22T15:40:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/post-77023>

Ok. So, what gives? I'm up 10 pounds, yes TEN pounds since beginning the BAB and CT in January. The scale just continues to climb. Another two pounds this week.

Right now, since eating very low carb (under 25) and high fat (60-80% of cals) for almost two years (no dairy, no potatoes, no nuts, lots of coconut oil and butter), my weight and moods are my biggest complaint. I have only managed to gain weight over the past 2 years. I'm 50+ pounds overweight and am edgy and irritable quite often. I'm doing CT as much as possible (with 4 kiddos that's only about every other day) for 40-60 minutes at about 55-60 degree water, turning off wi-fi and cell phones as much as possible, burning candles and wearing amber glasses in the evening, going to bed early (8-ish wonderful hours of sleep), and eating a BAB. I've started taking magnesium, ubiquinol, fish oil and D. I got my basic labs back and will schedule a consult for hopefully next week. In the meantime, what on earth more can I do? Soooooo frustrated!

Labs:
Triglycerides: 40
HDL: 120
LDL: 138
DHEA-sulfate: 210 (range 60.9-337.0)
Vitamin D, 25-hydroxy: 44 (range 30.0-100.0)
hsCRP: 1.24 (range 0.00-3.00)

Anyone have any suggestions they can offer???

### ashryn — 2013-02-22T16:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/post-77027>

I wish I could help.

### HeidiHen — 2013-02-22T17:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/post-77034>

Thanks, Ashryn. Just knowing someone sympathizes is helpful in itself.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-22T17:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/post-77036>

The answers are all here..........you need to realize issue one and two are pulsed EMF exposure and artificial light........start from there use CT daily, drink lots of non fluoridated water and eat Epi-Paleo Rx.

### HeidiHen — 2013-02-22T17:44:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-im-a-mess.5992/post-77044>

Thanks. I'll work on that.

# Thread: Who am I becoming?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-02-22T20:58:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77092>

As I'm sitting here having a picnic on my concrete basement floor, I'm pondering who I am these days . . . I'm not sure I know 'me' anymore. Anyone else feel this way? I don't mean this in a negative way and am certainly not insinuating any of this is wrong, but I just:

-   had a picnic on my basement floor (as mentioned) half naked so I could get all those free electrons
-   with a dozen oysters
-   half pound of sashimi
-   fermented daikon radish with ginger
-   wasn't full, so I topped it all with a bit of liver
-   and finished with some kombucha

All the while:

-   reading about EMF and figuring out the easiest way to tell the rest of the family we're cutting out wifi
-   trying to plan for some big future expenses: converting pool to saltwater, a floatation tank, magnetic mattress pads, a tanning booth, etc, etc.
-   trying to figure out how I can afford all of the above when I eventually quit my job since I am pretty much sold on the idea now that the office is full of EMF that is keeping me sick!
-   and fashioning tinfoil hats for DH and I since we're both working from home today and, for now, are still using the wifi
-   also trying to work up a schedule for turning off the breakers more than just overnight

And yet, somehow, I find myself oddly at peace with all of this! I'm feeling so peaceful, in fact, that I don't understand the arguing that seems to be occurring on the forum suddenly nor do I believe in engaging in any of it though I may have previously. My friends and family, I'm sure, would tell me I've truly lost my mind this time, but this feels very right. In my past life I'd be perusing Facebook, going out to lunch with friends, shopping online for the kids, all kinds of pointless activities. I love that my priority now is on keeping my family as healthy as possible and everything else is so trivial.

Forget the fact that our food bills are now on par with our mortgage and any extra spending money we used to enjoy is pretty much nonexistent because none of that matters if we aren't here to enjoy one another . . . this is my new mantra.

The only downside, if there is one, is that I find I can no longer relate to others very well. I've never been one to follow reality TV, or much TV at all, and since that is the hot water cooler topic I haven't "fit in" at the office for a long time now anyway, but things are just so much different now. It goes beyond "fitting in."

Off to finish those tinfoil hats now . . . just felt the need to share with _someone_ how strange I've become

### Inger — 2013-02-22T21:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77095>

BJK77,
hello from another weirdo in peace..lol
We got an EMF meter today, we buyed it all togehter, and it was like Christmas! I have been measuring all day long..lol I even took the meter to the shops! It was very intresting. Now I know a lot more. That my leptop (wired!) gives much much more EMF pollution than my other computer.. and many other things. Whole family thought it was so much fun. I am borrowing the meter to all my sibling so they can check their homes too. Tomorrow I am going to take it with me to work..
Ok it is a quite cheap trifield meter but it still gives you a clue how you can protect yourself better!

I have a real good earthing line now, from my bedroom into the river stream! Wow I am proud! Even the man in the electric shop said the river was the best place ever for earthing! I told him all about my experiments and he thought it was so cool!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-22T21:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77100>

I LOVE your story! I really appreciate the contrast between the weirdness and yet feeling at peace. We may say weird now but when you feel at peace something is going on inside of you saying this is right! Your gut is your best guide if you know how to read it. I use mine daily yet still learning what it means.

Relating has never been an issue for me. People see me as friendly outgoing and easy to get along with. But now I find I don't want to be around those who can't relate. I totally agree with you on that. I walk the beach (me...in the water, she...not so much) with a friend who "gets it" most of the time so I am lucky in this respect to have one person in the physical who I can talk to and not seem like a total freak or me wanting to run back to my more protected house.

### Hope — 2013-02-22T21:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77103>

BJK777 - I LOVE THIS POST!!!! YAYE!!!!! I feel the same way...and if you don't mind, I will print this and show this to my husband...our food bill has been bad (not as much as rent but there's only two of us but compared to many friends who spend maybe 400$ for 4 people, we live like royalty on this front). And we spend on supplements what others do on I don't know...fancy car payments? Nice clothes? Fancy overnight hotel trips and restaurants?

I am enduring people laughing at me in our ballroom dance class or monthly get-togethers with friends when I show up in the evening in my yellow sunglasses (over my regular glasses - I look AWESOME in that outfit!) and making silly comments....but I know who will have the last laugh...or giving me funny looks in an airplane when I eat a smelly can of sardines and a bit of sauerkraut or some other homemade food in a container instead of the junk meals they provide....or leaving the tap water glass untouched in a restaurant...but I don't care.

I have been bummed about EMF for years and trying to get my DH to get the significance of it but he doesn't care, still...though when I told him that the "MD I trust so much with all this stuff" is able to base a lot of his theories on Einstein (whom he also admires!!) and E=mc2 he was listening up a bit so I am dreaming some day I'll get him to read the EMF blog series. Not giving up hope and feeding him as much seafood, fat, and the MitoRx as possible (and he is gaining weight finally)...thankfully he loves seafood.

Anyway what I LOVE is that we have this community now....because not only do I have a place to get the best info from....but I KNOW there is all of you guys trying to figure out all this stuff too...I'm not alone. That was the hardest thing. And that is why I LOVE that u posted this, BJK777....my ONLY wish and dream is that one day soon, we can have our very own epipaleo reduced and EMF-free retreat to cook, eat, CT and jump in lakes, rivers and oceans together and learn. )

Woohoo and way to go!!!!

Thanks for sharing....

### BJK77 — 2013-02-23T16:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77259>

Glad to be in the company of so many other "weirdos in peace" as Inger described us! LOL!

I used to be such a high-strung, uptight person . . . not to mention a total control freak. I'm so amazed by the changes I feel in my everyday attitude and outlook on life! Just last week I admit I was a mental case, panicking about how we were going to deal with EMF and our impending doom  Now I just feel very calm, cool and collected. What a nice change . . .

Inger - I'm totally jealous of your EMF meter! I've been checking some out and trying to decide which one is best. I can't wait to 'play' with one so I can feel even more informed and know exactly what I need to do to protect my family as much as possible. I have to say, your river makes me a bit jealous too

Patty - I love that you have a "live" person you can discuss all of this with. Thankfully my DH listens and yes, even cooperates with all of this, but I can never get him to read the blog. The science stuff just doesn't interest him in anyway. He's a practical guy who just wants to know what needs to be done, so I try to decipher the science the best I can, explain it to him and then he does the research on how we can implement the necessary changes - now that I'm spelling that out I realize we are a pretty darn good team

Hope - You made me laugh picturing you in a ballroom dance class with your blue blockers on! I can only imagine what others are thinking!! And YES, we need a retreat for this community. Your description sounds perfect!!! Epi-paleo, EMF-free, CT, ocean, lots of earthing . . . sounds like a little slice of heaven

I really dislike when I'm forced into a social setting with "normal people". Even people who are health minded IRL would think living all epi-paleo + ct etc to be extreme. I've gotten to the point where I have zero desire to even have the random dinner out at a normal restaurant. At minimum you are dealing with bad oils, and it goes downhill from there.''

That being said, I'm not part of the Jack cult by any stretch of the imagination. I've gotten here by having to use some moderation, and making less than optimal choices - even when I knew better! For all that, I take the proverbial 2 forward, 1 back. Yet, I'm here all the same doing all the same crazy shit - I would bet real money my actual life is more JK authorized "optimal behaviors" than most here. Just took my own road to get here.

SO if you arrive at the same place as someone else, yet you took two different roads to get there - does that make one right or one wrong?

### endless — 2013-02-25T04:36:31+0200

I have shared a bit, and some have responded favorably. My SIL is doing cold baths and has a whole circle of friends doing face dunks. Unfortunately, those who most need it are not interested.

You have to be living under a rock not to understand the potential catastrophe of EMF. How hard can it be to walk barefoot in the ocean? On wet grass? And look at all the dissension here..........

### Inger — 2013-02-25T09:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77529>

When I do the good stuff.. collect electrones and take good care of me, some magic happends!
I gain power, energy, joy, peace, love.. and when I meet people it just touch them....! I give away from my electrones and they feel good when they are with me. And I feel good too because I have so many and when I loose some I go and collect for free from mother earth.. and I have plenty to give.. and that is when you actually need no words.. you need to not convince anyone, and when you tell something they will believe, because they see you are glowing...
This is what I want, and what I work for, and why I am healing me now. And it already works to a degree! And guess what.. it is the most beautiful thing....

I do not really care if they "listen", like that they do what I say, because it does not matter. What matters is the "now", the moment I am with the person, and that is a beautiful moment because if I am feeling so good it jumps over to the other person too... because the only thing that matters to me is how I feel really, because I have to be with me 100% of the time.
But weird is, when I feel great others feel great with me too. Magic..

### caroline — 2013-02-25T09:19:52+0200

### caroline — 2013-02-25T09:29:20+0200

Do you suppose that people we know are slightly jealous of our commitment to a new lifestyle that they don't understand?

Today I ran into a friend that I haven't seen for 4 months. She has always struggled with her weight. Last time I saw her she was slim and exercising like crazy and counting calories. Today - she seems to have gained all the weight back. Very sad.

### Inger — 2013-02-25T09:37:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-am-i-becoming.5998/post-77533>

Caroline,
it might help when you stop demanding anything from your family. Think unconditional love. It helps to think in terms like... okay I am the mother it is kind of my fault too that my child got like that because I did not teach her the right stuff.. but I did not know back then so I am not guilty in that way really, but today I know so I will live right and give her all electrons I possibly can. And that means love and caring and also accepting she lives her way because she does not see yet. But always believe, one day she will Caroline! Always believe that. Never give her up in your heart. But better use no words trying to change her if she is not receptive. Your intuition will guide you what to say and when. Just listen, keep silent and you will hear that little voice of wisdom. Always try to see behind the person. Look from 1000 mile view. It helps huge. And if you fail.. just raise up again and do better next time, I fail too Caroline! But I learn from it every time.
Just stop trying to help, and just be you, and help YOU first. That is how I think. I figure I need many years to be fully healed and until then I need not to help others because I do not have the wisdom to do it right. But I can always care, always love, always share electrones. If you have too few, go and collect and do not meet with people that have little and sucks yours out.
And guess what. I promise, if you do like this.. what you see will change.....

# Thread: Prometheus

### Inger — 2013-02-22T21:50:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prometheus.6001/post-77102>

Jacks last blog, quantum Prometheus.. I had to check who Prometheus was again..

> Prometheus (Greek: Προμηθεύς) is a Titan, culture hero, and trickster figure who, in Greek mythology, is credited with the creation of man from clay and the theft of fire for human use, an act that enabled progress and civilization. He is known for his intelligence, and as a champion of humanity.\[1\]
>
> The punishment of Prometheus as a consequence of the theft is a major theme of his mythology, and is a popular subject of both ancient and modern art. Zeus, king of the Olympian gods, sentenced the Titan to eternal torment for his transgression. The immortal Prometheus was bound to a rock, where each day an eagle, the emblem of Zeus, was sent to feed on his liver, only to have it grow back to be eaten again the next day. In some stories, Prometheus is freed at last by the hero Heracles (Hercules).
>
> In another of his myths, Prometheus establishes the form of animal sacrifice practiced in ancient Greek religion. Evidence of a cult to Prometheus himself is not widespread. He was a focus of religious activity mainly at Athens, where he was linked to Athena and Hephaestus, other Greek deities of creative skills and technology.\[2\]
>
> **In the Western classical tradition, Prometheus became a figure who represented human striving, particularly the quest for scientific knowledge, and the risk of overreaching or unintended consequences. In particular, he was regarded in the Romantic era as embodying the lone genius whose efforts to improve human existence could also result in tragedy**: Mary Shelley, for instance, gave The Modern Prometheus as the subtitle to her novel Frankenstein (1818).

[http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prometheus](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prometheus)

### Glamazon — 2013-02-23T00:48:45+0200

>
> You know Hope.. I was kind of thinking Jack might have ment Prometheus=EMFs....(...**whose efforts to improve human existence could also result in tragedy**)
>
> But yes I see what you see too..

Inger, there is a law out there that just makes me laugh in a bittersweet sort of way. It is called the law of Revenge Effects or the Law of Unintended Consequences. An obvious example: In Brazil, there were a lot of robberies of people at their ATM machines when they were withdrawing money. To be brief, Brazil began requiring that a finger on a pad to read the fingerprint be required to withdraw money. Security professionals thought that would stop the robberies. Not really because the criminals would just cut off the finger.

This article discusses more examples.
[
http://faculty.css.edu/dswenson/web/REVENGE.HTM](http://faculty.css.edu/dswenson/web/REVENGE.HTM)

I think that could be a promethean effect too.

# Thread: When trees die, so do people - interesting study
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-trees-die-so-do-people-interesting-study.6002/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-02-22T21:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-trees-die-so-do-people-interesting-study.6002/post-77104>

Came across this...fascinating study on the effect of nature on man, and how a tree-killing bug may also affect people near it....

[http://donovan.hnri.info/Studies/donovan\_et\_al.EAB.pdf](http://donovan.hnri.info/Studies/donovan_et_al.EAB.pdf)

Specifically, the natural environ- ment has been shown to decrease stress,9 increase physical activity,10 and improve air quality.11 In turn, stress,12,13 lack of physical activity,14,15 and poor air quality16 have been linked with cardiovascular and lower- respiratory-tract disease.
The pioneering work in the fıeld by Ulrich6 found that patients recovering from gall bladderremoval surgery in a room with a view of a natural scene recovered faster and took fewer pain medications than patients in a room with a view of a brick wall.

In Holland, Maas et al.19 reported a positive association between greenness and self-reported health. In a later study, Maas et al.20 analyzed the health records of 345,000 people. They found that those living in greener areas were less likely to be diagnosed with 15 of the 24 health outcomes examined. Results were particularly strong for anxiety and depression and for children and those with lower SES. Park and colleagues9 showed that walking in a forest reduced heart rate and cortisol levels. Finally, in New York City, Lovasi et al.21 found that children who lived in areas with more street
trees were less likely to have asthma.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-22T23:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-trees-die-so-do-people-interesting-study.6002/post-77123>

considering trees give us O2 it makes total sense......its non locality in real time.

Little guy is one year this month.

Would love to hear from some of you moms  One year olds are not my forte...

### ashryn — 2013-02-23T01:55:07+0200

### Hope — 2013-02-23T02:54:52+0200

### ashryn — 2013-02-23T04:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77214>

Someone had a cool story about a baby that looked to be going the way of a spectrum disorder that was sorted out by removing emf and adding sunlight, cold and time on the dirt..

It has been a long time since I had my own 1 year old, and she got put on formula at 6 months because I couldn't make enough milk for her to thrive. Oh how I would have done things differently if I had known then what I know now.. Unfortunately with a new baby and no support network, you trust what the child health nurses tell you, especially when there is the threat of _you will hurt your child if you don't...

_I hope someone more helpful than me responds to your question.. There are a few people around who know an awful lot about natural child rearing, but its probably going to be guess work unless we know all about mums lifestyle and diet and medical history.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77442>

How are baby's stools? Maybe something is going on there that is preventing him from digesting or absorbing properly.....

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-25T17:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77588>

has she gone to La leche league meeting? which growth charts is the doc using CDC charts are based off Formula fed babies - where as WHO charts are based off bf babies. bf babies growth slows after 6mths and just stays constant, they don't always gain weight like FF babies. additionally is baby still on his/her growth curve? how are diapers? how much solids are being used vs breastmilk - always offer breastmilk first, then solids - and as always solids before or at the age of 1 are just for fun, if nursing baby doesn't need solids...

my bet - nothing is wrong w/baby - just a doc noticing baby isn't in the bell curve and is an outlier...

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-23T02:14:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-truth-in-this.6008/post-77180>

I call hogwash. Weston Price (seriously, folks, you gotta read his book) documented that overbites & other orthodontic problems develop within one generation of introducing processed foods.

I see it around me still - nearly all first-generation African immigrants, most first-generation northern European immigrants, and most African-Americans I know who were still eating true southern soul food twenty years ago have great, wonderful, wide palates with perfect bites. Their kids have horrible teeth just like the rest of us.

# Thread: Get Outside
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-02-23T18:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77279>

A few months ago, someone asked me where am I happiest. I thought for a bit, and realized that almost every time I feel alive and have that special feeling in my heart, I'm outside.

I know from reading here now that there are so many benefits to being outside, and they keep increasing with many of the blogs:
1) CT - from snow, water
2) Grounding - on the ground, in the water (especially ocean)
3) Vitamin D from the sun
4) photosynthesis from the sun (have I got this right? The benefit of the protons from the sun)

I still feel great outside, though, with my shoes on and on cloudy days. Of course the sun can sometimes make my heart sing (and I always comment, which makes my partner laugh. He says I should be a weather girl LOL). What benefit are we getting from cloudy/rainy days?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-23T20:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77311>

I couldn't agree more about being outside, Miss Weather Girl! I love my walks on the beach and think I need a pair of grounding shoes or sandals to ground while I hike as I love the movement of hiking, being out in nature, and the scenery makes me smile.

Rainy days, in a place where it rarely rains, clean and freshen the air. This is just my opinion, although I think there is some science to back up this belief. It always looks better and the air feels cleaner after a nice rain. I find I want to be outside doing just about anything the day after a nice rain.

### caroline — 2013-02-24T01:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77358>

I love being outside as much as possible - I think it changes your whole attitude towards everything. It makes me much more mellow ..how life is meant to be lived......

### BJK77 — 2013-02-24T14:48:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77406>

Definitely agree with you all. I've been making it a priority to get outside more often lately and I'm remembering how it always made me feel so much more alive as a child. I wish I could say I was always earthing when I was out, but I still think there's something to be said for just breathing in the fresh air and appreciating the beauty around us.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-24T16:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77416>

Undoubtedly. Being indoors crushes my soul.

### PrimalPam — 2013-02-24T17:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77427>

Many years ago, I changed careers. I was inside an office with zero windows and lots of computers, and I switched from job to job, each more miserable than the last. I switched to an outside sales job; I actually earned 75% LESS at my first one than in my cubicle-world one, but I was 1000 times happier from Day 1. I eventually switched to different companies/sales jobs to get my earnings back up (gotta pay the bills) and have never looked back. Every single day, I'm outside as I go in and out of businesses. In the summer, I wear a lot of short-sleeve dresses without hose, and just spending time outside getting stuff out of my trunk to bring in, I get Vitamin D.

At that first outside sales job, I didn't understand why I was so much happier; I just knew on the first day that I had made the right decision. It just felt so...good...to be out and about, in and out of the sun and the elements. I'm in Houston, where it's a million degrees in the summer, and I just don't care. Or it's pouring rain, and I think it's lovely. Ever since making the decision to move my career to more time spent outdoors during the day, I'm just so much happier.

### nonchalant — 2013-02-24T18:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77447>

I've been trying to figure out a new path for myself, Pam. I think one of the keys is to avoid debt so that we are more flexible, financially. DH is wanting to buy a new vehicle, but I am adamant against it. We are now looking for a used vehicle that we can pay cash for.

All the outside jobs seem to pay less, and that's fine. I just have to juggle things around to swing it. Like using grounding, sunshine, darkness, cold, seafood and good water, instead of continually buying this expensive ubiquinol and other supplements.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-24T18:26:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-outside.6016/post-77450>

water and sun and CT.........critical

# Thread: EMF and Nueroplasticity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-nueroplasticity.6017/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike NYC — 2013-02-23T18:54:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-nueroplasticity.6017/post-77280>

So I'm about halfway through EMF 7, just re-read the food electrons blog, and I need to take a break and do some other work before I spend my whole weekend reading, but I have so many thoughts going on in my mind that I feel like I need to share.

Since EMF 5 & 6, specifically the paper and the video showing how EMF radiation changes glucose metabolism in neurons and how dark neurons developed in mice after EMF exposures, I have been wondering about neuroplasticity, specifically metaplasticity as in the work of Jeffrey Kleim. It seems to me that the more plastic we can keep our brains and the more plastic and flexible we are able to maintain the ability of our brains to continually rewire quickly and efficiently will help us to continue optimal function using non-damaged neurons to create connections that take over for connections involving a damaged one. In this way it seems that the better our cells can signal with one another, the better they can modulate that signal as needed as well. Perhaps they may even be able to signal in networks that have some sort of quantum quality where parts of the network can be effectively "off" and "on" at the same time? That last part is a bit of a random thought, and I don't have a great handle on quantum theory, but I'm just thinking out loud.

It does seem to me that the better we are at expressing this neuro and meta-plasticity , the better we will be able to navigate the problems modern life throws our way at the neurological and cellular level. This is why continued learning, novel movement, and providing the brain and body proper fuel and activation are essential for life.

If chronic pain can both create cortical plastic changes and can also alter DNA expression; but through movement, mirror box therapies, and other works of Ramachandran and Moseley, we can create cortical plastic change in the other direction that improves the pain, that would change DNA as well. Furthermore, thought, especially conscious critical thinking against the grain seems to require plasticity and an ability to change (dopamine). I think I am really seeing how true that "a thought can change your DNA" quote really is.

It also seems to me that environmental/evolutionary mismatch is a type of sensory mismatch (analogous to Visual Vestibular Proprioception integration problems), then EV mismatch is also likely a chronic nociceptive stimulus to the nervous system and the body.... however it may not have the amplitude to cause an acute pain experience and it takes time to develop into neolithic disease conditions.

Then I saw this post about quantum entanglement - [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5786-Quantum-Entanglement](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5786-Quantum-Entanglement)
That to me says something of the power of this metaplasticity expanded to a society, and type of uber-plasticity maybe, and the role of the starfish in being the change you hope to see in others... and the more people that make positive changes, the faster that change can spread. For a while the EMF series was making me rather sad when I thought about the future, but looking at it this way I see the potential for it to be bright. Though the path is not without challenges.

One thing I do know is that whenever I work with someone as a training client, after asking what their goals are and finding ways I can help them to achieve them, I always ask myself how what I am doing with them is affecting their brain and how their brain is affecting their movement/workout and the change they desire to make. Over the last 3 years or so, neuroscience and neurology has become a huge part of how I have approached physical training, and what I have learned on this blog over the last 1.5 years have had an incredible impact on how I view all of that.

Some cool links that helped these thoughts come together:
[http://charbonniers.org/2013/02/16/the-thalamus-as-conductor/](http://charbonniers.org/2013/02/16/the-thalamus-as-conductor/) - mainly as an idea of how quickly (and possibly temporarily?) brain connections can change, be rerouted, and differ.
[http://www.mcgill.ca/channels/news/chronic-pain-alters-dna-marking-brain-225012](http://www.mcgill.ca/channels/news/chronic-pain-alters-dna-marking-brain-225012)
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15326245](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15326245)

As a side note I found this last month writing a small article about how structure creates function in movement for a course I am doing (EMF2 made me look for a space example I could use). Its about bone loss in space in spite of exercise using resistance training and simulated "gravity" provided by bungee cords on a traedmill. Key point - the cosmonauts lost more bone and lean tissue than the "control" group which was patients on 4 months of bed rest on earth with no exercise! [http://www.ismni.org/jmni/pdf/2/leblanc.pdf](http://www.ismni.org/jmni/pdf/2/leblanc.pdf)

This has been a bit of a ramble, and I may not be correct in my understanding of everything, but that it how it is appearing to me at the moment.
Anyway, signing off as I need to do some work so I can get back to EMF 7 and checking out Jack's book.... if I didn't get these thoughts out of my head I never would have been able to focus on other stuff....
Have a great day everyone!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-23T20:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-nueroplasticity.6017/post-77307>

Mike you are spot on the money with it all.......that makes me happy because the message is getting thru to some starfish........awesome.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-23T20:05:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-nueroplasticity.6017/post-77308>

Most people can not comprehend how something on one side of the planet can affect the biology of someone else......it does and has forever. We are just realizing just how coherent life is. It is coherent with cells, Earth quake faults, Volcanos, weather, and tsunami, solar and lunar cycles.......and your partner. Nothing is not connected. When things go awry world wide we see ripples when we look........just like when you stub your toe we see a ripple in you post central gyrus that shows the pain and sends an perineural injury current to fix it.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Humans are semi-conductors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/humans-are-semi-conductors.6020/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2013-02-23T20:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/humans-are-semi-conductors.6020/post-77310>

After getting through EMF-7 this was one of the more interesting points to be made. The example given was about bone.

Semi-conductors are great because of their non-linear properties. A diode (an n-type (lots of available electrons) material attached to a p-type (lots of available "holes" = opposite of an electron = positive charge) has a junction potential, since holes diffuse into the ntype material and electrons into the ptype. The diode operates as a short circuit in the "on" position, and current readily flows through it. In the "off" position it is pretty much an open circuit. That's the basic principle, and it only exhibits this behavior when operated in the right voltage range. There's plenty of other non-linear effects to be had, and it only gets much more complicated when we talk about npn juntions, etc.

If bone is a piezo-electric material, then it would create an electric charge in response to mechanical stress, like other piezo-electric materials (crystals). And then what? I didn't understand much more in the explanation of the "circuit." Where is the diode (bone) in relation to the voltage source (what is the voltage source? How is the diode operated (when is it forward biased, when is it reversed), and for what reasons? A magnetic field (DC) doesn't have effect on a diode's electrical characteristics, so I'm still not putting that piece of the puzzle in with the rest of them.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-23T21:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77321>

I have a cat that came from a hoarder's house so she appears to have "issues". She doesn't mind other cats around her but has no use for them. She feels the same about dogs. However she pretty much dislikes all people and has all her 6 years except for DH and me. She runs and hides if the doorbell rings of hears the garage door open. She lives secluded in the bedroom 99.9% of the time.

I just recently switched my cat's food to a freeze dried fish based food. Since cats are carnivores she needs her daily dose of protein. I've read where cats cannot digest carbohydrates (ie kibble) very where if at all. It's only been two weeks but she has come out into the main part of the house about 30% of the time now and will sit with me peacefully or wonder around on her own. Her high alert mode is still there but in a much calmer way. We even felt a little earthquake last night and she just continue to lie next to me as the house shook! I expect more changes as time goes on.

Has anyone else had animal changes when switching up the diet?

### diane — 2013-02-23T22:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77324>

Absolutely. When I first switched my dogs from raw (and I was feeding them a good quality kibble) even the dog walker asked me what I did - they were just calmer. I've also had people switch their dogs from crappy kibble to good kibble and have noticed a change in hyperness.

Cats are usually much pickier eaters than dogs, so good to see changes in cats too!

### SeaJane — 2013-02-23T22:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77326>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=77321)
>
> I just recently switched my cat's food to a freeze dried fish based food.

May I ask the name of the food? I have 2 cats who eat grain-free high protein kibble that seems to work pretty well for them and I keep looking for better alternatives. Our dog is eating a dehydrated raw food that works for her and me (easy to rehydrate and not smelly like canned food). Thanks.

### freesia — 2013-02-23T22:25:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77328>

My dog has fresh protein, plus kibble...but her kibble is freeze dried fish and herbs...very low carb....I think maybe 20% max? (No grains).

The dog trainer we used for puppy classes mentioned many dogs do dramatically better behaviourally just from switching to what she called "more biologically appropriate" kibble, but I imagine they'd do even better on a fresh raw diet.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-23T22:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77331>

My cat has been on grain-free, high protein, and as high of fat kibble as I could get since I got her at 6 months old. My last cat became diabetic at 10 yrs old so I thought I should start this one out grain-free. At about 3 yrs old I thought she still had allergies to something but couldn't figure out what. Then someone told me their dog was allergic to chicken. Yep, so was my cat. But she still had these socialization issues. So that brings me to this post on her food.

My favorite of the brands is the KiwiPeak. Seems to be the best ingredients. I have used this as a topping on top of her kibble for a couple of years so I know she really likes this one. The second one I use is the Primal Freeze-Dried Formula. This one has a few veggies and fruits but it is 53% protein and 30% fat and this is the one she chows down on the fastest. I did quite a bit of research and nothing seems really perfect unless I were to make up food for her every day. Since she is improving I'll keep doing this as I believe it is really helping her.

And the quantity of how much she eats has gone way down. There is so much in these nuggets that they don't need as much. It's more expensive but I think in the end the overall cost may actually be less.

We feed the dog high protein and high fat, 100% natural kibble. Both animals get RO remineralized water. Geeze, they will probably outlive us both!

### freesia — 2013-02-23T23:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77336>

Patty we made that observation too in our pup compared to the neighbours, she eats way less bulk.
Cheap nasty petfood is a false economy....hmm...same as for humans with all the health costs later on.

It's great your cat's improving!

### diane — 2013-02-24T01:42:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77356>

I notice a huge difference in water intake on raw. When I go to kibble (which I occasionally do a week or so of high quality kibble here and there) they drink a ton of water. When I go back to raw, they hardly drink any. There's also a huge difference in the poop with raw, even vs. a high quality kibble. It's like picking up rabbit pellets from my 85 lb dogs LOL.

### Hope — 2013-02-24T02:36:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77361>

not EP but....

[http://feline-nutrition.org/nutrition/proper-feeding-from-pottengers-cats](http://feline-nutrition.org/nutrition/proper-feeding-from-pottengers-cats)

### endless — 2013-02-24T03:12:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77366>

My three cats and one dog are all on raw food. I used to feed a high quality kibble (grain free) but with cats especially it's asking for trouble eventually. I nursed one cat through kidney failure, and have another now that has kidney disease...the one I inherited 2 years ago after he'd been on kibble for 8 years!

All my animals smell better, have smaller, less stinky poops, and nicer coats since the transition. It's more expensive but I can't justify feeding them something that I know will reduce their quality of life in the long run. Kibble around here is reserved (for the cats) for when I am away and someone else is feeding them. It is a HUGE treat, they love it, but interestingly enough, they are eager for their raw lamb and chicken when I get home!

If frozen raw is too much of a hassle, the dried raw stuff is pretty good too, I've used that very successfully in the past.

Wow, NYC really is a mess when you look at this.... Can't wait to be in my new apt with less right on top of me....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-23T23:56:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/search-for-antennas-in-your-area.6023/post-77339>

MiKe I was at Bergdorf two yrs ago and picked up 58 WIfi in the store.......but i did have my tin foil hat on.

### Mike NYC — 2013-02-24T00:15:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/search-for-antennas-in-your-area.6023/post-77347>

I have 28 wifi networks in my apartment right now....... and almost all have at least a 60% or higher signal. I'm excited to see the difference when I move. Conveniently my lease is almost up and it was getting to expensive here anyway.
Given all of that I'm a bit surprised that the labs I had done over the summer weren't worse (though I know they were not ideal) especially after 3+ years here at the time. T was 934, DHEA-S 269, HS-CRP 0.2, vit D 55
I expect those are better now because I am sleeping a lot better and I feel better and have much better energy and cognition. I need to get them done again, maybe when I move and then again a few months later to re-assess.

# Thread: Finland Parents Let Babies Nap In Sub-Zero Weather
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finland-parents-let-babies-nap-in-sub-zero-weather.6026/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lily1 — 2013-02-24T00:56:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finland-parents-let-babies-nap-in-sub-zero-weather.6026/post-77353>

.
Interesting article about parents in Finland & other Nordic Countries.... Parents know the health benefits of being cold! Parents take their children outside in sub-zero weather so they can take a nap. The parents are doing two important treatments in one shot: CT & Sleep... Can you imagine what Americans would think if someone one told them to put their baby out in sub-zero weather for a nap? lol

**Would you put your baby or toddler outside in the freezing cold for their lunchtime nap? Most Nordic parents wouldn't give it a second thought. For them it's part of their daily routine**

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/magazine-21537988](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/magazine-21537988)

### ruby — 2013-02-24T02:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-this-a-good-idea.6029/post-77362>

[http://campaigns.dailykos.com/p/dia/action/public/?action\_KEY=322](http://campaigns.dailykos.com/p/dia/action/public/?action_KEY=322)

The Federal Communications Commission is considering a proposal to open up a wider share of the high-quality wireless spectrum for public use. This would be an important first step toward making fast, free, public Wi-Fi available to everyone.

Predictably, the wireless industry doesn't like this idea, and would rather the FCC auction off more high-quality wireless spectrum to private carriers, and leave less open to public use. However, a lack of competitionâ€”from public or private sourcesâ€”is one of the reasons why broadband service in the United States has become too slow, too costly, and entirely unavailable to many people.

# Thread: If you had the chance............
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-02-24T08:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77395>

If you had the chance to build a new home for your family ...... where would be your top pick? location?

What kind of a house would you build? What materials would you use? What eco-systems would you put in place?

Would it be a big house? or would you be more content with a small home now?

Would a log house be better? or a straw bale house? or...???? or would you build a bunker in the side of a hill?

When we know better ..... we do better. So how much better can we do???

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-24T09:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77397>

First I would choose my community. People and family are important. I've lived close to family in a community which was mean spirited and nasty. Choose both the community and family that are accepting and congenial and with other values that are important to you. A place with people largely like myself. (I've lived with diversity... nuff said.) A place without crime, no war and where there is no religious, cultural or ethnic persecution of your group.
Secondly I would want it where there were good growing conditions for food (good water with fertile soils), a place that didn't flood or get regular droughts (over a 300 year period) and that is not known for bush fires. It should have trees.
Where the temperatures were largely between 0 and 30 C. 0 is cold enough for me in winter.
Where there are both education and work possibilities for the children/grandchildren.

Then I would like to have a high mass house that doesn't need much heating or cooling. Probably solar powered. Not sure about the whole electricity thing, but frankly on a high protein diet I need refrigeration and freezer space. I probably also need good internet access - hubby would never move anywhere without it. Nor would the grandchildren. Its too useful. Though what this means in terms of EMFs I'm not sure yet.

Probably living with or next door to family - not because we live in each other's pockets - its just easier when we don't have to do important things alone. Dealing with car crashes, hospital visits, children's illness while the parent has to work, etc are all so much easier with extra adults around.

### ashryn — 2013-02-24T10:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77398>

I think a hobbit house in the side of a cliff overlooking the ocean and backing onto a forest.. Go out the front door, you get ocean, go out the back door, you come up in a forest.. With a deep natural spring.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-24T19:25:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77464>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=77398)
>
> I think a hobbit house in the side of a cliff overlooking the ocean and backing onto a forest.. Go out the front door, you get ocean, go out the back door, you come up in a forest.. With a deep natural spring.

Oh wow! I just got such visions of happiness from your idea. I love a green foresty setting with hills for hiking but can't seem to let go of my beach. Walking in the water, knee high right now, on the wet sand energizes me like nothing else. I feel at peace with so much in my life on the beach. Family to me are people you visit. I'm not tied to the norms of society of "doing" the family things unless I want to. So moving and being in a place that specifically works for me is so much more fulfilling. I have a great family too! But that is a part everyone's individual choices and I am fed more by nature than by family (blood family).

We had a well when I lived in the NW and I have yet to taste a water that tasted as good. I would love to have access to something like that again. Very nice addition to the perfect living spot!

# Thread: Louisiana style shrimp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/louisiana-style-shrimp.6036/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DarleenMB — 2013-02-25T04:17:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/louisiana-style-shrimp.6036/post-77520>

I found this recipe on the bag of shrimp I had thawed for dinner. It is BEYOND yummy:

Louisiana Style Shrimp

JUMBO SHRIMP 21/25 COUNT

20-22 shrimp, thawed, peeled and patted dry
4 TBS Butter
4 TBS Olive Oil
2 TBS + 2tsp Worcestershire sauce
1 TBS hot pepper sauce
Â¼ - Â½ tsp cayenne pepper
1 TBS minced garlic
1.5 TBS chopped fresh rosemary or 2 tsp dry
2 TBS chopped fresh parsley or 1 TBS dry
1 tsp dry mustard.

Preheat oven to 375Â°
Put butter & olive oil into a 9 x 13 inch pan. Put into ove until butter melts.

Remove from oven and stir in remaining ingredients making sure the bottom of the pan is even coated with the sauce. Add shrimp, turning to coat both sides Marinate at room temp for 15 minutes.

Bake uncovered for 14-18 minutes until shrimp turn pink.

The recipe called for leaving the shrimp unpeeled but it was an oily mess to try and do it after so I advise peeling them first.

# Thread: Diabetes and PUFAs and saturated fats

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-02-25T11:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diabetes-and-pufas-and-saturated-fats.6038/post-77541>

Reading the chapter of the book on diabetes being an evolutionary survival mechanism where cell membranes are swapped for PUFAs in preparation for hibernation because saturated fats are to stiff to work with in cold temps got me thinking...

Those of us still eating a fair amount of saturated fats who are struggling to get cold adaptation might be having trouble because the fats are too stiff to work with.. Another reason to get fish adapted i suppose..

# Thread: Longevity of cold-exposed rats, and cancer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrÃ©s — 2013-02-25T15:02:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-77559>

I know [AKMan has already pointed out](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?373-Does-Minimum-Effective-Dose-apply-to-Cold-Adaptation&p=14760&viewfull=1#post14760) [some study about air-CT effects on longevity of mice (none)](http://cbn.eldoc.ub.rug.nl/FILES/root/2009/PhysBichZoolVaanholt/2009PhysiolBiochemZoolVaanholt.pdf). I haven't been able to find mention around here of this other study using water-CT on rats giving rise to an non-statistical significant (I think) increase of almost 5% in mean longevity: [_Longevity of cold-exposed rats: a reevaluation of the "rate-of-living theory" by J. O. Holloszy and E. K. Smith_](http://jap.physiology.org/content/61/5/1656.short). Moreover, also from the abstract:

> The cold exposure appeared to protect against neoplasia, particularly sarcomas; only 24% of the necropsied cold-exposed rats had malignancies compared with 57% for the controls.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-25T15:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-77563>

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=yYnvUziSshA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=yYnvUziSshA)

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-02-25T19:10:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-77596>

Ok! I have read [the _wired_ article](http://www.wired.com/playbook/2013/02/ff-cold-weight-loss/). Too much focused on weight loss it seems, as everywhere else.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-26T01:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-77678>

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-02-28T16:49:01+0200

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=77677)

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=77677)
>
> Andres and Jesse, do you still do CT and if yes, how often?

I keep driving with full-blast-towards-me AC on whenever alone in the car (something a little short of an hour a day) wearing a T-shirt (everywhere I go), even at -1Â°C. I have developed a chilblain these _freezing_ last days on my finger nearest to the vent, though. I will use gloves beginning this evening on the colder days.

I keep wearing [my homemade ICE-vest](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5263-DIY-ice-vest-2-0) two or three times a week for an hour and a half.

I used to put on the electrical radiator in the morning for something like a quarter hour before taking my cold shower. Nowadays I open the window and get myself naked first thing entering the bathroom.

I keep my office heating off and window open all day long.

I have passed two colds this fall/winter. [The first one I have already commented on](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?4378-CT-vitamin-C-and-hibernation). On the second one I actually lowered my vitamin C intake to 2g/day on purpose while increasing the ICE-vest sessions to daily for three days. It certainly kept the cold on check, but finally I got rid of it by mixing again CT and vitamin C saturation.

Best,

AndrÃ©s

### hellojtm — 2013-03-20T19:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-81202>

> [Anna K. said:](/goto/post?id=77677)
>
> Andres and Jesse, do you still do CT and if yes, how often? thanks.

Hi Anna

I still CT seasonally. My cold season is a self-induced jan-April. I just do random exposures. Lots of 10 minute cold showers. Lesser 30 minute cold baths. These are both between 48-52f. I do the ice vest a couple times a week if freezer space is available (broth takes a lot of space  )

Outside of those four months I still do it for fun to see how my tolerance changes. Well it's only been about 16 months since the first time so.... I can't make any great claims except subjective ones. Subjectively I recover from workouts and sports faster. And also I get a lot leaner, though this year I purposely ate more to prevent weight loss.

### mila — 2013-03-28T18:49:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-82064>

I guess I'm going to have to be the asshole here. While I agree that CT is likely a good idea, we are not rats and what happens in rats may not happen in humans. It is simply an indicator of something interesting that should be further investigated--not FACT with respect to humans. _in vivo_ human testing is more meaningful and more accurate than rat studies. When communities constantly point to rat studies as the basis for their scientific "facts" with respect to humans, the community loses credibility. Is no one else concerned about how the paleo community is presented to the rest of the world? I am. I would like us to be taken seriously and we never will if we keep pushing these kinds of studies.

### endless — 2013-03-28T20:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-82072>

> I was wondering if using the A/C blasting while driving would help with the car EMF exposure or would convertible top down for more photons be better?

Well, when I tested my car with the Trimeter, blasting anything caused a huge jump in EMF. The fan was the culprit. I try to avoid using it, and will roll my windows down instead whenever possible.

Inger, I don't think they had osteoporosis until after the introduction of processed foods....but I could be mistaken.

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-04-25T18:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/longevity-of-cold-exposed-rats-and-cancer.6041/post-84789>

> [mila said:](/goto/post?id=82064)
>
> While I agree that CT is likely a good idea, we are not rats and what happens in rats may not happen in humans.

Of course, but it seems that all that CT is about is activating the Eutherian program still active on everyone of us Eutherians.

> [mila said:](/goto/post?id=82064)
>
> It is simply an indicator of something interesting that should be further investigated--not FACT with respect to humans. _in vivo_ human testing is more meaningful and more accurate than rat studies.

Of course. But I don't have the patience to wait for them. I don't have the patience to wait for intravenous or liposomal-encapsulated vitamin C random controlled trials to begin. I don't have the patience to wait for the optimal level of 25-OH-vitamin-D to be elucidated. I don't have the patience to wait for the poison status of milligram doses of iodine to be revoked.

> [mila said:](/goto/post?id=82064)
>
> When communities constantly point to rat studies as the basis for their scientific "facts" with respect to humans, the community loses credibility.

Well, I am not a community. I consider that experiments about diets on rats are near useless (Petro Dobromylskyj notwithstanding). I consider that experiments about Eutherian cold exposure adaptation on rats are very informative.

> [mila said:](/goto/post?id=82064)
>
> Is no one else concerned about how the paleo community is presented to the rest of the world? I am. I would like us to be taken seriously and we never will if we keep pushing these kinds of studies.

Ok! So let it be clear from now on: I am not paleo. I still drink orange juice and white coffee at breakfast. I gorged on hard cheeses.

I couldn't care less about the paleo movement, if there is such a thing. There are some of its proponents that I respect and some of them that I don't.

I take those contributions that make sense to me (epi-paleo, CT, Schumann resonance / grounding?: I will go swim at the ocean more often just in case) and I am skeptical of the rest (EMF, structured water outside the cell, gaining/loosing electrons and [standard Quantum Mechanics in general](http://www.optimalhealthcave.com/viewtopic.php?f=39&t=1774&start=50#p40788)). Everyone should do the same.

# Thread: Jared and Subway 15 years now ... Surprise: Keeping weight off is the hardest part
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jared-and-subway-15-years-now-surprise-keeping-weight-off-is-the-hardest-part.6044/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-02-25T16:15:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jared-and-subway-15-years-now-surprise-keeping-weight-off-is-the-hardest-part.6044/post-77571>

[http://www.usatoday.com/story/money/business/2013/02/23/jared-fogle-subway-diet/1928793/](http://www.usatoday.com/story/money/business/2013/02/23/jared-fogle-subway-diet/1928793/)

Fogle says the hardest part of all hasn't been losing the weight, but keeping it off. And when he puts on more than 20 pounds â€” as he did a few years ago â€” Subway comes knocking at his door and not-so-subtly reminds him to lose the poundage. Reason: Jared has been a gold mine for Subway.

"Subway generated the perception that it's a healthy place to eat through Jared, and it stuck," says restaurant researcher Malcolm Knapp. "That's a very powerful tool. You don't hear people saying that they can eat healthy at McDonald's." **_(wow ... CW at its best)_**

Jared Fogle's Six Diet Tips:

1) Watch portion sizes carefully.

2) Eat lots of fruit and veggies.

3) Drink lots of water. (He drinks six bottles daily.)

4) Exercise every day.

5) Ask for all sauces & dresses on the side â€” and put them on yourself, sparingly.

6) Don't let a bad day, or week, or month change your diet plans.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

I remember the days when I used to think Subway was healthy food. Only problem is that I was always hungry shortly after eating my Subway meal. Intuitively before discovering Paleo / Epi-paleo, I knew I needed more fat in my diet, and not less.

After 15 years of the Jared / Subway meme being promoted by w/ millions of dollars in ads, its not surprising many people believe it.

# Thread: To Eat the BAB or not....that is my question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Reset 2013
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2013-02-25T20:01:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-77602>

So, I'm wondering, would Jack, say eat the breakfast even if you're not hungry or is it ok to wait until I am hungry? Also, if my cortisol is on the lower end in the morning for a morning cortisol, and should be higher, does that also mean maybe I shouldn't be eating the BAB then.

Before Optimal Reset, I've been practicing intuitive eating or eating only when hungry and only until satisfied. I find this at odds with the BAB. I'm just not hungry. Wouldn't it be counterproductive to eat when leptin levels are high and I am not hungry? At the same time, I hear this voice very morning, eat breakfast.....

My hormones need balancing, my t3 is low, and I"m taking t3 only (low dose of 12.5 mcg per day), and I have some adrenal issues, too. I had a recent salivary test of adrenocortex stress profile. Morning is 11.3 (range is 6.0-42.0 nmol/L), noon is 9.2 (2.0-11.0), 4 pm is 9.1 (2.0-11.0), evening is 2.9 (1.0-8.0), cortisol daily, total 32.5 (11.0-76.0), DHEAS profile morning 13.9 (2.5-25.0 nmol/L) and DHEAS/CORTISOL AM is 1.23 HIGH (0.20-0.60 RATIO).

I did the Leptin reset already back in Nov. 11 to January 12, had all the signs, and stopped doing.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-25T20:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-77605>

I just posted on your other thread at the same time you were posting this! I will say I did the same thing with the Leptin Rx. Started in Jan '12 and ended in March because I did have all the signs or what to me seemed like all the signs. Again i think this is a contextual thing where some signs are more applicable to some and not as much to others. I felt better all around but didn't have the energy. Having Hashis I wondered if I would ever get that back.

After two months of HAB, I decided to go back to BAB and felt a vast improvement in my energy and overall well-being. There are definitely days I don't want to eat in the morning but I force myself and after a couple of bites I'm all in! It's become quite easy for me with my BAB. I have a burger every morning (or some grass-fed beef) since I stopped eating eggs as I wasn't sure if they bothered me or not. But it has become part of my overall morning routine and it seems really strange on those blood draw days when I can't complete my BAB process until later. So moral of the story is to go back and reread the Leptin Rx blogs and maybe think about revisiting the BAB. Then again maybe it's EMF which I don't understand yet so cannot comment there!

### curves64 — 2013-02-25T22:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-77617>

Hehe, I posted a similar question in the Ask Jack section. It has been my recent experience that there is nothing like the BAB. I ate the BAB last April-June, felt all the clinical signs as Jack outlines, and then went to about 30 grams of protein in the am. When I returned to the BAB again 10 days ago, the results were startling. I lost all cravings again, went down to two meals a day easy, and started to lose weight for the first time since hearing about the Leptin Rx last April. My body comp is even changing. I feel fantastic. Better than ever. At this point, I don't know if I'm ever going to give up the BAB -- I'm on a roll!

### curves64 — 2013-02-25T22:09:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-77619>

I should also say that this is in conjunction with about 8 straight weeks of earthing and CTing in the Pacific for about 35 minutes 6 days a week. I dry brush my skin and play in the waves and have been progressively getting more and more of my body into the ocean. It wasn't the cold but the craziness of the surf that held me back. It's hilarious because now I squat down in the shallow water while keeping my feet on the ground. Sometimes a group forms behind me to watch what I'm doing because no one plays in the ocean there without a wet suit usually. Anyway, my point in bring it up is that, by reintroducing the BAB at this juncture, I reached new levels of feeling really really good.

Midday is 9.2 (2.0-11.0), Afternoon is 9.1 (2.0-11.0nmol/L), and evening is 2.9 (1.0-8.0), DHEAS profile morning is 13.9 (range 2.5-25 nmol/L), and DHEAS/CORTISOL AM RATIO is HIGH 1.23 (-.20-0.60 range).

I also have low DHEA-Sulfate, blood was 71L (80-560 mcg/dL) and my iron is also low

my IGF 1 is 86 (range 52-328 ng/mL) Should this be much higher, seems low?

### Claudia — 2013-02-28T22:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-78385>

Ok, first day trying to get back to doing BAB and I had no hunger waking up. It's been over a year since I did a BAB. It was SOOOOO crazy hard. I had grass-fed burgers ready because I knew I wouldn't have time to cook myself food. I make eggs for the kids every morning and have to get that ready and drive them to school. So, I munched a little on my burger while they were eating. To be honest, I prefer freshly grilled burgers and these I had cooked in a pan last night. They were like hockey pucks. I could barely eat 1/4 of it so I had some greek yogurt, some butter on a spoon, bulletproof coffee. Then, I had a weird tummy ache the rest of the morning. I didn't really eat enough to qualify as a BAB. But I haven't felt like eating and it's 3:30 now. I will most likely eat dinner with the fam, which is going to be pork I have cooking in the crock pot and a little cooked cabbage in coconut oil. I know that BAB used to kill my hunger until dinner. Yet, today has just been strange because I didn't really do it. I think I'll go back to ground grass-fed beef instead of the burgers, easier to warm up and eat for BAB, and maybe some bacon or eggs on the side. Anyone cook bacon in advance? I just don't have a lot of time in the morning.

### diane — 2013-02-28T23:37:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-78403>

Yes, I cook bacon in advance. Will also cook pork sausages (I just check the ingredients that there's no sugar, wheat, etc added) and then heat up in the morning. I will make my own burgers out of ground beef, but if I cook them ahead of time, I leave them on the rare side so they're not hockey pucks when I heat them up.

A few people here do some onion, bacon and a can of salmon in the morning - that's pretty good and I find that one easier to get down sometimes. The weeks that I cook a few things ahead and have them waiting in the fridge are much easier for me. Then I also have a choice of what I feel like eating that morning.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-01T01:00:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-78414>

We often cook bacon ahead of time too and just re-heat in the morning. I'm off the bacon for the moment, just not feeling it lately for some reason, but back in my bacon hey-day I was making 50/50 burgers for my BAB. Half beef/half crumbled bacon and they were the bomb for breakfast, let me tell ya! They were good hot or cold . . . just total yumness
