### andee — 2013-03-07T01:27:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-79377>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=77658)
>
> I'm pretty sure I've read many instances of Jack telling people to eat a BAB whether they were hungry for it or not. I wonder if you read some of the comments on the leptin rx blog if you might find the info you're looking for. I know I really struggled with all that food first thing in the morning when I initially began this journey, but now I've been eating my BAB for over a year and I love it! I can't imagine starting my day without it now.

Yes, I believe we are supposed to eat the BAB whether we're hungry or not. I am never that hungry but force it down - and then am very happy to be free of thinking about food for the next 6 hours. My a.m. cortisol was low when I first started LR but has now edged into the mid-range. If I remember correctly what Dr. K has written, one benefit of the BAB is it signals the body: we are not in starvation mode, we do not have to focus on getting food in order to survive today. To me that would be a message that helps the adrenals relax and begin to function in a more normal pattern instead of the low am/high pm stress pattern.

I do have one question about the BAB: does anyone else feel really tired after eating it? (I think it's particularly the fat - my gall bladder is not the most efficient). These days I wake up early and feel awake - this is a real miracle for me - but after the BAB I feel like I need a nap. If anyone has any theories about this would love to hear them!

### Claudia — 2013-03-07T03:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-79408>

Still struggling with BAB. But doing more CT and walking barefoot on the beach. I've gotta do better with the BAB.

Also, got my orange glasses and have been wearing them. They are not that comfortable though. Bother me around the ears, in fact that's why I gave up glasses for contact lenses. Also, if I"m reading in bed, is it ok to just read with my amber light on or do I need to wear the glasses, too? What is the bedtime routine like for you? I find it hard to brush my teeth and take off makeup with glasses on, esp. makeup so I'm trying to do it in the dark. I take my contact lenses off in the dark and then, put my eyeglasses on under the orange glasses, not so comfortable either. Hopefully I'll get used to it.

### andee — 2013-03-07T20:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-79500>

BJK -thanks for responding re the post-BAB fatigue. I definitely have leaky gut though slowly improving. That's really interesting re the CT connection. One reason I signed up for the 90-day re-set was to get myself doing CT finally, no more excuses. I have the skin thermometer now and hereby commit to beginning face dunking on Saturday.

Destiny - good reminder, I haven't used ox bile in years - thanks!

Claudia - the other thing I remember Dr K writing about the BAB is if you really can't manage the protein, up your fat instead - though I don't remember reading exactly how much fat would compensate for how much less protein. Maybe you can experiment as you work you way up to full on BAB. I use the amber glasses or the amber light bulbs not both. I use a small lamp with an amber bulb in my bathroom at night. I am impressed you are doing CT and am envious you have a beach to walk on!

### Claudia — 2013-03-08T03:33:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-79584>

Thanks Andee, I remember that now, too, but had forgotten. I do eat a lot of fat so I will add some more in for BAB. I'm not really counting what I'm eating at the moment, working my way up. Today I had 2 eggs scrambled, ground beef...maybe 3 oz., and some salmon sushi, threw out most of the rice but had a couple with rice which amounts to like 1 tablespoon. It was about 5 pieces of raw salmon. Don't really know how much protein I actually had. I was stuffed for many hours, not really hungry again until about 4pm. Ate dinner at 6pm and that was pork in the crockpot with broccoli, cooked in coconut oil.

I am so lucky to have the beach, I know , and I love it and always have. I'm going to get another lamp with amber lightbulb and put it in my bathroom. I have the perfect spot for it and it's far away from the sink area.

Here's a lame question: Can I skip BAB on my 1 yoga day per week? Tomorrow I go to yoga class in the morning when kids are in school. I go to yoga once per week. The last time I went I had eaten breakfast, not quite BAB, but maybe HAB. It was really hard to do yoga with that much food only about 1 hour before the class. I normally go to yoga on an empty stomach. I'm hoping someone will say, don't worry about BAB on that one day. Yoga is so important for me and realistically I have to go in the morning when my son is in preschool. There's no other chance. I know for Leptin Reset, we're supposed to exercise in the PM and not to exercise a lot. I just do 1 yoga, 1 dance, and one HIIT run day. I might walk on one other day. That's it. Mostly exercise is in the morning during school hours but I can still eat BAB if I'm doing dance, HIIT, and walking. On the weekends, I can exercise in the afternoon because H is home.

### andee — 2013-03-08T04:28:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-79593>

### mila — 2013-03-31T04:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-82325>

The first day of the BAB (on my leptin reset do-over) was rough because I simply had NO appetite, but I did force it all down and felt fine for the next 12+ hours. After my dinner that I ate @ 5PM, I did start to feel sleepy and sweaty as hell! The next morning, I was hungry for breakfast and I felt great. No sleepiness, but about an hour later the horrible sweating started again and lasted all day. The same thing happened with dinner--12 hours later before I even thought about eating, but then I became really sleepy afterwards and the sweating was even worse than it had been earlier in the day. Same thing today. It's actually rather late for me now but I don't feel like I could go to sleep with all this sweating. I don't know if my leptin reset just happened to coincide with symptoms I may have begun to experience anyway due to perimenopause or if it's something else. I did add maca today for hormonal support. It just doesn't seem like I would be displaying any sort of symptoms (good or bad) so quickly, especially as I was eating a paleo diet before I started this.
Any thoughts? Or should this be an "Ask Jack" kinda thing?

### Inger — 2013-03-31T07:58:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-82333>

Mila, what exactly was you eating?
You could try to do different protein... How much protein did you had? How much fat?
If I eat much cooked meat/egg protein I have gotten too warm... IDK why. With fish it is different. I feel better with meat and eggs raw so I do not cook them ever. You could try that and see if the sweating disappear.
To drink lots of ice cold water cools me down too pretty good!

### yarralea — 2013-10-16T10:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-97979>

OOH is it okay to add a different, somewhat relevant question to this thread?

Well my BAB is more fat based that protein based. I eat 5-8 egg yolks, cooked in gf beef tallow with an avocado chopped up and a big spoon of kimchi. I found I was eating too much protein in my day (when I was low carbing and trying to remain in ketosis) so I took out the whites, opting to drop the protein and raise the fat. This is my favourite breakfast, at times there might be a rasher or two of bacon with a good section of fat still attached, but not always.

Does this its not going to do the trick as a BAB, as it is more of a FBB (Fat based breakfast)? I'd love to here anyone's opinions of this. Also, I a few morning a week go for a 30 minute walk to get to the ocean, a twenty minute dunk in up to my neck- getting a real shiver on. I would like to know if it preferable to eat first or after? My goals, to lower my body fat further- I'm 70 kilos, athletic and would like to shift 5 kilos of body fat. 70 kgs,er... about 155 lbs?

any comments, happily noted.

### cantweight — 2013-10-16T14:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-97987>

A lot of us have found we do better with more fat. Its not so much what you eat but when that is most important. Make sure you are eating within a half hour of waking. Enjoy your ocean dunks...that sounds wonderful

### Hemming — 2013-10-16T15:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-97990>

I've also experienced that I wanted more protein in the beginning of the reset whereas I want more fat now.

### suzgaray — 2013-10-17T03:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98064>

Everyone, I am trying to get through that hurdle with eating BAB at least 50 grams. Does anyone what BAB should look like besides eating eggs all the time?? Is there a book out there about how to get 50 grams in for Breakfast?? It would really help me

### nonchalant — 2013-10-17T03:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98067>

### nicld — 2013-10-17T04:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98070>

I think it is about 6-7oz of meat or fish. I usually have a small grass fed steak with uncured bacon every morning. Been tossing in 2 organic eggs too. Other great morning good is some pork loin fried up too. I just find that I do better eating red meat or pork for breakfast, keeps me fuller. Oh and the coconut oil in the coffee every morning helps too.

### caroline — 2013-10-17T05:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98085>

Think dinner - for breakfast........throw a leg of lamb in a crockpot before you go to bed .....and Voila - breakfast!

I had a really hard time with eggs after awhile -

### BJK77 — 2013-10-17T16:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98155>

Now that it's getting cooler in my area, I love to make a nice, gigantic bowl of soup for BF! Start with some bone broth, add some sea veggies and mushrooms and about 6 oz of roast beef or pork. I usually also throw in about an oz or two of liver. If you would have told me years ago I'd EVER be eating this at any time of the day, let alone breakfast, I would have thought you were crazy! But now, I love it and actually look forward to my breakfast every morning!

### vkiernan — 2013-10-17T20:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-eat-the-bab-or-not-that-is-my-question.6047/post-98172>

> [suzgaray said:](/goto/post?id=98064)
>
> Everyone, I am trying to get through that hurdle with eating BAB at least 50 grams. Does anyone what BAB should look like besides eating eggs all the time?? Is there a book out there about how to get 50 grams in for Breakfast?? It would really help me

I eat supper for breakfast. Just think about what you would eat for supper and eat more of the meat maybe with a side of bacon and you should be good to go. 7 oz of burger = 49 gms protein (I believe beef is 7gms/oz). If you buy a lb. then you would eat almost half of it for your 50 gms.

### Huck — 2013-02-26T02:55:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/humans-are-used-as-living-batteries-unintended-consequences.6055/post-77700>

A few months ago, I would have thought this was a great technological breakthrough.

> Fujifilm has used the Nanotech 2013 conference in Tokyo to demonstrate some progress with the **creation of a new thermoelectric conversion material. Such a material can convert temperature differences directly into electricity, which can then be stored or used immediately to power or charge some device.**
>
> The material Fujifilm has created in collaboration with Japan’s National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST) is desirable because it is both organic and has the highest thermoelectric conversion efficiency yet seen. Using a temperature difference of just one degree Celsius it can produce “several milliwatts” of electricity.

> So far two primary uses are seen for the new material. **The first is as an attachment patients can wear on their skin to power medical devices.** The second is as part of solar panels as a way of collecting additional energy and therefore making them more efficient.

[geek.com](http://www.geek.com/articles/chips/fujifilm-creates-organic-printed-sheet-that-harvests-energy-from-body-heat-2013026/)

Good idea or bad?

# Thread: Inability to lose weight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inability-to-lose-weight.6056/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2013-02-26T04:16:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inability-to-lose-weight.6056/post-77710>

(I wrote a similar thread months ago and was going to update, but I couldn't find it in the search...So I'll start fresh.)

I cannot lose weight to save my life. I was diagnosed with hypothyroid in Feb 2008 and started on 50mcg of Eltroxin (T4-only). At this time I was around 140lbs. For the next 4 years, I struggled to maintain my weight and stalled at 150lbs. During this time, I also developed major leaky gut problems. In Spring 2009 I eliminated gluten and then corn and then over the years, pretty much all foods. In January 2011, I started the GAPS diet, stopped counting calories, gave myself permission to eat fat and gained 20lbs in 3 weeks. And then over the past 2 years I gained another 10-15lbs to make a grand total of 185 lbs.

In Spring 2012, I switched from the T4-only to Erfa natural thyroid. My doc at the time started me off super low at 60mg. It was not until September that I could increase it to 90mg. This still wasn't enough, so I kept increasing it slowly. My labs at the time were pathetic -- very low ft3 and ft4. However, the problem with going up was I went hyper and it didn't dawn on me until January 2013. So I backed down again and now I am at 2 grains per day. At 3 grains in January, when I felt hyper, I had a TSH 0.01 \[.35 - 5.50\]
FT4 12.8 \[10.0 - 19.0\]
FT3 6.3 \[3.5 -6.5\]\*
\* This FT3 is from Nov. 2/12 when I was on 3.5 grains as the lab did not run my Ft3 in January 2013. (In Nov., this is only thyroid value I have).

I do not have Hashi's.
I also do not have a RT3 problem. We do not have this test in Canada, but in my consult Dr. K recommended other tests and those turned out to be fine.
So, I don't get it...My labs look OK on paper, but I cannot lose weight. I should also mention that in January, after reducing my medication to where I was no longer having hyper sx, I still felt very tired in the afternoons. I read up on the T3CM method and started this at the beginning of February. I quickly lost 5lbs and I noticed better energy during the day, most days of the week. So I am now 180lbs (at 5'7). But the weight loss stopped there and I don't get it.

The only other thing I can think of is that my terrible leaky gut is somehow interfering. I've literally been eating nothing but fish for the past 5 months. I am being checked out by a specialist on this and am waiting to get an endoscopy. He wants to see if I have refractory celiac (blunted villi that never grew back despite being off gluten since Spring 2009) and/or H. Pylori cell bodies in the biopsy (as my blood test might have been a false negative).

I was considering switching to a T4 and Cytomel combination to see if that would help....Since I have good days and bad days in terms of energy, I wonder if the Erfa thyroid is not consistent in their doses per pill.

Edit: I should also mention that I have been on the Leptin Reset since January 2012

### kleinb87 — 2013-02-26T05:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inability-to-lose-weight.6056/post-77719>

Hi Eve,

Have you tried the Metametrix GI effects test? It tests for every possible gut bacteria, pH, fatty acid, etc. It helped me determine what is causing my weight gain too. The cheapest place to order it is from integrative psychiatry online and I believe it is $400, but money well spent. If you are containing to gain weight that is a huge sign of inflammation. My guess is that it is coming from your gut. I would do this test over an endoscopy because it will tell a better picture of all types of data. I have gained 50 pounds since last March on at 5'2"which is a lot and 30 pounds was within a month and a half.

Use grounding and minimize EMF's and artificial light with CT to help combat inflammation until you get the rest of your results. Hope this helps!

if you cant lose weight it means your are losing more electrons to your environment than you take in.........

I found the part about water in EMF7 really interesting - and the importance it plays in the cells. Also reinforced the importance of CT.

It seems that we could be dehydrated, even if we drink a lot of water. If our cells are not working properly and can't use the water to unfold the proteins (did I get that right) then can that explain why you would urinate often when you drink, so water is not getting into the cells meaning dehydration. A comment was made a ways back about it being a good sign if you don't have to get up in the night anymore to pee - I'm assuming that's a sign that your body is able to use the water efficiently.

I had a test a couple of years back that measured how much water you had in your cells. I had higher than normal amounts. In reading this, I think that means that my cells aren't able to convert the water into energy - so it sits in cells. So if water can't be used for energy, is that because there isn't enough energy in the cells to be able to convert? And lack of energy I think comes from low DHEA and melatonin which means poor sleep.

So if many CW gurus recommend drinking water for weight loss because it staves off hunger, there are other reasons that it helps, not what they think. Anyone any thoughts?

\- in childhood: normal water drinking, normal peeing, never at night
\- around 20, drank 128oz/day because "they" told me to. Normal peeing
\- when pregnant, peeing at night, otherwise, normal
\- around 30, switched to unprocessed high fat ancestral foods, also realized that between whole foods and no longer being thirsty all the time, I didn't need to drink much. Normal peeing. Actually, less than most women, but it seems natural to me not to have to pee all day, ancestrally thinking.
\- last pregnancy, no need to pee at night
\- currently, still normal thirst, normal (not "8 cups/day") drinking, never pee at night

Yet my labs have always shown signs of dehydration in the urinalysis, and according to Dr. Kruse, other labs I previously didn't know to interpret as dehydration. My skin shows no signs of dehydration.

I retain water across my body like crazy at times, and when it goes, I pee all day till it's gone. It's clear I have a water problem, but I am still not sure what causes me to go in and out of the phases of water retention. It seemed for a while that the combination of ox bile + no carb was getting rid of it, but here I am again, and after a week of very strict eating, my thighs are still 1-1/4" bigger than they were a month ago. ### Shijin13 — 2013-02-27T19:12:44+0200

How does CT effect estrogen and how does it work to balance the Pg:E2 ratio? What are signs of being estrogen dominant? Are there signs of Pg dominance?
>
> Sorry if this has been asked elsewhere, but any insight is appreciated! Nice thread, btw, I am loving what I'm learning here!

Chrysanthemama - here's a good synopsis of Estrogen dominance symptoms:
Decreased sex drive
Irregular or otherwise abnormal menstrual periods
Bloating (water retention)
Breast swelling and tenderness
Fibrocystic breasts
Headaches (especially premenstrually)
Mood swings (most often irritability and depression)
Weight and/or fat gain (particularly around the abdomen and hips)
Cold hands and feet (a symptom of thyroid dysfunction)
Hair loss
Thyroid dysfunction
Sluggish metabolism
Foggy thinking, memory loss
Fatigue
Trouble sleeping/insomnia
PMS
Estrogen dominance has also been linked to allergies, autoimmune disorders, breast cancer, uterine cancer, infertility, ovarian cysts, and increased blood clotting, and is also associated with acceleration of the aging process.

Intuitively I would expect - that in an "optimal" male - you'd see Pg rise as you move in to winter - I wonder if Jack's labs show this. so both men and women can access the PPP. but here's the key - Jack said in the mitochondria RX that women pass the environmental ques to their progeny during pregnancy. so If you don't enter the PPP when pregnant - your telling baby its summer year round - and your setting up baby to a mismatched circadian cycle. the question is does pregnancy, which is HGH & astronomically HIGH Pg levels automatically = PPP? > [diane said:](/goto/post?id=77926)
>
> Freesia, I think given hibernation probably fat burning as well. Most animals chub up for the winter - even squirrels who store for the winter (believe me the dogs know when the squirrels are fat and slow LOL). So I'm thinking that CT and PPP burn fat.

thyroid doesn't create hormones - T3 is the key when coupled with vitamin A that unlocks LDL so it can convert to Pregnenolone - your state of stress determines if that is then turned toward Pg or Cortisol. If pregnenolone -> Pg -> remaining steroid (sex) hormones & vitamin D. If pregnenolone -> cortisol -> liver to make more LDL b/c we're not making steroid hormones (wash rinse repeat). all of this is controlled by the Hypothalmus -pitutary axis. go back and read CT 7. its all there.

CT allows you to access the PPP. PPP uses fat to make the ATP. you have to have enough cellular water, Cold, & progesterone to get it to turn on continuously. Its easier for men to access b/c they have a steady state of Pg, where as women have the fluctuation of Pg across the cycle - but that flux is far to wide these days, to access the PPP regularly. I think this is why men drop weight like mad when they go paleo - they have a constant supply of Pg, to support their T and can enter the PPP easier, especially when they embrace cold. Jack has said CT increases both Pg and T.

I've come to the conclusion - that when I'm CTing during the midpoint of my cycle I'm able to hit the PPP - why - I need less food (lunch is non-existent) during this phase of my cycle, my Pg is increasing, and sleep is effing awesome during this phase as well. I'm able to do this even living in the EMF soup I'm struggling with. Additionally, I used to just get hot after eating breakfast... I've noticed over the past cycle and this one, that I'm now hot all morning... and even hotter after I eat. I can't cool down - this is no hot flash but like I'm taping into something else... I think the ground in combination w/everything else - is helping me get into the PPP faster - even if I don't stay there... and this is why I'm suddenly fitting into my pre-pregnancy clothes of 6yrs ago... This leads me to the conclusion the Pg should naturally be HIGHER Across the entire cycle for all women... not the curve shown here: [http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikiped...terone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.png](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/02/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.png)

IME Pg should greater than or equal to Estrogen with the exception of during ovulation... so instead of the big bulge of pg - it should be a continous slope that tracks E, but peaks after E does and maintains then increases if pregnant or gently drops back to the beginning of the cycle...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-27T21:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.6058/post-78082>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=78044)
>
> Chrysanthemama - here's a good synopsis of Estrogen dominance symptoms:
> Decreased sex drive
> Irregular or otherwise abnormal menstrual periods
> Bloating (water retention)
> Breast swelling and tenderness
> Fibrocystic breasts
> Headaches (especially premenstrually)
> Mood swings (most often irritability and depression)
> Weight and/or fat gain (particularly around the abdomen and hips)
> Cold hands and feet (a symptom of thyroid dysfunction)
> Hair loss
> Thyroid dysfunction
> Sluggish metabolism
> Foggy thinking, memory loss
> Fatigue
> Trouble sleeping/insomnia
> PMS
> Estrogen dominance has also been linked to allergies, autoimmune disorders, breast cancer, uterine cancer, infertility, ovarian cysts, and increased blood clotting, and is also associated with acceleration of the aging process.
>
>
>
> So If your E-dominate, which a PCOS Woman is, then you're also dehydrated, in conjunction with low Pg, and you can't enter the PPP. However when you CT, it helps neutralize the E-dominance, by activating and increase Pg. so when your adverse to getting into the CT tank - your Pg is being dominated by E2, and your thyroid symptoms of being cold, and craving heat goes up. When in reality you really need to cold to ramp up the PG, and bring down the E2. So if you have a MTHFR +/- or +/+ snps - it can be harder for you to clear E2, but not impossible when you access the PPP. Accessing the PPP allows you to by-pass some of the MTHFR detox pathways (haven't figured out which ones - but this is why you've said that after EMF 7 we'd understand why MTHFR isn't as much of a challenge if you know how to block EMF and get into the PPP - am I close?) So, given you need Pg to access the PPP - its possible for some E-dominate women to access the PPP during the portion of their cycle when Pg is dominant - with the assistance of BHRT supplementation. but the goal is to access the PPP throughout the cycle, so that means Pg should actually be higher in the first phase of our cycle than currently accepted. Still Pg would increase as it currently does, but it shouldn't flat line, additionally E - will rise, but never become the dominate hormone across the entire cycle.
>
>
>
> Pregnancy is the reason for life, part of the cycle. Pg is the key hormone for entering the PPP when coupled w/Ketogenic diet and cold. both sexes have Pg. Intuitively I would expect - that in an "optimal" male - you'd see Pg rise as you move in to winter - I wonder if Jack's labs show this. so both men and women can access the PPP. but here's the key - Jack said in the mitochondria RX that women pass the environmental ques to their progeny during pregnancy. so If you don't enter the PPP when pregnant - your telling baby its summer year round - and your setting up baby to a mismatched circadian cycle. the question is does pregnancy, which is HGH & astronomically HIGH Pg levels automatically = PPP? Intuitively, survival for any eutherian mammal I think that's a yes. The next question is does artificial light and emf over ride that for us? I'm thinking yes again. so when your pregnant and not using the PPP b/c you're circadian cycles are effed up - you're running on the krebs and CP systems for ATP - so your body is robbing you to ensure survival of baby - hence why women have "pregnancy brain", cavities in their teeth after subsequent pregnancies, bone loss and heart attacks after giving birth... \*OH SNAP! mother nature just b!tch slapped us for being stupid - but ensured the survival of the species - even if it isn't optimal any more....
>
>
>
> thyroid doesn't create hormones - T3 is the key when coupled with vitamin A that unlocks LDL so it can convert to Pregnenolone - your state of stress determines if that is then turned toward Pg or Cortisol. If pregnenolone -> Pg -> remaining steroid (sex) hormones & vitamin D. If pregnenolone -> cortisol -> liver to make more LDL b/c we're not making steroid hormones (wash rinse repeat). all of this is controlled by the Hypothalmus -pitutary axis. go back and read CT 7. its all there.
>
> CT allows you to access the PPP. PPP uses fat to make the ATP. you have to have enough cellular water, Cold, & progesterone to get it to turn on continuously. Its easier for men to access b/c they have a steady state of Pg, where as women have the fluctuation of Pg across the cycle - but that flux is far to wide these days, to access the PPP regularly. I think this is why men drop weight like mad when they go paleo - they have a constant supply of Pg, to support their T and can enter the PPP easier, especially when they embrace cold. Jack has said CT increases both Pg and T.
>
> I've come to the conclusion - that when I'm CTing during the midpoint of my cycle I'm able to hit the PPP - why - I need less food (lunch is non-existent) during this phase of my cycle, my Pg is increasing, and sleep is effing awesome during this phase as well. I'm able to do this even living in the EMF soup I'm struggling with. Additionally, I used to just get hot after eating breakfast... I've noticed over the past cycle and this one, that I'm now hot all morning... and even hotter after I eat. I can't cool down - this is no hot flash but like I'm taping into something else... I think the ground in combination w/everything else - is helping me get into the PPP faster - even if I don't stay there... and this is why I'm suddenly fitting into my pre-pregnancy clothes of 6yrs ago... This leads me to the conclusion the Pg should naturally be HIGHER Across the entire cycle for all women... not the curve shown here: [http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikiped...terone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.png](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/02/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.png)
>
> IME Pg should greater than or equal to Estrogen with the exception of during ovulation... so instead of the big bulge of pg - it should be a continous slope that tracks E, but peaks after E does and maintains then increases if pregnant or gently drops back to the beginning of the cycle...

Write this up and I will make it a guest blog by you........we will call it Quantum PCOS. It is excellent and you are spot on, on it it all.......and yes my PG is best in Dec-Feb.

### Larry — 2013-02-27T23:23:20+0200

My two cents: after you push us to learn it is helpful to see concrete messages. Quick story for illustration: I was coaching freshman football 12 years ago & we were putting in the "Bear" defense for the kids. I was talking about the upside of the defense and then I said "you know, like the 85 Bears who dominated on the way to the Super Bowl". At that point one of my players raised his hand and informed me that he was born in 1987. Number 1, I realized "oh crap I am old" & number 2, I learned not to assume people have the same knowledge or experience that I do.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-28T02:41:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.6058/post-78189>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=78044)
>
> Chrysanthemama - here's a good synopsis of Estrogen dominance symptoms:
> Decreased sex drive
> Irregular or otherwise abnormal menstrual periods
> Bloating (water retention)
> Breast swelling and tenderness
> Fibrocystic breasts
> Headaches (especially premenstrually)
> Mood swings (most often irritability and depression)
> Weight and/or fat gain (particularly around the abdomen and hips)
> Cold hands and feet (a symptom of thyroid dysfunction)
> Hair loss
> Thyroid dysfunction
> Sluggish metabolism
> Foggy thinking, memory loss
> Fatigue
> Trouble sleeping/insomnia
> PMS
> Estrogen dominance has also been linked to allergies, autoimmune disorders, breast cancer, uterine cancer, infertility, ovarian cysts, and increased blood clotting, and is also associated with acceleration of the aging process.

That was me for 25+ years. My life was hell.

> IME Pg should greater than or equal to Estrogen with the exception of during ovulation... so instead of the big bulge of pg - it should be a continous slope that tracks E, but peaks after E does and maintains then increases if pregnant or gently drops back to the beginning of the cycle...

OK I have a question here. I have NO parts. Gone for 10 years or so. Been on the Wiley Protocol for 19 months which (for the uninformed) replaces estradiol and Pg the way your body produced it during your cycle. Which means I do E2 only for 2 weeks then add in Pg for the other 2 weeks. So I'm a bit confused. IS my body somehow making Pg in smallish amounts? I thought you only produced it when you ovulated. No eggs, no Pg. Is it part of the pregnenolone pathway? If it is then I still have a problem because when I had them test my pregnenolone levels they came back WAY low (12ng/dcl). That was a few months back and I started supplementing. I recently doubled my daily dose.

I plan to ask the NP next week to check both the pregnenolone and my DHEA again along with the vitamin D and RT3 and to order a salivary cortisol assay (Is that the same as the ASI?). I will be close to peak in the Pg part of my cycle but will ask to have both E2 and Pg checked as well. Thank god for insurance. Cause that's about $1,000 worth of lab work at our fracking hospital lab. But that's another rant.

So ...assuming things are still on the low side I'm wondering if I should then ask for additional PG for the 2 weeks where I'm only doing E2. If what you're saying is right then I would obviously need to supplement during that time instead of doing without it.

(scratches head in befuddlement)

And yes, Jack, I do certainly remember you telling me I probably needed to do that. No I didn't. I'll go stand in the corner and/or you can yell at me later.

right now I'm trying to sort this out. So if I'm understanding you correctly, Gretchen, I would need to match my Pg to the E2 during that 2 week period when I'm normally just doing E2?

Then, of course, there's the whole testosterone thing. Where, in the pathway, does THAT get made if you don't have any parts?

I've also had some other thoughts. Again going back to the advice about drinking water to lose weight. The CW theory is that water fills the stomach so the need for food is decreasing. Connecting the dots from the QB blog - the hunger is linked to a lack of energy or electrons. And since water is needed for semi-conduction, more energy is generated with water vs the wrong kind of food. So that's why water stops hunger, but also helps weight loss - not the lack of food, but the ability to generate energy.

Also thinking of why we gain water weight before the TOM. If Pg falls during this time, and we are too dehydrated to keep it high enough for the cycle, we hold on to water to preserve. Not really sure I'm there yet in understanding, but heading on the path.

But now with drinking that much "headache" is practically gone! I do not have to pee in the night either if I just take care not to drink huge amounts right before bed, but a glass or two only.

Discard..? No...! Not one drop..lol I fill clean well water in the jug, put in a handful of dried seaweed that will soon swell quite a lot.. and after a few hours I drink from it. When the water is all gone I add more fresh water and let sit for a while and drink again, maybe I do this 3 times or so. Next day I puree the soaked seaweed and eat/drink it.
I change the seaweed I use, one day Kombu, then Seaspaghetti, then Wakame.. Carragenan and so on. I would do more but I have to save some money for oysters too..

>
> I might have to see if I can get them to rinse with seaweed water after brushing teeth at night perhaps? What could I add that would give it an OK taste (to them) that wouldn't kill the B licheniformis? Hmmmm. Mission for today...figure something out!

OK, I did this. Soaked a handful of seaweed (I used dulse, wakame and karengo) in a cup of water overnight, and it made a beautiful bright pink/purple brew. I strained off the seaweed, which I used in my breakfast, then added 1 1/2 tsp xylitol and 1 drop of a good quality spearmint oil to the jar of liquid.

The kids were fine rinsing their teeth with this after brushing. Curiously though -over the course of the weekend it became completely clear - ie lost all it's pretty colour! I really don;t know if anything beneficial remained after adding the xylitol etc. Thats not something I'd usually use....but it is supposed to be good for teeth so I bought some especially. We'll keep using it til it's finished anyway

> [TheKid said:](/goto/post?id=79301)
>
> Can someone tell me why were are drinking more water? I don't have a full grasp of the dehydration issues, but from what I can gleen, it seems that more water would do you no good. Only BETTER water would make a difference; and by better I mean structured in a manner condusive to cellular uptake.
>
> I've been working my way through an interesting YouTube video ([http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7hNW7qxIMzg](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7hNW7qxIMzg)) of a lecture by a fella named Dan Nelson, who hawks his own version of structured water. Some of his assertions conflcit with what Jack explained here; however, he seems to have much the same focus on the importance and centrality of water for cellular function and health. He points to the angular structure of the molecules as being key and, more importantly, he asserts that poorly structured water is simply too large to penetrate the cell membrane. I can't really assess the veracity of that statement since I am not fully familiar with the actual mechanism of absorbtion. If someone could vet that, I would appreciate it.
>
> This all would make sense. In the same way quantity of food isn't as important as quality of food, quality of water should also trump quantity shouldn't it?

Gerald Pollack has a nice 2 part video on youtube about electrically structured water. Biological gels apparently create an exclusion zone in water whenever they are exposed to radiant energy/ body heat//sunlight/ especially infrared energy. Cartilage and cellular innards contain bio gels. The exclusion zone begins near the surface of the bio gel and creates negative and positive charged helical layers of water with a property of reduced friction. As the exclusion layer expands it pushes away impurities so that only pure liquid crystal water is favored. The negative charge is next to the bio gel and the proton (positive) layer is furthest from the bio gel. Those Swell Gel crystals you see in garden centers are such a gel made of polyacrylamide.

Hey,

I've been struggling with it it to be honest. I think its the way to go and thats why I am sticking with it. I dont think my body can outsmart the environmental factors hitting me everyday so I'd like to continue using it. I usually have issues whenever I put any new hormone in my body. I was already on thryoid meds before beginnging but even those took a while to work properly.

I have low hormones across the board: thyroid estrogen progesterone testosterone. Technically based on the ratio I'm considered estrogen dominant. They origonally put me on Progresterone and Testosterone plus supps. I had a hard time tolerating the P...they kept adjusting down and down and down bc I always got super dry eyes, super sleepiness no matter what. I was on a small dose but finally stopped it when I started noticing weight gain and got HUGE painful breasts. Once I stopped it went away. I also halted the testosterone bc - again - even though it was a small dosagage my body rebelled: I got pimples, and also started noticing after a few months that I was losing more hair then ever. I stopped it a long time ago and now have a bunch of new hair growth so I know that was it.

THye have me on supps including Pregneolone and 7keto DHEA.

I am now adding tri-est to the formula to use with the P...I'm hoping the E will help balance out the P. we shall see!

Ever since I stopped BCP 3 years ago, my shape has def changed. I'm thicker. Even my calves are thicker - they no longer comfortably fit into my boots I'm hoping getting the hormones right will restore my feminine shape

I prefer Dzugan over the others bc I havent heard anything bad about them. Also, with Gretchen and Colleen on board, and educating those doctors I'm confident I'm in good hands.

I think I just need to go EXTREMELY SLOW. let my body accept the dose, then titrate up a little more. Its what worked for my thyroid meds anyways.

### HeidiHen — 2013-02-26T21:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-my-friends-and-family-could-see-me-now.6063/post-77864>

Honestly, the past few days have been some of the best fun my family has had in a long time! I've spent the early evenings outside with my DH and kiddos, throwing a football around, barefoot in the grass. In the past I would have said it was too cold, or too windy, or too dark, or too cloudy, or .... that I needed to check my email! We've hung out as a family reading and playing games by candle light. It's been so great to get everyone off the computer and video games and handheld devices. Wow! Family time.... what a novel idea! LOL.

The kids all have blue blocker glasses and truly enjoy wearing them. My daughter has commented a number of times that her eyes don't hurt anymore.

Now.... when can I put tin foil in THEIR hats, too???

I'm so grateful that my hubby is totally on board! It would be soooo hard to do this all on my own!

### BJK77 — 2013-02-26T22:06:04+0200

I've spent the early evenings outside with my DH and kiddos, throwing a football around, barefoot in the grass. # Thread: Note to MamaG and KiwiLauren
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2013-02-26T19:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77805>

Ladies,

Thanks so much for putting into words my feelings about recent threads and posts. MamaG, I'm impressed with your gumption to say what I know many are thinking about the cruel aggressiveness presented as kitten-like-innocence. Truly a wolf in sheep's clothing situation.

I was pondering this. . .why do I care? As Inger said "why don't you go somewhere else". Harsh on the surface, but not in light of the fact that on Jack's site, to Jack, her POV is the correct one.

Why would I be here posting on a site hosted by someone else when my POV is considered incorrect and my ultimate contribution seems to be getting a few laughs?

It's because in real life, living the way I do means I sacrifice a very big need in my blueprint for a good life. The need to have a circle of friends and people to talk with (and disagree with!) but share some basic frameworks to start the conversation from.

In real life I choose not to go out to eat for the most part, so no girlfriend happy hours etc.
In real life I'm hyper aware of so many optimal details I choose not to go do the fun stuff with friends.
In real life my child doesn't get to eat "normal" foods and playdates and parties etc are an ordeal
In real life I go to bed and get up with the sun so I don't choose to go to parties and gatherings when my peer groups would

This list could go on. I'm in so deep I even want to bring my own bottled (glass only of course) mineral water to drink into restaurants, not to mention the whole "bad oil" proposition. . .and I don't expect my choices are going to lighten up much - as now that I'm honing in real things that improve my brain my thirst for more knowledge is still strong.

So, I've realized I can't couch all my posts and commentary in benevolence to help other people. It's to help me.

It's a dual simple need to:

1\. Make friends.
2\. Discuss Issues.

So I'll probably snark along until Banday, and hope that those of like mind that could be part of my friend circle recognize that in me and we somehow connect.

The alternative is what? I will "just go somewhere else" when there is somewhere else to go. Last time I checked there really wasn't a group of people doing this crazy shit anywhere but the wholly-mental-construct that is the internets.

### kathylu — 2013-02-26T19:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77809>

Wow, I'm sorry this is happening! I really feel at a loss to help anyone here. The funny thing is, I see almost everyone's POV...at least I think I see how they arrived where they are, whether or not I agree. I'm also very impressed at the collective intellect in this group. I am often amazed and humbled at the logic, reason and insight that people display on an incredibly wide range of subjects.

I guess what I am hoping is that people find a way to continue to keep posting here (or at least somewhere where I can find all of you). Cavemam, I feel a similar need for connection. I feel like a fish out of water with my friends, most of whom are more tolerant of my "idiosyncracies" than my family is. I certainly make an impression when I bring my own food to events, wear my blue blockers and try to give an simplified explanation of "why?". I quickly learned that too much detail hurts more than helps.

By the way, who is saying that you cannot express your opinions?

### Birdy — 2013-02-26T19:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77810>

Hey, Cavemam, I feel the same way. It is no fun to be kicked out of the tribe, but no other tribe will do. I see an almost endless stream of junk my friends do, posted on places like FB. I see their late-night partying with neolithic foods, their pictures of "comfort" vegetarian foods with the fat removed, their descent into ice-creamy decadence, their night-owl tendencies. I would love to meet a group of local people who enjoy living during the day and sleeping at night. But right now, fewer IRL people make me feel bad for wearing yellow glasses than some people here. It seems like any alternative place to go, and there are the folks who try to limit discussion on various topics. And that is sad.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-26T19:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77815>

> [Cavemam said:](/goto/post?id=77805)
>
> It's a dual simple need to:
>
> 1\. Make friends.
> 2\. Discuss Issues.
>
> So I'll probably snark along until Banday, and hope that those of like mind that could be part of my friend circle recognize that in me and we somehow connect.

Precisely. I have not found up till now that this is a place where we must be in 100% mental compliance or it's the highway. I certainly hope that will remain the same going forward. The biggest problem I've had with this past week is not disagreeing, but the idea put forth by not a small number of people that disagreeing is \*wrong\*, that it means ingratitude, stubborn refusal to change, etc. It's the ad hominem attacks instead of engagement in intellectual discourse.

That is truly discouraging.

It's okay if you disagree with me, so long as you do so with reason & kindness.

In reality, you & I & most of us here have long disproven any notion that we are unwilling to change. People who are married to the status quo do not bathe in 50F rivers without wetsuits, wear sunglasses at night, refuse even a bite of cake on their own birthdays, or eat fish for breakfast.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-26T20:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77817>

disagreeing is perfect......but details you dont like matter...........the science says too bad.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-26T20:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77825>

I wasn't looking for a tribe. I have a tribe. Thank goodness.

In my post I voiced two sentiments: gratitude and confusion. I still have both. And it is _not_ because I do not comprehend or reject the science.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-26T20:28:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/note-to-mamag-and-kiwilauren.6067/post-77830>

OK take it off line ladies to a PM. I am done with the BS here.

### prAna303 — 2013-02-26T20:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77823>

Weird header but i want to think about the new "us"...

We have learned alot thru this weird times... Jack must be the one i have found that really have something important to say and it has resonated well with my own life, maybe more of that later.

I like it simple, everything so my way of connecting dots goes like this:

In the very beginning we had earth, water, sky and so on. In these early years entropy was low, easy to use energy because of few things using it. Some magic happend and life started. The main source was not food or something like that, itÂ´s all about electricity. If we simply is a version of semicondutors, maybe more like a peltierelement, magic would happend again.

When we are grounded we get a flow (voltage) with connection to schumann resonace. When me move around the bones in our body starts to resonate and give us a inductance. What happends is that we tend to switch this energy to a more difficult version of energy, warmth. When we work as a semiconductor we need to work in the following environment:

\+ / -
Sun / moon
Warm / cool
Night / day
Wet / dry
Winter / summer
and the list goes on and on (yin yang)

Later on the entropy started to get higher, food was starting to be a part of life. The system would if all things were as in the beginning be simply perfekt because energyflow is optimal. We should not need any Kcal to live. Slowly we got to a point where we moved inside, flooring and walls. Disconnecting got to be the standard and to live as a semicondutor we would need FOOD! The peltierelement got into a warm world only and that is really bad, it burns out in short time. It need a cold side to create warmth. When we put voltage to it, magic happends and the opposit side gets warm if the other is cold, switch the polarization and the sides of the element switches. When we started to enjoy the modern things entropy got even higher...

Then came the end of the summer of 78. Nike got big with insulated soles on the shoes, Gore-Tex started to deliver clothing to keep us warm and dry. Later on Nokia and Ericsson got into the nuking powers of NMT and GSM... The rest we know now.

Today entropy is so high because of all EMF and the good life BUT we canÂ´t go back. ItÂ´s a law that we learned with thermodynamics. This is why we get serious effects with all Jack puts up here. ItÂ´s a simulation of the beginning of life...

Ok, whatÂ´s the point with this posting?

Can we have a simple discussion about the "new" science because i canÂ´t find it anywhere else. I feel like i would explode soon if i donÂ´t share my thoughts, tried my local university but only one person resonates with me (more about that later on).

So go on and challenge OUR minds because i think WE can do this and help other.

//Peter

trees dont eat food or have fat stores for a reason.......because they live in equilibrium with the photoelectric effect and grounding........People only need to food to offset the loss of disconnection as they move........the more you move the more food you need........the more food you need the more fat you need to store the said food..........it is not that hard to understand when you see life quantized and not static state. ### prAna303 — 2013-02-26T21:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77866>

Many years ago i worked with semiconductors. What i saw then is today my leading star and it explains everything i come upon. Every single bit of my life can and will be explained this way. My biggest journey started about eight years ago, working like a addict, not beeing sick in my physical body but my senses where set off. I was i salesman, a trader, a dealer, you name it. I did everything to get a sale and i was good at it, to the point that i became almost criminal. Today i know that i did do the criminal part as a way of getting away from all of it. Once i confessed i got fired, exactly what i wanted so bad. That day i promised myself that i would start a journey, finding the real ME and help other people. I knew i had the "talk" and could resonate with groups. One day i got the chance to work in i local shop selling sporting goods. I did have one special thing that i found useable, my lifelong passion for movement, motion. I started to help people with gaitproblems, bad posture and so on. Some think i have a "magic eye" when i see misalignments but i donÂ´t. I simply observe and watch for errors. All this got in sync with my whole mind so i could work further with my ideas about life. I think we can say that i found one of my passions but today i donÂ´t let it take over my life as it did before, i have a higher mission...

Where do this lead me now? I canÂ´t say i have the answers but i think we need to group ourself with people that comes from different worlds. We need doctors, singers, dancers, all kind of people that are openminded. We must change, either we accept the world as it is now and "thumb up" this frenzy. I know itÂ´s easy because i have been there, money rules... I thought!

I want to to discuss how we can let the "old" me behind. Tips & Tricks and so on. Trust me i have done many things the last 8 years. Really weird things in other peoples eyes BUT in the end i can only say it is positive. I resonate with so many of you without even spoken to you.

If we let this type of "language" dominate our thoughts, as some philosophers seem to believe we must, it will surely reduce us all to idiots who take everything literally, no?

### prAna303 — 2013-02-26T22:15:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77875>

**Why workout do not work out.**
I guess that Jacks reason is so right. For many years i have seen friends become addicts in the gym, spinning like a fool and still gaining weight. I have seen instructors at the gym break down to the point where they almost cry, when i discuss with them they are so sad, almost ready to give up on everything. Why? Simplified: semiconducting, when you start to move we build up inductance. Bones move in our bodies, flesh and blood get loaded and filled to the brink. We do not let the energy that builds up go, like loading a capacitor to the end. Boom, explodes. Today we think that working out helps us but very few of us is ready for it. If we do this in the new world also we see some bad bonus effects, mind goes off, we start to react to small things that we should not. The companies we work for pays BIG money for gymcards so their workers can do more, but be critical and always follow the money. This is why we see so many weird theories about diets today, we SCREAM for energy and working out makes us starved. The workout should be the exact opposite, i should give us energy thru the laws of nature and physics, but to do that we need to be polarized. When we do THAT magic can happend in an athlete, the engine becomes almost 100% efficient. Remember what we perhaps came from, a world full of energy and low entropy, but in that time we had MEGA difference between all sides like: Night - Day, Cold - Warm and so on.

### diane — 2013-02-26T22:29:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77881>

Peter - great topic!

Kind of along the lines of your post, I've been thinking lately about how much we've gotten away from what even our grandparents and great grandparents did. They were way more in tune with nature, and many of us are losing it (not all thank goodness). I think that there is a certain arrogance that we as a modern society have (broad generalization, but I am sure you know what I'm saying) that new is better. And in many ways, I think our ancestors had better intuition and sense as to what was beneficial for life.

Don't get me wrong, I like my modern toys, but there is a sense of simplicity and connection to nature that I've been drawn to over the past couple of years.

Music is something that has been with us humans from day one. We are made from it because it is natural frequencies and when combined we get harmonics, things start to cook up, resonate and connect. In that pot of magic things spread and we hear it but also sense something else because no tones are alone. What happends when we are disconnected, out of control we need entertainment in a simple form. Here i have my Lady Gaga theory, we are living fastfoodlifes in all aspects. We are so messed up with distortion that the only thing that penetrates our minds is LOUD, REPEAT and easy to connect with. Like food we must consume this fastmusic because our senses screams in hunger. When we start to get into a new "u" we are using music like any other energysource, it feeds us in everyway BUT it has to be a complex version, not the fastfoodLADYGAGAstuff. Classic music is made from nature and that gets us going. I drop into complex vs easy later on, it applies to much today.

### RGP — 2013-02-27T14:46:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77994>

Reminds me of a chapter in the book "Secrets of the Soil" by Peter Tompkins. The chapter was called Sonic Bloom, in which the natural frequencies of nature, birds singing and crickets chirping would cause the breathing holes under the leaves to open wider, thus allowing in more carbon dioxide and nutrients. So a guy named Dan Carlson tested different artificial sounds and frequencies trying to achieve the greatest amount of plant growth. It turned out different frequencies worked best for different plant species. Music that showed the greatest increase in growth included Vivaldi, Bach, and 1920's jazz, whereas hard rock music caused the plants to die. It appears certain frequencies resonated with the plants.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-27T15:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-77997>

Inspired by Peter today: What holds you steady in life? What are your principles for life? Attitude is a choice and it determines how you navigate your environment. How you see life and how you view others determines your journey through life. We are not born with courage, we are each born with potential. It takes more courage to reveal insecurities than to hide them, more strength to relate to people than to dominate them, more courage to abide by thought-out principles rather than a blind reflex. Toughness is in the soul and spirit, not in our muscles and an a undeveloped mind. The one thing we need to learn is that if we pay to much respect to our pasts we get stuck in the world "happened" to us, instead of creating the one we should be in. We get trapped in our own box and lose our edge. Today become unstoppable.

The chapter was called Sonic Bloom, in which the natural frequencies of nature, birds singing and crickets chirping would cause the breathing holes under the leaves to open wider, thus allowing in more carbon dioxide and nutrients. So a guy named Dan Carlson tested different artificial sounds and frequencies trying to achieve the greatest amount of plant growth. It turned out different frequencies worked best for different plant species. Music that showed the greatest increase in growth included Vivaldi, Bach, and 1920's jazz, whereas hard rock music caused the plants to die. It appears certain frequencies resonated with the plants.

This is the concept my son used back in grade school on a science project. He had three plants and exposed each one separately to rock, classical, and C&W. The classical music plant was healthiest and grew 3" more than the other two plants.

BTW, I really like this thread...has a lot of positive in it.

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-27T16:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78005>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=77982)
>
> I think everything has to do with resonance, EVERYTHING!
> Music is something that has been with us humans from day one. We are made from it because it is natural frequencies and when combined we get harmonics, things start to cook up, resonate and connect. In that pot of magic things spread and we hear it but also sense something else because no tones are alone. What happends when we are disconnected, out of control we need entertainment in a simple form. Here i have my Lady Gaga theory, we are living fastfoodlifes in all aspects. We are so messed up with distortion that the only thing that penetrates our minds is LOUD, REPEAT and easy to connect with. Like food we must consume this fastmusic because our senses screams in hunger. When we start to get into a new "u" we are using music like any other energysource, it feeds us in everyway BUT it has to be a complex version, not the fastfoodLADYGAGAstuff. Classic music is made from nature and that gets us going. I drop into complex vs easy later on, it applies to much today.

Have you ever looked into the discussion about tuning pitch Peter? My husband is a musician and a few years ago when I was researching resonance in connection with the horse/human therapy work I do, I found out that there is a movement to tune instruments back to the pitch that was popular for centuries until the 1930s when an international standard was agreed on. So instead of middle A being 440, it would be tuned down to 432. Well, I also read that the sound of 432 resonates with your heart and the higher 440 is more of a brain and head sound.....OK...there's a lot of information I'm skipping over, but you can read about why people are so passionate about changing how instruments are tuned.

In the case of my husband....I just said what about tuning everything down without talking about a lot of the woo. He play guitar, banjo, mandolin and bass. The change was really dramatic and interesting. He now calls it his "secret key." His voice fit the tuning better, his instruments started sounding richer AND his audiences started responding in a far more emotional way. He is a strong folk singer who always is singing a story...this is not technique, it's communication, so he was a good performer already. But we really noticed a difference.

Sorry for the long post...but I have to tell this one story that illustrates the power of these frequency differences.

My husband performs both for children and adults, he plays real concerts for children....non of this hokey, clown jump up and down stuff. He was asked to play for a school one time. In the audience were two boys who were autistic, non-verbal and constantly moving around without being able to sit still or focus. Their caregiver job was to keep them from disturbing the other kids...

When my husband started to play and sing, the two boys stopped moving around the room and came right up to him and put their hands on his arm. They didn't move while he was playing and as soon as he stopped they careened off again to move about. Their caregiver was in floods of tears afterwards and told him she had NEVER seen them stop moving for that long. The teachers were floored by what they seen too. Many other performers had played in the school and it wasn't as if this was the first time they had listened to music.

So R gave them two of his recordings and asked her to play them for the two boys later on and then tell him what happened. She called him back and said, it was funny but one of the cd's didn't do anything for them and the other one was like a repeat of the concert. They sat down and looked at the cd player and didn't move until the whole recording was over. Well, you can guess what the difference was, the older cd was recorded in standard pitch and the newer one he had done with 432. We are truly resonant beings and the power of sound is all around us.

### kathylu — 2013-02-27T19:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78045>

When I was in PT school years ago, one of my clinical rotations focused solely on therapies for profoundly autistic children. I was astonished to see that they all responded to classical music better than sensory integration therapy. They would stop or calm their repetitive behaviors (even the "head bangers") and it seemed they would truly _listen_ to the music. It seemed that Vivaldi, Corelli and Bach produced the most calming effects.

> So R gave them two of his recordings and asked her to play them for the two boys later on and then tell him what happened. She called him back and said, it was funny but one of the cd's didn't do anything for them and the other one was like a repeat of the concert. They sat down and looked at the cd player and didn't move until the whole recording was over. Well, you can guess what the difference was, the older cd was recorded in standard pitch and the newer one he had done with 432. We are truly resonant beings and the power of sound is all around us.

Band instruments and live orchestras are still being tuned A-440. Also pit orchestras for musicals . .if you want to see how the resonance can really effect the brain and body go to a drum corps show where they do 60-80 instruments all playing a 5 layer simple open chord and you realize real quick there is something going on there. The resonance waves created by the larger brass instruments is key - because you FEEL the waves in your body when the chord hits tune. Same effect when you get multiple voices in a gospel choir doing open chords. If you are in the room, you FEEL it.

When you layer that with a rhythm that syncs neurons firing (this is also a well known phenomena) it has an amazing cascade of brain effects.

When you layer that with YOU creating one of the pieces of sound that make up the whole resonate being it can be a very unique and moving experience.

A couple of composers to listen to some of the BAND (not orchestra) recordings are the old ones where a human is directing and it's multiple instruments/voices. My favorite is "Irish Tune from County Derry"

If you have never been to a pro level drum corp exhibition in person, I would highly recommend putting it on the list. You get a real experience with visceral effects that you won't soon forget. And you will become and addict quickly. Man, I miss those days. Too bad they are almost gone for good.

Drum Corp season starts in about June and wraps up with the world series in Aug/Sept.

**Timing**
We have lost our timing. In many ways as Jack describes it but how does it affect us?

We just discussed music and pitch but one big component is timing. Look at the magic when listening to a classical concert or a jazzband, timing is everything. What is time, just some frequencies again. We live in a time where everything is distorted with all new things...

In the making of semicondutors time is everything, a specification of a component depends on its timing. When we build a computer with components we must make sure that everything works in harmony, otherwise something get burned out and soon everything stops. When we look around us with every "new" thing we made itÂ´s a copy of something basic, nature or life or even the earth.

In this time i think we need to sync our clocks so we can unlock the powers of the nature BUT also the powers of the manmade things.

One way?

We must get the polarisation of things back. The difference between + / - and ground because when this is made we can use the energy between these fields by the Laws of nature and Quantums...

### prAna303 — 2013-02-27T20:30:07+0200

The thing i want to say is: donÂ´t forcefeed yourself with music you donÂ´t resonate with just because someone else say itÂ´s the right thing. Nobody can tell you whats right, only YOU! Listen to the music that lifts you and ditch the one that locks you to personal memories and moments (nostalgia)...

### prAna303 — 2013-02-27T21:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78083>

**Language**

What makes us human can sometimes be described that we talk and write. Ok, not only that but it is one thing that separates us from other species.

But have we forgotten our words?

Not long ago we used words and sentences like: get down to earth (if you are spaced out), go on vacation to reload our batteries and so on.

We have learned this by our parents and they by theirs... Funny that way back in time and history we knew probably somethings that had serious effect on us but we could not describe it. Could it be that words simply was a way to describe semiconducting from the beginning? Like making a blueprint for us to use in the future...

Weird thoughts by me.

### prAna303 — 2013-02-27T22:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78101>

I want to know what love is - Foreigner -

Ok, on to a subject that we crave so much today. Like everything that happends in life we can explain it with quantum and other electronic theories. But love?

I have said earlier on that i choose to find my girlfriends on how they "smell". This has nothing to do with if they take showers and use a perfume. It is simply about the following: RESONANCE

It has been some time now since a dated a girl but i do not panic about it. Love can be shared with others in so many ways but what happend just a week or two ago is simply weird but so right in time...

I just sat at one of my local places, zipping a cup of black coffee, reading a good book. I felt that someone was outside, next to me. Somekind of wonderful creature was standing there looking in, entered the place, just browsing around. Passed by my table and gave me a glance, a smile.

The next day, history on repeat. This time she took a snack but no words just a smile. Wonderful game of flirt, simply.

For the next five days we meet in strange places, streets, shops and so on.

On that sunday i was at work, helping someone with their problems with their feet. Boom, there she was again and this time with her boyfriend. They where there to talk about shoes and barefeet living. In just a second everything fell into place, we resonated like we had know each other forever. The funny thing is that the first thought i had when i saw her was with that present and gait she must be a dancer and boy was i right...

Ok, it will not lead to something but just this episode made everything i do worth it.

She gave me so much energy that i can say that now i can die without any regrets, we had our minutes of complete sync. Told an old friend of mine about it and she was sad that i thought it would not lead to anything else than this but what makes us think that we must own a love? Like a bird in cage, it will be bored and die soon. Better to let i fly free and have that second of love when we see each other.

What do i mean? Love has to resonate with the person you are now. If you change perhaps you have to let go of people and things to let everything become their best even if it is without you...

### prAna303 — 2013-02-28T13:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78276>

The old me vs the new...

Back in time when i worked as a salesperson, doing big business and living the crazy life i used technology as my number one tool and weapon against my competition. I lived with it and used it to the max but it had a prize. I knew all the time that just this little computer in front of me not only helped me BUT also made me a bad person, made me a very BAD human.

I knew that i had to leave it all behind otherwise i would be stuck. The thing that made it so bad is that when you have a lot of "new" information infront of yourself you can collect data in an instant BUT... Today the information we have is low bandwith, we only live in a brainiac world. The problem is not that we have to much information, the problem is that we have to make something understandable from almost nada, zipp, zero... We boil from the inside and out in our brains.

My point and some other who share this idea is that when we gadget up we only use low bandwith data, you must have ten windows open on your computer at the sametime watch a cat skateboard on youtube.

Your whole body screams for input but you have closed down every way for your body to get input, clothes and so on.

When we instead use a mix of body and brain, today we think it is easy things but they are complex. Take a simple task like making a dinner from scratch, so many things that have to sync (clock and timing). Take walking in the woods, barefeet. So advanced and very much like playing chess but this time you are doing it LIVE.

I think that if we should have any chance to overcome this frenzy we live in today we MUST put back some kind of animal / nature in us. Let the basic and easy stuff back into our lifes because they are so complex. The gadgets we use to make our lifes more rich are doing the exact opposite.

All of this is what Jack is trying to tell us bit by bit. Simply by adding the most basic things back into our lifes. We cannot go back and live in the beginning of times but we can choose our fights, that is unique this time. Back then we had a constant battle against nature going on and today we battle on the BIG WEB witch iPads and iPhones...

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-28T14:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78286>

Yes....the difference between information input...data and sensory knowledge. There is a great book called Hamlet's Blackberry by William Powers. From his book (which isn't about health affects of technology...more about the philosophy of being human in a digital age)

"Digital society affords a kind of street dancing, through social networks and he like. But is feels more like the Saint Vitus' dance that Thoreau talks about, frantic rather than joyful. If more modems were to shut down on Friday nights, I can see windows being thrown open and people wandering outside the way they do when there's a power outage, meeting neighbours they barely know. There might even be dancing in the streets."

I was thinking about the video I posted yesterday. It's beautiful and yet we can't rely on others to create the beauty we want to experience. I'm not trying to be anti-art, but as you say it's the LIVE part of life that we are trying to connect with...and it's so easy to leave it to others now and get it streamed in through our computers.

> "I think that if we should have any chance to overcome this frenzy we live in today we MUST put back some kind of animal / nature in us."

Couldn't agree more.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-28T14:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78291>

All that begins well ends well. Why don't you focus doing just that starting today? We can have all the wonderful dreams and goals, we may have great plans written. But until we implement things nothing begins to happen. We simply let aimlessness in life take over, circumstances dominate, and pressures eventually define us. When the ball labeled "opportunity" comes in your life, you need to follow through with the swing. Good intention for using your time here on Earth wont get you anywhere. Your actions must redeem your time. The real value in a life is using it for something. Knowledge without implementation is a burden. Today lighten your load and begin that wonderful idea.

Yes, good actions.. I love that. Always makes one happy!

I realize I like the sounds of the nature the most.. I like the silence and the silent sounds. They never make me tired like listening to music does at least when longer times. I do it less and less. It is just too much.
I sleep with window wide open and below my window is the waterfall/river. I am addicted to it actually..! And the birds too... and the wind in the big pine trees right next to my window. It is all so sacred.
