### prAna303 — 2013-03-02T08:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78581>

Have you ever got into a discussion about how you choose to live your life? One of the things that many have problems with me is that i can sometimes tap into science and this gets some people going nuts. One second into a discussion they ask what kind of school i went to, what kind of papers i wrote and when i say no school and no paper they always get that funny look in their eyes, like talking to a kid with delusions of grandeur... I always talk in easy words, simple layout and that i should not according to them.

When i explain my passions they really starts to get uncomfortable. I often say and think that the words written yesterday was good then, today i can use them to guide myself in the present but everything has changed from the moment they wrote it. This is why so many get stuck in science because of laws not bending the laws to the actual moment of NOW.

I have friends that get thru some really advanced classes by simple remember the whole book. Talk about a system that i dead wrong, what kind of new do you get with that. This is a BIG problem in Sweden i can promise you.

Simply get out there and digest all wonderful things, skip the bad ones and start create ART.

### prAna303 — 2013-03-02T11:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78583>

How much we can still learn from mother earth...

[http://www.swellvoyage.com/2013/02/the-mighty-marvelous-and-endangered-bluefin-tuna/](http://www.swellvoyage.com/2013/02/the-mighty-marvelous-and-endangered-bluefin-tuna/)

"I later learned that Bluefin tuna are one of the few species of fish considered ‘warm-blooded’ due to the way they can increase their body temperature beyond that of the surrounding waters by transferring body heat to their muscles similar to a ‘heat exchanger’ in an engine. This evolutionary advantage gives more power and efficiency to their muscles, allowing them to swim at speeds near beyond 50mph, dive more than 3,200 feet, and swim in constant migration ranging into some of the coldest parts of the oceans! Mighty Bluefin! Flawless hunter! Tireless traveler!"

This is something that i have used in my life for a long time. My friends called my weird, psycho and crazy. Someday i will tell you what i do and why but timing is everything. I set myself on this journey many years ago and everyday i get paid, not in money but in LIFE, energy and it is not only by kcal...

### Inger — 2013-03-02T12:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78584>

Peter, I am exited to learn more about you!

About other getting uncomfortable faced with certain things.... I understand that.
I think many have sensed a "false sound" surrounding many people speaking about "quantum theories" etc.... I have people close to me suffering from bad coherence.. even if "understanding" and speaking about the deepest things. One example; Words are deep and true but the person lives as a parasite in the community failing to take truly responsibility for themself.. smelling bad because lack of hygiene and failing to care about others feelings... failing to see the whole picture and that we are all tied together... forgetting what love is about...
Those things are very hard for me to communicate. But I can sense them very clearly, it is something weird, it is like something is missing. it is disharmony in the deepest sense...
I wonder if it has something to do with a brain being still very broken. people suffering from this often also have a thinking drugs make them "see" and get enlightened and greater understanding. IMHO drugs are just a shortcut to Eden and there really is no shortcut but instead it will make you suffer in the end. I am very sensible about this stuff because I really have had to face it close.. and I can tell it is not pretty...

I bet the people that get uncomfy might have faced those things too and naturally their minds put the pieces together fast.. how can we expect something else? That is why we need to do it all with much wisdom and IMO it is possible to meet people if you are coming from the right angle. The pieces have to fit really good in our whole being and that will help. If we get silent and listen we will find the answer and what road to travel and what words to use, I am sure.

You are so right about that timing is everything. It needs to ripen, also in us. Those things do not happen over night or they will get ugly... our whole being needs to develop and change.. then it is coherence..
My bro said to me once, many tend to get "fatal"... or "Ã¶desdiger" in Swedish... I am not sure if fatal is the right word in English but if we go that road it aint going to be pretty... that is when the words get big and our actions do not.. (or they can get big but in a bad way.. they have a bad effect on the once close to us and instead of carrying others burdens we just add to them) I bet this is one of the reasons Jack often says "speculation" (OMG I cannot remember the word he used for this but "speculations" have to do..) without execution is a really bad idea...

> Have you ever got into a discussion about how you choose to live your life? One of the things that many have problems with me is that i can sometimes tap into science and this gets some people going nuts. One second into a discussion they ask what kind of school i went to, what kind of papers i wrote and when i say no school and no paper they always get that funny look in their eyes, like talking to a kid with delusions of grandeur... I always talk in easy words, simple layout and that i should not according to them.
>
> When i explain my passions they really starts to get uncomfortable. I often say and think that the words written yesterday was good then, today i can use them to guide myself in the present but everything has changed from the moment they wrote it. This is why so many get stuck in science because of laws not bending the laws to the actual moment of NOW.
>
> I have friends that get thru some really advanced classes by simple remember the whole book. Talk about a system that i dead wrong, what kind of new do you get with that. This is a BIG problem in Sweden i can promise you.

> Simply get out there and digest all wonderful things, skip the bad ones and start create ART.

While science couldn't find the mechanism that gave us real answers, religion or spirituality provide the answers without any mechanism. When science has provided the answers in the mechanism, it seems it only reconfirms what spirituality has already talked about. This concept for me is disconcerting, yet perfect, because it explains why Einstein was blocked by Heisenberg's uncertainity principle. God really does roll the dice and always has..........his game is called evolution.

### prAna303 — 2013-03-04T20:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-78933>

The art is still out there but it comes in different ways. As a dear friend of mine called me, you are analog. I rant about dance and music both here and in real life, we are missing so much and i hope that my stubborn way of living proves for people around me that you can have a better me.

Have you felt that people canÂ´t stand that you look at them? In just a couple of years people have become afraid of human touch and connection.

Went to a local bar this saturday just to talk to an old friend (bartender). He said that everything is so different nowdays. People just buy their drink and then connect to the net, updating status on twitter, facebook or instagram. The old way of flirting has gone, even the deadtalk with the bartender is long gone. I felt like an alien and soon i went home...

IÂ´m not nostalgic or romantic in my thoughts, just a little scared whatÂ´s going on... No wonder that relationships donÂ´t hold, how can you love someone on the net? If someone talks about cults or sects they should really look into the everyday use of junkmedia...

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-16794606](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-16794606)

One thing that really helped me understand the differences between being high through drugs and having altered states of consciousness without drugs (meditation, dance, music, love, floating in water etc. etc.) is that you don't maintain coherence or congruency throughout the experience when you take drugs...it comes from the outside in and you don't know the steps and stages of transforming along the way and you are not able to mediate and play with these states of mind...the drugs have the control.

And there is nothing like an ocean or a forest to feel alive again, I agree.

### prAna303 — 2013-03-05T12:55:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79051>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=78977)

> One thing that really helped me understand the differences between being high through drugs and having altered states of consciousness without drugs (meditation, dance, music, love, floating in water etc. etc.) is that you don't maintain coherence or congruency throughout the experience when you take drugs...it comes from the outside in and you don't know the steps and stages of transforming along the way and you are not able to mediate and play with these states of mind...the drugs have the control.
>
> And there is nothing like an ocean or a forest to feel alive again, I agree.

Just some wise words from one of my masters:

If you have a golf-ball-sized consciousness, when you read a book, you'll have a golf-ball-sized understanding; when you look out a window, a golf-ball-sized awareness, when you wake up in the morning, a golf-ball-sized wakefulness; and as you go about your day, a golf-ball-sized inner happiness.

But if you can expand that consciousness, make it grow, then when you read about that book, you'll have more understanding; when you look out, more awareness; when you wake up, more wakefulness; as you go about your day, more inner happiness.

David Lynch

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-05T16:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79081>

This is why when people point to spelling and grammar I just move past them............that behavior tells me about their focus.

Your dreams don't make you great.....your work ethic that grows them does. Dreams are free but the journey is not. When we roll up our sleeves, pitch in and work hard together, united for a common cause, there is no telling how successful we can be. Teamwork will make our dreams work. Why do I think work is the best 4 letter word? We should work for just the pleasure we find in work, the satisfaction there is in developing things, in creating something new. Also, the associations business begets. The person who does not work for the love of work, but only for money, is not likely to make money nor to find much fun in life. Today think about making you vocation your vacation in how you perceive it.

### caroline — 2013-03-06T01:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79204>

There are so many wonderful truths in this thread. I always loved what I did so much and couldn't wait to get back to work after a vacation. While on vacation - I would constantly read cookbooks and talk to everyone about food, food, and more food. Picking the brains of Chef's in different countries was always a hi-lite and I would go back to work just overflowing with ..... life. I would often be invited back into kitchens to talk to chefs and I would come away just glowing.

You really can't underestimate loving what you do and what it does to your health if you hate going to work every morning or are just doing it for the money.

Mind you .... some money is nice.....

### Hope — 2013-03-06T01:48:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79210>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=79204)

>

>

Beautifully said, Caroline.....your job sounds like a dream...or former job  I unfortunately got stuck in a job I came into each day gave me a stomach ache because my boss was disrespectful and I was bored out of my wits. A bad fit on so many levels but my body and brain were broken so much, I had no confidence to do something different, like apply for a different job at a different company...until I got sick enough and was mistreated enough to get out of that environment, (five years ago) and start over...doing something different, on my own, little by little, pursuing my dreams, working hard, learning and teaching myself stuff and making less money but getting paid in so many ways..not the least of which, better health. And some day, I will make much, much more money than what I ever did in that sucky job...with freedom and passion and love. I wish I knew then what I know know but I also know I had to get sick enough to get it...to be willing to risk, to pursue my dream, against what people, some friends, even in-laws said....ugh. Follow that gut....and a little more every day as my brain gets better and better with each little step...

### prAna303 — 2013-03-06T19:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79314>

Every day need some new missions, when we stop to move we slowly die off...

I have been thinking alot about going off grid for some time and have been looking for the place to go, seeking an old farm or something like that high up in the mountains. Today i just know that i cannot wait until that moment is right, timing is vital and when you let the things around us affect our choices time flies.

Since i live in a place that has mountains, deep forest, open sea and minimal human prescence in just five minutes by foot i came to the simple conclusion that i take the ultimate control of my life again. With my know how and what i come from i simply have my place and start to place myself in the elements even more. Why wait to buy something when itÂ´s free? So i start as soon as possible to sleep and retreat to the wild as much as possible, not living on one spot day in and day out. If iÂ´m gone for a week so be it, i will be a visitor and observer in the "normal" world...

The bottom line, do not wait when you feel in your whole existence that you resonate with all timeages, the past, the present and the future.

### prAna303 — 2013-03-06T19:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79320>

Clarity.

In this world we live in i come to think about clarity, especially hearing.

Today at the local cafÃ© i counted about 15 persons with headphone/sets. All of them very far off not by not listening but far off in their 3D view of the room. They went into things, dropped spoons and behaved odd. The next thing i felt was that i heard everyone, not every word but the way they said things. A very sublime and disturbing feeling i had because i felt like a visitor from another place and time.

Deep inside of me i know why this is happening to me just now and how i can use it. Some calls it a strange gift but itÂ´s just about energy and electricity. How i use it? I work sometimes with people with gaitproblems, bad posture and simply pains, they get shocked when i can tell them exactly where the have an issue and itÂ´s not magic. Simply observing and reacting.

Please use this thing called sensory and go out and observe... Put down that iPhone with headphones and suck in the beauty of life, get shocked that many is so "off" today, when you do this you will NEVER want to go back. When you see all this you start to enjoy what other calls simple things like a walk in the woods, a stroll on the beach, a performance at the theatre, a dance act.

When everything is digitalized today we loose so many electrons and our brains cooks when it tries to make sence of nothing. All the smells and dirt is gone.

### caroline — 2013-03-06T21:11:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-79324>

That is why - when I go to the ocean - I always take a book but I never once open it. I would miss all the incredible things going on around me. The other day was so magnificent I could hardly contain myself........ Opera was coming from somewhere ..... The waves were crashing......the sky was a brilliant clear blue.......it was pure bliss. I just sat and took it all in........it was like this out of body experience......just simply perfect.....

### prAna303 — 2013-03-12T13:10:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-80170>

Travel.

How do you move your body thru your day? How much of the time do you spend in a car, train, plane or something modern?

Today all the modern things that we put our bodies into is build with tons and tons of hitech. We sit in a cage of components and we also are a component (or many in sync). When we move at speed perhaps above our own ability i think many bad things happends. One of the biggest may be loosing our sync. So in line with the modern city we travel high speed together with high speed communications, see the connection? COMMUNICATION, it is all about electricity... This is why itÂ´s not about training or working out any longer when we jump from our crossfit box to the big SUV blasting away to do something else, we fast forward the computer in our self with the lost of ground and adding unnatural speed to it. The processor getÂ´s wacked, 400MHz Pentium III no longer but a 4MHz 386 tomorrow.

Wonder what else happends when we do this "timetravel"...

I think this is one of the most ignored things today, travel is human but not with unhuman technology.

### caroline — 2013-03-13T00:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-80252>

I have been thinking lately about why I have so many experiences with people and nature etc. when others don't seem to. It is only because I walk everywhere or take public transport. My friends are always saying that I have the best experiences all the time and it is true - I do!

I get to exchange smiles and greetings and often much more with perfect strangers every single day. As Dr. K. says "Quantum Entanglement"

My life is enriched beyond measure from the very simplest of things......I am totally in awe......every single day....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-13T04:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-80312>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=78940)
>
> **The woods** Have you ever got lost in a deep forest?

Where I live you can literally see forever. If you are in the trees, hike up the closest hill and you can see again. That is what I grew up with, and what I've always known. I never got lost. When I was 16 we travelled to Europe: France, Switzerland, and Italy. I never got lost, ever. They would drop off my group in these walled cities and when it was time to head back, my companions would be clueless as to which way to go. For whatever reason, I was usually successful and guiding us back to our transportation.

When I was in college, I lived in the Pacific NW where the trees are huge and blind out the sky along with the constant rain. I discovered that even in pitch black I could trust my instincts and intuition to get me back to my starting point 9 times out of 10. But one time, I was headed back home after a weeklong field trip and we stopped to see the largest spruce tree. The tree was about 400 yards from where we parked. We were in a National Park. Somehow we got lost, one classmate and I. We kept doing circles, over and over for hours...always arriving back at our starting place. It was the weirdest feeling for me. My classmate started to lose it big time. I started looking for places to sleep that would be dry, since it was downpouring. We were in our rain gear, so we weren't going to freeze. At one point, I just remember feeling very still, very quiet. The rain was pouring down off every surface above us. Very far off I heard the echo of a logging truck. The echo grew louder and more obscure. I had a moment of clarity, and I said, "This way." My classmate, still freaked out, didn't believe me. I said, if you really want to spend the night in the woods, I'll join you, but I'm certain that the way out is this way." I head straight south, and we ended up at highway 101 in less than an hour. Because we'd been missing for so long, they'd sent out search parties with boats and dogs. I was SO embarrassed! I never get lost!

### prAna303 — 2013-03-17T08:36:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-80853>

Quote: Become super human in a modern world that is tearing us apart.

This is far more easy than most think. This thought was in my head eight years ago because i saw what was coming, working in a business of Hi-tech. When i had to quit that life and never look back these words were in my mind from that day on...

During the last four years i have been rewarded so many times that iÂ´m speechless, so many connections to people, life, motion, art, science and other things.

How do we do it?

I like it simple, very simple... In the beginning of times when entropy was low it would be damn hard to be more superhuman than the human standing next to you but today it is far more easy. With this i donÂ´t mean that we should think we are Ã¼berhumans that rule over others BUT...

Being a super human today is the person who puts the human BACK into us, letting our brains work, bodies function, being physical, BEING HERE!

Proof: just a couple of days ago i sat with my cup of coffee at the local cafÃ©, alone, writing in my book with a REAL FOUNTAIN PEN (not scratching an iPod), reading some books. Nobody else does this, everyone use their phones and gadgets the whole time. Twice during that sitting unknown girls sat next to me and after about 10 minutes the started to talk to me, asking questions who i was and so on. The last one said something funny: ItÂ´s weird, i never talk to strangers but you i felt like i known for years and you are so present in this place.

I have read about others on this forum that have the same things going on now and i think that some people are so hungry for connections today that they really do anything to connect when they see someone that is HUMAN, this is how we can change this crazy shit storm thatÂ´s going on out there.

### prAna303 — 2013-04-26T21:58:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-84904>

Back again. Everything changes and we must adapt... Starts with a quote.

â€œmake a radical change in your lifestyle and begin to boldly do things which you may previously never have thought of doing, or been too hesitant to attempt. So many people live within unhappy circumstances and yet will not take the initiative to change their situation because they are conditioned to a life of security, conformity, and conservation, all of which may appear to give one peace of mind, but in reality nothing is more damaging to the adventurous spirit within a man than a secure future. The very basic core of a man's living spirit is his passion for adventure. The joy of life comes from our encounters with new experiences, and hence there is no greater joy than to have an endlessly changing horizon, for each day to have a new and different sun. If you want to get more out of life, you must lose your inclination for monotonous security and adopt a helter-skelter style of life that will at first appear to you to be crazy. But once you become accustomed to such a life you will see its full meaning and its incredible beauty.â€￾

― Jon Krakauer

### SeaHorse — 2013-04-27T04:05:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-84926>

I like his writing...and I would add that not everybody has to climb Mt Everest to have a changing horizon. There is a lot of internal adventuring that can go on without needing to sell the house and hit the road. Newness can often be found in your backyard or down the street...

One of the things that has bothered me about the personal development world is that there is often a subtext that implies that everyone should be doing the same kind of work in life...that everyone has the same kind of "leader" inside them and there is no recognition of the variations that leadership has, or the different gifts that we are all born with. I'm thinking about this because i find that people who are on the cusp of real change and transformation should be guided by what is unique and true for them, not by pressure to conform to outside validation. Does that make sense? A life lived to its fullest degree is going to look very different for each of us....

Thanks for continuing to post such great things!

### caroline — 2013-04-27T05:49:26+0300

### prAna303 — 2013-04-27T08:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-84935>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=84926)
>
> I'm thinking about this because i find that people who are on the cusp of real change and transformation should be guided by what is unique and true for them, not by pressure to conform to outside validation. Does that make sense? A life lived to its fullest degree is going to look very different for each of us....
>
> Thanks for continuing to post such great things!

Very true. Over the last three months i have been observing that almost everyone around me is hunting fame. We do so by "liking" the most wicked things today in the DIGITAL world, not the real. We are pushed with happy comments and shut down when someone gets into a discussion with us because we canÂ´t handle arguments. I belive that when someone goes another route/trail with a purpose that is for our own best we see these actions more clearly.

One of the best ways to find your own meaning of life is to push yourself thru your own history and try to find where you lost yourself. I did it seven years ago and found that i was fifteen when i lost it. Even if it was painful i reversed my own history and took up from there, now i feel like i am 22 again, i got my own timing in sync.

Book and writings should not be read as lawbooks. They are simply clues to your own person, watch how you react and build your own world around them. This is true for everything in life BUT there is one big thing that i think this does not apply to: The Digital Age we are living with now. Today we are forcefeed with so much junk that we work overtime to sort thing out. The things that should make our lifes easy makes it hard but not in a physical way. We are trying to make a tree out of a illusion beacuse we have never seen or smelled a tree in real life, this is happening 24/7 among alot of people right now.

### Inger — 2013-04-27T21:31:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-84954>

Nice stuff here.......

Weird what happends when one let go of hunting fame. All of the sudden everything becomes way more peaceful.. and the connection within one self becomes stronger. Like more earthed? IDK how to explain this but I must say I like it...
Peter me too have gone back thinking about when I lost myself. I realize the times I was so lost I have not many memories, or they have little color... and feel not so good. Kind of sad actually... But you are so right about feeling 22 again. I often think I got back to being a teenager, and this time I will live all those years again - in a different way. I really feel so very young too.. only the wrinkles on my face and hands tell me I am not..lol but who cares.. only how we feel counts. And at work they always get shocked when they ask my age and I tell I am 36. I bet it is not because how I look, because I really have some wrinkles here and there, I have seen many my age who have way less wrinkles. Have been smoking, stressing, and all. But maybe they see what I feel? And not so much how I look. funny...

### prAna303 — 2013-06-09T21:23:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87852>

We live in the best of times...

"Humanity has the stars in its future, and that future is too important to be lost under the burden of juvenile folly and ignorant superstition."
Isaac Asimov

I think we should be lucky, most of us (at least at this forum) live in the most special times of many ages. Why? We do have a choice.

Simply put, we have more money, freedom, abilities and ways to create our own lives. Just a couple ages ago we had to fight to survive, fight wars and struggle. Today we all can read books, documents and study things that where only meant for choosen ones.

ItÂ´s not the modern world thats wrong, it how we act upon it. With our freedom we can have the best of all angles. We can connect, learn, study, discuss in the digital world and at the same time take a walk in the wild. Not at the same time i think you know.

Why do we always stuck into some kind of crazy fanaticism? Mostly because we cannot se ourself in a distance, we think the world spins around just me. DonÂ´t get me wrong, ego is not bad but when we only want credits for our prescense we are doing it wrong.

This applies to everything, think diets, training, music and so on... We get fanatic.

So with this i want to say that, stop blaming the things that is around us. Sit down, think deeply and start to act by yourself. Try a little of this, that or even use a smartphone BUT, big BUT donÂ´t get stuck in itÂ´s alluring nature. Use smart tools to become even more free. If you give five hours on a computer to get a free day from work, do it. Go away, look at the sea, climb a tree, smell the pine.

For sure som weird things happends around us now, bad food, bad lifestyle and so on... Why should you react and act to everything? Ignore it and in the end it will go away IF you learn someone else to ignore things they donÂ´t like. DonÂ´t smash a window because you hate the store, stop buying their shitty stuff instead.

I live in the best of two worlds now... Most of my time i spend in the wild and the other time i spend in the modern world, when iÂ´m there: i absorb fine art, fine dance, fine music, fine food and wine. I donÂ´t even spend time on weird things anymore. Sure i look at others and feel that their ways of living is insane but i do not want to share their dreams. If someone needs my advice i share but i do not force feed anyone, it do not work that way...

For everyone, go out tonite and just absorb... Think that we are lucky bastards and LAUGH!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-09T22:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87855>

I can't believe I've missed this thread until now. Reading through the entire thread and it's astounding. Really resonates with me and my spiritual/philosophical nature (that I need to reconnect with more often). It's threads like this that make me wish we could set up some sort of "Optimal Community" and all harness our combined energy, lol. I guess rather than wishing, I should push forward and accomplish just that by creating bonds with those around me. That reminds me of Mark Sisson speaking about 'creating a tribe'. I believe building a tribe is optimal. In a way I want to live out like the Amish or some similar culture, only with some less restriction. I don't view technology as anything inherently destructive or evil, but it can be. For instance, we know we arose out of the Savannah's but adapted to various environments, I feel as though that could've/could be possible with technology and what not, but it simply exploded at such an exponential rate our epigenetic switches couldn't compare with. Hence why we suffer so many ailments these days that are increasingly being attributed to technology. I also believe technology can be used as a light in the dark for information and even art, and that's what I see life as - art.

This is all making me want to stop doing my homework and read some QB posts and related materials, lol. It's also odd, because I've recently been trying to get some more connection in my life. I read some work of Paul Jaminet's in regards to walking and movement, so I've been taking daily barefoot walks. Normally my mind wanders. Yesterday it was different, I focused on my breath and if my mind wandered I actually allowed it. Rather than struggle to contain it, I just allowed it to "be". I noticed I had no anxiety whatsoever, something I've been struggling with for slightly over a year. I used to find myself not being capable of taking long walks due to random bouts of anxiety, and if I didn't bring my cellphone, it would be increased, almost as if I lost connection to society (the cellphone serving as that connection). Well that is no more. Although I may not bring that connective device with me, I'am connected to something grander - everything that is, including myself. Even if anxiety arises I just allow it to be. The reason I find this odd is because I've been experiencing so many "synchronicities" for the past year, and it has been occurring even more frequently the past few months.

What do you think about seemingly impossible synchronicities? Is it something that should naturally occur when we are in touch with ourselves and nature and when our sense of timing is proper? It's hard for me to explain what I mean about 'synchronicity', but for instance, there are many times that I think of something simultaneously with someone else, only it is a thought that would normally be expected not to occur at the same time. For instance, Joe Rogan was speaking about a time on the Podcast when he was cracking jokes about Pikochu, and right at the exact moment, Brian Redban walked in the door with a Pikochu outfit on. The weird thing is they NEVER speak about Pikochu, and Brian Redban never wore that outfit - he just randomly purchased it and decided to wear it. One day I read about some conspiracy about world bankers and the sinking of the Titanic, and I came back on these boards and a member told me something along the lines of "You are the Titan of your own ship," it was weird lol. Does that have to do with resonance, or what? Concepts like these are normally much more captivating to me than nutrition/physiology/optimal performance, but then I realize they are all interrelated, Jack's posts about quantizing various nutrients (DHA for one) really reinforced that.

Sorry for the rant lmao.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-09T22:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87856>

Our reality is a singular vision of what we see........the road to optimal is paved with stones that make you realize that the perception of your reality is bits and pieces that form the whole. The reason you are not aware of it is because consciousness like vision just appears before your eyes when you open them.............therefore you "believe" this is how the world works.

It does not.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-09T22:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87859>

I will look for your reply to the email, seems rather intriguing. That's what I'm trying to do now, realize the HOW, I've already grasped the fact that everything is connected. I wrote a term paper for my World Religions class detailing this and how many religions argue the same exact beliefs and concepts. That's why when I read you're comment about science reinforcing spirituality, I had to comment, it's just so true. Ancient peoples were simply "in tune" and we lost all of that, I could only imagine where we'd be today if we didn't lose sight of reality, nature, interconnectedness, etc.

@prAna: That's so true. It's odd you say that, again another synchronicity. Before starting my homework I was taking notes on some BBC America films on nature and various animals and how they lived (Bears namely, also lions, then a piece on how the sun/moon/seasons affect animals around the world). While watching I was absolutely captivated. I was literally entertained just watching how the animals moved and communicated in their seemingly foreign manners. I'm not who enjoys being jealous, but I had to say, I became jealous of members such as Inger and others who live in other parts of the world. I would love to live what is viewed as a "simple" and "boring" life, yet experience the beauty of it all. I mean, just watching other species grow and interact, watching plants, living in tune with the weather no matter how harsh or beautiful. Growing my own herbs and spices and maybe raising a small amount of animals. Hunting. Everything, the whole nine yards. I would honestly still probably own a computer, but not t.v.'s or anything else, and even the computer would be a **maybe** (I'm not gonna lie, although I don't game anymore, it is an old passion of mine I'd like to get into again, lol).

### prAna303 — 2013-06-09T22:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87860>

I think most of the so called paleo world is rambling about nostalgy. I see that way to many get stuck in a dream of old days when the strongest would survive. In the same way they talk about training, like itÂ´s about killing a bear. What started as a nice community has grown to a elitist world where every repetition counts, nothing else matters. Bet many of them would cry themself to sleep when their tech would be gone. When we talk tribes i think i found the magic key... Eight years ago i choose to go a different way, during this time i have joined and been welcome to some of the most wonderful humans ever. No one demands anything but everyone loves the company we bring in ourself, the thing that connects us?

The pure love to motion and elements. We are climbers, surfers, trailrunners, freedivers and we love planet earth and fights for itÂ´s future.

### SeaHorse — 2013-06-10T03:06:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87878>

I agree Peter...there is a world of difference between nostalgia for a "golden age" and the creative power to create your own wonderous world in whatever time period you live in. There really was never a golden age anyway...it just seems to us that is was easier to live more completely...and perhaps that is true, but the awareness of a connected life is there for those willing to create it and there have always been many people throughout the ages who have not taken that path.....

BPC77....it's great to feel your passion coming through your posts....just remember that we can walk on the ground, soak up the sun and (hopefully) wade in the water wherever we are...we don't have to have a wilderness outside our door to be "wild" I have a poster on my wall that is a woodcut of a boar running through a forest and the title is "Wildness is necessary." It's an internal thing.....

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-10T03:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87886>

This is a wonderful thread, I would really enjoy for it to continue. Thanks, SeaHorse. I have come to that realization - and I can see that it is almost a 'skill' or 'set of knowledge' that I'am training and developing. What I mean is, I was always a curious individual, but I would never really be able to go and take a walk and just "be", and not only "be", but come back and reflect upon the walk and realize I **enjoyed** that simple act. I can do that now, and I'm starting to apply that to all other aspects in my life. I can look at a piece of art and get lost in the corner of a painting before even exploring the rest. That's why I laugh when people I know say they don't want to live really long lives or perhaps even forever. I don't think life would ever get boring. There would definitely be bouts of boredom, but life itself would not become boring. I enjoy so much already and I can't even fully express the humanity in me (if that makes sense). Soon enough I will be engaging in martial arts again, and that is my physical form of artwork manifesting itself. I love it. I can picture it so vividly in my minds eye, just rolling around in the dojo, giving your all. Even though to some wrestling/boxing/MMA may just look like violence - it's not, it's art. After you myelinate your nerves and what not everything is instinctual, and you know precisely how to move in the most eloquent manner to perform a strike with more force and more accuracy - something that actually takes a lot of thought but is done in a split second.

This isn't even getting into my new love of the aquatic life - I want to start swimming and surfing. There's just so much, lol. Don't even get me started on the sensation of love either. Love is like no other drug. When you speak of your poster on your wall, that reminds me I need to continue building my art collection. I purchased this really trippy paiting at my local college and I want to add on to it. I'm going to continue taking my daily walks and just training myself in mindfulness all while getting the benefit of grounding and movement. I'm also thinking of starting to meditate during my cold showers. Wim Hoff was actually in an interview and spoke about using meditative breathing when doing CT.

I also agree with you about golden ages. I think a 'golden age' is purely subjective, whether to the individual or to culture itself. Thinking back to one of Dr. Kruse's posts about the electrical charge in just one individual mitochondria - the equivalent of 30 million volts per meter in our scale. We have a tremendous amount of energy to harness within, we could create the reality we envision, all it takes is the effort (really reinforced by QB-8, absolutely brilliant blog).

wonderful , engaging and thought provoking posts .... thanks everyone ........

This journey just keeps getting better and better .....

### prAna303 — 2013-06-10T21:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-87937>

What kind of OS are you running on?

Picture this: Today we tend to use either two OS on our computers, we sit back and let someone else decide how we should act, create and live our live with the computer. Just because we have gone from using computers in labs to running around with iPads we have accepted a mainstream version of how we should interact.

Do you stand up and say NO? Do you agree that everything should be that way? Do you want APPS to show a cat skateboarding? If you take a stand and just create your own OS, the one that suits you, what would you do?

Computers today is a reflection of us humans. We live lifes made up by others, not you. This is how science, healthcare and much more act. The make a norm and that should apply to everything.

When your life is made up of old versions of software, how do you think your hardware will act? The computer does not run smooth without the fans roaring like hell. Now and then you must restart and hope that a firmware update will solve the problem. When you run programs that are not in sync with you, do you think that it will do it without conflict.

If you are writing your own OS to your own HW then magic happends. Nobody is the same as the one sitting next to you. Not a single person is working exactly like you so why use a generic software with flaws...

### prAna303 — 2013-06-18T10:26:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88336>

Emotion thru motion.

I strongly belive that humans are moving by nature. When we get to a halt we become sick and worried, if we stay in that zone we move less and end up totaly still, dead.

I do not mean that we shall "train" we should always move in the most effective way with such small amounts of gears, garments and other "helpful" things. We must understand that nothing is separated, we are humans in a suit that is part of earth and earth is part of space and so on... When we loose ability to move by body, mind and soul we are dying. This is also why so many get stuck in their thoughts today, we do not get the mind in motion.

Many of us come from people who come from people who where nomads. We travel with all the changes around us. The sun starts our morning and we do some sun salutations outside and barefeet, grounded... Think about what we do when we simulate yoga indoors, do you think we get the original effect? Thru the day we move in fluid motion, thru rain, sun, snow, sand and other elements. We stop at a nice place with water, shade and peace. We eat the food in itÂ´s natural state and tell stories, learn our children our story and fall to sleep under the stars.

This blueprint is in us i strongly belive and because we canÂ´t live that life today we have to start to simulate it. But not with going fanatic on one thing but with a mix of moderate changes. We can have computers and cars but we choose what we do with it. Start easy with microadventures, get a tent, go outside for 24 hours and simulate the world we came from. You will resonate with some parts from minute one and other will take longer to like.

We are everything and elements of nature is us.

### prAna303 — 2013-06-18T15:07:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88343>

Sublime, my simple rule for everyday living.

I like the way of simple, when we overdo something it gets ugly and ugly for me is stay away from it.

The sublime things are the things hiding in the shadows, the silence, the small things that the big mass of humans do not bother with.

We do so many things today without grace and beauty, take training for example...

\- we act like killing machines at the gym, lifting weights at a tempo of panic. We stand on a nordic trainer and sweat to the beat of the latest tunes on spotify... Is this looking wonderful?
\- when we see a dancer move with fluid motion and presenting the small things in the music, that is what resonates with me.

This can be applied to everything and i do so. Is a food made of dried grounded unknown stuff beautiful? Is a piece of salmon that?

The nature gives us clues of this everyday, if a bear is peaceful and walk by you it is sublime, if it gets up on their feet and showing their teeth, it is scary.

Sublime is so underrated today because many thinks it is elitist and only for the gifted ones. I say it is up to you ONLY.

### prAna303 — 2013-06-21T19:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88598>

A small part of the latest blog resonated with something i see alot now...

"Vitamin A is critical in the quantum photo-transduction of light in our retina. A lack of Vitamin A makes it impossible to see in low light environments because Vitamin A is needed for the optimal physiologic optics found in our cornea and our retina. These optics all operate under the laws of quantum mechanics."

I am often talking about art and things that is sublime. Today this is really nailing something that is bugging me. Modern technology has made movies non vivid, boring and dumb. I have thought a great deal about this and have been shooting both film and stills in my life but today i think everything looks like crap. After breaking it down piece by piece from audience to camera i can only say that the tech is killing everything in the lowlights, the shadows and i miss it. As i said before, it is in the shadows the magic happends, in life, in art, in music (the silence) and so on. When you miss your regular life with normal lights and shadows, this kind of art gets boring, you turn up the volume but there is still nothing there. Then you feed yourself with more action, sounds and AI lights just to get the kicks, think about the movies made the last years.

If the art is boring to you then perhaps it is not the art that is wrong, it can be you and how you see the world.

### SeaHorse — 2013-06-21T23:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88624>

Too right Peter....my husband has been changing how he plays music these last few years and it is akin to what you are saying with visual art. Instead of a single note meaning something, it is now considered boring and simple....so everybody's adding lots more notes to melodies, lots more layering, all the instruments are being playing flat out all the time instead of complimenting and supporting each other. And the biggest insight of all is that silence has such an important role in music. A single note can have a wide range of meaning if there is some space and silence around it. A shadow can hold so many different shades of light if you don't just dismiss it as "dark."

### Dali Dula — 2013-06-22T14:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88653>

So I'm swimming in a pond and the temp of the water is stratified this time of year. Warm on top, cold below. I'm swimming doing a slow breaststroke and I'm thinking about wavelengths cuz that's what you do when you swim right? Any way I start fooling around imagining the layers of temp are colors as I move up and down. so I'm swimmimg through this fluid spectrum imagining the colder water below is blueish and the tones change as I rise through the warmer layers to red at the surface. No drugs involved here. I'm sensing the colors with my skin.

Our pond here at home has the same layers of temperature that you write about Dali Dula. ### prAna303 — 2013-06-23T11:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88727>

Summer solstice.

The time for reflection and action.

Ever since i started my journey eight years ago i have been using this time of the year to reflect on life and my position in that giant thing called living. I simply think about the things i have given up and the things i have gained...

Many of my friends, new or former think of me as a somewhat crazy person, sure i have done so many things that really do not resonate with them but they see the results. Never have i been stuck in my own thoughts and stuck in bad situations. Sure, i been in some really nasty periods but i have always had a plan on how to release and go on.

The thing that many cannot understand is how i can live without money, i do get some by working just some hours per week but i am way under the limit for being in need of socialcare. They think i am crazy when i hold speeches without any money for it but i have a rule that information, storytelling and more must be free. It is a law of nature because when you start to make money out of a passion it gets to be a regular work and alot of must, should, will and so on.

The big thing i feel is that i am on my right trail, finding new angles everyday and make my own future. Just by giving up simulating life as i did before i have found everything in my connection to nature and the elements. I love the idea of living in the wild mixed with the chances to study, observe and enjoy the art in the modern world. I am a human that adapts to both worlds without getting the most ugly things.

My projects are clear now. I have come to have at most seven different MUST in my life, when it getÂ´s more something has to go. I can always catch up with that later on but i never have more than that.

Just one of the projects that has gone is the hunt for a cabin in the woods. This requires money (alot) and instead i just go out and take my camping spot whenever i need that freedom.

Simple will always be my guideline, thinking about how i would do something if i was a wild animal helps me all the time, makes dogma and rules look crazy.

### prAna303 — 2013-06-23T21:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88756>

Recovery.

Today everyone is talking about recovery and in an instant they mean recovery from their training regime. Everyone i meet talks about days off and refueling but i think they are missing a big thing. Today we need recovery from so many more things than that. We have sick relationships with partners and friends, weird ways to act with technology and most of all, we are so overloaded with meaningless stuff that makes our brains work overtime to make something useful of it.

When i discuss this with people around me they first look a little puzzled but after i explain this deeper they see a bigger picture.

I have taken "super athletes" to a dance performance and after they felt like burning up with feelings and energy.

Have been to concerts in churches even though i do not belive in HIM and seen people energized by the feeling.

Recovery must be considered for all of us not the athlete only.

Everything must have the other side, nothing can be only feeding our system.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-23T22:14:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-88761>

how do we recover? We do it like a forrest does when it kisses lightening from above. Today, set fire to your broken pieces; start anew.

Set your life on fire like the forrest faces naturally. Seek people who fan your flames.

### prAna303 — 2013-06-29T22:02:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-89206>

To loose something is to win another...

Last week i said goodbye to some of my friends.... They are leaving this place to go live their dreams. Two of them is heading to the mountains to climb for a couple of years, living in a van (dirtbags). Another girl is heading towards the waves and surf. This means that my tribe is shrinking, soon im alone again in my town.

The pure beauty in this is that even though loss i gain the knowledge that they are living their dreams, because i do it myself everyday. Maybe we will meet again, who knows BUT thats the wonderful thing about loss, you never know what you gain.

So to celebrate this i took up dancing again, been over 20 years since i did it in any serious way but now is the time. With all this alone time i have again i can stretch my mind and body to new places.

Someone said that love and passion is like a bird. Catch it and put in a cage because of its beauty only for the reason you want to see it everyday, soon it gets ill, sick and die. Everything must be free, body, mind and your soul. Do accept loss, it is about letting things live their own life.

### prAna303 — 2013-07-09T16:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-89767>

**Ohm's law**

How about resistance? Do we get enough of it today?

If we never live a life with resistance we have a really bad conductor. We need it to build strong current. If we force feed ourself with energy in any form and do not force resistance we fade. All of the new stuff that should make life easy do exactly the opposite i think. We think that a smart human is the one that does most things 24/7 and own as much status thru that.

When we stopped to make things with our own power we let resistance out of our lifes. Everything we see as things in this spectra of life we live is made from electricity. Everything is information and memory, i so stongly belive.

We must get difference between things and build ourself strong again with that. As i said before, cold must meet warm, light against darkness, dry versus wet, high and low. I like to solve all my personal problems with a mindset that always goes back to nature as far as possible, one question is how would i do if i was totaly wild? Then act on that mindset, sure it can hurt but it is a sweet pain (resistance) that i love when i can get past it. Nowdays i never tend to stop in my thinking for more than a day, it is so more easy when i finaly let myself be natural and act in a natural place. Sure, i take a stop at my local cafÃ© everyday but im in the wild for more than 6 hours per day so i feel that time like a difference not a hellish place.

Some of the pieces are really falling into place thanks to above and it is FREE... I think all information is free by nature.

### Linz — 2013-07-30T10:59:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91165>

[www.telegraph.co.uk/earth/10176217/The-underground-forests-that-are-bringing-deserts-to-life.html](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/earth/10176217/The-underground-forests-that-are-bringing-deserts-to-life.html)

### prAna303 — 2013-07-30T11:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91167>

The reset.

Have mother earth finally started to give up OR is she rebooting?

Over the last three years i have seen some radical changes in nature and this summer it is insane. I know that every season changes from year to year but this is not the same. Since i almost live in the wild i spend many hours there and live with the seasons so small changes really make big differences for me.

It started with a winter that was really long, dark and cold. Snow to my breast many times. The spring came and exploded in just a couple of weeks so the summer came around may and early june. Ok for these small variations BUT what came next is wicked.

\- The trees have grown really big, in three years more that ten before.
\- The birds are everywhere, all the time. At the sea the seagulls are extremely alive.
\- The trees have much bigger leafs.
\- The berries are everywhere AND bigger that ever.
\- The silence in the woods are growing bigger, thanks to the leafs but even more energy is lurking.

So i spoke to a former coworker about his business and the are having some hard and rough times BECAUSE these changes, do not think he see why but in the mobilephone business the are loosing the connectivity in the wild. They used to get good connections in the wild before but not anymore. Reason? They see that leafs shadow the signal and the are starting to give up the fight against the fast growing birch.

Everywhere i go on regular roads in my surroundings the trees and plants are starting to grow up thru the asphalt, just like when i was a little kid.

So is finally the energy we cannot see taking over and feeding the nature and killing us?

### prAna303 — 2013-08-05T10:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91542>

Nomad

When you loose the connection to ground we loose our past, future and most of all now.

Modern life has always been odd to me, from early years i loved to camp in the woods that surrounded our house, go fishing in the sea next to us and made snowcaves in the winter. I was very much like our dogs, hated to be inside even though we lived in a house made of stone and wood.

This is why all of my former friends are sick today, some played with me but often i was alone. They never felt the same connection like i did and do. Meet up with a couple of them some time ago, everyone is sick today. This is sad and really annoying to me. But they stopped acting like a animal and grew "normal" like their mother and father. They own all the things you should have according to the social norm today, house, car, three kids, jacuzzi and so on. What do i own of these things? None, sure i have a lot of things, quite weird and crazy things but i did not do the norm, the standard. Ok, i do not have a family in traditional way, no wife, no kids, no house but i choosen freedom everyday, that means that i DO interact with people but i never feed on them or feed them.

Why nomad?

I think that we are on this place to be movers... When we stopped living like our history the system started to act weird. The software is not compatible with the hardware anymore. I tried this time after time when i travel with others in the wild, by day two or so they start to go on autopilot and begin to THINK again. We are not meant to life on one spot 24/7, we must travel with the world around us. This is nothing new but if you start to do some small things like this, we disconnect the modern "cat on skateboard on youtube" thing and see what others planted in our DNA.

I always said that life is emotion from motion. This is how we should work, making and delivering energy as the component we are in this universe. When we stopped being that part in the motherboard the whole computer went off sync. We need to do the part that we should do, being that energy transfer that almost nobody does today. When we disconnected BIG time in the end of 1978 everything started to tilt.

Is this why we have big climate problems? I think it is a quite big reason, we are not electrical beings anymore. We are giant capacitors that is growing from day to day because we only feed it. What would happend if we over night shifted from houses to live on the ground again? Instead of being a big problem for earth, would we be part of it again? Who knows...

When we move in elements we become of resonance again.

Now i am going to the sea, a cabin with almost nothing. Doing my freediving, my running, my blueberry eating and start to change for the coming winter. Four days of change in my body, just like my ancestors (the swedish lapps) did. Soon the foods in nature starts to rotten and left is my dried fish and fats. The sea is getting colder now, it calls for me.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-06T05:51:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91606>

"Primitive peoples, for example, have something in common with very small children. They are beautiful, spontaneous, natural, but utterly unaware of what they are; they don't have any awareness. They live joyously, but their joy is unconscious. First they have to lose it. They have to become civilize, educated, knowledgeable; they have to become a culture, a civilization, a religion. They have to lose all their spontaneity, they have to forget all about their essence, and then suddenly one day they start missing it. It is bound to happen." - Osho, Pharmacy for the Soul, page 51.

That little excerpt just reminded me of the above comment from Peter (if you don't mind me referring to you by name). I think this is what is happening to us right now. Dr. Kruse is laying out a 'modern way' to connect with our past(s) - our _primordial_ past(s). Hence the blogs of the primal intuition/sense and what not. experience tells you what to do and confidence allows you to do it.......but nature loves the courage that impossible brings to us.......She strives for survival by creating order from abject chaos........she hates an average or moderate approach......in fact she naturally selects away from it........so the lesson? ### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-08T21:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91850>

There are three legs in life that never change.........these three things life is based upon.......called natures laws. The photoelectric effect, water, and magnetism. Earth has all three.......Mars has 2/3 and is a dead dessert...........learn that lesson and use it in your experience. The only thing I base my decisions on now is nature's laws......that is how I guide my healthcare decisions......it is my ultimate straight edge.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-08T21:46:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-91852>

What if?

Everything and everyone is energy and we get sick, overfeed ourself with foods BUT if the problem is much bigger?

In my head this idea why my friends get sick and performing at a low level has been building up for years... Many of us shared the same childhood and foods but along the line we got separated. I followed a different path and have seen with other eyes what happened to them. No diet works, no workout is giving them pleasure anymore and all they think is food in and energy out. My sister is ill but will never change even though she sees the problem (in a vague fashion).

Energy is shapeshifting 24/7 and so are we. Everything we put our bodies minds and our whole being thru gives, steals and balance our energy. This is why food is not the biggest problem today i think, many agrees with me but then we loose our treads.

I have always said that if we do not calculate the energy we force ourself thru then food is the smallest part. If you give your senses no chance to take in and transform, what matters? Like Jack said many is depleted of energy today and i agree, we must use our senses that works like antennas and not dress up in GoreTex and iPhones. If someone should start to count on how much energy we loose and gain thru modern technology we would be scared i guess. I talked to a friend that is a quantum specialist about this and he said i was insane to think of life like this, this gave me the sign that i have found something. When a expert gets so angry that he cannot speak, we hit the nail i think.

Dressed up with all the clothes our skin does not work in unison with the magnetic fields around us, we compensate with food.

When natural food smells odd (natural) we eat cyberfood. Our smelling stops to work and smell is vibrations.

If we do not stress our senses we do not gain energy and like many other animals to get vibrations they have some parts filled with fat. So perhaps the real reason for obesity is loss of input. When we separate our bodies from life, the response is getting fat and big to simply get some natural signals. This means that the things we today call culture and arts with natural connection with the audience is more vital than ever. Irony is that in my country we tend to shut down the performing arts.

I have ranted about arts a long time here but i find the reason more and more exiting now.

If your are hungry for foods, drinks, candy or some time on your iPad. Give yourself some natural vibrations instead, when longing for sweets, listen to Brahms played thru some real speakers or live. Food is like Jack said so many times, backup, a UPS ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uninterruptible\_power\_supply](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uninterruptible_power_supply) to a optimal lifeform.

Ok i do not say that i have it all right but so much of my ideas are falling into the right spots the more i look around. We need to go back in mindset and get excited about nature again, what we found out before must be observed again AND again. We cannot stop looking around us and sit down and accept that the rules are correct, they were perhaps when someone dictated them at that exact moment BUT everything changes forever until it changes into another energy form that we cannot see.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-13T18:46:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-92383>

Remake your living.

Since this place is the only place i can share thoughts about life i share my view on living...

Have your ever felt that you spend more time on decorating your home than actually live your own life? Do you put a serious amount of your time buying and changing the stuff that surrounds you? Do you clean your home every single day?

So many questions on something i see many do. Why are we so stuck in this kind of acting? Ok, many will think that if you go minimalistic everything will be grand, so neat and clean but i tend to go the other way around. I own weird amount of thing but the do not own me, they are tools to do funny things, art, comfort, pain and most of all memories. Memories, is not life just a giant memory that slowly fade and by doing many things wrong we die before we can realize how blessed we are. For me the obsession of perfect homes is just as great illness as overweight, stress or other bad things...

I am lucky that i have made my own world today, nobody can tell me how i LIVE my life but everyone who is open in their mind is welcome. The funny thing is that almost everyone loves to be a guest in my home, some say it is like a museum, some a junkyard but almost everyone think it is ME. In sweden we call them originals, the ones that live deep in the woods, own junk, eat cat food and so on, but what we have in common is that life is great.

How do i see my home? Think basecamp... I tend to spend more and more of my time in the elements all year around. I often sleep in the woods because i sometimes feel trapped in the stressed modern world. I have a place where my gears to do adventures can be secured, a place to warm up if i need to, a place to repair myself IF nature is to harsh on me. The thing is, i travel a lot not by car, plane, boats or something like that, just by my feet and hand and the rest of my body. The more i perfect this way of living the more i love this freedom.

Bottom line, do not be afraid of owning things just do not let them own you...

### prAna303 — 2013-08-19T21:32:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-93134>

Are we the grid?

Many talk about our need of mother earth and nature but how many talks about that we are needed also. Can it be that she needs us more than ever?

When we examine animals, plants and all the other things that live in the place called nature, we seek their function and see that this needs that and so on, what happened to the question about our function? Many talks about that we should walk this earth and simply fart to be part of the eco system, some say that we are simply containers for bacteria but if...

The whole universe is just some kind of resonating electric thing. All that is around us is energy and so are we but perhaps we have a more complex part in this. If earth during some period needed something that could spread energy in a controlled way just because the energy from the sun got to much, the whole system was failing and with resonance we evolved into something that looks like us. Do not now if this idea is real or made up by someone else but it can explain some weird things about us.

The romantic part of life is just a way to explain in wonderful words something that is not that sexy, we as humans are just a piece of advanced electronics that today got to smart and stopped doing our thing.

This weird idea of mine explains in my weird world why we have a giant brain, walks on two feet and spread out over the whole planet today. This explains why we feel connection with others and why we sleep (and should) at night and walk the earth at day. Could this be a reason why the climate is going haywire? We are the problem but not just in the way of polluting everything, we are not doing our part of the original work and now we pay the price by changing the rules to something that we cannot be part of.

This idea have been nagging in my mind for years and when i spill some drops of it people goes crazy, we are smarter than that. How could we be a function in universe? But why do we feel so good when we put our self in the nature, why do we feel a purpose when we look at the sunrise, why do we dream weird dreams? Is it simply that easy that when we give some information to our brain we start to act in ancient ways because we have a function in universe, a small brick that is needed. Why do we feel so great when we get cold? How can we feel love? How can we travel long distances by foot?

I think it is energy distribution in the highest class... We cannot see it but we know it deep inside. We need to collect and share with others because it is our purpose in life so earth can function at it highest level.

Many do not like this idea, this electric way of being but remember how we from early days have described life with words and letters.

\- Get down to earth
\- Sit down and resonate
\- Guides to yoga
\- Massage and zone therapy
\- Manuals (bible and so on)

We put physics into our talks but forget that they mean something else than just words, we let other touch us and we feel electric, we have religion that looks a lot like information sharing if you change the theme to physics. The list goes on and on.

What i mean with all of my rants is that, like Jack said "food is just a small part of it" and i strongly agree with him. We have something much more complex and at the same time very simple about our meaning of life and when we isolate us from all this we become a missing link in a bad way.

This applies to me in a big way but i think we all need to think again, again and again on the real meaning of life. Mine is not yours so i cannot point in one direction and say it is right, i can only follow my own gut and do my thing and resonate with people that see the change in me. Manuals can not be made universal, we must seek our own manual.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-07T10:33:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-95121>

End of summer... For sure this one goes to history.

The autumnal equinox is coming and it is time to let go once again, the last few days i will simply look back and enjoy every single memory because this summer has been the number one, for sure. The trees are letting go already, leaves on the ground.

So many small things that changed this summer, for good and bad. The big thing is that you can not live without one or the other. In a romantic way this has been just like the summer you used to have as a child, time really is up to you and how you use it. Not a single day without the sea, in and out of the water for hours with a beginning in the early april. One beauty of this is that the water have never been this clear before, places where i used to see for a couple of meters i can now see for ten. The trails into the wild has been mine this year, big time. The weird thing is that nobody else is around, i know why but i still hope that people will find their call once again. I found so many places that resonates with me, places with weird energy where i can tap in and load up, and let go.

The search for a place to live on is now thrown away... I have it all right outside my door. My home is nowdays like a storage for my gears to go into the wild. I do not need to buy a home because the nature is free, i take my space where i feel good and use it. At the same time i feel more and more fed up with modern iTech world, i have lived this life many years ago so i do understand the weird feelings you get when you have the latest gears, latest apps and being ahead of the others in using it. The big irony for me now is that all of this is not hi-tech anymore. Been said about me is that i perhaps is 10 years ahead or maybe 100 behind. I hope i live like many others will do in ten years. For me today the best modern human is the one who is tapping into the old thoughts and making them new. Just the way we stopped examining the nature today is so weird for me, everything is changing so why should not nature and its laws change. This summer is for sure a big changer for me in that way of thinking.

I long for the winter. I need the cold now...

The downside is what happends around me... Like i said sometimes, i work with humans with problems with their posture. And boy how many strange stories i have now. On the other side i have the so called healthy ones that really crashed this summer, just like Jack said so many times. I see illness in athletes big time now, even some of them died this spring and nobody has a clue why... I think most of us in this forum does. Almost everyone in my near sphere that lives "normal" lives is going down like a 747 now... This is so sad in one way because it makes one feel alone BUT in some places people come to me and ask why i do not suffer as bad. All of this comes down to another thing.

The Master, how many have not seen an film about some guru, master of martial arts and felt resonance. Even if it is a nerdy movie without any realism but it for sure feels good. This is what i feel is missing now, where are the masters? The onces you travel to and sit and listen and do practice for hours, days and years to become the new one. Our stories is not chared in this manner anymore and here we loose so much. I think that the people who try to learn us things today is so out of sync that we really do not connect with them. Simply, walk the talk. I see this in sports many times now, because i do get to meet them live, top athletes and coaches and i see only problems. I would not be able to take advice from a coach that has every kind of illness, fatigue. Today athletes listen to them and follow their rules 100% and crash and wonder why...

This fall is going to be critical for many in my nearest sphere, for sure. I hope that people start to listen but many will not.

### Inger — 2013-09-07T17:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-95135>

Peter I love the winter too. I think here in Stavanger / Norway there will be little to no snow. I already know I will miss it! Maybe I'll stay until winter maybe not. But I totally share your passion for the real winter.

You know what I feel. There is so freaking many needs.... for love. I sometimes feel like a dried out raisin..LOL I crave the wilderness, just to be there, alone. I do at times, and that is when I fill up my energy. Guess what, behind/around my tent is a "forest", my tent is in my sisters backyard under a hazelnut tree. There is a path going through it. But no one is ever there. I go into that beautiful place every morning, and it is pure magic. I wonder why there are no one else as there are many houses around, and people have dogs they need to walk... but seems they use the roads.. not the magic forest? So I have it all alone. Weird. But I appreciate the loneliness very much.

IDK. The modern world is so full of noise. huh. It is annoying. In the wild nature there is only beauty... the noise is gone. I bet all the "stuff" and EMF makes the noise.
I am addicted to my tent now Peter.. How are you sleeping outside when it is raining? It is raining a lot here, so I kind of need that tent. But I have the doors wide open all night long. I really need to feel the wind on my skin. I have many friends in my tent now. Lots of spiders and stuff..lol I do not care one bit!

### prAna303 — 2013-09-08T21:00:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-95256>

> I am addicted to my tent now Peter.. How are you sleeping outside when it is raining?

This summer has been without any rain in the nights but i often seek up places with alot of firs or something that look like a cave. Since i do climb i know anout places with overhanging roofs so thats a good place.

I can sense the winter is coming, forest is opening up and the mountains roars when the wind is blowing... I feel a deep tingling sensation every time this change is near. The sea is changing also, down to 10C this evening, wonderful. Some friends came along before they travel to Russia to hike and they could not stand the water today, they wear wetsuits and i surfing trunks, haha.

I feel like a child, i long for the first snowflakes that melts on my skin.
