### prAna303 — 2013-09-09T12:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-95335>

The noble art of letting go.

Today i celebrate...

Last night it came to my attention that someone i love deeply, but cannot be with me, found someone to love. This has been nagging in my mind for some time now because i think she deserve to be all the things she needs to. It feels so good to know that she can share herself with someone that really likes her.

She will always be the one that rumbled my world and she walked a path much like mine but just not at the same timing as me. I do not know if i ever meet her again but this little thing makes it complete... It feels like letting a wild animal out into freedom after it has been caged up.

So, i am free in every aspect from now...

Thinking about that wine from another thread now but it has to be something cheaper...

### prAna303 — 2013-09-09T20:19:37+0300

But also, the world is out of sync big time so why should not nature act crazy.

> "One need only shut oneself in a closet and begin to think of the fact of oneâ€™s being there, of oneâ€™s queer bodily shape in the darkness (a thing to make children scream at, as Stevenson says), of oneâ€™s fantastic character and all, to have the wonder steal over the detail as much as over the general fact of being, and to see that it is only familiarity that blunts it. Not only that anything should be, but that this very thing should be, is mysterious! Philosophy stares, but brings no reasoned solution, for from nothing to being there is no logical bridge."

### prAna303 — 2013-11-11T09:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100406>

The soundtrack of our lifes.

Do we listen to music anymore?

Today everything has a theme, go to a store and you get music, stand in line waiting and you get music, see a movie you get music but what is really coming thru? We as humans have been using music for many things during our lifetimes, there is music for pleasure, music for healing, music for connecting and so on but what has happened today? Everyone uses digital music only, with that i mean that we use technology not only to make it but to mix it, record it and publish it. Is this also a big clue to why we are suffering today?

Today music is decoded into a digital stream that gets turned back into waves thru technology. It is broken into small pieces, particles that is non information unless you got the key to put it back again. The big problem is that you do not get clean waves. When we listen to music produced and published the new way we feed ourself with chaos that looks like tones but is not, just like a program that looks good but in reality is a virus. The other problem is also that we consume it on gadgets and by using earphones.

Music is in my opinion not only arts, it is food for your unit called human.

But what is missing today? Simply the pure form of resonance... A digital wave will not resonate with you, it is like fastfood for you, it looks like real but is not. And together with all the chaos we add into our system we can not get clean noice inside us, we need tunes to make our bodies and minds sync together, lower the entropy in the unit.

I think we all have moments that we connect with music in all kind of ways, laughing, crying and just feeling good. The things is that if you are of age of 30 and above you can remember music how it was when you hear a digital tune but nothing more. The problem is with younger that is born and feed with digital music, they do not remember those things, i think they should because of genetics but those are in another mode for them.

What i do. I found that the only way to make this ancient way of feeding us is to go analog. Listen to live music, made by humans and their instruments, getting all tones into my body, not only ears FEEDS me. I started a month ago to listen to vinyl recordings again and by making it a test i found that it makes me grow. It expands my mind and my body, makes me travel... To listen and get a room that is bigger than the one i am sitting in is a huge mindblowing thing.

So by this i mean that we must get music back into our lifes... Not the easy one on spotify, the real one with real tones.

### caroline — 2013-11-11T10:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100407>

I wonder if this is why I have never had an ipod etc. I love music and dance but I have never been able to relate to having earbuds in my ears and getting my music that way. I totally want to be in the middle of it and absorbing it on every level so that it pushes everything else out of my mind. I want to be free to enjoy the physical and the auditory and the visual at the same time ....to be totally encompassed thru and thru by the experience and in the midst of others doing the same....

I am still trying to understand why I started crying when a young man started to play and sing The Tennessee waltz. Obviously it struck a chord deep within - and it still hasn't surfaced.....

### caroline — 2013-11-11T11:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100409>

I have just been reading thru this thread again..... I know I should be reading mind bending stuff ......but this entire thread is simply gorgeous...... actually some pretty mind bending/ beautiful/ life altering stuff here.....

One of these days I am going to print this whole thread off and save it so I can keep all this .... for me...to feed my spirit.

The lovely thing is - so many contributed beautiful thoughts from much the same, yet different, perspectives ....and heading in the same direction... to infinity and beyond!

### prAna303 — 2013-11-11T11:58:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100411>

> I wonder if this is why I have never had an ipod etc. I love music and dance but I have never been able to relate to having earbuds in my ears and getting my music that way. I totally want to be in the middle of it and absorbing it on every level so that it pushes everything else out of my mind. Just as WiFi and other human made frequencies alters our water and proteins, the same can natural tones. When we make analog digital we sample the waves and make copies that acts like waves but in a bad way, think about a great actor vs a bad one. When these hits us they make us rise entropy within ourself, the system gets an error because of the mixed message. On the other hand, real analog waves makes the parts of us resonate in different ways and depending on their own frequency, in the whole the effect is felt in the whole unit.

This is why dance is complex, like looking at quantum theories live and direct, the same if you dance yourself.

Many have had moments when time stood still, i get them when i move in harmony with the environment. Music can open doors to the modern human that had been shut for many years, in some crazy way i think we all can make a sort of timetravel when we listen to music that tap into hidden parts of our minds. This is because i think we must rethink the concept of time and how we look, live and act with it. Something inside of us say things that we must start to listen to now, hidden from the gadget world we have a higher level and it is simply waiting on us, free to use when we can start to think new again, without dogmas.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-15T11:42:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100768>

I settled at Cold Mountain long ago,
Already it seems like years and years.
Freely drifting, I prowl the woods and streams
And linger watching things themselves.
Men don't get this far into the mountains,
White clouds gather and billow.
Thin grass does for a mattress,
The blue sky makes a good quilt.
Happy with a stone underhead
Let heaven and earth go about their changes.

Quote: Han-Shan

### caroline — 2013-11-15T23:20:23+0200

### prAna303 — 2013-11-16T10:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100827>

> Has that always been your mindset? even as a little boy? what about your parents? siblings? or were you always an outlier?

Yes, even before i knew what a mindset is... I remember some things from my early years that then looked odd but today is perfectly natural. I always lived with no rules from my parents, i was free to do what ever i wanted. If i fell and got hurt, good lesson my parents would say. I have been cold adapted my whole life i would say, when i was a young kid i always played in the snow in just some jeans and a shirt, no hat, no gloves when other kids where dressed up like a "michelin man".

I lived by the sea and played in the sea the whole days, my first CT was when i almost drowned during a winter, i was six years and went thru the ice, alone. Good lesson that gave me more life afterwards, a game changer i feel today.

My sister is quite the opposite of me, different dads. Here i today see what history and how one acts in the elements make big difference. I have always been quick to try new stuff, like computers when i was eleven (early then), working in high tech worlds but at the same time have one leg in the cold. My sister is living the "normal" life, with the "normal" problems, overweight and some deep problems with her brain, do not remember the name of the problem. So when you would see us together we are like yin and yang. Parents just living a quite normal life today (EMF hell).

I always had a much deeper connection to the nature than the people around me, it has been and still is my playground...

This has always put me in places where i often is alone, company only works with the people that has some kind of connection to the elements. Like i said before, i find my tribes in climbers, surfers, freedivers, skiers and hobos. The big thing is that i only truly love people with a body and mind, a complete living unit and that is becoming a minor part of the society today. Look around and you see the big parts of the race humans becoming something with a gadget in their hands and earphones in their ears. This makes me even more motivated to be a change, when everyones goes digital i go analog. I know that i do the right thing because my labs say so and my unit also say so. So many clues today that becomes clear with all this quantum theories we learn about, things i done with a feeling in the gut.

I deeply think that we all must go out and change the world we live in, we must go back and at the same time head forward in a different path than the big crowd do today. Only when people see what we do and get interested in that change they will start to think for them self and stop listening to experts of 2013.

### caroline — 2013-11-17T01:16:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100871>

As I was out walking today in the rain...I was thinking about you. It is amazing how my gait has changed. Much more fluid and natural and connected to the ground. ### prAna303 — 2013-11-17T10:36:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100888>

> He said when we put down our thoughts - we become more 3D.

Yes i agree with this one to. But for me i can feel a great difference between writing on a computer vs writing in a book. When i go analog i first sit for a while, think, drink some coffee, look at people. Then after a while i go into a state where i write how i think or feel right there, the thing is that mind must be in sync with my hand to do the writing, i must do my errors in mind before i write, once it is on paper i cannot erase those words. This is why i think it is vital to write for us humans, everyone has a need to get mind and body in sync and writing is just one way of doing it. When i go digital, things can go a little bit to fast and words can be sent away without the proper thoughts... Misunderstandings can be done...

So for me the analog way is always 3D and even more dimensions.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-17T10:45:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-100889>

> I finally see that our gaieties are a valid indication of who we are. That should, of course, be gait! .... But I like how it spell corrected. My ipad knows better!

I see gait as a big clue to how humans really feel and live their lifes. I use it in my work where i try to help persons with back pains, bad feets and other problems. Many others use computers and software to correct feets today but for me that is so wrong. They look at the feet and say that it is not working when you must look at the whole person and when i do my kind of reading of the body i look for the details that makes the music sound bad. When someone is walking without any kind of soundtrack i see small errors in the music, a hip that is out of sync with the shoulders and so on...

Today i know, thanks to Jacks way of teaching us Quantum Physics why gait and posture is a good sign if someone is loosing energy to the world around us. When we get fried today with technology we loose the sublime systems first, we loose the ability to be filled with energy and move without errors. So today i look deeper into the humans with problems because i feel that a sore hip can be caused by a trauma to the head, shoulder or something else.

Like many others said, look at the human with problems also, not only the problems in that human.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-19T18:24:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-101032>

When going back is a quantum leap.

For some time now i have pushed myself even further, the people around me is getting really sick and i do not want to catch their reality.

Many things in my life is backward, therefor the name of the thread... So by getting the science by the teaching of Jack i can put my instict into different theories and that is good for me today, the ones that always thought of me as crazy are now asking questions. The thing is today that i have been asked to present my way of health, by a couple of groups / media and others and i stand with many questions... As always i have to go deep into nature to clear this matter now. So many thoughts inside me now, should i, could i and would i?

My latest old-fashioned way of living is eating wild... Some of you know that i do eat quite a strict menu, raw seafoods and fats, mainly because the winter is here. The next thing for me was simply, eating outside, grounded and what a leap it has been...

The first weeks, nothing special about is, think i am quite used to natural fields but now, WOW... It is like taking some crazy drug, i feel no things in my gut but my whole body is vibrant. When sitting in the grass i feel just like i live in a bubble, like a field that covers me, for sure i sound like a drug abuser... So many details in my life is becoming extra clear now, it goes hand in hand with my spruce showers, made myself a little hut of spruce where i rest at least for one hour a day.

The magic is also when i go to the sea now, have always been good at handling the cold in waters but now... Doing my statics in 4C water is just electric and i try to make others understand this... The elements is a part of us.

Do not know if i do computers for some time again, must make more connections to nature.

Therefor i am off to the sea again, just reporting that going back IS possible and there are some great things about doing so...

### prAna303 — 2013-11-21T21:19:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-101274>

Virus from a different world.

Are we going thru the world today with something way bigger affecting us than many can phantom? Is it a side to how the modern technology create errors in us but not in the usage but what the information from gadgets mean?

Is it possible in a scary way that a computer virus could transfer to us?

Why i have thought about this for many days now is the way each little part slowly alter our reality and how we can miss things when we do not lift every little rock and look under it.

My thought is that everything modern today is digital, simply put 1 and 0. This is a very simple sampling of what the world is made of, a simulation if you put it that way. I talked alot about analog vs digital for some time and this is what binds it all together, at least for now. Us humans have a big quantum computer inside our skull, the brain. This piece of art is working in quantum fashion and in the computer world that would mean that it can handle 1 or 0, but also 1 and 0 at the same time. This would mean that information reaching us must be analog, full of alternations, not sampled.

So if you use a computer, phone, pad or something digital AND at the same time have it WiFi, 4G or even started it opens up parts of our system that allows for bad information to get into us. We have seen that barriers in the brain stops it function with this particle storm. This would mean that natural defence systems stops and can not act because our body lets the digital errors inside of us. When the brain that can use 1 or 0 or 1 and 0 at the same time get simple information it loose the details in the quantum part. This could then mean that the system inside us goes into chaos, we loose the quantum information that our brain needs to calculate the world.

When this is the reality, would not weird errors show up just like that? We started a computer boom around 1978, what else have started at that time? Extreme usage of plastic on our feets (nike), isolation in our clothes (gore tex), tar on roads, houses with electrical heating... Diabetes started to spread in sweden, some mental illness and also something that many can not find a solution to AIDS.

Could all this modern illnes be awaken and produced inside us with a simple binary code? The complex information that makes our brain do magic gets only FASTFOOD like information. Is this also a part of why nature heals us so quick, the gigantic information load that a simple spruce gives our system is not of this digital world, but instead of going into chaos it resonate with us.

What is your defence against this? If it is possible, can this be a small part of what hurts us? Can many rules be out of order today with all WiFi, 4G and gadgets, i do think so...

### prAna303 — 2013-12-08T11:20:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-102819>

Am i living in a box?

I think it is quite disturbing to live inside a box. Now i do not mean the way we use this when we should think outside the box but the way we build things.

When i explore nature i never ever see the static formation of box, okey it can be quite squared but never locked up into boxes. Can this way of building things be one of the big errors of modern time?

If you place yourself in a natural place everything is dynamic and frequencies can bounce of into millions of ways. Trees never stand still, sea is always moving and wind can pass thru. When we instead place ourself in a world of boxes (houses), lined up into squared lines (town) we would get weird bouncing frequencies. Think of it as this, in nature you get resonace, free waves that do not cloth together in a bad way. Meanwhile in town you get so much errors in that sound, gets into a error, maximum chaos. Would this make us errorlike also? Is modern illness just waves that get stuck in us, we become errors in a error world.

It started when i was quite young, i hated corners in my home. I did even make something i called corner killers, i made them rounded instead. It felt better to my mind, rounded made the acoustics more natural.

Since i really do not like to clean my home i saw something else, i remember this clearly now. Dust did not cling on to the corners anymore. Is dust just possible in man made world? How often do nature get messed up with dust and other crap? This would mean that dust is something that is a result of our ways of being, it is near death stuff, maximal entropy.

So squared is out of fashion for me...

### prAna303 — 2013-12-10T12:18:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-103049>

Catching a cold...

It is in the news every day now in Sweden. Among my friends, it is their reality every day it seems. Many have them for months now and whatever they do they can not get cured. But is cure the right way?

Imagine that cold is not about any virus, the virus is the product of the state before, and virus maybe just a name for something bigger.

Perhaps the feeling of "getting a cold" is information for you to act upon. The unit "you" is starting some old response that say that something is missing... Energy is lost, warning! Why do the symptom start in our head? Because the head contains the energy device called brain, and that would be a first to go when energy is on the downside. We get a swelling in throat and ears and that is a sign to act upon. The energy problem can be somewhere else but head warns first. If we ignore that scream for energy and enjoy company in a room, with others... Would the chaos in you affect others? Is the energy level in total starting to fall which makes it harder for the others to feed on it. You get a global energy crisis in that local spot.

You get warm (fever), you must chill your semiconductors but what do modern human do? Fleece hat on and down in bed with extra blanket... Of course drink a cup of tea and honey, warm... Yummie! When you should instead act on the warning and over feed yourself with nature... Sun in the summer, water all year around and cold in the winter... Natural magnetism for a day says the modern doctor.

So is the flu and the cold just information getting lost? Information in form of energy...

### prAna303 — 2014-01-28T20:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-107196>

Obsession with manuals.

Are we becoming less creative? Do you ask if the "rules" applies to you own world?

The shift i felt for many years now has started to speed up, we make our world simple but in a stupid way. The last couple of months i seen this more and more, perhaps because i been so much in nature and little in the normal world... Everywhere we have big screens with messages pumping out, simple guidelines to almost everything.

Have been seeing that many nowdays can not fix a simple error such a door that is stuck and other things, what is happening?

I have been in some quite rude fights over the issue health, many tend to hook up with me in that subject but do not feel that easy after a while, i brake rules. When we live in a country as i do where rules says that my own house MUST be built on rules that say, it is not accepted with cold and drag inside, you will not get permitted to build that way, i feel more off than ever.

Had a fight with some yoga gurus that where angry because i did not see the meaning with Bikram yoga in Sweden in december, i said my yoga is to be free in the cold when it is cold outside.

A specialist in gait and posture called me insane because i looked for "the music" in how people move and from that could pinpoint that a girl had been in a car accident ten years ago while he only looked at the sore back and hip.

All of this is now my fuel to even more stand out and brake the rules, re write the manual. Just as in many things in semiconduction, the complete negative (for most thinkings) is often right...

Started to play music, have never ever been able to read notes but i do not like them either. I just play the black keys on the piano, sitting for hours just listening to how everything is building up inside me... Fixed an old guitar, just letting go.

Everything IS not made yet, everthing is not fixed in rules because we have forgotten a simple one. Just what you thought was right is not always that a second later, the world changes and with it, the rules and the manual.

### Josh — 2014-01-28T20:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-107198>

Prana is the basic unit of the whole of life. Rocks, trees, birds, man, God, everything is a
manifestation of prana, on different levels, on different understandings, on different integrations.
The same prana moves and manifests as millions, as many, but the basic unit is one.

Unless you come to know prana within yourself, you will not be able to know what God is. And if
you cannot know it within yourself, you cannot know it without, because within you it is so close.
That’s why Patanjali could know it five thousand years before Albert Einstein and company. Five
thousand years is a long time for science to come to understand it, but they were trying from the
outside. Patanjali dived deep into his own being; it was a subjective experience. And science has
been trying to know it objectively. If you want to know something objectively, you have taken a very
long route. That’s why so much delay. If you go within, you have found the shortest route to know
what it is.

Osho: Yoga the Alpha and the Omega vol 8 #9

All I can say after 25 years is "simple, not easy..." or one can go the other way from Chuang Tzu:

CHU’I THE DRAFTSMAN COULD DRAW MORE PERFECT CIRCLES FREEHAND THAN WITH A
COMPASS.

HIS FINGERS BROUGHT FORTH SPONTANEOUS FORMS FROM NOWHERE. HIS MIND WAS
MEANWHILE FREE AND WITHOUT CONCERN WITH WHAT HE WAS DOING.

NO APPLICATION WAS NEEDED, HIS MIND WAS PERFECTLY SIMPLE AND KNEW NO
OBSTACLE.

SO, WHEN THE SHOE FITS, THE FOOT IS FORGOTTEN, WHEN THE BELT FITS, THE BELLY
IS FORGOTTEN, WHEN THE HEART IS RIGHT, ’FOR’ AND ’AGAINST’ ARE FORGOTTEN.
NO DRIVES, NO COMPULSIONS, NO NEEDS, NO ATTRACTIONS: THEN YOUR AFFAIRS ARE
UNDER CONTROL. YOU ARE A FREE MAN.

EASY IS RIGHT. BEGIN RIGHT AND YOU ARE EASY. CONTINUE EASY AND YOU ARE RIGHT.
THE RIGHT WAY TO GO EASY IS TO FORGET THE RIGHT WAY AND FORGET THAT THE
GOING IS EASY.

What i really mean is that we must make something that matches our present field. We can not apply ancient methods to this wicked, weird world me made today. That is what i mean with the example of Bikram yoga in sweden in january, it is like Jacks banana in december.

What i DO feel we should do is look at the ancient thoughts and find somekind of mindset that makes us hungry for finding answers. Find the reason why they meditated, not how... ### prAna303 — 2014-01-30T12:40:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-107433>

Let me give a simple clue on how i think about life...

My name on this forum is "prAna303" not Peter as i was named in the analog world. The meaning of my screen name is two fold.

prAna as in: the Sanskrit word for "life force" and so on, guess you all know this.

303 as in: Roland TB-303, a bass synthesizer with built-in sequencer manufactured by the Roland Corporation from 1982 to 1984. A quite modern thing that resonates with my passion for nightclubs, being a former DJ and dancing at clubs for hours.

The mix of both ancient and modern, something that has a mindset for discovery and ability to go deep into one single thing for hours.

The funny thing with the TB-303 is also that when it was made it did not really behave like it should, it could go mayhem and that is what many makers of electronic music loved, many times we have tried to make new ones with exactly the same components, do not work. It simply sound like it should, not going and braking rules. The components today is much more precise and do not fail.

This is my way to deal with the world today, we can not make just new things of old that once worked, everything around us has changed and me as unit also.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-01T11:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-107697>

> Great post.
>
> Makes me wonder.. why are we so afraid of change?

My take on how we feel things has always been like this...

Using words to describe these things is not easy for me because i always felt and acted. Fear of change, like so many other things is based upon a lack of energy. When we have lost all natural energy in the unit you we become simplier, the system goes into a primal state. All the fine details we can see, feel, sense when we are in balance is gone now, we have lost energy to the environment in a bad way. If our minds is the last thing we got from nature, it is the first to go. Left over is some of the most simple actions that is vital for survival, hunger, fear and some other. Perhaps this is why we tend to get stuck also in feelings, like eating.

When we reach our potential with natural energy the whole system gets filled up from the bottom and in the end the brain gets everything back again so those feelings can get sorted out.

Every single time i feel a need for change i have gone into the wild for days. Out there every little doubt is cleared up, because i get so many factors of energy supply.

Same with the connection gut and brain, i feel this everytime i act in the elements. Fear is adjusted to proper actions, problems get solved easily when i do not need to think, just do.

This is why climbing for me is like natural chess, movement, problem solving and natural connection.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-05T20:57:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-108520>

Bits of a puzzle.

Is the world as we think, on a changing course?

Attacked by media every day that we are using up all of natures resources. Kids get robbed in first grade for their gadgets. Illnes has become the norm by society. Is it time that we shuffle the puzzle and start laying it again.

Writing at the local café today i could not resist to think a little wider, taking the bits and making the people i talked to a little stressed out, why is he thinking about this? Get a life, you know the drill.

Every system is within another system, it goes on and on. Every unit is depending on energy and with that energy we can explore the world, try to read the manual for that place and slowly make a bigger picture. I am a unit, in my environment, on the earth, in the whole universe and who know how long it goes on. The problem with the world is the same as the problem within me. If i loose energy, loose my memory, loose my thinking i also focus on my own local health also. If i can not be of the memory i came from i can not care for anything else than my own sphere. To go beyond, i need my memory to get sorted, loaded with good supply of power. I feel when i get into the wild my memory is put into the best state i can have. Every little thing feels like, nothing, just crisp and sharp. When all of the things that my memory is based upon i am drenched with energy to let myself go, i can not think about my own world anymore, i must create something bigger. The earth is in the same state as many today, loosing energy to the environment. This makes us look at special effects like global warming but is that also the same problem we have in viewing modern illness like diabetes also? We look at the physical effect, the "body" that shows errors not that we are part of bigger things.

Is this why many companies today is having problems? Entropy in the system is reaching a serious high. The people that work there is getting mentally ill not because of stress but of the simple fact that all natural energy is in total chaos, memory is lost.

When we do not have enough energy by nature we can not see that we are hurting others. We as humans have lost our minds, last in, first out, energy wise. Earth has lost us as a entropy factor. What else comes next?

This comes to a special place in my heart, the arts. When you have energy beyond your own needs you must create, think and just do something because YOU get out of your own way. This is what i feel is disturbing in arts today, we live by the rules of booms, muscle and advertising. Go to a movie and it is all about the machinery, not the details. If you have not had a boom within five minutes, people start to use their iPhones, we must have that BOOM to feel.

Bottom line, fill your physical body with pure energy, load the unit you to explore everything. Let go of yourself when time comes and start to create, do the things so many others have done before us but make it from our perspective, our reality now.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-108524>

beautiful Peter. I find it interesting how quickly things come together for me now that I am reconnecting. its as if my soul has been fired, and is burning bright. I can feel it, I can sense it. And what's amazing is nature can sense it too. hence why would deer walk past me no more than 5 feet from where I stand, stop and look at me before moving on. Wild animals come up to me - almost seeking. When I'm working from home and grabbing water or lunch - squirrels will come up to the window and "knock" to get my attn, if I don't notice them. Time moves both quickly and slowly within the day. I look up and the hours are gone, yet it still feels like its morning... my mind is sharp. I'm able to discern patterns that others aren't seeing, or if they see them they choose not to acknowledge them. work is easier to complete... I'm more focused. I swear I can see the moss growing on one of the large trees outside my window.

what's interesting is its not just happening to me. DH is having similar things occur - the other day at work a brown hawk landed on the banister next to his window - he took a picture and the hawk was looking right at him. he said it was as if it was looking into his soul.

the changes we make, small though they maybe ripple across our loved ones as well...

### prAna303 — 2014-02-05T21:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-108533>

> And what's amazing is nature can sense it too. hence why would deer walk past me no more than 5 feet from where I stand, stop and look at me before moving on.

That kind of connection is happening to me also. I have runned with foxes a couple of times, confronted by bears and elk without any kind of stress, just simple hellos.

When we get back to the memory we came from, we match in, we have a meaning.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-06T01:32:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-108609>

> Is this why many companies today is having problems? Entropy in the system is reaching a serious high. The people that work there is getting mentally ill not because of stress but of the simple fact that all natural energy is in total chaos, memory is lost.
>
> When we do not have enough energy by nature we can not see that we are hurting others. We as humans have lost our minds, last in, first out, energy wise. Earth has lost us as a entropy factor. What else comes next?
>
> This comes to a special place in my heart, the arts. When you have energy beyond your own needs you must create, think and just do something because YOU get out of your own way. This is what i feel is disturbing in arts today,

Thanks for writing this, Peter. I noticed this right around the turn of the century, about corporations. No one seemed to really be running the show. Running on momentum only. And the arts also seem to be lost (or smothered).

### athene noctua — 2014-02-09T01:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-109158>

i think a little entropy is good, it inserts novelty and change into an otherwise closed system. that's why it's a part of nature. but somehow, maybe, our exaggerated attempts to control nature and take all randomness out has actually backfired (as nature always wins out eventually) by creating interference patterns and a different kind of chaos, or too much. maybe this is just a result of our place in the natural system - if we take things out of balance, the pendulum will swing the other way in ways we don't expect.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-09T11:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-109246>

Today i celebrate...

The olympics is on and yesterday we as a nation took our first medal. Not the golden one but the next best.

Apart from that, i see the connection between human body, mind and spirit. This person is living in the same town as myself, been following her for a couple of years, seeing how she been building up and connecting dots. Meeting her many days in the woods with just that special glow. I told many of my friends that this is the year she would perform at the top again, and she does. As i said so many times before, there is something magical when you take the nature and put it back into us...

Thank you for the show last day, we will meet in the elements again.

Interesting article about winter Olympics and Norway.
[http://online.wsj.com/news/articles/SB10001424052702304450904579365011902608806?](http://online.wsj.com/news/articles/SB10001424052702304450904579365011902608806?)

Wondering if you agree w/ these statements in the article about Norway and Sweden?

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Still, most experts say the biggest reason behind Norway's success is the culture that propelled it atop the medal table from the outset. Norway's cities are relatively close to the wilderness, and children are encouraged to play outdoors even on the coldest days.

Neighboring Sweden, by contrast, has its major population centers farther from the wilderness, and the Swedes are more inclined to play indoor sports in the winter, such as tennis or hockey, rather than bundle up and go skiing.

Norway remains a largely agrarian society that places a large premium on being outside. A Norwegian concept called _friluftsliv_—enjoying outdoor life—has been studied in books and represents whole areas of study at universities.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Congrats to Sweden on the Gold.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-09T19:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-109294>

> Still, most experts say the biggest reason behind Norway's success is the culture that propelled it atop the medal table from the outset. Norway's cities are relatively close to the wilderness, and children are encouraged to play outdoors even on the coldest days.
>
> Neighboring Sweden, by contrast, has its major population centers farther from the wilderness, and the Swedes are more inclined to play indoor sports in the winter, such as tennis or hockey, rather than bundle up and go skiing.
>
> Norway remains a largely agrarian society that places a large premium on being outside. A Norwegian concept called _friluftsliv_—enjoying outdoor life—has been studied in books and represents whole areas of study at universities.

For sure this is some of the things that makes a difference.

Then, you should think about how we have four complete seasons here. The people in Norway has some of the worlds most dramatic nature that has shaped them for many years, still they live in it all the time.

Also, they are doing almost all their training outdoors like the article said, but this is rule here also.

Bottom line, i have some got some good look into the whole scene of skiing and just the simple thing as every kid skies and they have a ton of material to choose from. But, today, even they start to have problems because kids want to do more soccer and other group based sports OR spend time on cyberspace.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-13T20:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-109999>

Such a strange week...

Makes me think of the movie, The good, the bad and the ugly.

First, it came to my knowledge that someone that i know died the other day. We have been joking for the last couple of years that he should do as i, drop the crazy workload and enjoy the life. He did what i did many years ago, doing endless hours and the result i know today. Quite sad that he only a couple of weeks ago said, you have found your way, which i could do the same. Not that i am secured from illness but i intend to go the other way in this crazy world, trying to steer my ship thru it all.

Second, a dear friend has moved back, part time only but hey, good to see her again. We had a crazy summer a couple of years ago with just surfers attitude... Hanging at their house, climbing, surfing, just living, making the neighbors think we were crazy.

Third, another dear friend and her family is back in town... Had a cup of joe and a chat today. The great thing, she is living quite off grid and they have a little extra house by the treeline, guess what we discussed. This is a chance that i must take, we will talk more...

Fourth, a silver once again in the olympics. So great to see "the locals" do the magic... Like above, she is a star!

And to end it all...

[http://www.svtplay.se/video/1802216/min-ungdoms-var](http://www.svtplay.se/video/1802216/min-ungdoms-var)

congratulations on your Country's medals!

### prAna303 — 2014-02-14T11:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-110144>

Time and clocks.

Have the so smart human made some big off sets in our biology when we once created the clock?

Just the idea to measure our world with a standard norm, something that should apply to everything that lives makes me wonder. Has the idea of syncing our clocks so they show the same time for everyone bigger effects than we can imagine? I do not know but for me, eight years ago, time changed. I stopped working with time as a factor to rule my days, i stopped the hunting for more slots of personal time, i gave up.

Read the other day, some of googles ideas for the future. Almost feeling a weird pain in my gut, how they think that their datamining is going to be connected into our minds. This is how they want to controll it all, we humans have become slow, stupid and wrong, because we think today that the one with most controll over data is king. All of this is synced with global timing as they say, no one should have to wait to the next day to tap into the newest information, one global time. This makes more and more sense with their gadgets they make, lenses and so on.

When i stopped my time, i reversed it and went back to the day i took the wrong path. Just by stepping back to the day i was sixteen, picking up the lost road, letting everything else go, i found a meaning once again. Time has destroyed so much today!

Now i live by the elements, not the clock. Simply by taking away the constant syncing against my clocks, others and the thechnology i found my days endless. My nights so large and most of all, accepting that i do not live like all the others. I have found that time is one of the biggest factors to destroy a living unit, the time of modern thinking i mean. Just by putting away the things that steal time, not just the mental way but also the physical i feel like a young adult once again. Perhaps this is also why i always had girlfriends the same age even though i have became older (the normal way of thinking), we split up because i feel that we do not share the same passion anymore, just because time has changed them and not me?

So, with all global syncing... I hope many others begin to think about your own time and how you perceive it, i can not think that it has a start here and some highlights at special times. Simply start make your own pattern because we must start to think again.

### caroline — 2014-02-14T12:00:57+0200

so amazing and life affirming - do you do this Peter? - the free rock climbing?

I need to feel that energy - If I don't - I prefer to be by myself.....I love to feel the energy from the ocean and the trees and the rocks and the sky ....and best of all - the children.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-01T10:19:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112800>

Strange days.

Excuse me, where did my winter go? Okey, got some snow but soon it will be gone... Today it is raining and we have about +5C outside, last year it was -19C.

The gap between me and the rest of modern world seems to grow everyday. I have grown a rash against modern technology, simply feeling disturbed of how it is used instead of what we could use it for. Robots everywhere, pushing the message, love in an iPhone, the modern Tamagotchi. And at the same time, the more i stop using these things the more attention i get, it is like a magnet, you will make people react when you drop the technology.

The last couple of weeks i had this thing going on in my mind, had it way back before but it is like a dream you get back into, night after night. Since i stand on the edge between modern world and the old fashioned one i balance back and forth. I am thinking of becoming homeless. I start to think that to let go, drop my rules of what is normal perhaps comes to this. Today, the most disturbing for modern man is to have no home, no income, no insurance and so on. In my personal case, i see something much different, peace and simple things. For the last eight years i slowly built up my own strenght and pride in being what i am, a human on this earth, i am a product of nature. I strongly belive that we do not need any of this stuff, homes, cars, gears and fancy lifestyle.

I have been connecting thru many years with the outsiders, the surfers, the climbers, the big mountain skiers, the dancers and the living ones. They all share the same longing for nature and elements. I do the same and that voice i must follow.

Just some movie about another weirdo, just like myself.

[http://m.epictv.com/media/podcast/t...s-in-wild-sht-|-a-day-in-the-life-ep-2/273563](http://m.epictv.com/media/podcast/this-is-sketchy-andy-he-specializes-in-wild-sht-%7C-a-day-in-the-life-ep-2/273563)

### caroline — 2014-03-01T10:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112804>

Peter - I have never once thought you were a weirdo ..... to me - you are the one with the courage to live.

I love ..... "go out and find your family" .... that is what we have done here me thinks

### prAna303 — 2014-03-01T10:51:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112805>

The cemetery.

Dear friend of mine and i had one of our nightly rants at the café. We break down the modern world and how we many times do things backward.

Hospital vs cemetery, could it be more life giving to change the scenario? Instead of putting dead people in the ground should we make the switch and let the sick ones go to the cemetery?

I mean, hospitals are houses, often squared ones for logistic solutions. Rooms with tons of things that say "bing". Lights that makes your eyes sore, food that looks like crap, water? does it exist... Walls filled with nothing. Can a human with illness even have a chance to cure?

When you say, put a sick human back into the nature, most will cure, you get that angry eye today but i can not help to feel that this is a solution. Make the things that should be done, surgery and so on and then, as soon as possible, send them to the cemetery. Here the grass is green, trees are pround, benches to sit and think. Birds sing and it is quiet, no iPhones, because you pay resepect to the dead ones.

Can not help to feel that we bring most of our problems to ourself and dear ones by doing things the "normal" way.

### caroline — 2014-03-01T10:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112807>

I often think, when Dr. K. talks about his hospital and ICU - his microwave .... and he speaks of his extra ordinary risks and what he has to do to mitigate the damage ....... this is where we put the sickest people, our beloved - the ones that are the most vulnerable.......

and we go outside for a short walk and to breathe some fresh air .....and we leave our loved ones to be microwaved...

### prAna303 — 2014-03-01T11:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112812>

Yes. And how we put ourself into the hospital is also the same kind of microwave. Modern living, modern thinking (or not thinking), cars, the complete sum of todays "great" solutions. I often put a little play into my friends, i tell them to sketch a day in their life. You have 24 hours and now you start to paint out your whole day and night. Try to specify it at great detail with a notion IF you are outside. Not many spend more than half an hour a day outside, most only when going from house to car, car to work, work to car, car to shopping mall and so on.

The great thing is that i see also a growing pain in many that urges them to change...

[http://www.epictv.com/media/podcast...es-with-gillian-gibree-ep-1/267019?header\_b=1](http://www.epictv.com/media/podcast/meet-roxy-paddleboarder--fitness-model-%7C-ocean-minded-adventures-with-gillian-gibree-ep-1/267019?header_b=1)

### fitness@home — 2014-03-01T16:06:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112824>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=112805)
>
> Hospital vs cemetery, could it be more life giving to change the scenario? Instead of putting dead people in the ground should we make the switch and let the sick ones go to the cemetery?
>
> I mean, hospitals are houses, often squared ones for logistic solutions. Rooms with tons of things that say "bing". Lights that makes your eyes sore, food that looks like crap, water? does it exist... Walls filled with nothing. Can a human with illness even have a chance to cure?
> .

I haven't spent much time in or around hospitals. After spending 8+ hours overnight in an ER earlier this week, I can honestly say I don't know how they (doctors, nurses, staff) do it on a daily basis. I am _thankful_ they do though.

So many electronic devices, bright lights, etc... The water my husband was given to drink smelled to high heaven (tap water). The staff on duty that night looked so tired and it was only three hours into their shift.

Peter, you are right about cemeteries. They are always peaceful places, full of trees and nature.

### Inger — 2014-03-01T19:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-112858>

haha, no need to be sorry Peter, I never watch sport on tv or anywhere else.. I am just not interested enough... i have so much to do lol and I find it weird to look at someone being out skiing and making sport and just sit on the couch lol

I think the hard part/challenge might be to find a balance between the new and the old... and make a beautiful and magic reality full of love....

I admire people who work in health care too. I could not.. The surgeon that operated on hubs as he got shot.. he was one amazing man. Very strong personality... just someone special! I will never forget him for sure.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-07T20:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-114026>

The sacred human.

In sweden we have a rule that is called "fridlyst". This means that animals, plants and other living things is protected by the law. Every region has some unique species.

Is it time to "fridlysa" the human also? I see that a human that is protected by the law would not be feed man made foods, because we know that animals tend to get sick of it. We should not let the sacred ones be locked inside squared boxes, they are wild and must run free for a minimum of six hours a day. She must also have free access to the sun, water and earth whenever she feels a need. That also puts us in the world where we can not put leaches on them, we are not allowed to chain them up, that is torture, physical and mental. Our thoughts should not be forced upon them, they are sensible and must be able to read the environment in peace, they should also find their own mates, not us telling them who is right for them. It also means that you can not go mayhem in their place, walk with soft steps, not making any noice so you do not stress them. If the get close to you, slowly try to pet them, the will decide if you are worthy.

So, which side do you choose, the Sacred or the Zoo?

### prAna303 — 2014-04-16T23:20:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-121991>

Attraction.

How do two humans find each other? What lights that sparkle that we all search do deeply...

If you consider that we are all made from the stuff that the whole universe is built upon, we have all the same elements in our constructions. Together with this we have some quite spooky actions going on, something that we today must dig deeper into in my humble opinion... All these things ties all together, humans, animals, plants, stars and the milky way.

If a living unit has filled up with energy, good transfers, are we becoming a small universe by ourself? Do we indeed become something way bigger than just a name tag, a title, a husband... When someone is so strong in their way to get, collect, use and drop energy we become radiant, this is what makes actions over distance. Since you and me are of the same stuff, i would be able to make some of your particles start to resonate even if we have not touched, talked or even had a chance to connect. From a distance energy from me would make a small lift inside you, just that little heartbeat that made you squirm, because i became the sun in your universe.

You fill someone with that small lift, because the message you send is not out of our social behavior, it is the magic of the physics.

This is why everything is possible, when someone is so loaded you can not stand back, you suck up those pieces of energy, just not predictable. Here our smart thinking gets in our way, we do not suck it up and forward anymore... So lift your eyes up, leave that dumb iPhone home and go out, get loaded and start to load.

If and when you find that one who is lifting you, the art is to become that sun by yourself otherwise the magic ends...

So by this... How do you feel about being wild? That word that many think is so simple and not human. Wild is the one that for no reason hold back, just letting nature take you and let everykind of memory reset and start all over again. Wild is how you get your power, sorry the real thing is the shit. Even if many call me crazy, many say that it is easy for me to do that because...

The only thing i want is to live, alive... To be able to sparkle like a sun, chill you like the snow, move you like the wind and take you home like the mossy ground.

### prAna303 — 2014-06-10T20:29:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-130387>

Crossing a fine line.

> **The ability to change the power or energies of small proteins is the critical force that turned us from ape to human.** This very same protein can morph into a new protein and lead us to extinction. Today, it is the loss of power that is robbing us of our humanity. A power loss causes the prions to shift from an alpha helix to beta sheet due to electron/photon loss. The result is autoimmunity and neuro-degeneration of the proteins related to the MHC 1 gene.

The big news today is that the computers finally broke that fine line of perfection. It was so well set up that it fooled humans in the classical turing test.

I can not help to think that perhaps we are the missing piece nowdays, not just the computers that has become smart. When i was in town today i thought a lot about this by observing how we interact, live and behave.

The people that was fooled by a computer, could this be by the simple rule that we are loosing so much energy today that we have lowered our own levels? Have we become digital to a degree now that we are equal to computers? All of this makes me also think about some of the things we talked about here, the connected world, the online world that everyone crave and base their lining days on... What will this lead to, when we can deeply belive what a computer tells us, what comes next? Is googles vision already here.

This makes my own choices so much clearer, i feel not joy about this news, i feel that i must change my world around me because this is killing the humans i love.

[http://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-ne...er-program-convinces-judges-its-human-n125786](http://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/turing-test-computer-program-convinces-judges-its-human-n125786)

Attraction, part two.

Is it wrong to say that someone can steal energy from you?

Had many moments of see you later during past weeks, down to a level that i am almost alone in this town which is good for me, i must reload and make new connections, time changes. But down to this thing about attraction...

When living units meet we can find a connection but i have been thinking a lot about what many friends call energy thiefs, i feel that they do not really exist except in our minds. When we are alive we would be able to shine, we become small suns in our small worlds. That would mean that you share photons to make that lift inside your dear ones. When we touch we would do the same things as the earth does to us but in a different way, but steal?

Is the steal syndrome only in our minds? I mean, if someone makes you buzz you can become addicted to that energy and forsake many of the natural sources because it is making you feel so special. Would this mean that when you lost the sparkle of love it is because you and your partner do not shine anymore? When you loose more energy, here the stealing begins but it is not because of your partner, look at yourself and ask, do i shine?

If you meet someone who shines, sparkles and is high on life you get a that small lift once again but if you meet someone who is low on energy he or she would not drag you down if you have loaded systems, instead you can lift her.

Is someone making you feel bad? Is it because you as a unit is low on energy. I know that this is how i work because everytime i say "see you later" i go away and reconnect with nature once again, to be able to shine once again.

### prAna303 — 2014-06-21T08:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-131637>

Summer solstice.

I have not known it before, or rather not given it any attention but this day is always strange for me.

It starts days before with a deep feeling of being nervous, stressed even a slight pain in my body. I feel so anti social that i can not stand public spots and it has gotten worse for each year. My whole body and mind is only focused on one thing on these days, nature.

Looking back, i have always changed a bit after tonight, stepped out into the unknown. Just like so many times before, i so called lost some of my dearest friends last week, they moved away for this time but it has always been like this.

Now i will reset myself again... Got myself a package of A4 papers to start write once again, got new ribbons for my typewriter. Found my old pens so that i can paint again. I have a guitar that got new strings, the things is how do i play? Never understod how people learn to play instruments, tried it but that kind of "rules" feels odd. Invested in a new needle for my turntable, some new vinyls also.

Walked the city this week and i feel like a stranger in a new town again, sure i say hello to many but really only two i know deeply. It makes me excited, it is up to me again and i love it.

Sadly, not a single ballerina in sight...

### prAna303 — 2014-07-04T21:05:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-133182>

Autopilot... Or healing can be stealing.

Is your head in the way of making that last healing? Do we even have a good chance today with modern way of living, the thing we call lifestyle.

Healing takes big amounts of energy and in a natural way, we can come close with "fake" but the real things rocks. But then what is making that last things so hard, is it the brain? We know that it takes a lot of energy and have powers to shut down things if we do not feed it well. I think modern life and that started when we got away from the animals, making us "the others".

Summer would be great to load the system because we have much for free but what do we not have? Freedom. We are stuck in life with work, homes and other things. We are in a constant attack with brain draining activities all of us, we also must try to make sense of computers and systems that are digital. When we do that we must fill the blanks because they are so dumb, so simple and that is not what we need. A couple of weeks in the summer on vacation, do not think it is enough. From early spring until fall we have a source that feeds us almost 24/7 but is the big advantage lost because lifestyle, i do think so.

Summer means, easy... Easy to get food, easy to get sun, easy to get life going on if you let it. This is a big subject today because it will demand the shit out of a human to stand on the side that contains free sources of energy.

By the way, summer makes us hot... What do we do? sweat... Think about that and why we would put extra stress into our lifes with tons of thinking in the summers. Our semiconductors will shorten its MTBF, guess why a computer gets fried when the cooling stops, the same thing happens in us in summer when we overload our systems.

Perhaps, staying on course with the season and taking life a little bit easy would start a tsunami of healing just by LETTING GO for that moment.

So instead of reading heavy mind things i go with comics. Lying on a beach with a bunch of Lucky Luke and Tintin, feet in the sea and nothing planned makes me feel extra vivid, try it.

### Inger — 2014-07-05T20:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-133254>

I was for 4 hours by the lake today, just like you says Peter, reading, with my feet in the lake. Forgetting about everything. I did not have comics, but I had " Man by himself" by Eric Fromm.. I love that book... On the way home through the woods i found my first wild berries this season.. and i can tell the path through the woods took forever lol I could not stop eating those delicious blueberries!!! This is my absolute favorite time of the year.... gorging on wild berries. But then... in winter i loved it so much I kind of hoped summer will never come... Seems I enjoy every season so much more after meeting Jack...! I am about to explode form happiness pretty often - ouch! I sure heard the song today in the woods.... i had to suppress my own singing because the sound of the silence was so amazing - even if I wanted to sing so much and i did too but the silent music was absolute stunningly beautiful....

On the way home through the woods i found my first wild berries this season.. and i can tell the path through the woods took forever lol I could not stop eating those delicious blueberries!!! This is my absolute favorite time of the year.... gorging on wild berries. But then... in winter i loved it so much I kind of hoped summer will never come... Last week the birds change their themes... From being exicted, almost speeded to a soft calm tune. The forest went into a nice and mellow state, like it is dreaming and going on autopilot. When this happens, i know the first berries will soon show up. Had my first handful of blueberries and wild strawberries (smultron) this week, one week ahead of last year.

Those small berries are so dense in information that wakes so many memories in me... They are batteries of the nature, loaded with sun in a small package.

### prAna303 — 2014-07-15T16:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-134467>

\- I put a spell on you -

So once upon a time we had believes in mystery and then modern age came along.

With sticks and brooms we cleaned it up, everything from dirt in the corners to magic of life, we became educated. With this change we slowly started to simulate life and science, with what? Technology. Almost everything we use today that contains ones and zeroes is a simple copy of what nature and the universe delivers. Take computers with their processors, memory to smartphones with the touchscreen and extend it to the "cloud" where we store our thoughts but is it any news in this?

Can it be that those who once where magicians simply understod a little more about the universe? Could it be that they just felt what could be done, the things others could not phantom. Take this little thing like a spell. Go catch a little particle of the one you would like to control, do your thinking and magic and with that thought you get an instant connection with the original, now you can start to do your stuff.

But then again, is this magic? Is it mumbo jumbo or is it quantum physics?

After i attended to a circus school just a couple of weeks ago i start to feel out of this world, big time. What we can do to a body, what we can do to manipulate a crowd is outstanding. If we allow us to let go of the rules of today we can become what we once had a chance to be, everyone of us can do this.

The more i live this life with nature as my teacher, artists as my guides and motion my force i also feel that i can steer others. I tried it this weekend and almost scared that it is so easy today, if you are dedicated to life, the natural way, others listen and you can really put a spell on them.

### prAna303 — 2014-07-22T00:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-135134>

Out of sync with the rest of the world.

Have you ever been called outdated? I have so many times i stopped counting them.

It feels like i should have lived a hundred years ago, always loved the stories about native americans. The first genre in movies i loved was westerns, still enjoy them with a feeling of how we perhaps should live. On and off the land.

My friends call me ancient or futuristic, in terms of living out of sync with my surroundings... I feel like a cartoon figure, like Lucky Luke and at the same time like Luke Skywalker. Yes this is a mind of a child according to many but the weird things is, i live like this...

Turning things upside down, like stopping to work and only love my way of playing my life is so forbidden in this country but everything is possible. These eight years now, wow what a trip... Just to become everything i dream of without any restrictions, hey wanna join me for a ride thru the desert.

Why these words? Perhaps some will accept to be out of sync, it is the normal that is killing us.

### prAna303 — 2014-08-02T20:57:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136523>

Endless summer.

When i look at the clock on my computer right now it says, august 2...

The obvious thing to reflect on is the fact that time do not matter, timing does. Overheard some people today that said that summer is over and now we must work again, what a crappy summer also... The funny thing is, we had in terms of weather the hottest, the warmest but also some big rains and soon i hope some lightning and thunder. Everyone around me has lost it, some more than others but also some who go against the stream... Went to a beach last week and i am sorry to report, it is "la la land" out there. Kiddos with smartphones, babys holding iPads, many want to take a bath but the sea is yucky and slimy so the land in the pool with +33 C and chemicals. Where did beachlife go? This small window we who live in Sweden can use and always has is now filled with nonsense.

So yesterday i hang on my own beach... No one there to mess with me, just waves seagulls and the salty smell of seawater. The funny things is that i feel exactly and i remember still my child days, the ones with warm air, fresh fish and girls running around in to little clothes... I feel the same as then but without that questions about what i should be when i grow old, now i know...

Girls, oh girls... After some sleepy weeks i found new ones, boy they are extraordinary, sun for sure can make magic with some of them. Once again i let the game go on in the same way i always have played it, everything is possible just not predictable.

So tonight... I celebrate the sun flares, the darker nights and my vinyl collection. But first some pages on my typewriter, thinking of an era gone by and times to come...

To lift my senses, i got a new bottle of my favourite rum today, from the only country outside Sweden i felt at home with, Republica Dominicana.

### Inger — 2014-08-03T10:04:10+0300

simple... IDK...... he needs many women to get satisfied...... i really wonder about you Peter

### prAna303 — 2014-08-03T10:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136596>

Today we have built a system based upon what we are teached from early years and into our death.

I have never felt at home in those guidelines, it does not matter if it is work, living, social skills or how we have relationships with other humans and other living things.

From early years i been more wild than tamed, grown up without fingers pointing at me saying what to do or how to behave, my friends from school had many rules....

All of this also made me question the normal family very early... When others got their first girlfriend i already had been with a few. The thing is that they are always big parts of my life but never the reason for my living. They have no restrictions from me at all and can come and go just like a cat would. Family for me is to have special loved ones around me but also at great distance, family for me is to have a connection to nature, to the sea, the mountain and the woods. Todays world is so focused on systems and i hate them...

For me love is life and in that soup i blend in, girls, trees, sea, sun, music, dance, art, movies and books... Top it of with some good wine, some great rum or simply a glass of natural wild water.

Even if i lived in one single spot my whole life i travel a lot... My nature gives me my journeys without planes, trains or automobiles. I love girls, although not one for a very long time... They come and go just like the harvest in the late summers do, some years great others not so.

Up till this day i have not found that woman who would change my mind, therefor i do not search for her. I want to taste the life no matter what, for good or bad. So i date quite a few, i flirt with many more... It is very simple because i do not have the final destination in my head when i meet some nice ones, just want the magic of the moment.

### Inger — 2014-08-03T10:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136597>

Interesting.... i still wonder tho. My mind always have way too many questions

### caroline — 2014-08-03T11:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136600>

I have known a few men in my lifetime who thought like Peter ...they love many women ...and all women.

I loved to watch them ...they knew what they needed and wanted out of life. It really wasn't superficial at all. they loved and then moved on. It was always interesting to me to see the women that thought they could tame them - they would be positive that they were the one ...and they never were.

It is impossible to tame an animal that must roam free .....you can try but it very likely isn't going to end well.

As Peter also said ... they almost always remained good friends and came and went out of one another's lives.

It is knowing who you are and what you want out of your life .... what you are willing to give ..... and when it is over and when to move on to another chapter of your own magnificent journey...

I think it is rare to truly know what we want and look forward ...instead of in our rearview mirror.

Jack has said some people think he is nuts - and he is okay with that .....

we all need to live our own life.....no regrets, no compromises ....

### Inger — 2014-08-03T16:42:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136620>

So true.
So much to learn

### prAna303 — 2014-08-04T20:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136726>

Your reality.

What do you base your reality upon?

Have we come so far now that almost everyone base their thought, action and being on static information. The world we live in is built upon products today, not much natural is part of modern human now...

When we do live like this, everything around us is dying in terms of energy... What once was water is now dehydrated into bricks and oils from deep within is turned into plastics... The material from the elements, turned into simulations of real world, also known as technology. Everything is in a locked position, not resonating with other parts anymore, we are trapped in the labyrinth of human greatness.

If we have a world like this, time is golden... Timing on the other hand is lost. You mesure everything against things that is in a thermodynamic, entropy maximum. What have we become?

We have for many years based every little theory on so many dead measure points, called those who walk on the other side many names. Have we been following the wrong rules and laws? Why do we accept great innovators from a gone by era? Are we so scared to question them like many others done before with their masters that we accept being pushed like cattle in a modern farm.

So, a reality based on dead things makes humans... Dead, we equal the thing we see and base our life upon.

I, personally seek nature more for each day... Here nothing is static, here no place is messy, here every bit of information from the sun, the ground, the wind and water is giving me challenges. This is where i want to loose the static clock, the global sync and technology from hell, our manmade world.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-05T01:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136768>

The road to success is never an individual battle but a collective effort. So who you hang out with and allow to help mold your thoughts will help craft your version of reality. So choose your confidants wisely. I don't claim to have all the answers but I do think I look under more rocks than most. The real oxymoron of life is we always seek to find the enemy in our health when in reality, the enemy is really us allowing other's craft the environment we live in.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-05T02:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136769>

Our observed reality of our world should be enough to get us to change.......but as you have seen on this site.........it often is not enough.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-05T02:14:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-136779>

What has life fundamentally taught me? For me, it was what I can do to my life. Do I accept life as what is? Or do I reject my current life and recreate what can be? This year taught me circumstances may be beyond my control, but how I respond to them is a recreative force in me. The situation is not my boss, I am. If I want something new, I had to stop doing something old. Just changing your ideas about what is, is how creative destruction begins to transform what you will become.

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-26T22:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-138965>

So today a friend of mine posted the following on facebook

_"In 2014, I only know two people who have held out and stubbornly refuse to get even a basic call & text cell phone.

These people are like rare, exotic birds in our decade.

So I've made a point to ask these curious creatures how and why they continue to live without this seemingly essential ubiquitous little modern basic.

In that time, they have managed to pay their bills, maintain proper communication with their employers and their families, make appointments, tell time, calculate tips, and keep tidy a calendar.

Oh, and they have survived a heart attack and being hit by a car, respectively.

They both swear that in the last 15 years or so, neither of them has once encountered a situation they could not manage without a cell phone.

One of them put it best when he told me, "It's very simple. Any time a cell phone has ever been even remotely useful to me, from standing on the side of the road with a flat tire to getting directions, someone else has one to offer me. And any time those people every really, REALLY need them, it's out of battery power or they have no bars."

Touche.

But why? Why not just bite the bullet and move forward into the 21st century?

Well, here's some of the best reasons I've heard from them.

1\. " I used to be a smoker. I'm telling you, cell phones are the same thing. They have an obsessive, controlling effect on the user that requires him to 'check out of life' in order to attend his addiction."

2\. "It's expensive. Eleven hundred dollars a year? I could fly to Brazil for that. You think I'm going to give up a trip to Brazil for a phone?" Good point. I like that one.

3\. "I have the internet at home. I don't want it anywhere else. At some point, you just become whoever you are on Twitter."

4\. "Have you ever seen a family go out for a meal and spend the entire time looking at their phones? Like all of them? Or shading their screens from the sun on a beautiful day? Yeah. No thanks."

These two people have never been cell phone users. But it made me think about what would happen if the average cell phone user tried to "quit." How hard would it be? Have you even thought about what you would have to change? What you would do with all that extra time? How much more would connect to the people around you, notice where you are right now? What would you do with the extra cash? Buy a saxophone? Take tango lessons? Run a half marathon?

I think I'm going to start a movement."_

Pretty profound. Her observations hit home. really? do you need this stuff? if I look at nature the answer is no.

### nicld — 2014-08-26T22:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-138967>

^^^^^ Very, very good. I am trying to reduce my computer time at home greatly and the cell phone is never on at home. Now to just leave it in the car at work.

### Shijin13 — 2014-08-27T19:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-139055>
