# Thread: What you have measured and learned about EMF radiation / protection..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-02-28T18:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/post-78328>

Okay I thought I will share what I have been measuring with my EMF meter and maybe other have stuff to add too, with different and better meters maybe. I have a Trifield Meter...

Here at home I was happy to see that my bedroom was practically zero.. yay! I always have the breaker off there and zero electricity too. I only sleep there so it is easy!
Then I measured my laptop.. and my old fashioned wired computer (all wired, mouse too). The laptop was horrendous..!!! Even if not plugged and only working with battery. Really bad! My other computer was bad only on the backside of the computer, there it was really bad but screen and mouse and dashboard were quite fine! So I just keep the computer as far away as it gets and it has zero radiation where I sit then.
Fridge was not bad but my parents fridge downstairs was way worse.. it is a big American fridge.. and mine is a Samsung.. so it looks there are big differences!
Hairdryer was horrendous.. but hair straightener was way less bad. Still bad though.
All dimmed lights were really bad, the more I dimmed the stronger the radiation.. crazy! Who would have thought that...? I was so happy to be able to dim the light in my bathroom when I needed to use it late after dark.. Now I use only a candle instead. Much more cosy. I shower with the candle lit and I love it.. after realizing water is always good I shower again (in addition to my dip in the river) and in candle light and wow it is magic..lol I have this beautiful "stony" corner in my bathroom and I put the candle there and it is all amazing! I use the light as little as possible even in day time.
Heaters were bad too, and it was huge difference on lamps! Old fashioned light bulbs had very little radiation but fluorescent were really bad.. As my parents saw it they immidiately replaced them all with old lamps and everything looks more cosy for sure! Cool..

I took it to work yesterday too and measured in the train and in the city too.
In the train the worst place was where you come in, the entrance and then close to the windows. The best place to sit was in the middle, it had almost zero radiation. If someone was sitting close and talking in their cellphone.. it was a different story.. help! In the city many places were so bad it was way way more than 100 milligauss.. no idea how much because that is the max on my meter..
My workplace was pretty bad too but not all places. Many places were a bit below 3 milligauss, which is where it starts to get really unhealthy. I showed my boss and my coworkers where to not spend too much time.. and my boss where pretty pleased when I tested he's desk to be less than 3 milligauss... ha It was really fun in fact. All the guys wanted me to test how strong they radiated... lol.. guys!

Some shops in the city was way over max.. I feel bad for the ones working there.. OMG. to be in that strong radiation 8 hours a day.. crazy!

Then my hubby, ex-hubby or best friend.. I dunno..lol but anyway, he was here and I measured he's cellphones.. he has two and one in each pocket. He got so scared that when he leaved for work next morning he said, Inger, check here! And he had placed both cellphones in a portfolio he showed me proudly.. yay! Now he is here again for visit and he is sleeping on the couch right now and had the cellphones at the floor by the couch.. and I took some thick tinfoil and wrapped them in...
He said he is going to create some business how to protect yourself better.. ha. He is a conservative, that must be why..lol
He spend pretty much time, days and night, in the big city, and always when he is here he sleeps so much! A crazy amount.. huh! I bet he can relax so well here because of the way less radiation.

I have to say I am really happy for the meter even if it was not too expensive, it helps huge to know what to change for sure!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78456>

From the new series.............Quantum biology Have you ever put a bottle of water in the freezer before bed and awaken in the AM to take it out and drink and it begins to freeze top to bottom in seconds? Well if you have then it means your water is super clean and has inherent abilities to become supercooled without freezing even at -20 C? Water needs something to start it to crystalize......some impurity, dust, kink in its container to begin the nucleation or crystallization of ice to begin. This is an example of water memory. When water is super pure it is considered metastable in its liquid state making water act strange to some who have never seen it. This is how I do deep CT in water below 30 degrees without frostbite. This strangeness is what allows super conduction to occur, because in a cooled superconductor the current speeds up and not down as it does in an ionic fluid. This is more common in desalinated or RO water because it is so pure............

### IceBarbie — 2013-03-01T05:25:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78458>

We had a RO filter and our ice cubes always had these cool inverted icicles sticking up from the cubes--some have been 2" tall by 1/4" wide. Then our filters needed replacing and we didn't get around to it. But now we noticed that our well water causes the same thing from time to time. I'm hoping that's a good thing. I've never noticed these icicles on the cubes anywhere else I've lived.

### AK Man — 2013-03-01T07:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78471>

I would think the phenomenon of bottled water freezing upon opening would more likely be due to the pressure gradient. Water under pressure can become supercooled and opening the bottle relieves the pressure and allows freezing.

An even more interesting water fact comes from plants. Have you ever noticed the USDA Plant Hardiness Zone maps? The zone indicates the coldest expected temperature in those areas. Plants hardy down to 32 deg F will quickly die if the temp hits 25 deg F. There are plants that can survive down to -60 (Zone 1). This is possible because plants that evolved at the temps have the ability to super-purify the water in their cells, tropical plants obviously don't have this ability, but arctic plants do. Water can actually remain in a fluid state down to its homogeneous nucleation point of approximately 231 K (−43 deg F).

### bogman — 2013-03-17T18:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-80870>

.
I hope this helps to clarify how important elecrical charge is in the body. Even in college level chemistry classes they don't mention pH is tied to electrical potential in our body. More importantly how the change in pH alters electrical potential causing disease or healing to take place. I could be wrong here so feel free to correct something I missed

Crazy Water company makes a **No. 4 mineral water** that is the strongest mineral water on the market. More than 2,600 TDS and a proper pH level. A pH of 7.00 has a zero electrical charge - only with a pH lower @ 7.00 can water conduct electricity. 7.1 to 14.9 ==== Distilled water is 7.00 which means it can NOT carry electrical charge.

The electrical potential is measured in water with an ORP meter which measures the very slight voltage in the H2O. When we do this we are actually measuring the presence of oxidizing or reducing agents by their specific electrical charge, thus Oxidation Reduction Potential. High pH (alkaline) water has more "reducing" agents (negative \[-\]ORP) and low pH (acidic) water has more oxidizing agents (positive \[+\]ORP). measured in Millivolts (mv)

An oxidizing agent, it is expressed in +ORP \\ this steals electrons causing free radical which is not helpful to healing.

A reducing agent is expressed in terms of â€“ORP \\ this is termed an "anti-oxident" that counteracts free radicals, good for healing.

Therefore, acidic foods and drinks have a pH above 7.00 / ranging from 0.00 to 7.00 which steals your electrons causing "DIS" ease.
The human body functions best with a pH hovering around 7.35 to 7.45 or in electrical terms, -20 mV to -40 mV. Some teenagers can hover between -40 mV to -60 mV they are bouncing all over the place with energy. If your pH dips below optimal that means your electrical charge drops below healthy levels - Cancer can only develop in a certain pH range & therefore, your electrical charge will drop below -5 mV all the way to +50 mV. In order to heal from disease/injury you need to become more basic with your pH to bring the electrical charge back to healthly levels (-20 mV to -40 mV) That means you need to be over -60 mV before you can heal. Without getting to in depth.... Cancer takes 1 mol of glucose to make only 2 ATP - a healthy cell takes 1 mol of glucose to make 38 ATP. That means the Cancer Cell needs to make 19 x's more Glucose to survive. It's in panic mode looking for any extra Glucose. How does it fix this problem? Cancer Cells figured out how to produce insulin receptors - sort of akin to fly paper to attract more flies (glucose). Pretty clever. That's a whole other topic that gets deep fast.

The real kicker is EMF, stress, life ect... all tend to steal your electrons causing your pH to be altered which leads to a loss in your electrical potential from healthy -20 to -40 / to unhealthy levels or +ORP. A tiny change in pH from a healthy level of 7.65 & your health tanks causing your pH to fall to 7.35, sounds like a small charge right? However, pH is measured in a Logarithmic Scale. Therefore, 7.35 is 1,000 times more acidic than 7.65, a much larger change than we realize.

This is way you can drink tap water til your bladder will explode and you're still dehydrated.......... drum roll....

The ORP of most tap water in the USA is between +200 to +600mv and so is an oxidizing agent !!!

So much for regular water being healthy - furthermore, bottled fancy pants water is worse. Most bottled water is over +500 mV!
If you're on the treadmill running your buns off and drinking bottled water it's like riding a bike & smoking at the same time.

Drinking mineral water from Mineral Wells, TX - aka Crazy Water - will help keep you hydrated & in proper pH range. Mineral water will help your body to conduct it's electrical current. Equals healthy.

Why do Vitamin C IV's help ? Ask this question to a Naturopath or Medical Doctor sometime. They won't know the answer!
The answer is: you're giving electrons. Electrons = healthy you. A healthy cell with good electrical charge burns ATP better. Your engine is running like a race car & not like a lawn mower engine (unhealthy). Did you know a fever is the body's amazing ability to turn up the furnance to create more energy (more electricity) It's the body saying help! & going into healing mode. Cold blooded animals, a lizard, when they get injuried seek a warm place (laying in the sun) to increase their body's temp - therefore, increasing their healing ability. What happens now when you get sick? DRUGS ! Masks the body's own ability to heal. You only get worse.
Try explaining that to your family doctor - they'll think you need to be in a padded room lol

Before the Great Flood during Noah's time on Earth - people did in fact live over the age of 200. Can't be done? The men of Monte Picchu are fathering children at 110 years old! No Viagra - they can get a healthy erection at 110 years old  WOW right! Our ultra-modern lifestyle of today is killing us. Time to go back to living in nature, naked, free from modern life.

Cooked food causes the body to produce more white blood cells. The body sees food as an evader causing our pH to get worse. Even pain causes the body to freak out and cause a loss of electrons. Why does lidocaine help? yep! Gives you more electrons and therefore, less pain.

Anywho - hope helps. I know there's so much to understand and learn.

Voltage = pH

one last point. Did you know you can take a voltmeter & measure the mV of your teeth? if a tooth is dead or has a metal filling it'll show up as +ORP. A dentist uses this same device (called Cavitat or Oral Potential Meter) the dentist can see early decay in your tooth. Who knew.... Sad that dental schools don't teach the importance of this concept - only thing they teach is reading x is bad and reading y is good. ugh

Can you tell I love to dig deep into electrical potential & pH

### bogman — 2013-03-17T20:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-80879>

.
I hope this helps to clarify how important elecrical charge is in the body. Even in college level chemistry classes they don't mention pH is tied to electrical potential in our body. More importantly how the change in pH alters electrical potential causing disease or healing to take place. I could be wrong here so feel free to correct something I missed

Crazy Water company makes a **No. 4 mineral water** that is the strongest mineral water on the market. More than 2,600 TDS and a proper pH level. A pH of 7.00 has a zero electrical charge - only with a pH lower @ 7.00 can water conduct electricity. 7.1 to 14.9 ==== Distilled water is 7.00 which means it can NOT carry electrical charge.

The electrical potential is measured in water with an ORP meter which measures the very slight voltage in the H2O. When we do this we are actually measuring the presence of oxidizing or reducing agents by their specific electrical charge, thus Oxidation Reduction Potential. High pH (alkaline) water has more "reducing" agents (negative \[-\]ORP) and low pH (acidic) water has more oxidizing agents (positive \[+\]ORP). measured in Millivolts (mv)

An oxidizing agent, it is expressed in +ORP \\ this steals electrons causing free radical which is not helpful to healing.

A reducing agent is expressed in terms of â€“ORP \\ this is termed an "anti-oxident" that counteracts free radicals, good for healing.

Therefore, acidic foods and drinks have a pH above 7.00 / ranging from 0.00 to 7.00 which steals your electrons causing "DIS" ease.
The human body functions best with a pH hovering around 7.35 to 7.45 or in electrical terms, -20 mV to -40 mV. Some teenagers can hover between -40 mV to -60 mV they are bouncing all over the place with energy. If your pH dips below optimal that means your electrical charge drops below healthy levels - Cancer can only develop in a certain pH range & therefore, your electrical charge will drop below -5 mV all the way to +50 mV. In order to heal from disease/injury you need to become more basic with your pH to bring the electrical charge back to healthly levels (-20 mV to -40 mV) That means you need to be over -60 mV before you can heal. ### bonsaiz — 2013-05-13T11:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-85937>

> [AK Man said:](/goto/post?id=78471)
>
> I would think the phenomenon of bottled water freezing upon opening would more likely be due to the pressure gradient. Water under pressure can become supercooled and opening the bottle relieves the pressure and allows freezing.
>
> An even more interesting water fact comes from plants. Have you ever noticed the USDA Plant Hardiness Zone maps? The zone indicates the coldest expected temperature in those areas. Plants hardy down to 32 deg F will quickly die if the temp hits 25 deg F. There are plants that can survive down to -60 (Zone 1). This is possible because plants that evolved at the temps have the ability to super-purify the water in their cells, tropical plants obviously don't have this ability, but arctic plants do. Water can actually remain in a fluid state down to its homogeneous nucleation point of approximately 231 K (−43 deg F).

Coincidence. I thought the same about the water bottle thing. But as much as you are describing.

# Thread: New CT Supplements worth trying?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ct-supplements-worth-trying.6126/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jamezduffy — 2013-03-01T17:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ct-supplements-worth-trying.6126/post-78507>

I was doing a search on Google Scholar and found a couple recent studies which caught my eye in regards to CT.

In one study, they found a plant from the ginger family, _Aframomum melegueta_, was found to activate BAT in men! This plant, nicknamed, "Grains of Paradise," is available online in raw form, but I was unable to locate an extract of it like which was used in the study.

Read more about the Grains of Paradise here: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aframomum\_melegueta](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aframomum_melegueta) &
[http://examine.com/supplements/Aframomum+melegueta/](http://examine.com/supplements/Aframomum+melegueta/)

Here is a link to the study: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23308394](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23308394)

Secondly, I also found a study in which mice were given Conjugated Linoleic Acid (CLA). The mice given a dosage of 0.2% CLA relative to their bodyweight were found to decrease their WAT and increase the compounds in their BAT! CLA also increased the UCP1 genes in the BAT tissues. This was 10,12 CLA, and I don't know how that compares to CLA that is available as a supplement, but interesting nonetheless! Doing some math, I figured I could reach that level of CLA supplementation taking 9 pills a day of these: [http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-cla-1500](http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-cla-1500). In the study, they did 7 weeks of supplementation, so that would take 5 bottles to get a similar dosage (for me at about 200lbs).

Here is the study: [http://www.jlr.org/content/early/2013/02/11/jlr.M030924.short](http://www.jlr.org/content/early/2013/02/11/jlr.M030924.short)
More on CLA: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conjugated\_linoleic\_acid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conjugated_linoleic_acid)

Worth trying? Anyone tried these yet?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-02T05:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ct-supplements-worth-trying.6126/post-78573>

I tried CLA.........did nada about 5 yrs ago.......me.....I stick to ice cold water.

# Thread: Oysters, Oysters and hey, Oysters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-oysters-and-hey-oysters.6130/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christy — 2013-03-01T20:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-oysters-and-hey-oysters.6130/post-78529>

ok, so, I'm trying to add oysters to my menus, maybe even up to 8-10 times per week, IF I can figure out enough different ways to prepare them. (Can't do raw yet, gut too leaky) And don't want to get bored with them.

But I wanted to be able have some in the fridge already cooked so they can be a quick fix - say with paleo cocktail sauce and lime juice. except they are so delicate that even steaming mad them dry out a tad, so even a little reheat can make them tough.

So I went looking for another way and found out how to poach them - and it worked out so well!

below are links for recipes for both shucked (I buy Hilton Point shucked/raw)

[http://www.coastseafoods.com/shucked.html](http://www.coastseafoods.com/shucked.html)

and they are oh so convenient and still taste fresh! And were soooo good reheated the next day, too!
Here's the poaching recipe I used, (but next going to get creative with the poaching liquid for added flavor!)

[http://www.silverpointoysters.com/recpoached.html](http://www.silverpointoysters.com/recpoached.html)

and here's a poaching recipe if you want to poach them in the shells:

[http://www.newenglandprovisions.com/...edoysters.html](http://www.newenglandprovisions.com/...edoysters.html)

so I am wondering how everyone's fixing their oysters AND what kinds of sauces you are using on various seafoods, (if any) that might lend itself to oysters, as well???

thanks!

Christy

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-02T04:31:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-oysters-and-hey-oysters.6130/post-78571>

the leakier your gut the more your oysters should be ironically........you got that all wrong.

>
> Domination is what I have always been after, if I am being honest. Is that a criminal aspiration? If you publicly claim you aspire to power, it's considered immoral and everybody throws up their hands in protest. Ironically, I think this is what we're all after, which is why people will try to deter you away from that very objective. Power is conducive to control, and control is conducive to happiness in my opinion. This community has provided me with more control over my health than could have ever imagined previously.
>
> As much as I dig this community, I have been taking a page out of Martin's book and trying to avoid the Internet as much as possible following the atom bomb that has been the EMF series. I picked up a pair of used leather shoes for five dollars and have been on foot patrol for a week now. Everything remains copacetic.
>
> I am done trying to "save" anybody and continue to lead by example, because any time I mention anything I have learned here to my family I am met with a brick wall of narrow mindedness. You're just an "Internet guy" anyway, and nothing valuable can ever be learned outside the context of an academic institution. Heads are going to inevitably turn when I get back in the ocean and ramp up the CT. Last summer was just the morning skate. I turned heads and dropped jaws with the amount of physical work I was able to accomplish at the cottage last summer. This summer is going to represent the first period, and it's going to be nothing short of biblical.
>
> Watch this space people!

Ivan - this is just awesome... and 100% true - its all about who's big man/woman on campus. its realizing the tribe you're currently with is on a fast track to nowhere and it's time to break away. its pretty awesome when you realize the power one. its about how we adapt and over come the adversities laid before us. we figure it out and make it happen. its the only thing we can do.

### scghsv70 — 2013-03-02T16:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biology-lecture-number-one.6131/post-78610>

I haven't had a chance to introduce myself yet, but I have read through all the blogs by date and I want to say what you are offering is truly incredible. I am 70 years old and have been biohacking myself for 40 years trying to improve my health. I love how you have crossed over the sciences. I majored in Anthropology in college in early 60's, loved biology and geology so had a smattering of knowledge. Ended up owning a health food store so had access to good food and books about nutrition etc. Also subscribed to a weekly science magazine that covered everything from astronomy to zoology. I read about epigenetics, when they identified leptin, that even light under a door can mess up sleep cycle. Over years I have slowly unraveled some health issues but never satisfied that optimal had been achieved. You have answered so many questions that remained unresolved in my mind with knowledge that I did have, (i.e. if we are supposed to be meat eaters how can gorillas have such massive muscle structure and eat leaves? When and how does chemical become electrical in the body?). In my mind, our diet had been mostly decent the past 30 years until my 64 yr. old husband developed colon cancer! (Luckily stage 1) I was shocked because I have all the risk factors and we ate WAY better than the SAD. Anyway I then had to do a crash course in what causes cancer and what could we do about it. And then we found you. Luckily we have a doctor that is alternative and she does paleo, specializes in things like autism etc. and she had a link on her facebook page of all things that led to you. So I have her to thank! So now after probably about a year, I have a better understanding about why my spouse got cancer instead of me. He has lost 40 pounds, reduced blood pressure, changed sleeping habits, wearing blue light blockers at night, drastically increased seafood and other diet changes, etc. She recently did an extensive battery of tests mostly what you recommend so we are working on tweaking things based on test results. Also, we belong to a Unitarian Universalist church and I am organizing an epi-paleo group. So far about 7 people have signed up and a couple are already doing paleo. Some are interested because they have seen the changes in us in the last year. Just so you know, we are leaving for two weeks to spend time in the Yucatan near the crater area to get sun, sand, sea water and seafood and recharge. So there are folks out here in cyberspace that may not say much, but are greatly impacted with your efforts and information and most appreciative! It may be too late for us but at least we might be mini starfish for you!

This was a hard thing for a scientist to realize much less admit. I was brought up to believe in RCT's because of my training. Medicine/science is the world I come from and it is why they can not provide answers for humans today. When you understand how to balance energy/information in your system from Nature, because you understand you are a collection of hydrated semiconductors, that must work as close to perfect coherence as possible. that only can happen when the EMF you program your semiconductors with is the Sun and Earth. Nature has the antidote to eliminate mitochondrial disease and cause time dilation. Then and only then, you have found optimal for you. I can not give optimal to my family, son, daughter, wife or my patients. I can show them the way we are built and they need to re-build coherence for them based on where they find themselves now.............
QED tells us that an outside observer changes the system's entropy. This is why I do not believe in Nora G's book at all........this idea in QED tells us the mind and body are not separate unless you believe Newtonian time scales..........time can be held still if you live coherently. I know it and most of you have yet to find it. Where I was in 2008 and where I am now has no basis in modern biology but yet you have seen the before and after in me and some of my family and patients. There is a reason our reality differs from yours. It's called details. In semiconduction details matter a lot. As hydrogen fractions change in the cell water around a semiconductor so does its capability. There is no primal mind or body separation unless you allow for it in your reconstruction of time mind-matter dualism that makes us strangers to ourselves, and the rest of the living systems nature has built. I now reject that because I understand the lessons of QED implicitly and this understanding will stay here for all who come later...........

# Thread: Facial Symmetry
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facial-symmetry.6137/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-03T05:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facial-symmetry.6137/post-78709>

Last year, during my first visit to my osteopath, he commented how he could see that I had inflammation as my face wasn't symmetrical. I have actually seen it in the mirror at various times as well. I was noticing today while watching tv that I could see many people with an asymmetrical face.

I have read before that beauty is as a result of symmetry of features. I am thinking that asymmetry is more of a result of inflammation in many people rather than genetics. Has anyone else noticed this? Is this due to inflammation of the brain that causes imbalance, or just general inflammation.

### caroline — 2013-03-03T08:11:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facial-symmetry.6137/post-78722>

How interesting - can't wait to learn more.....

### Glamazon — 2013-03-03T18:41:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facial-symmetry.6137/post-78757>

to really know you would have to have an xray of your face and compare sides for symmetry. Then you could see if there was actual tissue inflammation or bone changes. I can look at a face straight on and see changes that point me to look for other things...such as swelling in the face. As a nurse I have been trained for that.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-03T19:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facial-symmetry.6137/post-78759>

I wonder if this relates to the old way of Chinese face reading:

How the Face Reveals Clues to the State of Your Health

• Slightly green facial hue: decreased liver function

• Overly red facial hue: heart disease or high blood pressure

• Yellowish facial hue: low function of spleen and stomach

• Darkened facial hue: low kidney function

• Whitened face: low lung function

• “Folding” between eyebrows: low-functioning lymph, weakened immune system

• Red spots or pox around the eyebrows: increased likelihood of flu, or sign of flu in recent past

• Reddened eyebrows: overactive nervous system, lack of quality sleep

• Reddened eye sockets: inflammation in the kidneys or back pain

• Reddened nose: bladder inflammation or back pain

• Swelling veins on the nose: deteriorating function of kidneys and bladder

• Red lips: overactive stomach

• Dark lips: low function of spleen and kidney

• Pox spots or cold sores near lips: stomach ulcer

• Red cheeks: active liver

• Black eyes or black/ brown spots: low kidney function

• Dark skin around eyes: deteriorating kidney function, likelihood of kidney “stones”

• Black moles between nose lips: high levels of acid and toxins in the body

• Darkening of black moles between nose and lips: possible sign of risk of cancer

• Region between chin and ear has depressed scars, pox marks, or other anomalies: poorly functioning intestine (if this region becomes red, possible sign of impending diarrhea)

\- See more at: [http://undergroundhealthreporter.com/chinese-face-reading-reveals-your-health#axzz2MUuYEnub](http://undergroundhealthreporter.com/chinese-face-reading-reveals-your-health#axzz2MUuYEnub)

# Thread: Scientists discover protein that controls evolution
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientists-discover-protein-that-controls-evolution.6139/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-03T06:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientists-discover-protein-that-controls-evolution.6139/post-78718>

> Evolutionary changes are supposed to take place gradually and randomly, under pressure from natural selection. But a team of Princeton scientists investigating a group of proteins that help cells burn energy stumbled across evidence that this is not how evolution works. In fact, their discovery could revolutionize the way we understand evolutionary processes. They have evidence that organisms actually have the ability to control their own evolution.

[http://io9.com/5083673/princeton-scientists-discover-proteins-that-control-evolution/](http://io9.com/5083673/princeton-scientists-discover-proteins-that-control-evolution/)

### Owl — 2013-03-03T20:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-threads-being-deleted-and-members-banned.6145/post-78774>

What is going on here? Why are entire threads being not just closed, but deleted? I had bookmarked the Titanic thread, and it is completely gone.
And Cavemam, SoulDanzer, and others have been banned?

Does this mean it is not ok to speak one's mind here, and that everything is censored?
Jack, I thought we were supposed to "question everything"?

This is not right. It is not the mark of an open mind.
Why not answer the criticisms with intelligence and let us decide who is correct?

You may not like everything that is said here, but censoring every criticism makes you look like a tyrant.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-03T20:45:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-threads-being-deleted-and-members-banned.6145/post-78775>

dont like it........there is the door. anyone who sucks electrons from here will no longer be welcome........i have a trigger finger now. When you think your entitled to more than you think.........you can find somewhere else to bitch and moan. This is not a doctors office..........

### lioness7 — 2013-03-03T21:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t-our-families-say.6146/post-78786>

I saw the previously posted video regarding \*\*\*t paleo says.....So I thought to start a thread more closer to home. I am sure we have all heard a fare share of criticism, comments, etc....I was inspired (read pissed) by a comment dh made this morning.

I have spent the last 25 yrs trying to do my absolute best - reading, researching, experimenting, implementing, educating my family, blah blah. So this morning, I run outside to chill a bottle of champagne on the patio in a snow bank for the spontaneous family sunday brunch ( which the dog ended up bringing back to the patio door) in a tank top and bare feet in the snow. I could hear the younger kids in the house "omg, mom's outside in her bare feet walking in the snow again!" Upon entering the kitchen, dh proclaims "you know, you are going to be miserable without me when you out live me!"  If I wasn't 50 and worried about an injury, I would have drop kicked him right then and there..........

### JoeBranca — 2013-03-05T18:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t-our-families-say.6146/post-79125>

**the bread**

> [RGP said:](/goto/post?id=79062)
>
> "But Jesus ate bread."

This one I flip around, because it's actually an exercise in symbolic irony if the meaning and context doesn't get lost on why Jesus chose bread to represent the interim gatherings of his followers. He did this at the time of Passover, likely the scene was something like him reaching across and \*past\* the passover lamb and took some unassuming bread to demonstrate the nature of his mission and the intended character of his disciples. Bread is a miserly substance meant to sustain one's life in circumstances when nothing else is available in abundance. The bread is a "humble element" keeping his followers going on the pilgrim journey in their spiritual wilderness, in contrast with the great wedding feast at the end. The bread wasn't meant to literally nourish anyone, it was a semiotic event referencing the humble state of affairs they were in.

I used to say this one too...and tried to reconcile "Give us this day our daily bread" with Wheat Belly! Interestingly, it was only 1 apostle, Judas, who betrayed Jesus for 30 pieces of silver. It's always been said that Judas went off to hang himself...but, there is more detail about Judas' death in Acts 1:16-18. Seems that when he attempted to hang himself, somehow either the rope broke or he fell over a cliff and ended up dying by falling and hitting a rock. It's described as this: "and pitching head foremost he noisily burst in his midst and all his intestines were poured out."

But as for bread, it was part of their diet back then. It was more nutritious and didn't cause the issues it does today. Hybrid strains of wheat have been altered to change their protein levels etc plus they are now mostly "Round-Up Ready" with the pesticides part of the seed. Those pesticides work on insects by destroying their guts. Now Monsanto's "studies" say that there is no affect whatsoever on human intestines; however indepedent testing says otherwise. Modern wheat is detrimental to our guts when we eat it. It affects some worse than others. I also just read that there is a component of gluten that is literally called "gluteo-morphine" (I hope I'm remembering that correctly) and when undigested in the gut is addictive. This explains a lot!!

# Thread: Coconut water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-04T00:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78836>

I love coconut water, and it seems to have good properties that could help with hydration.

From Wikipedia:

> Coconut water has a high potassium content and contains antioxidants.\[4\] It also contains cytokinins\[5\] which promote plant cell division and growth.

I just am careful and read the ingredients as many brands have added sugar. But there are some that don't.

Does anyone else drink coconut water?

### BJK77 — 2013-03-04T01:07:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78839>

Fellow coconut water lover here, but unfortunately I've found that even the higher natural sugar content doesn't suit me very well. I've tried fermenting it to reduce the sugars, but for some reason it doesn't always seem to work.

I do find if I'm feeling super dehydrated though that a couple of ounces helps without any adverse effects.

### diane — 2013-03-04T01:54:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78844>

Interesting. What happens when you drink it?

### endless — 2013-03-04T03:30:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78865>

I find it a bit too high sugar for me too, unless I make kefir out of it. I love it though, and will have it occasionally in the summer for a treat.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-04T13:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78896>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=78844)
>
> Interesting. What happens when you drink it?

I get pain in my back between my shoulder blades . . . any form of 'sugar' tends to do this to me. I usually end up with brain fog too and occasionally even heartburn.

Thanks for the answer.... but Jack how can you say EMF is a problem for me when you know nothing about where I live and my lifestyle? I am willing to wager that I have the LEAST EMF exposure out of everyone on this forum! I live in the south of the South Island of New Zealand, on a farm, the nearest town is 15 km away (a town of 400 people), I have to drive an hour to the nearest big town to get my groceries. Our nearest neighbour is 1 km away, the nearest cell phone tower is 10 km away and the only one probably in a 50km radius. We live off grid - run our house off a generator which is only on 2 to 3 hours in the evening, and sometimes in summer not at all, so no electricity is running in our house most of the time. We don't have wifi. So you see EMFs are not a big issue to me. Yet I'm still fat, etc.

# Thread: How To Make Your Own Earthing Sheet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 80
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-03-04T07:43:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-78877>

I have been sleeping grounded via stake in the ground for about a year now and I continue to experience remarkable results. My results include faster muscle recovery, restful sleep, reduced nerve pain and inflammation, lucid dreams, and better dream recall. I seem to be able to fall asleep much easier and wake up easier around dawn. It seems that the rise of the sun seems to some how affect the ground I am plugged into and gently wake me from my slumber. It seems to almost make it harder to fall back asleep after the sun is up as I am guessing the sun has slightly increased the frequency of the Schumann resonance that I am directly plugged into.

With how much earthing has helped me I am very passionate about our inherent connection with Earth and I have been on a mission to help spread this knowledge with others who are receptive to the idea. For this reason I made a video called "How to make your own earthing sheet." I posted this video on YouTube to help others who are interested in the concept but cant afford to buy earthing sheets, or would like to test the idea before they do. In the video I use all locally available good and warn not to experiment with this if you dont not understand electrical. I hope you find it useful as I share many things that I have leaned along my journey.

Thanks, Huck.
Earthrunner, I like the low cost solution you presented. You can find metal screen fairly easily.
I think you would get much better results, however, with a longer stake in the ground. A pipe, or some rebar, maybe. A short stake in the ground doesn't penetrate far, and the upper layer of dirt can dry up, reducing conduction. Or the stake can become loose in the ground.

### andee — 2013-03-05T05:22:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79017>

**Earthing several stories above the actual earth?**

Amazing info and video! Thank you. Wondering what happens if you don't have direct access to the earth ... I live in an apartment building on the third floor. Recently I found an article co-authored by earthing guru James Oschman, which claims that using the grounding wire in your electrical outlets does ground you and implies that the "dirty electricity" from outlets still provides the benefits of grounding - at least that's my interpretation! The article is here:

[http://www.earthinginstitute.net/statements/understanding\_earthing.pdf](http://www.earthinginstitute.net/statements/understanding_earthing.pdf). It states:

"Some people are concerned that Earthing may increase oneâ€™s exposure to so-called â€œelectromagnetic pollutionâ€￾ or â€œdirty electricity.â€￾ Earthing actually decreases oneâ€™s exposure to these potentially disruptive fields. To understand why this is so, one must look at the basic physics and biophysics of electricity and magnetism. Confusion about this topic is due in part to the fact that research on Earthing and the human body is opening up new perspectives, and requires a fresh examination of the basics of electricity and magnetism as applied to physiology and medicine..."

No idea whether these "new perspectives" would include a quantum perspective on dirty energy in the absence of the Schumann resonance, though...well must admit it's not too likely that was factored in, is it...

EarthRunner, I know you said in the video that grounding to the earth was superior, but from your experience was grounding through an electrical outlet still beneficial?

Thanks for any input.

### Huck — 2013-03-05T15:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79071>

> [andee said:](/goto/post?id=79017)
>
> Amazing info and video! Thank you. Wondering what happens if you don't have direct access to the earth ... I live in an apartment building on the third floor. Recently I found an article co-authored by earthing guru James Oschman, which claims that using the grounding wire in your electrical outlets does ground you and implies that the "dirty electricity" from outlets still provides the benefits of grounding - at least that's my interpretation! The article is here:
>
> [http://www.earthinginstitute.net/statements/understanding\_earthing.pdf](http://www.earthinginstitute.net/statements/understanding_earthing.pdf). It states:
>
> "Some people are concerned that Earthing may increase one’s exposure to so-called “electromagnetic pollution” or “dirty electricity.” Earthing actually decreases one’s exposure to these potentially disruptive fields. To understand why this is so, one must look at the basic physics and biophysics of electricity and magnetism. Confusion about this topic is due in part to the fact that research on Earthing and the human body is opening up new perspectives, and requires a fresh examination of the basics of electricity and magnetism as applied to physiology and medicine..."
>
> No idea whether these "new perspectives" would include a quantum perspective on dirty energy in the absence of the Schumann resonance, though...well must admit it's not too likely that was factored in, is it...
>
> EarthRunner, I know you said in the video that grounding to the earth was superior, but from your experience was grounding through an electrical outlet still beneficial?
>
> Thanks for any input.

I'm no expert, but I think there are at least 2 potential problems:

-   The ground in your electrical wiring is not actually grounded.
-   The ground is connected to ground in more than 1 place setting up a potential ground loop.

If the ground wire is properly grounded, then it should work. I believe someone here earlier posted about a potential problem with grounding to a water pipe.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-05T15:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79072>

I'll just put in my experience with grounding through the outlets...it was a properly grounded outlet, but I could certainly notice the difference in affect between the wire grounded to the earth and through the outlet. We had a terrible time sleeping and whatever it was that we picked up through the outlets made me achy and inflamed. This was not the case with the wire out the window and in fact I've notice a big decrease in my hands swelling since I started this form of grounding.

Somebody mentioned earlier that a wire conditioner could eliminate the dirty electricity issue....it's in one of the EMF threads that started up a few weeks ago.

I think Misty spoke about a similar experience on the QandA.

I actually think I did get some benefits from using the house ground, especially while using a computer and near other strong EMF.

But grounding directly to earth, especially at night, is quite different. I have already bought another long ground rod to use away from the bedroom, for some fabric covering a chair, and a pad for my feet. Gee, if DH really loved me, he would get me a house with a dirt floor...

### shilohman — 2013-03-06T02:30:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79227>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=79218)
>
> Hmmm....the grounding rod that is sold at [www.earthing.com](http://www.earthing.com) is not long, and is small diameter. I was able to push it in easily with my foot. I wonder if it's not long enough to be effective?

When I use to install grounds for data centers we would use a 6-8 ft copper rod. Had to go that deep to get a good ground. Depending on where you live i.e. how close to cell tower/smart meters/etc., going down only six or eight inches will only pick up a noisy ground (lots of voltage spikes). I would imagine that depending where you live that just walking barefoot isn't going to provide a very 'clean' ground either, but its kind of hard to live in a foot of water in the Pacific

### HoneyChild — 2013-03-06T12:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79277>

I live a couple of flights up in an apartment block that's over 100 years old and the external walls are made from sandstone. Because stone conducts electrons I wondered if I could try to earth using the sandstone. I'm not very technically minded but I hope to buy some sort of circuit tester to see if this would work as per the instructions here: [http://naturesplatform.com/earthing.html](http://naturesplatform.com/earthing.html)

Any thoughts?

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-03-06T23:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-79352>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=79277)
>
> I live a couple of flights up in an apartment block that's over 100 years old and the external walls are made from sandstone. Because stone conducts electrons I wondered if I could try to earth using the sandstone. I'm not very technically minded but I hope to buy some sort of circuit tester to see if this would work as per the instructions here: [http://naturesplatform.com/earthing.html](http://naturesplatform.com/earthing.html)
>
> Any thoughts?

I think this could work. If the sandstone doesn't work you could always use the piping as explained on the website you mentioned. I would use a voltmeter ($15-30) to test as I am not familiar with the method used here. After setting the voltmeter to AC current I would stand somewhere you have access ground (sandstone wall or metal piping) and you are also exposed to an EMF (near a outlet or an appliance plugged into a outlet). I would then connect one lead to ground (sandstone wall or piping) and the other lead to you skin. If you ground is working it will read the voltage of your body (somewhere around 1-3 volts). Then touch your skin to the same grounded surface and see if your voltage drops close to zero.

Hope this helps. I think the piping is your best bet.

Excellent article and to me the take away from reading it is grounding pads are problematic and in my opinion the best way to ground while sleeping is to wear a grounding strap on your wrist or somewhere and attach the other end to earth ground. This way you completely eliminate all of the problems that you get with a grounding pad. Just say NO to grounding pads.
P.S., you can simply negate the antenna effect of grounding pads by wearing a grounding strap, but then why bother with the grounding pad.

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-03-12T00:01:04+0200

One thing to note about this study is that people were not actually in contact with the the grounding pads, they were used as shielding. This is what Martin Zucker pointed out.

My experience: Comparing the effects of grounding via strap vs earthing sheet I have better results with the the sheet. My understanding is the increased surface area allows for a better connection (more electron transfer) for our not so conductive skin.

### kathylu — 2013-03-12T03:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-80136>

This is my project for tomorrow...making a grounding sheet. Definitely doing a copper pipe in the earth though. I have been thinking a lot about using your house ground through an outlet. It doesn't make sense to me, because you are making yourself part of the house circuit. So, theoretically every bit of "dirty electricity" that flows through the house ground would be flowing through you as well! Doesn't seem like a good idea. Maybe that's why some folks have noticed they feel worse when trying this?

### shilohman — 2013-03-12T03:56:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-80142>

You are correct, unless you have an electrician install an isolated ground at your circuit breaker box and then connect the outlet grounds in the bedrooms to the isolated ground, then a separate earth ground is what you want.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-19T17:06:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81093>

This weekend I woke up knowing something was wrong. My grounding sheet felt lifeless, still. I checked the wire, and sure enough, it had fallen off in the night. I must have pushed it or something. I was upset, having wasted hours of good grounding time.

But that got me to thinking. I didn't use to notice it when I'd first lay down, but where the sheet touches my skin, I do feel something after all. An extremely small tingle. Not everywhere, just at random places, in a fairly continuous fashion. Like a TENS unit on a very low setting. Feels a bit like a single tiny hair moving gently.

Last night I had a decision to make. I had helped move a cast iron wood stove, and my back was talking to me. I was going to get an ice pack, but I didn't want to block the grounding in that area, because it helps so much. So I went to bed without the ice. But soon I realized I could just put the ice under the grounding sheet. So I got back up and grabbed some. I had my fishcake and ate it too!

### Sistagrok — 2013-03-19T17:28:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81100>

Just wanted to say thanks to Earthrunner for the how to video. I've been using our home made grounding sheet for about a week now. I used a 5 foot copper pipe and pounded it in the ground about 3 1/2 feet. My bf drilled a hole in the pipe and put a screw with a washer in it. Then we used a speaker wire, spliced both ends. Then we ran it down the side of the house from my bedroom window, and wrapped the exposed copper wiring around the screw and tightened it on. Then we twisted the other end to the screening. It turned out that we had some old screening, well not old but new, but was laying around for years. The outside package looked like it was ancient. But once opened up the screen was impeccably new. Anyways. it only cost me about 13 bucks for the copper rod and line, which I didn't really need as I have it laying all over the house. But when your a woman, you don't really notice those kinds of things laying around the basement and garage. Oh well I tested it with my voltage meter, and it definitely shows being grounded. Which is good for me as I like to see something showing me that the nice relaxing feeling I get when I lay on it isn't in my mind. It only took about 10 minutes to hook it all up too.
So again, thanks!

### kathylu — 2013-03-23T16:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81538>

Advice quick!! I finally got around to setting up the home made grounding sheet last night...well, it was mostly hubby that did it...lol. Anyway, what followed was possibly the WORST night of sleep both my husband and I have had in a long time! First, we did make sure the sheet was properly grounded. Pounded copper pipe into the earth, ran speaker wire from the sheet to the pipe, attached it to aluminum screen via metal tape, etc. Used the Tri-field and a voltage meter to confirm both voltage drop and current flow (indicating the circuit was complete and grounded). He went to bed a bit before I did, but we both had similar experiences. We both felt a slight "tingling" when we laid down...I attribute that to our charge equilibrating with the ground. I sometimes have the same feeling in my feet when I step onto grass barefoot. It disappeared after a few seconds. Anyway, I woke up no less than 20 times during the night, just a few seconds at a time, but it disrupted my sleep terribly. I experienced some back pain, so I kept turning onto my right side and this morning I woke with a slight headache and the muscles on the right side of my body were painful where they contacted the grounding sheet. The first thing my husband said was "I slept terribly! Kept waking up and now my back and shoulders are sore. What gives?" So we checked the circuit again and it was grounded. No electrical spikes, no detectable field surrounding the area of the wires, wall or bed.

I am definitely not willing to try this again...I still feel terrible! Also, this was completely unexpected. I have been sleeping pretty well for months and I was looking forward to even better sleep.

I've been laughing a little...my odd sense of humor tells me that grounding sheets really do re-create the experience of sleeping on the ground! The cold, hard ground with no padding so you can't sleep and wake up feeling like someone has worked you over in the boxing ring...lol

Can anyone explain this? What did we do wrong?

Your experience is very similar to the experience I had with my homemade grounding sheet that wasn't grounded.

I had a similar experience with an electric mattress pad. This is like an electric blanket, but it goes under the fitted sheet. My feet would tingle and feel like I was getting a mild electric shock. Some nights I would feel the same tingle when the pad wasn't even turned on. I bought a new pad and had the same results. I threw them both in the trash.

I'm starting to think sheets are not a good idea. They collect rf and some of it, I think, might flow through the body before going to ground. The grounding part is good, but not the antenna part. I agree with an earlier post by Shiloman that a grounding wrist strap might be a better option.

This is based on my experience, not expert knowledge. Please correct me if this is wrong.

### Amy — 2013-03-23T21:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81572>

i use shielded wire from the ground rod (outside the bedroom window) to the foot of the bed-- then, that attaches to a short segment of speaker wire (lightweight and not bothersome) and then to a wrist strap (radioshack). easy peasy. I wear the wrist strap around my upper foot. I imagine that attachment it is a little more "physiologic" in a biped grounding sort of way. I've had excellent results with the setup and it does not bother me at all -- and I am a picky sort of sleeper....

### shilohman — 2013-03-23T22:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81574>

> [kathylu said:](/goto/post?id=81538)

Anyway, what followed was possibly the WORST night of sleep both my husband and I have had in a long time! First, we did make sure the sheet was properly grounded. Pounded copper pipe into the earth, ran speaker wire from the sheet to the pipe, attached it to aluminum screen via metal tape, etc. Used the Tri-field and a voltage meter to confirm both voltage drop and current flow (indicating the circuit was complete and grounded). He went to bed a bit before I did, but we both had similar experiences. We both felt a slight "tingling" when we laid down...I attribute that to our charge equilibrating with the ground. I sometimes have the same feeling in my feet when I step onto grass barefoot. It disappeared after a few seconds. Anyway, I woke up no less than 20 times during the night, just a few seconds at a time, but it disrupted my sleep terribly. I experienced some back pain, so I kept turning onto my right side and this morning I woke with a slight headache and the muscles on the right side of my body were painful where they contacted the grounding sheet. The first thing my husband said was "I slept terribly! Kept waking up and now my back and shoulders are sore. What gives?" So we checked the circuit again and it was grounded. No electrical spikes, no detectable field surrounding the area of the wires, wall or bed.
>
> I am definitely not willing to try this again...I still feel terrible! Also, this was completely unexpected. I have been sleeping pretty well for months and I was looking forward to even better sleep.
>
> I've been laughing a little...my odd sense of humor tells me that grounding sheets really do re-create the experience of sleeping on the ground! The cold, hard ground with no padding so you can't sleep and wake up feeling like someone has worked you over in the boxing ring...lol
>
> Can anyone explain this? What did we do wrong?

Grounding sheets are very problematic. Check out this URL [http://www.electricsense.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/plugin-FINAL\_ACHTUNG\_ABSCHIRMDECKE\_WEB.pdf](http://www.electricsense.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/plugin-FINAL_ACHTUNG_ABSCHIRMDECKE_WEB.pdf)
Why are grounding sheets (GS) so problematic? First some background. Your body whether grounded or ungrounded acts as an antenna and if ungrounded absorbs much of the EMF that hits it. When your body is standing barefoot on the grass, it is still acting as an antenna to the EMF that is hitting it, but because you are grounded and because of the 'skin effect' of electricity electrons flow from the ground over the skin of your body and neutralize any voltage potential that you have due to EMF. When you are using a grounding sheet, you now have two antennas 1) your ungrounded body and 2) the GS which is grounded. If you read the above URL you would see that you have to measure with a meter to find out where your EMF source is coming from to determine the effectiveness of your GS. This is why for some GS work and others they don't.

To avoid the complications of GS, I would just say no to using a GS and use a grounding strap on your wrist or ankle. This means that you are the only antenna, but you are grounded. Yes, one can argue that there are still two antennas, you and the wrist band, but for all intents and purposes here the ground strap is so small an area compared to your body that any two antenna effect is insignificant.

Try using a grounding strap and see if your results are better.

### shilohman — 2013-03-23T22:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81576>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=81569)
>
> This makes me very nervous. My poor husband has been driving the 8' copper stake into our rocky soil for the last hour now because I've been insisting on properly grounding my sheet. We used to just plug the grounding sheet into the outlet, but as soon as I learned better we just stopped using it altogether.
>
> We don't have a voltage meter, so can't check that. Now I'm debating whether we shold give it a try tonight or not even bother. Nothing is easy, huh?!

Have your hubby rent/borrow a hammer drill, buy a hammer drill drill bit that is over eight inches long. If you have solid rock underneath to go through, then buy a drill bit that is larger than the diameter of the grounding rod. If its just real rocky, then buy a drill bit that is a little smaller than the diameter of the grounding rod. If through solid rock with a larger drill bit you can put some concrete cement around the rod. If just rocky soil you should be able to pound the rod into the hole once drilled.

Thanks shilohman. He was finally able to get the stake deep into the ground, but now I'm so hesitant to even use the grounding sheet. I think I'm going to have him look into the wrist straps instead.

Kathylu, my first night on the sheet was quite an odd experience. I found myself twitching and flinching quite often. I'd say at least 20 times, yes. I was a bit nervous about it also.
The second night was oddly quiet, however. And yes, I got headaches the first couple weeks, from dehydration. When I wrote about my strange first night, Jack told me to drink 1.5 gallons of good water per day. Helped tremendously with the headaches.

Currently I don't twitch or flinch, and I don't get headaches any more. I test the sheet every night to be sure of a good ground connection. I'm only drinking a gallon of good water per day now, with no headaches. Personally, I think that first night got rid of a lot of inflammation in my body. And it is helping my back nicely, especially if I spend more time lying flat on my back. Good thing, because I can't afford the chiropractor any more.

I urge you to try it again.

See my post in Quantum Prometheus, and Jack's response:

### enyaw — 2013-03-25T00:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81656>

Are magnetic mattress pads better to sleep with than earthing/grounded sheets? Why? Aren't they both ways of getting more free electrons?

### shilohman — 2013-03-25T19:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81726>

> [MooseMan said:](/goto/post?id=81656)
>
> Are magnetic mattress pads better to sleep with than earthing/grounded sheets? Why? Aren't they both ways of getting more free electrons?

Magnetic mattress pads and earthing/grounded sheets are accomplishing two different things. The mattress pad is providing you with free electrons from the magnets. The mattress pad has nothing to do with grounding you. The sheets are an attempt to ground you while you sleep and protect you against EMF your body receives while you sleep. If the sheets, which are problematic, do ground you, then assuming you have a good earth ground or a clean electrical ground, then you will get free electrons from the ground. A ground that is not clean may also offer some electron protection, but it would depend on if the voltage spikes being induced on the ground are constant, strength, etc. The mattress pad does not protect against EMF received by the body while sleeping. The mattress pad will help neutralize any voltage potential that the body receives while sleeping, but how much protection you get is variable.

IMMHO, you want to 1) eliminate EMF exposure while you sleep. The best way to do this is to sleep in a faraday cage. The second best way is sleep with a grounding strap attached. The third best way is to use a grounding sheet/pad which may or may not work for you. 2) sleep with a magnetic mattress pad in conjunction with number 1 above. The most optimal method would be sleeping in a faraday cage with a magnetic mattress pad. The problem with faraday cages is they are expensive and have to be done correctly to provide protection from EMF. Regardless of what you may read on some websites that sell faraday cages for the bedroom, you always want to ground your faraday cage.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-25T19:15:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81730>

Shilohman,

Since you seem like our forum 'expert' on all of this and I am really just at the beginning stages of trying to understand it all, can I pick your brain for a minute? Just curious what you think of this situation:

\- Sleeping on a 10-gauss magnetico pad + a grounding sheet that is connected to an 8' copper stake in the ground. If the breakers are cut during the night, so now 'active' electricity in the bedroom and this is in a fairly remote area where houses are all on 1-3 acre lots (so limited EMF from neighbors), would you be concerned about the grounding sheet acting as an antenna and picking up EMF?

### shilohman — 2013-03-25T19:29:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-81735>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=81730)
>
> Shilohman,
>
> Since you seem like our forum 'expert' on all of this and I am really just at the beginning stages of trying to understand it all, can I pick your brain for a minute? Just curious what you think of this situation:
>
> \- Sleeping on a 10-gauss magnetico pad + a grounding sheet that is connected to an 8' copper stake in the ground. If the breakers are cut during the night, so now 'active' electricity in the bedroom and this is in a fairly remote area where houses are all on 1-3 acre lots (so limited EMF from neighbors), would you be concerned about the grounding sheet acting as an antenna and picking up EMF?

I am no expert on this, but I have worked as an engineer at several TV stations and I have worked in data centers where protecting expensive servers uses the same grounding techniques.

Can not say definitively about using the grounding sheet, but like charges repel each other and I would think that the grounding sheet would
interfere with the pad. Would have to test to know for sure. To me the best thing to do is to eliminate the grounding sheet and use a ground strap. This is what I personally do.

### JanSz — 2013-03-25T21:32:45+0200

> Ground loop? I had to google that. Thanks.
> I agree with the difference between a house ground and a direct ground. I had tested the house ground, I had a good connection, and I used a surge protector, so perhaps some of the dirty electricity was filtered. I actually think I did get some benefits from using the house ground, especially while using a computer and near other strong EMF.
>
> But grounding directly to earth, especially at night, is quite different. I have already bought another long ground rod to use away from the bedroom, for some fabric covering a chair, and a pad for my feet. Gee, if DH really loved me, he would get me a house with a dirt floor...

I have always known that my Mom and Dad loved me a lot.
Likely because we lived in the house with dirt floor (and a straw roof).

### sooperb — 2013-09-05T20:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-94903>

A bit late to the debate but I found this forum through a google link and just had to join in. I started earthing using a commercial sheet which I plug into my domestic earth. I'm really responding because I see some of your members had a difficult time when they first used the sheet and that very much mirrored my own experience. First night on the sheet and I was expecting, well, not what I got. I noticed the tingling straight away and settled down to enjoy the benefits. I had an awful night, couldn't sleep, felt anxious, was wakeful and by the morning was glad to get up, not rested and not hugely impressed. I tried for a couple of more nights with much the same result. I posted about it on an earthing thread on a blog that I really enjoy, Dr. John Briffa. I had a response from a very helpful chap who assured me this was often reported. He said that he advised all new users to go slowly, to try sitting for an hour or so in the evening with the sheet wrapped round them. Work up gradually and eventually try a full night. I took his advice and after several evenings of exposing myself gradually I put the sheet back on the bed.

The rest as they say is history. For those of you who were put off from trying to earth, maybe taking it slower will help you too.

I completely believe that this way of sleeping has brought me more stable and refreshing sleep, it has sorted out some health issues, reduced my level of anxiety to an all time low and quite honestly I wouldn't be without it. I have read Clinton Ober's book which is how I became interested. I spend as much time as is practical and possible barefoot but in the UK that really is restricted to the summer months, but on holiday I am barefoot as often as I can be.

The sheet was a quite expensive leap of faith but for me it's been worth it. I have become curious about whether the domestic earth is as beneficial as the ground rod so I am about to order one to find out.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-05T21:28:49+0300

Yes that's exactly what I mean . I live in a modern house in the UK and all domestic electrical systems are required to be earthed through the sockets. As for sinking the earth in damp soil, that won't be a problem either lol, we get plenty of rain. A remarkable difference, sounds interesting, I hope I don't buzz too much!

I was wondering, does anyone here use the commercial sheet and if so, do you think that over time the effect lessens or perhaps the sheet wears out a bit?

### nonchalant — 2013-09-11T18:06:15+0300

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=95625)

> do you think that over time the effect lessens or perhaps the sheet wears out a bit?

The effect lessens, for me. After a month or two, I didn't notice any tingling on a nightly basis. However, I hurt my shoulder recently (having to ice it during the day for pain relief) and at night I now feel some tingling in the shoulder and upper back area. I think if you have lots of inflammation, you get tingles or twitching. Inflammation carries a positive charge, and the grounding sheet is negative.

### sooperb — 2013-09-27T22:20:41+0300

So glad the forum is up and running again . I invested in the earthing rod and I am now a bit nonplussed. Since I started using it I have seen a sort of surge of the effects I had when I first tried to use it. On top of that, I am experiencing palpitations every night, most unpleasant and unwelcome as they had pretty much ceased. I was hoping for more benefit, instead I am having to reconsider whether to use the rod or not. Funny, I get like a rolling/rising feeling in my throat, a bit like globus. The sensation rises up and then there's a slight pop and then it pauses until it does it again. There seems to be so much going on of which I never had an inkling.

### Michael Polito — 2016-07-21T22:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-your-own-earthing-sheet.6158/post-198758>

I basically purchased metal tape (40 yards-$12), aluminum tape i think it is. I formed a grid overlapping the tape over a piece of 2x3 foot vinyl. I connected an alligator clip which is connected to the ground plug in my wall outlet. In my bedroom while stand on the floor i registered 3.300 volts. Upon lying directly on the mat without a shirt, the multi meter went down to 0.002. . When i covered it with a cotton bedspread, i registered at between .100 and .045. BTW, I connected the black wire to the grounding mat and was holding the RED prob in my fingers during the test.

You can do the same thing using a piece of aluminum or copper window screen. Make sure the screen is not coated.

# Thread: Rapid weight loss...neurotoxicity..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rapid-weight-loss-neurotoxicity.6160/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MonteD — 2013-03-04T08:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rapid-weight-loss-neurotoxicity.6160/post-78882>

Just wondering here....

If someone went on a paleo low cal diet and lost significant weight (15#s in 3 weeks) is a possible complication the release of neurotoxic viruses/bacteria? Could it be sudden onset?

I ask because I got some crazy shit going on! After losing 15#s in 3 weeks I woke up one morning with right side of my face numb and tingling. No loss of motor skills! I had a bad case of chills, no fever, mild diarrhea and phlegm deep in my esophagus. Every time I would eat I would spit up a lot of phlegm. I know this is gross, but I even had one diarrhea that was pure mucous!!!

Anyway, **ALL of that subsided after a few days**. What I was left with was fatigue, brain fog. My scalp tingles and I got a dull mild headache (for days now).... CAT scan turned out negative. ER doc just said I had a tension headache and gave me some flexerill...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-04T14:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rapid-weight-loss-neurotoxicity.6160/post-78903>

I just answered this and now the answer is gone? Something is up. The reason is an acute pregnenolone syndrome caused by acute dehydration due to loss of water from high carbs starch use most likely........this causes a massive down shift in BDNF and NGF and this can releases any stored viral particles in ganglia.......pretty common in older folks and why they get shingles when they get older.
