### fitness@home — 2013-03-07T12:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79443>

Glad you started your journal! Looking forward to hearing more about your journey Tjcam1.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-07T14:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79452>

I like new journals. Why? It causes you to reflect. Most of us have stopped doing it now because we have technology loading our heads with other peoples ideas........Every morning I post my best thought on FB and today I decided to put it here.

Have you lived half a life to this point? It is time to embrace the small area under the curve of life called the positive 3rd standard deviation. Who do you think you are ? Exceptional should be your only option. To live exceptionally in tail of life you have to have the gut to do what only 5% are willing to do to get it. If your life is severed short are you ready for that consequence? Are you even aware of this possibility? Are you willing to take the risk to empty all your pockets full of miracles to leave here on empty? Well forgive yourself for your faults and your mistakes and move on. Don't make excuses for why you cant get it done. Focus on all the reasons why you must make it happen. Never be limited by other people's limited imaginations. Today go get it.

### caroline — 2013-03-08T01:17:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79553>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79452)
>
> I like new journals. Why? It causes you to reflect. Most of us have stopped doing it now because we have technology loading our heads with other peoples ideas........Every morning I post my best thought on FB and today I decided to put it here.
>
> Have you lived half a life to this point? It is time to embrace the small area under the curve of life called the positive 3rd standard deviation. Who do you think you are ? Exceptional should be your only option. To live exceptionally in tail of life you have to have the gut to do what only 5% are willing to do to get it. If your life is severed short are you ready for that consequence? Are you even aware of this possibility? Are you willing to take the risk to empty all your pockets full of miracles to leave here on empty? Well forgive yourself for your faults and your mistakes and move on. Don't make excuses for why you cant get it done. Focus on all the reasons why you must make it happen. Never be limited by other people's limited imaginations. Today go get it.

That is what the words ....."when you know better...you do better" means to me. Forgive yourself for your faults and shortcomings and mistakes and move on.

I love what you said "take the risk to empty your pocket full of miracles to leave here on empty" That is my new motto!! thanks...so little time - so much to do.....

I hope you are still going to post your thoughts on FB?

### Tjcam1 — 2013-03-08T04:39:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79595>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=79553)
>
> That is what the words ....."when you know better...you do better" means to me. Forgive yourself for your faults and shortcomings and mistakes and move on.
>
> I love what you said "take the risk to empty your pocket full of miracles to leave here on empty" That is my new motto!! thanks...so little time - so much to do.....
>
> I hope you are still going to post your thoughts on FB?

Thanks Jack and Caroline! I have absolutely been living half a life, based on others expectations! I know I can live better, be better and feel better and I have been scared to do it by myself, wanting really to just belong, therefore not living how I've needed to for most of my life!
Now I have decided that its really ok to not always fit in, it's more important to me now ( motherhood has done this) to be inspired and to inspire, so that's what my aim is to do, alone, or otherwise. Wish me luck!

So I'm going to break this journal up into 5 parts- song of the day, vibe (my thoughts), food, training, and sleep. This is to primarily assist me in looking at the many facets of this reset, to stop me from just focussing on just one or two.

Song for the day- "Anyway you want it"
[http://m.youtube.com/#/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&desktop\_uri=/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&gl=GB](http://m.youtube.com/#/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&desktop_uri=%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DatxUuldUcfI&gl=GB)
\- try to watch this without smiling or grooving just a little bit!

Sleep- less than awesome, my girls had the most horrendous bout of gastro, which saw us into the emrgancey section of our hospital 3 times! The best effort this week was a broken 7 hours and that was after 5 days of not much sleep a all!
Girls are much better now, thank goodness so we'll see.

Training- weight training 4 times this week with some HIIT and sprints. Cold showers every day for about 10 minutes. The C.T thing is so new to me so I thought cold showers may be a great place to start? I have felt exhausted most of this week so my training didn't have much energy, but I showed up and put on some tunes and I was good to go!

Food- eggs, seafood, nori, greens, avocado, prawns, scallops, tuna and salmon, oh and bacon and coconut oil. I am NOT used to eating like this but I am LOVIN it and trying so hard to not to count these calories in my head! I am a reformed (because I said so! ) binge eater/excersiser, low fat, fruit eating vegetarian, who weighed and counted everything for years!
Did I mention I was also a 6 cups of coffee a day gal who never slept and was TOTALLY miserable. As I said- 'reformed'. Yep, as of today.

Vibe- I am hard on myself and have a 'parent type' voice that often pipes up in my head and says things like- 'don't do this, don't do that, try harder, that wasn't good enough.... Etc!' Hence my former binge eating/ excersising problem. I have absolutely had enough of this voice being the primary in my thought life (that pops up especially when I am tired), so I am counteracting this by practising mindfulness, and focussing on what I have achieved! This requires the most effort out of everything on my part. But I think it may be one of the keys to living optimally for me!
I need to be the best person I can be to myself right now, regardless of all circumstances, especially to myself.
Xxxxxx

### Tjcam1 — 2013-03-10T05:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79903>

Hi everyone,
Thanks Jack for your thoughts! I absolutely have been driven in the past of what other people have thought/said what I should do with my life versus my own intuition and heart. Hopefully I can work in changing this over the next while!

To make things easier I'm breaking up this journal into 5 parts:
Song for the day, 'Vibe' (my inner thought life), sleep, food and training!

Song for the day: Ben Harper - With My Own Two Hands -
[www.youtube.com/watch?v=aEnfy9qfdaU](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aEnfy9qfdaU)

Try to watch this without being slightly touched! I dare ya!

'Vibe' ( thought processes)- Have SUCKED for the most part this week! Does anyone else out there have an inner 'parent' voice that just sometimes just nags you constantly? I have found this voice to be louder than normal this week- "could do this better, shouldn't have done that, that wasn't good enough etc" these challenges can work to my benefit I know, but often it's just irritating! I also think this sets me back, and is definitely bought on by lack of sleep!
My goal is to be a lot better with mindfulness and looking at achievements ( no matter how small) this week!

Sleep- was not awesome this week! My twin girls had terrible gastro, and so I was up most nights tending to them or worrying about them which couldn't be helped! They are improving wonderfully now in which I hope my sleep will too!

Food- eggs, prawns, oysters, tuna, coconut oil, bacon, avocado, nori, onion, chicken, and the list continues.....
Big achievement for me was throwing my stevia down the sink and having 1 coffee! This doesn't sound like much but I used to be a chronic sweet tooth, 6 cups of joe a day gal! I also haven't counted a calorie or weighed a morsel which is HUGE as I was an avid counter before now- 3 habits have already changed!

Training- cold showers all week for at least 10 minutes. This is not much I know, I hope though its an ok beginning?
4 weight training sessions incorporating HIIT and sprints! I never thought I wold enjoy a cld shower but I am totally addicted to it and the freshness in my body!

Also thanks to a tip from Inger I used seaweed on my face las night! Totally awesome facial ladies!!!

### Inger — 2013-03-10T07:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79908>

Nice song Tjcam1, and welcome! You are doing so well...!

Instead of listening to this stupid "nagging voice".. live 100% each moment, I bet it will help! To put my focus on the beautiful things, and always keep my eyes on where I am going helps me.. Life is NOW... You are so blessed to have those little sweet twin girls!

### Tjcam1 — 2013-03-10T13:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79928>

Thanks Inger! I will certinally apply this advice! Xx

# Thread: Thyroid lab interpretations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-07T03:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79412>

I'm looking at my thyroid labs and I think I'm seeing what's going on, but this is all new to me.

TSH 2.68 mIU/L(.35-5)
FT3 4.4 pmol/L (2.6-5.7)
FT4 11 pmol/L (12-22)
RT3 13.4 ng/dL (9.2 - 24.1)

If I convert my ft# to ng/dL I get 56.6 and that gives me a FT3/RT3 ratio of 42, which is over the 20 minimum.

Because T4 is low and T3 is mid range - it looks to me like my issue is I"m not generating enough T4, and possibly some issue in converting to T3. I started upping my iodine (seaweed) today, as I already eat a lot of fish, to help with conversion to T3. (read BG 12 last night).

Any other thoughts are welcome!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-07T03:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79415>

youre not too bad.........TSH is a bit higher than I like but your FT3 is not bad......you need the photoelectric effect and epi paleo Rx pushed hard and cut bad water......you are on your way Diane. I just got to convince you being naked is cool. LOL.

### diane — 2013-03-07T04:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79421>

Excellent - that's what I thought - not too bad. I feel like the key is in the ignition and it's trying to over. I'm just waiting for the engine to start.

I sleep naked - that's a start! LOL. Just need to find a private place to get outside naked.

### Destiny — 2013-03-07T14:00:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79450>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=79421)
>
> Excellent - that's what I thought - not too bad. I feel like the key is in the ignition and it's trying to over. I'm just waiting for the engine to start.
>
> I sleep naked - that's a start! LOL. Just need to find a private place to get outside naked.

A private place is an issue for me too. What about itsy bitsy bikini? Does it have to be naked???

# Thread: Lets talk about optimal beds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freshveggies — 2013-03-07T18:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79487>

I have been thinking about my bed that is very full of metal coils that act like an antenna. Here are a couple of articles that talk about beds:

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2010/08/18/your-box-spring-mattress-could-be-acting-as-a-gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx)
[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2009/04/30/Why-Where-You-Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx)

Now to find a bed that doesn't have coils and that is comfortable. The ones I have looked at so far are not around here and I would have to fly across the country to just try it out. Some do have a return policy. The other aspect that I was also concerned about is chemical free.

another option. What if I got a regular foam mattress--not memory foam as I do not do well with those - and encased it with this:
[http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html](http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html)

pore size is 2.9 microns. I wonder if that would stop the chemicals from leaching out?

Any thoughts?

### dkenward@yahoo.com — 2013-03-07T18:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79488>

What about futons? I slept on one for years and found it comfortable. I still have one I use for a guest room bed and for taking naps.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T18:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79489>

WE've got a sleep number bed - which I love - though I now wonder if its a good thing? and if maybe going with a memory foam type mattress might be a better solution?

We've had the magnetico sleep pads now for close to 1 month and I love that thing... the kids are sleeping great - less night wakings etc...

as far as emfs - we've gotten very good at turning off both the wifi and router when we use it and most of the time its turned off (going to be adding the printer/computer/monitor to that as well. duh (face palm - why wasn't I thinking about that?)

I loved sleeping on tatami mats when I lived in Japan...very comfortable - there are times that I'd love to have a tatami room for sleeping in... awesomeness (now how do I talk DH into that????

### cantweight — 2013-03-07T19:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79490>

We are due for a new bed (13 yrs on this one!) and was planning on memory foam, but I would suspect they have mad VOC. Natural mattresses are way out of my price range. Any thoughts?

Christi

### shilohman — 2013-03-07T19:38:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79491>

Something you might try since you already have a sleep number mattress is attach a ground wire to the box springs and bed frame, this way any stray voltages that are picked up via the coils/frame can not act as an antenna and expose you. Turning off the circuit breaker to your bedroom would eliminate any EMF from you sleep number electronics. Add a grounding mat grounded to earth to your bed and there really isn't much more you can do.

### Hope — 2013-03-07T19:44:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79493>

I love our lifekind organic latex mattress which we got from lifekind.com...the best gift ever. No coils, no nothing. I had to fight for the non-fire-retardant cover of it with a note from my chiropractor (as I didn't' want to have to breathe in those chemicals), but we did it. It's six years old and still feels new to me, but a bear to move if you have to ever move it. I also love my pillow...though it's not organic  it has done wonders for my neck...and it has a gel cover that keeps my head a little cooler, especially in summer...I really believe in it. Maybe it's just placebo but I love it anyway. Check that out here if you like.....

[http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/](http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/)

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T19:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79495>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=79491)
>
> Something you might try since you already have a sleep number mattress is attach a ground wire to the box springs and bed frame, this way any stray voltages that are picked up via the coils/frame can not act as an antenna and expose you. Turning off the circuit breaker to your bedroom would eliminate any EMF from you sleep number electronics. Add a grounding mat grounded to earth to your bed and there really isn't much more you can do.

That's a great idea shiloman... the one great thing about the sleep number - is the base is plastic & the actual mattress is memory foam and air tanks. but grounding it would be a great idea....only just recently discovered that our bedroom & office outlets are not grounded... going to have to remedy that. I need to have DH help me put a grounding sheet on our bed and ground it outside....

one of the best places I've slept in - is at my aunts house that's about 1hr north of los cabos - she's off the gird, has a water cistern, 150m from the Pacific Ocean & limited cell service - you need a satellite capability in your cell phone to get it to work. Every single bed frame is made of adobe, with just a mattress on in... best sleep ever there... now I know why!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79509>

My optimal bed as i said in an earlier post is simple a reindeer skin in the wild. Do sleep outside for about 100 nights a year but will start doing it even more soon. At "home" a simple bamboo mat with a sheet.

> [http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html](http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html)

**Bedding Thoughts abound....Cannot yet post at 'Raise head of bed 10-30 degrees or leave flat?' thread but if anyone is looking to Elevate their head at night.I found Avana Elevator Mattress a USA company out of Atlanta... they have 5", 7" and mentioned a 12" as well. Fits below current mattress and above box springs or slats. They sell on _Amazon_, also saw on _eBay_ and _Overstocks_ and at their own web site.**
**[https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html](https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html)**
**I have lots of questions and have left word for _Avana Comfort_ to get back to me.

Just thought I'd post this if someone is looking for a wedge to elevate.

I spent years looking for a 'healthy' bed... settled on a very comfortable but very heavy king 100% natural latex with 2 twin slat foundation and now interested in elevating head as well as adding a Magnetico Sleep Pad. With Magnetico so many choices and I can't imagine how this will layer and if slat foundation will handle all the weight. Good thing I am losing weight on the Epi-Paleo Rx.**
**I am also 'princess and the pea' sensitive and hen pecking picky.
**....seems our bed is kinda like a complex puzzle and also similar to a boat as a bottomless expense.****

# Thread: Kicking it "Old School"  - what have you done this week w/o the help of technology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79506>

So, on monday I had to pick up my cousin at the airport - she flew in from Kenya. she lost her iphone sim card that allows it to work in the US. So what did we do - I went to pick her up, and instead of going to the cell phone waiting area - I just drove around waiting for her to come out, after clearing customs/immigrations. I waited to get to the airport 1hr after her flight landed and had to circle for 15-20min - but it was easy we connected.

As I was doing this - I really began to understand how "Dependent" we've become on technology - especially after Peter's post in the Technology addiction post. But the reality is we're not dependent - we're addicted and we really don't need this crap anyway.... yeah it helps make our lives easier but at what costs?

so in what ways have you been kicking it "Old School"?

### prAna303 — 2013-03-07T21:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79508>

Stopped using technology to "hook up", today i simply show up at friends and if the are not there, so what. If someone needs to ge in touch with me, go to my door and knock, find me at the local cafÃ© or send a letter. I never use a phone today, i simply stay present in the world and boy such nice meetings one can do there.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:27:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79512>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=79508)
>
> Stopped using technology to "hook up", today i simply show up at friends and if the are not there, so what. If someone needs to ge in touch with me, go to my door and knock, find me at the local cafÃ© or send a letter. I never use a phone today, i simply stay present in the world and boy such nice meetings one can do there.

Love this! I want to get to this point....

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-07T23:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79533>

I just don't answer my phone most of the time because I don't keep it on my body. This gets people to stop calling/texting and they will use email instead. This way I can get on the computer and do a check in with friends. But it's on my time and trying to cut the computer time down.

Although it is raining today the weather is getting nicer. I'm starting to get my DH to eat dinner outside and not in front of the TV. Once we are out there he will sit in front of the fire pit for a few hours. Cutting his EMF exposure even if he doesn't know there is a method to my madness!!!

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T02:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79576>

My cell phone -that is one habit I don't need to kick. Never was a big fan of it. I don't get that some people can talk and talk on their phones all the time. My family and friends always complain that I never answer my phone. I am famous for finding some messages a week or more later. Very often, my phone is just dead since I forget to charge it.

I don't know why, but I don't like it, it just bothers me.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-08T03:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79586>

Just for kicks this week, I used the paper bank statement and balanced my checkbook (without a calculator) to see if I still had that skill. Remember paper and pencils?

It's much easier to log into your account and keep up with it daily...but if technology ever takes a crap I can still manage the old way.

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T14:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79640>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=79586)
>
> Just for kicks this week, I used the paper bank statement and balanced my checkbook (without a calculator) to see if I still had that skill. Remember paper and pencils?
>
> It's much easier to log into your account and keep up with it daily...but if technology ever takes a crap I can still manage the old way.

It is also good to keep your brain sharp!

### BJK77 — 2013-03-07T21:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79511>

I always take my DHEA and Pregnenolone in the morning with my BAB. I had to start work extra early this morning and it just hit me that I forgot to take them! I can't recall if there was a reason behind my taking them first thing in the AM or if I just did it because it was (supposed to be!) easier to remember then.

So, the question is should I take them when I get home around 4 PM or is that too late and I should just skip them? When do you all take them? My gut says it should be fine since DHEA helps with sleep anyway, but I just wanted to make sure.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79515>

I think you should be ok... I take an afternoon/evening dose... and no such issues that impact sleep or anything else from my perspective...

### diane — 2013-03-07T21:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79517>

I would say try for a few days and find out if it works for you.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-07T22:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79521>

# Thread: G'Day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-03-08T02:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79567>

Hey all,

I'm new to the forums here, and mainly interested in adding/experimenting with CT in my personal regimen.

A little about me:
I have been slow carb, transitioned to primal since January 2011. By July 2011, I had lost ~30 lbs of fat to achieve a <10% BF, which I have maintained since. Also in July 2011, I began intermittent fasting on a daily basis and
currently only eat 1 meal/day in the evening.

Additionally, I am interested in the impacts of paleo and CT on individuals with cystic fibrosis, which my girlfriend has. She is in the process of transitioning to a more paleo diet currently, and considering CT due to how degrading cystic fibrosis can be.

### caroline — 2013-03-08T10:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79616>

Welcome from down under.....

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79625>

Welcome! I have a question... If you fast daily, it isn't intermitTent really, is it? What is the rationale behind it?

### sjoshua — 2013-03-09T02:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79761>

Thanks for the welcomes!!

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=79625)
>
> Welcome! I have a question... If you fast daily, it isn't intermitTent really, is it? What is the rationale behind it?

My understanding was "Intermittent Fasting" referred to any eating pattern that possesses (scheduled) altering periods of fasting/feeding. So I believe intermittent would still apply. Maybe a better way of saying it would be "daily intermittent fasting" to avoid confusion?

The rationale is mainly driven by efficiency. I no longer wanted to have to spend the time to make and eat breakfast every morning, so I had eliminated that meal first (note I was already LS at this point). Once I realized how easy it was to do that, I figured why bother having to make a lunch either? Now I only have to prep, cook, clean once/day in the evening when I have the most free time. And, an added bonus is I get to take daily walks on my "lunch" break!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T03:13:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-79895>

SJoshua, has your GF started any CT? I was thinking even a cold wet washcloth might be a good way to start.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-12T00:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday.6229/post-80104>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79895)
>
> SJoshua, has your GF started any CT? I was thinking even a cold wet washcloth might be a good way to start.

Nonchalant: Sorry for not having replied back to your suggestions in my other thread yet... I have now tried to post twice, but both times recieved an error that a moderator has to approve my posts, so here's hoping they show up soon!

She will be starting CT this week, and I think the washcloth is brilliant as it won't impede her breathing. That will be a good first step!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: CT impact on individuals with CF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-03-08T02:33:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79572>

I have searched the internet the best I could, but was not able to find any data regarding the impact/experience of CT on individuals that have cystic fibrosis.

The reason I ask is my girlfriend is a mid 20's female who was diagnosed with cystic fibrosis at 3 months old. My concern is her body, especially lungs and digestive system, have degraded significantly from the disease. Her current lung function (Pulminary Function Test) is ~50-55% of "ideal" for her age/height/body type.

Does anyone happen to know of other cases of cystic fibrosis patients who implemented CT?

Any recommendations for modifying the default protocol to cater to a cystic fibrosis patient? (Example, due to the lower lung capacity she will have difficulty with holding her breath for the initial face dips. Should she phase in another method?)

Would there be an alternate skin tempurature to stop at instead of 50-55 deg. for a "normal" person?

She is definitely going to discuss this with her doctors before beginning, but I am concerned that they will tell her to stay clear of it sheerly due to the fact they know nothing about it. Any input would be appreciated!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-08T02:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79575>

Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.

She also might want to consider d-ribose.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-08T14:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79649>

Hi Sjoshua!

Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:

[http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-08T15:26:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79652>

Wait til you read the QB bipedalism blog........ties into this whole thing.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-09T02:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79758>

Hey all, thanks for the great welcome and quick replies!

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79575)
>
> Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
> I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.
>
> She also might want to consider d-ribose.

Nonchalant: Ok, that sounds good. We will start small and keep a close eye on everything as we ramp up the time and transition to ice packs/baths. She is going to begin phasing in the CT next week. I felt bad that she was going to be doing it alone, and decided to jump in head first myself and started doing cold showers yesterday... WOW! they are invigurating, but I like it. I think I am going to start cold baths when she starts face dunks.

Regarding your second note, I haven't read much about d-ribose yet... could you point me to a good article for some background information and practical uses (such as this)?

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=79649)
>
> Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:
>
> [http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

BJK77: Great article! That is actually one of the regular treatments she uses: an inhaled saline solution that was actually developed by CF patients that realized how beneficial the ocean air was to their breathing. We do get down to the ocean when we can, but being ~45 minutes away makes it difficult more often. Moving even closer to the ocean is definitely a hope for the future. This past summer we relocated from upstate, NY out here to San Jose, CA for the beneficial effects of being in a more temperate, beautiful climate.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)
>
> Wait til you read the QB bipedalism blog........ties into this whole thing.

Jack, I just stumbled onto your site indirectly through Mark's Daily Apple about a week ago and I can't get enough! Thank you for providing such a logical, detailed analysis on so many important aspects of understanding how these "human machines" we occupy function. I haven't came across the QB bipedalism blog as of yet though... is that still forthcoming? Either way, I am looking forward to reading how these tie together.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-12T00:08:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80103>

Sorry that I haven't replied back to this yet... I actually typed up a whole post and submitted it, but there was an error that said something about a moderator having to approve the post... Good thought on the washcloth, that my be an even better way for her to phase in since it won't hinder breathing as much. Your note on d-ribose: I am not familiar with this. Could you please point me to a good article or source so I can learn what it does and specifically how it could help here? Thanks!

Our society would be in a much better place than putting the Rx bandaid on everything.

As far as the blog post you mentioned above, I haven't come across that one yet. Is it filed under another name, or is it still forthcoming? Thanks in advance!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80529>

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-14T21:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80540>

I haven't seen any posts come through from you sjoshua that needed to be approved. At least not today or yesterday. Maybe that darn internet monster ate them! The same one that keeps eating Jack's webinars! LOL

### sjoshua — 2013-03-20T19:10:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81198>

Ok, one more time. let's hope it posts this time!!

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79575)
>
> Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
> I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.
>
> She also might want to consider d-ribose.

Nonchalant, thank you for the recommendations. We have been doing ice water-soaked wash cloths placed over her face (with a second folded over her forehead at the same time) for 15 minutes every other day since last Wednesday for a nice, easy phase in.

Additionally, I did some research and picked up some D-Ribose, which she has been loading on at 5g 2-3x/day since CT started.

So far things are going well. I have a skin thermometer and have been measuring her before and immediately after temps on both cheek and forehead; by the end (at at intervals throughout) we are keeping her face temps at around 64-67. Do you think we should try to go colder, or not yet?

At one point last night about 4-5 minutes in, she said her temples were too cold and she had to take a break for ~15 seconds; her forehead temp at that point had been 64.

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=79649)
>
> Hi Sjoshua!
>
> Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:

BJK77: Thanks for the welcome, and also great article! I have seen it before but its always nice to see when other people are knowledgeable about CF, since it is quite rare. Although we are in San Jose, we are still about ~30-45 minutes drive form the ocean... We do get down there when possible!

I believe that a CF surfer discovered how beneficial the salty sea air was, and they have since made it into an inhaled Rx. Using a saline solution in a nebulizer, she can experience a similar effect on a daily basis

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)

> Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:
>
> [http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

BJK77: Thanks for the welcome, and also great article! I have seen it before but its always nice to see when other people are knowledgeable about CF, since it is quite rare. Although we are in San Jose, we are still about ~30-45 minutes drive form the ocean... We do get down there when possible!

I believe that a CF surfer discovered how beneficial the salty sea air was, and they have since made it into an inhaled Rx. Using a saline solution in a nebulizer, she can experience a similar effect on a daily basis

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)

Jack: I stumbled on your site indirectly through Mark's Daily Apple a few of weeks ago now, and I have not been able to get enough of your articles! I have read through quite a bit, and (although occasionally above my understanding) have really enjoyed your logical perspective on the body's functioning. ### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-21T01:24:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81240>

This is a post from sjoshua:

Ok, one more time. let's hope it posts this time!!

Quote Originally Posted by Nonchalant View Post
Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.

She also might want to consider d-ribose.
Nonchalant, thank you for the recommendations. We have been doing ice water-soaked wash cloths placed over her face (with a second folded over her forehead at the same time) for 15 minutes every other day since last Wednesday for a nice, easy phase in.

Additionally, I did some research and picked up some D-Ribose, which she has been loading on at 5g 2-3x/day since CT started.

So far things are going well. I have a skin thermometer and have been measuring her before and immediately after temps on both cheek and forehead; by the end (at at intervals throughout) we are keeping her face temps at around 64-67. Do you think we should try to go colder, or not yet?

At one point last night about 4-5 minutes in, she said her temples were too cold and she had to take a break for ~15 seconds; her forehead temp at that point had been 64.

Quote Originally Posted by BJK77 View Post

Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81243>

Patty, Thanks for posting that! Any idea why it wasn't working for me??

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T02:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81247>

Sjosh, glad it wasn't all lost. I know that iodine is also in ocean spray, not just salt. If you stand where the waves are churning up the water, iodine is thrown into the air.

Yeah, Jack is teasing about the QB biped blog. It isn't up yet, though we are anxious to see it.

About the face CT, I'd suggest whatever your GF wants to do. Continue as she is doing, get colder, dunk in water, whatever. Does she feel that she might be adapting to it? Does she like it? Does it make her feel refreshed/energized/happy?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-21T03:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81262>

Sjoshua, no clue why you couldn't post before......but glad you were able to get something posted now!

### sjoshua — 2013-03-21T17:47:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81316>

Thanks to Patty Cakes recommend, I reached out to the [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) e-mail for help about my post. They thought I may have just been posting too long of a response and it was getting flagged as spam. No fixes for it, so I will just have to post more, shorter replies in the future I guess

To be completely honest, she wants almost nothing to do with the CT, and at this point she only thinks it makes her cold. I have explained some of the "why" behind it, but the reason she is doing it at all is because we are amidst a 30-day experiment during which she is letting me control her diet, exercise, journaling, relaxation, etc. aspects of daily life, to see if the lifestyle ideas I have for her are more or less beneficial than the boiler plate stuff she hears from her doctors.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T18:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81326>

When I first started the CT protocol, I only did face dunks for about 15 seconds, and only repeated them 2 or 3 times each day. Can she do 15 seconds in ice water? Even if only once per day? She may find it surprising. Better than the face cloths. When I first did it, I was a bit scared, but I was amazed that I didn't feel the cold very much. It's like the nerves in my face just shut off the cold sensation. The second and third dunks, however, felt much colder than the first.

Sorry that part of your 30 days was affected by posting issues.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-25T18:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81719>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=81326)
>
> When I first started the CT protocol, I only did face dunks for about 15 seconds, and only repeated them 2 or 3 times each day. Can she do 15 seconds in ice water? Even if only once per day? She may find it surprising. Better than the face cloths. When I first did it, I was a bit scared, but I was amazed that I didn't feel the cold very much. It's like the nerves in my face just shut off the cold sensation. The second and third dunks, however, felt much colder than the first.
>
> Sorry that part of your 30 days was affected by posting issues.

nonchalant: Thanks again for the feedback. This week I was planning to switch her over to face dunks, but last night she made it apparent that she did not want to move past the wash cloths, so I am not going to force things further.

The goal of my 30 days was to put as many positive concepts in place regarding her health, nutrition, fitness, etc. I hope that she takes away as many of them as possible, but I know that maintaining CT is something someone can really only decide for themself. It was the wild card I threw in

At leasth the take-away from this is I have been enjoying my cold showers daily; they are definitely getting easier and are a really great way to wake up in the morning! I plan to continue them, but at this point I am 99% confident that my girlfriend will not be sticking to the CT after the experiment ends.

# Thread: Adjusting to daylight savings time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-08T04:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-79590>

I'm assuming that daylight savings time can throw the circadian rhythms out of sync. Does anyone have thoughts on how to compensate? I'm wondering if using melatonin for a couple of days will help, similar to a jet lag situation. I know I always feel like crap for a few days after.

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-79620>

Not sure that it would throw the rhythms out of whack.. It's really us adjusting to the fact that we had imposed time on out lifestyle.. Really, if there were no clocks and starting times, we'd adjust our sleep wake cycles to the light anyway..

### mrc — 2020-11-01T21:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-293187>

[https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1322919465693122561](https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1322919465693122561)
"Standard time is equivalent to living and eating things grown locally under the sun.
Daylight savings time is like eating Keto/Paleo/Vegan snacks made in a blue light lab with no protection of photosynthetic algorithms nature provides.

11/6/16
Daylight savings time CREATES a circadian mismatch you need to hack. I do it every year. DST give us a 17% rise in fatal traffic accidents
Between 1986 and 1995, fatal traffic accidents rose 17% the Monday following the switch to Daylight Saving Time.

Source: University of Colorado. Want some more? Over $9,000,000 in electricity cost due to DST and this tells you why the government allows DST to persist. It is about money. And that electric power grid causes circadian disruption because of its artificial light and the electric power grids electric and magnetic fields.

A 2006 report found DST led to a 1 percent overall rise in residential electricity use in Indiana, costing the the state an extra $9 million. Researchers said that cost could be even higher in other regions of the U.S.
Source: National Bureau of Economic Research. More? There is a 68% increase in days lost due to workplace injury

From 1983 to 2006, there was a 68% increase in lost working days due to workplace injury on the Monday following the switch to DST.
Source: American Psychological Association. More? 25% increase in heart attack risk
A 2014 study showed that the hour of sleep lost when switching to DST can increase your risk of a heart attack by 25%.

[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...-us-energy-but-who-uses-ene/1543507012380314/](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/daylight-savings-time-was-created-in-1918-to-save-the-us-energy-but-who-uses-ene/1543507012380314/)

9/23/20
Daylight "Saving" is morning robbery—bad for sleep and health.

# Thread: Wine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-08T04:19:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79592>

I threatened to start a thread on wine in someone's journal.....

Ode to wine.
Yes, I love it.

Similar to what other people have said, I think I just have to give it up. Although I love it so. I'm assuming that's related to my addiction to all things sugar too. I have definitely cut back, and most times, I have a glass or two, although occasionally more. I was hoping that the water in it was beneficial  but read Jack's post about only 1 glass.

Share your thoughts on wine! And maybe drink a glass while posting. LOL.

### endless — 2013-03-08T04:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79598>

Don't tempt me.....

I too love wine. I used to be fond of beer as well, but since going gluten and then grain free, I've switched almost entirely to wine. I am also fond of really good tequila. I have a hard time stopping at one glass....I usually like to have two, or sometimes even three. Unfortunately it makes my sleep SUCK terribly. I'm not sure if it's the dehydration that causes that or what but it's getting to the point that it's not worth it.

### Inger — 2013-03-08T09:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79614>

I love wine too.. as a waitress I know quite a lot about them too and I am picky... Bad quality wine makes me feel way worse.. so if I take a glass it must be high quality.

I need to be very careful with wine too. If I drink even one glass too often I have the feeling I get a tiny bit depressed somehow. I also can get pain in my hip bones from it when I drink 2-3 glasses. So to feel the best I need to drink very little. I am happy that wine is expensive here and I have too little money to buy it often. That is saving me!

I really feel I am way better of without it. At least now in winter. I really feel I tolerate cold way less when I do alcohol (I only drink wine, nothing else). I can feel it the days after too, I think so at least..

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79623>

No more than three glasses and I still bounce out of bed in the morning.. Sleep all the way through, feel rested and good all day... Only since turning off the lights half arsed CT, and grounding a lot though. Before than I took codeine to make sure I slept ok.. I have been thinking I need to ease up though. If I want to kick off the rest of the weight.. I know it messes with hormones

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T13:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79632>

Yes, I love it too but my max has always been 1 glass or even less. I know I don't tolerate much of alcohol.

Lately, I am not drinking any. I had 1/2 glass during Christmas and got very dehydrated during the night (I was thirsty like a camel). Then, I felt like having a sort of hangover in the morning.
It was very unpleasant so that really spoiled my appetite for wine.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-08T14:31:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79642>

Wine is one of the few things I find I really miss . . . . that and chocolate chip cookies! I always found it strange in the past that red and white wine affected me differently. Of course this was always drinking more than one glass, but white wine seemed to give me a headache whereas red wine messed with my sleep . . . or maybe it was the other way around! It's been so long since I've had more than one glass that I can't remember now

I think sulfites are a problem for me now too and I know there are a few wines without it still seems like too much of a bother to go looking for them, so for the time being I'm wine-less

### janagram — 2013-03-08T18:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79675>

I adore wine and my DH makes his own (we have about 75 vines in yard, but he buys grapes from vintners too). His wine is awesome......but I drink far less of it than I used to or than I am inclined....leaving me at about 7 glasses a week. Oh, and I like many other spirits too, but partake vey rarely. I had a "skinny" margarita yesterday, tequila, limes, soda water. Loved it.
Oh, I was hoping that a good islay (pronounced "eye-la") scotch would be full of iodine and be "prescribed"!!! (research!)

### Rachelle — 2013-03-08T21:19:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79694>

I gave up wine and only drink a good vodka if and when I drink.  I get a hangover with wine and do not with vodka. Have no clue why

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T21:26:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79695>

Only the good stuff, only a glass, and only every couple of months. I savor it when I drink it now and really taste it. If I drink two glasses or more my sleep is disrupted and I get the dehydrated thirsty thing going on in the middle of the night as well. It used to be a form of entertainment but now it is pure pleasure since it's so rare.

# Thread: Finished round of HCG...questions about getting lab work drawn
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finished-round-of-hcg-questions-about-getting-lab-work-drawn.6233/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 8

### sums-sums — 2013-03-08T05:59:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finished-round-of-hcg-questions-about-getting-lab-work-drawn.6233/post-79607>

I just finished my 4 round of HCG. I am interested in getting lab work done to measure thyroid/hormones/glucose/HS CRP/ect...

I think I read somewhere on this forum that I needed to wait about a month or two into P4 for lab work to be drawn. The reason being the HCG can throw off some of the labs and it is best to wait about 3 months after P3 to get this lab work done.

Can anyone confirm or deny this? I am really interested in regulating my hormones & thyroid as I have a sneaking suspicion that they are out of whack.

Thanks in advance for your help & direction.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-08T14:16:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finished-round-of-hcg-questions-about-getting-lab-work-drawn.6233/post-79638>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finished-round-of-hcg-questions-about-getting-lab-work-drawn.6233/post-79927>

### MsYo — 2013-03-12T22:13:43+0200
